title: Notarial Copy - Heshuysen & Compagnie / James Balmer, Dominica Plantation Paaschal Mortgage, Amsterdam, December 21, 1777
description: Notarial copy (Copie) of a complex financial transaction recorded before notary Hermanus de Wolff Junior, Amsterdam, December 21, 1777. Concerns a mortgage (Mortgage of Hypotheeq) on Plantation Paaschal, situated on the island of Dominica, originally established August 13, 1772, by James Balmer, London merchant, for the benefit of Adolf Jan Heshuysen, Floris Visscher Heshuysen, and Frans Jacob Heshuysen, composing the firm Adolf Jan Heshuysen en Compagnie, Haarlem. The mortgage secures a sum of 7,100 pounds sterling. The plantation had earlier been encumbered with life annuities of 1,269 pounds and 10 shillings per annum on various lives in Great Britain. The document records the formal presentation of this mortgage before the Amsterdam notary.
creator: Hermanus de Wolff Junior (Notaris); Adolf Jan Heshuysen en Compagnie
notes: Amsterdam notarial copy; Plantation Paaschal, Dominica; Heshuysen & Comp. / James Balmer; mortgage £7,100; life annuities £1,269.10/yr; notary Hermanus de Wolff Junior; December 21, 1777
transcription:
# Transcription

**Original (Dutch):**

Copia.

Op den negen en twintigsten December des jaars zeventien honderd zeven en zeventig, compareerden voor my CORNELIS VAN HOMRIGH, openbaar Notaris te Amsterdam, by den Hove van Holland geatesteerd, De Heeren REYNIER ISAAC en HENDRIK ROTARIUS, beiden in den Hove van Civolie justitie der Colonie van Surinamen, &c. &c. door den Heer FREDRIK CORNELIS STOLKERT, zo voor syne justitie en de in meldese Plantagien, mitsgaders als ingevolge treue onderteekening, als Eigenem der Acte van Hypotheeck vermelde Decurateurs, Gemaatigde zo van den Heer ISAAC STOLKERT's, Vrouve DOROTHIA ELIZABETH WERNER, Echtelieden, de Eigenaars der eerste tweede paaren van de twalve Plantagien, geletterd, en verder door drie comparanten was voldoende betragt dedzalve Heer FREDRIK CORNELIS STOLKERT, also in syn privé of nog in onopgeelde guarantee qualiteit, en dus ook in de namen der voorenoerde Commitanten heelt behend, respectively te samen en anderen in het byzonder en voor hetgeld sonder cenige finalstelling, onder behoevlyke renunciatie, wel en onderlyks schuldig te syn aan ende ten behoeven van hen Heeren Comparanten, een Somma van Duizend honderd en twintig duizend Guldens Hollands Courant Geld's, fruitende eeritelijk uit hoofste oin der zake van en Somma van Twee honderd en acht duizend Guldens, Hollands Courant Geld, door hen Heeren Comparanten mede een behoeven van hun Heer FREDRIK CORNELIS STOLKERT privé en qualitaetque uites ir febtiecen ter volding van het Hypotheeck ten behoeven van de Heeren H. HARTSINGH, S. D. PLICHOT en A. WALLIS'en gespecificeert, Corresponants mede een behoeven van hun Heer FREDRIK CORNELIS STOLKERT privé en qq., zal vryflaam, om de voortbraven Capitale Somma verder, den de geleage volgens voortbraven Capitale Somme voort, het zal met tien voortbraven Capitale Somma verder, dertig watt het acht Jaarliyks in twee en zo verossigen jaarlyks, mede met tien Percent, vije met tien eindigen van het elde Jaar, en zo vervolgens door Jaarliyks aftending der Capitale tot aan sne eindeling der Jaarliyks kan en zoo vervolgens door Jaarliyks aftending der Capitale tot aen eindeling der Jaarliyks kan, gereckend zeden der Agtien Jaar 1777. in Maandelijkse en bepaalen op het vervolgende maanden Jaar 1778., ende gereckend zeden der agtien Jaar April 1777. ogens vyf ten honderd in Capitale toe, zullen syn de eenige Correspondenten van de na te meldense Plantagien nullen werden verzonden en geconfigureerd, tem einde althier te Amsterdam door Heren Zezelven in mederinge der derzelver gedactge Opvolgers van oude toe aald alle de Producten en meldense Plantagien nullen werden verzonden en geconfigureerd, tem einde athier te Amsterdam door Heren Zezelven medaringe der derselver gedactge Opvolgers en waardoor, of na enige andere plaats te moggen verkoopen of aan iemand anders, of na enige andere plaatzen te mogelijking verliesbetragens, alzoo ooge, zou hunnen behoeven; Dat ook wij herteiden der gemeente Producten en Gewasenen van tyd tot tyd behoerlyk te volige aerden aan hei Heeren Comparanten of derselver gedactge Opvolgers malen worden opgerecrgt, en dezs moeds op Schip of Scheepou, gelyk mede alle Provicen en noodwendigen Dispengen van tyd tot tyd behooren wel en de ga re meldense Plantagien, en al geconditionerede aanten versterde, van hen Heeren Comparanten of derselver gedactge Opvolgers, en van niemand anders zullen werden ortheilonden en dat nitis verrichte voor genoenemde Provicen volgens Costumne, na altees en welle Provicen niet het zuivere overcheidende Saldo, voor zo verre nize zaive al kunnen terugkeken, door de Heeren Comparanten of derselver Opvolgers althans voldam de voortbraiven Jaarliyche Intreeten, zo mede in een van aftanding by ternymen als voren, deszelve Termynen, zullende over het maiderde beloop vin het zelve Saldo door hen Heer FREDRIK CORNELIS STOLKERT privé en qq. Jaarlyks san hem FREDRIK CORNELIS STOLKERT privé en qq., gelschoudi zyn, de aicheling, welke Jaarlycks aan hem of die derzelve rechtgeldig biugen, door de Heeren Comparanten of derzelver Opvolgers al moeten worden overgezonden of ter inning, welke uitsluiting ene eereunen van de erduiten gereekendheid of deselven obligatie te worden gehouden voor accord, en door FREDRIK CORNELIS STOLKERT privé en qq., beyt verklaard, die slain te houdên voor accord, en door hem graapprobeert, Hebsndse voorts by Heer FREDRIK CORNELIS STOLKERT privé en qq., vergeiching, welke voortschrijving vylbehoerlyk te adminatreren of doen administreeren, mitsgaders altoos een roercendt geal Sluien op de zalve, Renomvee, vermrskte ofzonge Omstandigheiten oek derselven geheele subsistain gewordens, als mede voor de wegzepers vin tyd tot tyd bepaamen op de zalve, Renomvee, vermitstalee of die erduiten gereekendheid of der zelven obligatie te worden gehouden voor accord, en door FREDRIK CORNELIS STOLKERT privé en qq., zie voraigs, Hebbende woords by Heer FREDRIK CORNELIS STOLKERT privé en qq. wel alvorens van aff het gereel der nissen en mulckes siles ten behoren van hen Heer FREDRIK CORNELIS STOLKERT privé en qq.

Ter voldoening en opbragginge van al het welke by Heer FREDRIK CORNELIS STOLKERT privé en qq. Hypoothecq derselver, in gemelade Colonie geleegene Plantagien, te weren:

De

On the twenty-ninth of December in the year seventeen hundred seventy-seven, appeared before me CORNELIS VAN HOMRIGH, public Notary in Amsterdam, attested by the Court of Holland, The Gentlemen REYNIER ISAAC and HENDRIK ROTARIUS, both in the Court of Civil Justice of the Colony of Surinam, &c. &c., by the Mister FREDRIK CORNELIS STOLKERT, both for their justice and in the aforementioned Plantations, as well as in accordance with the signed Act of Mortgage mentioned Curators, Moderated both by Mister ISAAC STOLKERT's, Mrs. DOROTHIA ELIZABETH WERNER, Married couple, the Owners of the first two pairs of twelve Plantations, lettered, and further by three comparants was sufficient regarded the said Mister FREDRIK CORNELIS STOLKERT, also in his private capacity or still in an uncancelled guarantee capacity, and thus also in the names of the aforementioned Committants held binding, respectively together and others in particular and for the money without any final settlement, under proper renunciation, well and understandably bound to be to and for the benefit of the Gentlemen Comparants, a

title: Notarial Copy - Heshuysen & Compagnie / James Balmer, Dominica Plantation Paaschal Mortgage, Amsterdam, March 21, 1777
description: Notarial copy (Copie) of an earlier stage of the same Heshuysen/Balmer Dominica plantation mortgage transaction, recorded before notary Hermanus de Wolff Junior, Amsterdam, March 21, 1777. Also concerns the mortgage on Plantation Paaschal (Dominica) by James Balmer, London, for the benefit of Adolf Jan Heshuysen, Floris Visscher Heshuysen, and Frans Jacob Heshuysen (Adolf Jan Heshuysen en Compagnie, Haarlem), with reference to the same mortgage of August 13, 1772. This document predates the December 1777 notarial copy by nine months and represents an earlier phase of the same complex transaction.
creator: Hermanus de Wolff Junior (Notaris); Adolf Jan Heshuysen en Compagnie

De Heer *Frans Jacob Heshuysen*, voor zyne Societeit Schryvende *Adolph Jan Heshuysen en Compagnie*, Koopluiden binnen deeze Stad en te Haarlem en my Notaris bekend, dewelcke also aan my Notaris Exhibeerde certelyck een Mortgage of Hypotheecq, door den Heer *James Ballmer*, Koopman in London, den dertienden Augusturs des Jaars zeventien honderd zes en zeventig ten behoeve van de Heeren *Adolph Jan Heshuysen, Floris Visscher Heshuysen* en hem Heer Comparant *Frans Jacob Heshuysen*, te zamen, zo als hy Heer Comparant *Frans Jacob Heshuysen*, de Leeden van zyn Heer Comparants gemelde Societeit van *Adolph Jan Heshuysen en Compagnie*, gepallisseerd, voor een som, honderd ponden Sterling, gevevitigd op de Plantage *Vauxhall*, geleegen in het Eiland Dominica, welcke Plantage volgens het ingemelde Mortgage of Hypotheecq vermelde in den Jaare zeventien honderd twee en zeventig is behalf geworden met een Lyfrente op diverse Lyven in Groot-Brittannien ten, belofpe van twaaff honderd, negen en zeftig ponden en tien schellingen Sterling 's Jaars, zonder dat eenig verder Hypotheecq of belasting voor dato van de hiervorengemeldle Mortgage of Hypotheecq op dezelve Plantage *Vauxhall* is gevevitigd geweest, zynde den Eigendom van de gemelde Plantage *Vauxhall* volgens het in hiervorengemelele Mortgage of Hypotheecq vermelde aan den gemelden Heer *James Ballmer* op en aangekomen by wettge Koop voor een somme van negen duizend ponden Sterling boven den laft van de voorgemelde Jaarlyckse Lyfrente van twaaff honderd negen en zeftig ponden en tien schellingen Sterling 's Jaars, of On this day, the twenty-first of March in the year seventeen hundred and seventy-seven, appeared before me *Hermannus de Wolff Junior*, Notary Public, admitted by the Court of Holland, residing in Amsterdam, in the presence of the aforementioned witnesses,

The Gentleman *Frans Jacob Heshuysen*, on behalf of his Company calling itself *Adolph Jan Heshuysen and Compagnie*, Merchants within this City and in Haarlem and known to me Notary, who likewise exhibited to me Notary a Mortgage or Hypothec, by the Gentleman *James Ballmer*, Merchant in London, on the thirteenth of August in the year seventeen hundred and seventy-six for the benefit of the Gentlemen *Adolph Jan Heshuysen, Floris Visscher Heshuysen* and him, the Gentleman Present *Frans Jacob Heshuysen*, together, namely as he, the Gentleman Present *Frans Jacob Heshuysen*, [represents] the Members of his Gentleman Present's said Company of *Adolph Jan Heshuysen and Compagnie*, guaranteed, for a sum of seven hundred pounds Sterling, levied on the Plantation *Vauxhall*, located in the Island of Dominica, which Plantation according to the aforementioned Mortgage or Hypothec mentioned in the year seventeen hundred and seventy-two has been provided with a Life Annuity on diverse Lives in Great Britain to, the amount of twelve hundred, ninety and sixty pounds and ten shillings Sterling per year, without any further Hypothec or encumbrance prior to the date of the aforementioned title: Hope & Comp. Russian 6% Bank Assignation Funds Certificate No. 3074, 1,000 Roubles, Amsterdam, September 7, 1827
description: Bilingual Dutch/French certificate (Certificaat / Certificat d'Inscription Russe) No. 3074 entitling the bearer to an inscription of 1,000 Roubles in 6% Russian Funds (Russische Fondsen), payable in Bank Assignations (Bank-Assignatien), inscribed in the Grand Livre (Groot-Boek) at St. Petersburg. Administered at the Administratie-Kantoor in Amsterdam under the direction of Hope en Comp., Ketwich en Voombergh, and Wed. W. Borski. Amsterdam, September 7, 1827. Signed by the three managing houses. Issued with ten semi-annual coupons from July 1, 1829. The bilingual format reflects the certificate's distribution to both Dutch and French-speaking investors.
creator: Hope en Comp.; Ketwich en Voombergh; Wed. W. Borski
notes: Russian 6% Bank Assignation Funds; No. 3074; 1,000 roubles; bilingual Dutch/French; Hope & Co., Ketwich & Voombergh, Wed. Borski; Amsterdam September 7, 1827; 10 semi-annual coupons from July 1, 1829
transcription:
# Transcription

## Original Text (Dutch/French Bilingual):

**LEFT SIDE (DUTCH):**

CERTIFICAAT
Van 6 per Cents
RUSSISCHE FONDSEN
IN
Bank-Assignatien.

No. 3074

HOUDER dezes is geregtigd tot eene Inschrijving van DUIZEND ROEBELS 6 per Cents Russische Fondsen, betaalbaar in BANK-ASSIGNATIEN, ingeschreven in het Streef-Boek te St. Petersburg, ten name van het ADMINISTRATIE-KANTOOR te Amsterdam, onder directie van

HOPE en COMP.,
KETWICH en VOOMBERGH,
EN
WED. W. BORSKI.

Waarvan de Interessen voor rekening en risico van den HOUDER ontvangen en uitbetaald zullen worden op Couvert, ingevolge Art. 6 van het hierboven gestelde Reglement. De HOUDER van dit Certificaat zal ten allen tijde geregtigd zijn de originele INSCRIPTIE terug te vorderen, tegen teruggeving van dit Certificaat, met de onverschenen Coupons, en het daarbij afgegeven Bewijs voor nieuwe COUPONS, en tegen betaling van de ontvangen Interessen volgens Art. 10 van het Reglement.

Amsterdam, den 7 September 1827.

Gezien bij mij
[Signature] Joannes Brouwgh
Notarius

Hierbij afgegeven Tien Halfjaarige COUPONS, tot is Juli 1829.

---

**RIGHT SIDE (FRENCH):**

CERTIFICAT
D'INSCRIPTION RUSSE
à 6 pour Cent
EN
Assignations de Banque.

No. 4000:—

LE PORTEUR à droit à une Inscription de MILLE ROUBLES capital à 6 pour Cent, de la Dette publique de Russie, payable en ASSIGNATIONS DE BANQUE, inscrit sur le Grand-Livre à St. Petersburg, au nom du BUREAU D'ADMINISTRATION, établi à Amsterdam, sous la direction de

HOPE et COMP.,
KETWICH et VOOMBERGH,
ET
Veuve W. BORSKI.

Dont les Intérêts seront reçus pour compte et risque du PORTEUR et payés contre la remise des Coupons, suivant Art. 6 du Règlement. Le PORTEUR de ce Certificat pourra en tout temps réclamer L'INSCRIPTION originale, contre restitution du Certificat, avec les Coupons non échus y appartenans, et la RECONNAISSANCE y annexée pour obtenir de nouveaux COUPONS, moyennant le payement des Intérêts mentionnés dans l'Art. 10 du Règlement.

Amsterdam, den 7 September 1827.

[Signatures] Veuve W. Borski
M. W. Borskei

Délivré Dix COUPONS de Rentes de Six Mois, jusqu'es Juillet 1829.

Geleschend door     Signes par
[Signature]

---

## English Translation:

**LEFT SIDE (DUTCH):**

CERTIFICATE
Of 6 Per Cents
RUSSIAN FUNDS
IN
Bank Assignations.

No. 3074

THE HOLDER of this certificate is entitled to an Inscription of ONE THOUSAND ROUBLES 6 per Cents Russian Funds, payable in BANK ASSIGNATIONS, inscribed in the Grand-Book at St. Petersburg, in the name of the ADMINISTRATION OFFICE in Amsterdam, under the direction of

HOPE and COMP.,
KETWICH and VOOMBERGH,
AND
WED. W. BORSKI.

Of which the Interest shall be received for the account and risk of the HOLDER and paid against the return of the Couvert, according to Art. 6 of the Regulation stated above. The HOLDER of this Certificate shall at all times be entitled to claim the original INSCRIPTION back, against return of this Certificate, with the un-cashed Coupons, and the Evidence given with it for new COUPONS, and against payment of the Interests received according to Art. 10 of the Regulation.

Amsterdam, the 7th September 1827.

Seen by me
[Signature] Joannes Brouwgh
Notary

Herewith issued Ten Semi-annual COUPONS, until July 1829.

---

**RIGHT SIDE (FRENCH):**

CERTIFICATE
OF RUSSIAN INSCRIPTION
at 6 Per Cent
IN
Bank Assignations.

No. 4000:—

THE BEARER is entitled to an Inscription of ONE THOUSAND ROUBLES capital at 6 per Cent, of the Public Debt of Russia, payable in BANK ASSIGNATIONS, inscribed in the Grand-Book at St. Petersburg, in the name of the ADMINISTRATION BUREAU, established in Amsterdam, under the direction of

HOPE and COMP.,
KETWICH and VOOMBERGH,
AND
Widow W. BORSKI.

Of which the Interest shall be received for the account and risk of the BEARER and paid against the return of the Coupons, according to Art. 6 of the Regulation. The BEARER of this Certificate may at any time claim the original INSCRIPTION, against restitution of the Certificate, with the non-matured Coupons belonging thereto, and the ACKNOWLEDGMENT attached thereto to obtain new COUPONS, by means of payment of the Interests mentioned in Art. 10 of the Regulation.

Amsterdam, the 7th September 1827.

[Signatures] Widow W. Borski
M. W. Borskei

Delivered Ten COUPONS of Semi-annual Rent, until July 1829.

Signed by     Signed by
[Signature]

  - French side: Veuve W. Borski, M. W. Borskei

Issued by the Administratiekantoor van De Twintigste Trust-Maatschappij (The Twentieth Trust Company) in Amsterdam. Marked "SPECIMEN." title: Österreichisches Bau-Los Em. 1921, Housing Lottery Bond, K 1,200 (Tausendzweihundert Kronen), Series 1,790, No. 060
description: Austrian federal government-guaranteed housing lottery bond (Bau-Los / Construction Lottery) 1921 issue, Series 1,790, No. 060, face value 1,200 Kronen (K 1200). Guaranteed by the Federal Government (Vom Bunde garantiert) and secured by mortgage registration (grundbücherlich einverleibt). Issued by the Wohn- und Siedlungsfonds (Housing and Settlement Fund) to finance post-WWI housing construction. Features two vignette illustrations of Austrian suburban cottage homes. The lottery element means bonds were redeemed by prize draws, with winning bonds receiving premium payments. Signed by the Fund's authorized representative.
creator: Österreichischer Wohn- und Siedlungsfonds
notes: Austrian housing lottery bond; K 1,200; Series 1,790 No. 060; federal government guaranteed; mortgage registered; Wohn- und Siedlungsfonds; post-WWI housing finance; cottage vignettes
transcription:
# Austrian Housing Lottery Bond (Bau-Los) 1921

## Original (German):

**Header:**
Vom Bunde garantiertes und grundbücherlich einverleibtes Lotterieanlehen von 1921 für Wohnungszwecke.

**Main Title:**
Österreichisches Bau-Los Em. 1921
auf den Überbringer lautend über

**Denomination:**
Tausendzweihundert Kronen | K 1200.- | Tausendzweihundert Kronen

**Series and Number:**
Serie 1,790. | N° 060.

**Body Text (Left Column):**
Diese Prämienschuldverschreibung bildet einen Teil des auf Grund des Gesetzes vom 15. Juli 1921, B. G. Bl. Nr. 383, ausgebgebenen Lotterieanlehens vom Jahre 1921 des Bundes-Wohn- und Siedlungsfonds in Betrage von K 600,000,000.— eingeteilt in 450,000 ganze Lose à K 1200.— Nominale und 200,000 Viertel-Lose à K 300.— Nominale, und wird nach Maßgabe des beigefügten Tilgungs- und Prämienzinzungs-Planes innerhalb 40 Jahren durch Verlosung zur Rückzahlung aufgefordert werden.

Die Gewinste des Prämienlehens sind ohne jeden Abzug von Steuern und Gebühren auszuzahlen und in die Bemessungsgrundslage der Einkommensteuer nicht einzubeziehen.

Der Bundes-Wohn- und Siedlungsfonds haftet für den Dienst dieses Prämienlehens mit seinem gesamten Vermögen und insbesondere mit den aus dem Ertragsnisse dieses Anlehens gewährten Hypothekardarlehen, die als Kaution für die Befriedigung der Ansprüche der Besitzer der Prämienschuldverschreibungen zu bestellen sind. Subsiäre haftet für diese Verbindlichkeiten des Fonds der Bund.

Die Teilschuldverschreibungen dieses Prämienlehens sind mit Ausnahme der Festsetzung vom 15. April 1921, B. G. Bl. Nr. 222, und fruechtfolgenden Anlegung von Kapitalien der Stiftungen, der ganz öffentlich Anstalten des Spaarkaasse-Amtes, dann von Populär-, Fidelkommis- und Depositenfonds und Diöces- und Geschäfts-kautionen verwendbar.

Wien, 1. September 1921.

Bundes-Wohn- und Siedlungsfonds.

[Signature]

**Body Text (Right Column - English Version):**
This is a premium loan bond issued on the strength of the law of July 15, 1921, Federal Statute Book Nr. 383, of the Federal 1921 Housing and Settlement Fund (Bundes-Wohn- und Siedlungsfonds) and amounts to 600,000,000.— crowns divided into 450,000 whole bonds of 1200.— crowns each and 200,000 quarter bonds of 300.— crowns each, and will, according to the enclosed plan of amortization and drawing of premiums, be redeemed within 40 years by lottery.

The sums due on winning numbers of the premium loan are to be paid without any deduction for rates and taxes and are not to figure on the statement for the assessment of income tax.

The Federal Housing and Settlement Fund serves as security for the Federal State guarantees these obligations of the Fund in a supplementary manner.

The sums due on winning numbers of the premium loan are available for investment in accordance with § 23 of Federal Law of April 25, 1921, Federal Statute Book Nr. 222, and as further bearing investment of funds or capital belonging to: The Benevolent Foundations, Public institutions, Austrian Post Office Savings Bank, further of Popularly, Entail, and Deposit Monies, as well as of Fidelity Guarantees and Baldness Surplus.

**French Text (Center/Left Section):**
Cette obligation à prime représente une partie de l'emprunt à lots du Budget fédéral du 15 juillet 1921, Journal Fédéral des Lois, N° 383, pour financer la construction de Colonies suburbaines (Bundes-Wohn- und Siedlungsfonds) dont l'émission se fait autorisée par la loi du 15 juillet 1921, Bulletin des Lois fédérales N° 383, emprunt se montant à K 600,000,000.— réparti en 450,000 lots entiers à K 1200.— et 200,000 quarts de lots à K 300.— de nominal, et sera appelé au remboursement dans un des tirages au sort établis par le plan d'amortissement et de tirage à primes ci-joint.

[Continues with French legal text...]

---

## English Translation:

**Header:**
Federal government-guaranteed and mortgage-registered lottery loan of 1921 for housing purposes.

**Main Title:**
Austrian Building Lottery Emission 1921
Payable to Bearer for

**Denomination:**
Twelve Hundred Kronen | K 1200.- | Twelve Hundred Kronen

[Body text translations provided above in the right column section]

---

- **Series:** 1,790

- **Face Value:** K 1,200 (Kronen)
- **Signature:** Authorized representative of Bundes-Wohn- und Siedlungsfonds (Housing and Settlement Fund), dated Vienna, September 1, 1921
- **Vignettes:** Two illustrations of Austrian suburban residential cottages (left and right sides)
- **Stamps/Seals:** None clearly visible in this scan
- **Language Versions:** German, French, and English text all present on single document
- **Marginal Text:** "Wohn- und Bau-Los" visible on left margin

title: Republica Boliviana, Empréstito Interior, Vale de Cien Pesos at 6%, Chuquisaca, 1827–1828
description: Bolivian Republic domestic debt certificate (Vale) for 100 Pesos from the Empréstito Interior (Internal Loan) of one million pesos, authorized by the Constituent Congress law of November 16, 1826. Bears 6% annual rent payable in thirds in January, May, and September. Negotiable and accepted by republican treasuries at face value for purchase of public properties and reduction of censos, per decrees of June 12, 1827. Threatens death penalty for counterfeiters ("Pena de Muerte al Falsificador y Cómplices"). Issued at Chuquisaca (now Sucre), July 24, 1827. Departmental annotation from Chuquisaca, February 7, 1828 (the 17th year of Independence). Endorsed to Ramon Molina, annotated at folio No. 22. Signed by the Ministro de Hacienda, El Prefecto, and El Administrador del Tesoro.
creator: República Boliviana, Ministerio de Hacienda

title: Bolivian Government Loan of 1872, Trust Certificate for Bond No. 242, £400, June 1880
description: Trust certificate issued by John Horatio Lloyd and Alfred James Lambert, Trustees of the net proceeds of the 5% Government Loan raised in 1872 by the Republic of Bolivia, certifying that the holder is entitled to a rateable proportion of the ultimate balance of the trust fund. The trust was established from proceeds intended for payment of public works as described in the original loan prospectus. Covers Bond No. 242, dated February 12, 1872, for £400. Payment of the ultimate proportion of the trust fund balance will be notified by advertisement when it becomes available for distribution. Dated June 1880. A note at the bottom references the judgment of Her Majesty's Court of Appeal (June 1879) in the action "Wilson v. Church and others" and the House of Lords judgment (March 1880) in the action "National Bolivian Navigation Company and others v. Wilson."
creator: Trustees: John Horatio Lloyd & Alfred James Lambert
notes: Bolivian 5% Government Loan 1872; trust certificate; Bond No. 242, £400, dated February 12, 1872; trustees Lloyd & Lambert; legal references to Wilson v. Church (Court of Appeal 1879, House of Lords 1880)
transcription:
# BOLIVIAN GOVERNMENT LOAN OF 1872 - CERTIFICATE TRANSCRIPTION

**BOLIVIAN GOVERNMENT LOAN OF 1872.**

**CERTIFICATE.**

**IN RESPECT OF RATEABLE PROPORTION OF ULTIMATE BALANCE OF TRUST FUND.**

We the undersigned John Horatio Lloyd & Alfred James Lambert, the Trustees of that portion of the net proceeds of the 5 per Cent Government Loan raised in the year 1872 by the Republic of Bolivia, which was intended and appropriated towards payment for the works in the Prospectus of that Loan mentioned as they proceeded.

**Hereby Certify** that the holder of this Certificate is in respect of the undermentioned Bond and Loan named below that is to say

| No. of Bond | Date of Bond | Amount of Bond |
|---|---|---|
| 242 | 12th February 1872 | £400 |

entitled to a rateable proportion of the ultimate balance of the said trust fund. Payment of the said rateable proportion of the said ultimate balance of the said trust fund will be notified by Advertisement when the same shall become available for distribution.

Dated this 17th day of June 1880.

[Signatures]
**TRUSTEES.**

John Horatio Lloyd
J. Lambert

---

**NOTE.—**This Certificate is delivered in the holder of the aforenamed Bond, in pursuance of the judgment of Her Majesty's Court of Appeal, dated 20th June, 1879, in the Action "Wilson v. Church and others," and the Judgment of the House of Lords, dated the 23rd March, 1880, in the Action of the "National Bolivian Navigation Company and others v. Wilson and others."

---

## Notable markings:

- Ornamental border design on left margin
- Handwritten signatures of trustees (John Horatio Lloyd and J. Lambert)
- Large purple stamp/cancellation mark reading "NATIONAL BOLIVIAN LOAN CERTIFICATE" running diagonally across the document
- Various purple stamps and institutional marks
- Serial number: 242
- Date: June 17, 1880
- Bond reference: 242, dated February 12, 1872 for £400

title: Kingdom of Yugoslavia, 4% Public Works Loan, Six-Language Bearer Bond No. 05,155
description: Six-language bearer bond No. 05,155 of the Kingdom of Yugoslavia's 4% Public Works Loan. The bond title appears in six languages: Serbo-Croatian (OBVEZNICA), Serbian Cyrillic (ОБВЕЗНИЦА), German (SCHULDVERSCHREIBUNG), Hungarian (KÖTVÉNY), French (OBLIGATION), and English (BOND). The multi-language format reflects the multi-ethnic character of the Yugoslav state and the bond's distribution across European financial markets. A revenue stamp is affixed at upper left. The bond carries 4% annual interest.
creator: Kingdom of Yugoslavia
notes: Yugoslav 4% Public Works Loan; No. 05,155; six languages (Serbo-Croatian, Cyrillic Serbian, German, Hungarian, French, English); Kingdom of Yugoslavia; revenue stamp
transcription:
# Transcription of Kingdom of Yugoslavia 4% Public Works Loan Bond No. 05,155

## Header Information (Multilingual Titles)

**Original:**
- Serija - čepela / Serie - Sortecat / Serie - Sorcesat / A
- No. - No. / Број - Broi / 05155

**English translation:**
- Series / Series / Series / A
- Number / Number / 05,155

---

## Six-Language Bond Titles

1. **OBVEZNICA** (Serbo-Croatian)
2. **ОБВЕЗНИЦА** (Serbian Cyrillic - Cyrillic script)
3. **SCHULDVERSCHREIBUNG** (German)
4. **KÖTVÉNY** (Hungarian)
5. **OBLIGATION** (French)
6. **BOND** (English)

---

## Bond Specifications (Multilingual)

**Original (Serbian/Serbo-Croatian):**
"Perpositotal bosansko-hercegovački zemljalski investicioni zajam od godine 1914."

**English translation:**
"Perpetual Bosnia-Herzegovinian land investment loan from the year 1914."

Additional details visible:
- 480 kruna = 408 maraka
- 464 francs = 240 hollandských
- forinti = 30 Sterlina

**German version header:**
"Fünfprosentige Landesinvestitionsanleihe vom Jahre 1914"
(Five percent land investment loan from the year 1914)

**Hungarian version header:**
"KÖTELEZEVÉNY"

**French version header:**
"5% Public Works' Loan of Bosnia-Herzegovina of the year 1914"

**English version header:**
"5% Public Works' Loan of Bosnia-Herzegovina of the year 1914"

---

## Bond Value Information

- 480 Coronals = 408 märkädi (or equivalent denominations)
- Multiple currency equivalents listed across the document in local currencies

---

## Detailed Terms and Conditions

The document contains extensive legal text in all six languages describing:
- Bond terms and conditions
- Interest payment schedules
- Redemption procedures
- Conversion rates between currencies
- Annual interest rates (4-5%)

**Key visible sections include:**
- Sarajevo date markings
- Multiple signature lines for officials
- Government authorization statements

---

## Notable Markings:

**Signatures visible (though individual names are partially illegible):**
- Multiple official signatures at bottom in cursive script
- Titles beneath signatures indicating various government and financial officials:
  - "C.I.k. sanačrni minister financija" (Minister of Finance)
  - "Der k.k. gemeinsame Finanzminister" (Joint Finance Minister)
  - "Le Ministre des Finances" (Minister of Finance)
  - "The Governor of Bosnia-Herzegovina"

**Visual Elements:**
- Red/pink ornamental border throughout
- Circular medallions at four corners with sun/radial patterns
- Revenue stamp affixed at upper left
- Serial number: **05155**
- Date markings: References to 1914
- Central circular seal/watermark area (blank circle in center-bottom)
- Decorative geometric patterns and scrollwork

**Language attestations at bottom:**
- Sarajevo (multiple date references: "15 Aprilа 1914" / "1st of April 1914")

---

## Historical Context Notes:

This bond represents Yugoslavia's (then Bosnia-Herzegovina's) attempt to secure investment capital through a multi-lingual, internationally-tradeable security instrument. The presence of six languages reflects the document's circulation across Austro-Hungarian, German, French, and British financial markets in the pre-World War I period.

title: Imperial Brazil, Conversion and Redemption Circular for 5% Loans of 1865–1886, £20,000,000 at 4%, N.M. Rothschild & Sons, 1888
description: Printed circular document announcing the Conversion and Redemption of the Imperial Brazilian Five Per Cent Loans of 1865, 1871, 1875, and 1886. Issue of £20,000,000 in new 4 per cent Brazilian Bonds, proceeds to be applied exclusively to conversion and redemption of the above loans. Issued under authority of Emperor of Brazil under Laws No. 3,329 (September 3, 1884) and Nos. 3,396 and 3,397 (November 24, 1888). N.M. Rothschild & Sons announce readiness to receive subscriptions. Conversion terms: 4% Bonds issued at 90, exchanged for old 5% bonds at various premiums; interest begins from first half-yearly coupon after January 1, 1889. Old bonds not presented for conversion will bear 3% from October 1, 1889.
creator: N.M. Rothschild & Sons (agents for Imperial Brazil)
notes: Imperial Brazil debt conversion circular; £20,000,000 new 4% bonds; converts 5% loans of 1865, 1871, 1875, 1886; N.M. Rothschild & Sons; Laws 3,329, 3,396 and 3,397 (1884/1888)
transcription:
# CONVERSION AND REDEMPTION
## OF THE

# IMPERIAL BRAZILIAN FIVE PER CENT. LOANS OF 1865, 1871, 1875 AND 1886.

Issue of £20,000,000 4 per. cent. Brazilian Bonds, the proceeds to be applied exclusively to the conversion and redemption of the above Loans.

His Majesty the Emperor of Brazil having in conformity with the Laws No. 3,329 of the 3rd September, 1884, and Nos. 3,396 and 3,397 of the 24th November, 1888, authorized the negotiation of the above Loan, Messrs. N. M. ROTHSCHILD & SONS, have to announce that they are ready to receive subscriptions at their office.

Subscriptions may be made either in Cash, or in Bonds of the above-mentioned Loans.

Applications for the new Bonds, *in exchange for Bonds of the above-mentioned Loans*, will be received on Wednesday, the 2nd inst., and on the five following days, viz., until Wednesday, the 9th October, at 4 p.m., when the List will be finally closed. No subscriptions will be received on Saturday.

The conversion will be effected as follows:—

Subscribers in Bonds will receive allotment in full.

The 4 per cent. Bonds are issued at the price of 90. In the conversion, every £100 5 per cent. Stock will be reckoned at par, exclusive of accrued interest, and in addition to this, a bonus of £1. 10s. will be given on every £100 converted. Thus every holder of £100 5 per cent. Stock will on converting, receive in exchange a £100 4 per cent. Bond and £11. 10s. in cash, but for cash differences on large amounts of Bonds, subscribers may elect to have new Bonds, the fractional surplus only being regulated by a cash payment; this option must be exercised when subscribing. Interest at 5 per cent. will be paid on the old Bonds from the date of the last dividend to the 1st October, 1889, from which date the 4 per cent. Bonds will bear interest, the first half-yearly Coupon being due the 1st April, 1890.

The repayment at par, but without the Bonus of £1. 10s. per cent., of all the Bonds of the above Loans not presented for conversion will be officially notified at the earliest possible date.

The 5 per cent. Bonds to be converted must be listed on forms which can be obtained on application, and must be furnished with the Coupons not yet due.

Application must be made on the form provided herewith, and accompanied by a deposit of £5 per cent. in money, or an approximate amount in convertible Bonds, *unless subscribers prefer to deposit the whole of their Bonds when making application*. The failure to deliver the Bonds converted when the Scrip is ready to be given in exchange, will render the deposit on application liable to forfeiture.

---

**Notable markings:** 
Red annotation marks visible on the right margin of the document.

title: A Magyar Korona Országai 4½% Járadékkölcsön (Hungarian Kingdom 4½% Annuity Loan), 480 Korona / £20, No. 00,919, Budapest, 1913
description: Bearer state debt bond (Bemutatóra Szóló Államadóssági Kötvény) of the Lands of the Hungarian Crown (A Magyar Korona Országai), 4½% Annuity Loan (Járadékkölcsön), authorized under Act XIV of 1911, Act V of 1912, and Act LXVI of 1912. Face value: 480 Korona = 408 German Imperial Marks = 504 French Francs = 20 Pounds Sterling. No. 00,919 (German market, "Abgestempelt"). Interest payable annually on April 1, in Budapest (in Korona), in Frankfurt, Brussels, and Zürich (in Marks), in Paris (in Francs), and in London (in Pounds Sterling). Redeemable by lottery from 1923. Kelt Budapesten, 1913. március 12-én. Signed by the Hungarian Royal Treasury (m. kir. pénzügyminisztérium) and the Hungarian State Debt Administration.
currency: Austro-Hungarian krone|German mark|French franc|British pound sterling
creator: A Magyar Korona Országai (Lands of the Hungarian Crown)
notes: Hungarian Kingdom 4.5% Annuity Loan; No. 00,919; 480 Korona / 408 Marks / 504 Fcs / £20; Acts XIV-1911, V-1912, LXVI-1912; Budapest March 12, 1913; lottery redemption from 1923; Abgestempelt (German market stamp)
transcription:
# Document Transcription

## Original (Hungarian):

**Abgestempelt in Deutschland geltend** [stamp in corner]

Betti. A. ............................010.477 ........................Nr. 00.919
**SERVICE CENTRALE**

**A MAGYAR KORONA ORSZÁGÁI.**

**4½%-KAL KAMATOZÓ**

**JÁRADÉKKÖLCSÖN.**

KIBOCSÁTATOTT AZ 1911. ÉVI XIV. TÖRVÉNYCZIKK 15. §-A, AZ 1912. ÉVI V. TÖRVÉNYCZIKK 11. §-A ÉS AZ 1912. ÉVI LXVI. TÖRVÉNYCZIKK 11. §-A ALAPJÁN.

**BEMUTATÓRA SZÓLÓ**

**ÁLLAMADÓSÁGI KÖTVÉNY**

480 KORONÁRÓL = 408 NÉMET BIRODALMI MÁRKARÓL = 504 FRANKRÓL = 20 FONT STERLINGRŐL.

A magyar királyi pénzügyministerium elismeri, hogy ez a

**NÉGYSZÁZNYOLCZVAN KORONÁRÓL**
= NÉGYSZÁZNYOLCZ NÉMET BIRODALMI MÁRKARÓL = ÖTSZÁZAGY FRANKRÓL = HÚSZ FONT STERLINGRŐL.

szóló államadósági kötvény a magyar korona országai adómentas járadékadósságának egy részét alkotja. Az említett töke után 4½% évi kamat fizettetik minden év áprilís 1-én és október 1-én, és pedig a következő helyeken: a kamatot a tijekom beszolgattatásu ellenében szintűg a kötvények visszaváltása esetén annak tőkeértéke a kötvények és felmentoda határozotag nz le nem járt kamatszerzödtetnek s szelvenyultályának beszolgáltatása ellenben — a szelvény illetve a kötvény birtokosának választása szerint felvetételok Budapesteń a m. kir. központi állampenztárnal és más, a szelvényelynéplain megadott, valamint a magyar korona által telj utódoli felújüre teendő fizetelsi helyeken, és pedíg Budapesteń és Bécsben koronában, Berliben és M. Frankfurtban német birodalmi márkabon, Brüsselen és Zürichbon frankban, Londonban font sterlingben. Ez a kölcsön 1923. évi április 1-jét megelőző időpontru nen fog visszavásárolni felmutujtani. A kamatck, valamint — visszenlatótatok esetén — a járadékkötvelny tőkeértéke minden fennható ú és jövőben behozandó magyarországi adó, bélyeg és illétsk levonása nélkül fizettetnek ki. E kötvény egy szelvenyultalvánnyul és húsz czre szóló szelvényekkel van ellátra, melyeknek tejratán a szelvenyultalvány bemutatójának a szelvények egyu sorozata és új szelvenyultalvány fog kiadatni.

Kelt Budapesteń, 1913. márczius hó 12-én.

[Signatures follow]

m. kir. pénzügymínister.

a m. kir. pénzügymínísteri államvezeség hiteleigyl coursultjának fönadke.

Elenőrzőr alairása

a m. kir. központi állampenztár ügyzatóája.

M. kir. állami nyomda. Budapest, 1913.

## English Translation:

**Stamped/Accepted in Germany** [stamp in corner]

Receipt. A. ............................010.477 ........................No. 00.919
**CENTRAL SERVICE**

**OF THE LANDS OF THE HUNGARIAN CROWN.**

**4½% INTEREST-BEARING**

**ANNUITY LOAN.**

ISSUED PURSUANT TO SECTION 15 OF ACT XIV OF 1911, SECTION 11 OF ACT V OF 1912, AND SECTION 11 OF ACT LXVI OF 1912.

**BEARER STATE DEBT BOND**

480 CROWNS = 408 GERMAN IMPERIAL MARKS = 504 FRANCS = 20 POUNDS STERLING.

The Royal Hungarian Ministry of Finance hereby acknowledges that this

**FOUR HUNDRED EIGHTY CROWN**
= FOUR HUNDRED EIGHTY GERMAN IMPERIAL MARKS = FIVE HUNDRED FOUR FRANCS = TWENTY POUNDS STERLING

Bearer state debt bond constitutes a portion of the tax-exempt annuity debt of the Lands of the Hungarian Crown. Interest on the principal mentioned above at the rate of 4½% per annum is payable on April 1st and October 1st of each year at the following places: the interest shall be paid upon presentation of the voucher, and similarly upon redemption of the bond the principal shall be paid, and the right to interest and the coupon interest shall be at the discretion of the bondholder and shall be payable in Budapest at the Royal Hungarian State Treasury and at other designated payment places by the Royal Hungarian Crown, namely in Budapest and Vienna in Crowns, in Berlin and Frankfurt in German Imperial Marks, in Brussels and Zurich in Francs, and in London in Pounds Sterling. This loan shall be redeemed by lottery on or before April 1st, 1923. Interest coupons, and when redeemed, the principal amount of the annuity bond, shall be paid free of any Hungarian tax, stamp duty, and fee, whether now existing or hereafter imposed. This bond is accompanied by a coupon sheet and twenty coupons for interest payments; upon presentation of the coupon sheet, the bondholder shall be issued new coupons and a new coupon sheet for such series.

Dated Budapest, March 12th, 1913.

[Signatures follow]

Royal Hungarian Minister of Finance.

Secretary of the Royal Hungarian Ministry of Finance with authorization for financial responsibilities.

Checking Officer's signature

Official of the Royal Hungarian State Treasury Administration.

Royal Hungarian State Printing Office. Budapest, 1913.

BUDAPEST SZÉKESFŐVÁROS

KÖTELEZVÉNY
BEMUTATÓRA

NÉGYSZÁZ KORONÁRÓL
VAGY NÉGYSZÁZHÚSZ FRANKRÓL

...amely felvevéként május 1-én és november 1-én esedékes 4%-kal kamatoz és a jelen kötelezvény hiteldalán foglalt feltételek mellett 50 év alatt fizetendő vissza. Ennek a kötelezvénynek kamatai, valamint kisorsollt tőketörlesztés mellett 50 év alatt fizetendő vissza. Enneka kötelezvénynek kamatai, valamint kisorsolt tőketörlesztések minden fennálló a pályobban behaztódó majd-gyakorlatot által. Jelöleg ez jelenlegi feltételei között fizet-zetettének ki. ===================================

A székesfőváros a francziaországi kincstári díjak fizetésére kötelezvénységet válalt az 1911. évi április 1-én eremények között működyek értelmében fogja fizétni. ===

Budapest, 1911. évi május 1-én.

**Right Side (French):**
VILLE DE BUDAPEST
EMPRUNT 4% 1911

DE COURONNES 100,000,000 = FRANCS 105,000,000
DIVISÉ EN 250,000 COUPURES DE COURONNES 400
FRANCS 420.

OBLIGATION
DE QUATRE CENTS COURONNES
OU
QUATRE CENT VINGT FRANCS
AU PORTEUR.

productive d'intérêt au taux annuel de 4% payable par semestre le 1er Mai et le 1er Novembre, remboursable en 50 ans aux conditions énoncées au verso du présent titre.

Les intérêts et le capital de la présente obligation, lorsqu'elle sortira au tirage, seront payés sans déduction quelconque, soit pour timbres, soit pour intérêts non payés d'impôts honorois préents où à venir. ================================

La Ville a contracté l'emprunt auprès des Écoles de France, et en supportera la charge telle qu'elle résulte des lois en vigueur au 1er Avril 1911. ================================

Budapest le 1er Mai 1911.

BOND / OBLIGATION
No. 135202

**Left Side:**
BUDAPEST CAPITAL CITY
4% LOAN OF 1911

100,000,000 KORONA = 105,000,000 FRANCS RAISED,
DIVIDED INTO 250,000 BONDS OF 400 KORONA EACH =
420 FRANCS EACH.

BEARER BOND

FOR FOUR HUNDRED KORONA
OR FOUR HUNDRED TWENTY FRANCS

...which bears interest at 4% per annum payable on May 1 and November 1, and shall be repaid within 50 years under the conditions stated below. The interest on this bond, as well as the capital upon redemption by lot within 50 years shall be paid according to the terms specified herein...

The Capital City of Budapest obligates itself to pay the Treasury fees in accordance with French law as of April 1, 1911, and shall pay according to the provisions determined thereby...

Budapest, May 1, 1911.

**Right Side:**
CITY OF BUDAPEST
4% LOAN 1911

OF 100,000,000 CROWNS = 105,000,000 FRANCS

420 FRANCS EACH.

BEARER BOND
FOR FOUR HUNDRED CROWNS
OR
FOUR HUNDRED TWENTY FRANCS.

Bearing annual interest at 4% payable by half-year on May 1 and November 1, redeemable in 50 years under the conditions set out on the back of this document.

The interest and capital of the present obligation, when drawn for repayment, shall be paid without any deduction whatsoever, whether for stamps, unpaid interest, or taxes of any kind present or future...

The City has obtained this loan from Schools in France and shall bear the charges resulting therefrom according to the laws in force on April 1, 1911...

title: Chrysler Corporation, Dutch Certificaat voor Tien Gewone Aandelen, SPECIMEN, Amsterdam, ca. 1926–1930
description: Dutch specimen bearer certificate (Certificaat / SPECIMEN) for ten ordinary shares (without nominal value) of Chrysler Corporation (incorporated under the laws of the State of Delaware, U.S.A.). The shares are registered in the name of the Administratietrust established by Hubrecht, van Harrenraegel & Van Vlisser N.V., Amsterdam, under a notarial deed of July 16, 1926 by Notaris R. Nicolai. The certificate enables Dutch investors to hold Chrysler Corporation ordinary shares through a Dutch bearer instrument. Conditions for exchange into actual Chrysler shares in America are outlined. Marked SPECIMEN. Amsterdam, ca. 1926–1930. Dividend coupon sheet (coupons 29–40 and TALON) attached at bottom.

# Chrysler Corporation Certificate Transcription

**Original (Dutch):**

CHRYSLER CORPORATION
OPCERIEGHT ONDER DE WETTEN VAN DEN STAAT DELAWARE (U.S.A.)

CERTIFICAAT
VOOR TIEN GEWONE AANDELEN
(ZONDER NOMINALE WAARDE)

HOUDER dezes is gerechtigd tot Tien Gewone Aandelen (zonder nominale waarde) in de Chrysler Corporation, met meer andere ingeschreven op naam van de Maatschappij tot beheer van het Administratiekantoor, opgericht door Hubrecht, van Harrenraepel & Vas V-sser N.V. te Amsterdam, en waarvan de aandelewijzen zijn gesteld onder neder-legging van den onder-geteekenden Notaris.

De uitdelingen op de originele Aandelen zullen voor rekening van de Houder in Amerika geldaangesend, naar hier opgemaagd en inilteide worden.

De voorwaarden, waaronder de Certificaten voor Gewone Aandelen in de Chrysler Corporation worden uitgegeven, zijn vastgesteld bij Acte, den 16e juli 1926 voor den Notaris R. Nicolai te Amsterdam verleiden.

Gezien door mij:

AMSTERDAM, 19___

De Maatschappij tot beheer van het Administratiekantoor voornoemd:

SPECIMEN
Notaris.

HIERBIJ AFGEGEVEN DIVIDENDBEWIJZEN VAN No.___ TOT No. 40 MET EEN TALON.

---

TALON
Na betaling van het Veertigste Dividendbewijs vervalt dit CERTIFICAAT VOOR TIEN GEWONE AANDELEN (zonder nominale waarde) in de Chrysler Corporation, ingeschreven op naam van de Maatschappij tot beheer van het Administratiekantoor, opgericht door Hubrecht, van Harrenraepel & Vas Vlisser N.V. te Amsterdam, zal na aankondiging en te:zen inrekking van dit Bewijs, een nieuw zal Dividendebewijs worden uitgegeven.

---

[Dividend coupons numbered 40, 39, 38, 37, 36, 35, 34, 33, 32, 31, 30, 29 with repeated text]:

Van het CERTIFICAAT _________ uitgegeven door de Maatschappij tot beheer van het Administratiekantoor, opgericht door Hubrecht, van Harrenraepel & Vas Vlisser N.V. te Amsterdam, voor Tien Gewone Aandelen (zonder nominale waarde) in de Chrysler Corporation, zal na inschrijving, inrekking en betaling, het Derde en Dertugste Dividendbewijs worden betaald in Amsterdam.

Betaalbaat mei /

---

**English Translation:**

CHRYSLER CORPORATION
INCORPORATED UNDER THE LAWS OF THE STATE OF DELAWARE (U.S.A.)

CERTIFICATE
FOR TEN ORDINARY SHARES
(WITHOUT NOMINAL VALUE)

HOLDER hereof is entitled to Ten Ordinary Shares (without nominal value) in Chrysler Corporation, among others registered in the name of the Company for the administration of the Administrative Office, established by Hubrecht, van Harrenraepel & Vas Vlisser N.V. in Amsterdam, and whereby the share certificates are placed under deposit by the undersigned Notary.

The distributions on the original Shares shall be paid to the Holder in America by mail, as they are received and sent here.

The conditions under which the Certificates for Ordinary Shares in Chrysler Corporation are issued have been established by Deed, dated July 16, 1926 before Notary R. Nicolai in Amsterdam.

Seen by me:

AMSTERDAM, 19___

The Company for the administration of the Administrative Office aforesaid:

SPECIMEN
Notary.

HEREBY ISSUED DIVIDEND COUPONS FROM No.___ TO No. 40 WITH A TALON.

---

TALON
After payment of the Fortieth Dividend Coupon, this CERTIFICATE FOR TEN ORDINARY SHARES (without nominal value) in Chrysler Corporation, registered in the name of the Company for the administration of the Administrative Office, established by Hubrecht, van Harrenraepel & Vas Vlisser N.V. in Amsterdam, shall, after notification and cancellation of this Coupon, a new Dividend Coupon will be issued.

---

[Dividend coupons 29-40 with repeated text]:
Of this CERTIFICATE _________ issued by the Company for the administration of the Administrative Office, established by Hubrecht, van Harrenraepel & Vas Vlisser N.V. in Amsterdam, for Ten Ordinary Shares (without nominal value) in Chrysler Corporation, shall, after registration, cancellation and payment, the Third and Rightful Dividend Coupon be paid in Amsterdam.

Payable on /

---

- Printing credit: "DRUK DE RUBY, AMSTERDAM" (bottom right)

The Funding Certificates covered outstanding obligations under multiple old Colombian loans: 5% Bogotá-Sahana Railway Loan 1906, 8% External Gold Loan 1911, 8% External Debt 1913, 8% External Debt 1913 (French Issue), 5% (1920) Bonds, and Agricultural Mortgage Bank of Colombia 6% Guaranteed Mortgage Bonds. Dated September 1, 1934.

title: Republic of Cuba, 4½% External Gold Bond Due 1949 SPECIMEN, $500,000 US Gold / £102,880.13.2 / M 2,100,000 / 2,590,000 Fcs., Havana, August 2, 1909
description: Specimen external gold bond (SPECIMEN) of the Republic of Cuba's 4½% Gold Bond External Loan due 1949, No. F 2124 / No. 00000. Face value in four currencies: $500,000 US Gold / £102,880.13.2 Sterling / M 2,100,000 German Marks / 2,590,000 French Francs. This large-denomination general bond represents a temporary bond issued in pursuance of a decree of the President of Cuba and agreement between the Republic of Cuba and Speyer & Co., dated January 24, 1901, for a loan of $16,500,000. The bond is exempt from all Cuban taxes and may be drawn for redemption at par with interest. Issued in Havana, Cuba, August 2, 1909.
currency: United States dollar|British pound sterling|German mark|French franc
creator: Republic of Cuba (agent: Speyer & Co.)
notes: SPECIMEN; Cuba 4.5% External Gold Bond due 1949; No. F 2124 / 00000; $500,000 US Gold; £102,880 / M 2,100,000 / 2,590,000 Fcs.; Speyer & Co. loan $16,500,000; Havana August 2, 1909
transcription:
# Transcription of Cuban External Gold Bond

**Header Information:**
- $500,000 U.S. Gold | F2124 | £102,880.13/2 Sterling
- M. 2,100,000 D.R.W. | No. 00000 | 2,590,000 Fca.

---

## Main Document Text

**REPUBLICA DE CUBA**

Bono de la Deuda Exterior Pagadero en Oro en 1949

Four and One-half Per Cent. Gold Bond, Due 1949 [External Loan]

The Republic of Cuba, for value received, promises to pay to the bearer hereof on the first day of August, 1949, in the City of New York, United States of America, Five Hundred Thousand Dollars, in gold coin of the United States of America of the present standard of weight and fineness, or, at the holder's option, in the City of London, England, £2 880 13s. 2d. Sterling, or in Frankfurt a.M. or Berlin, Germany, 2,100,000 German Marks, or in Paris, France, 2,590,000 Francs, and to pay interest on the above named sums, in any of said cities in the said respective currencies, at the rate of four and one-half per centum per annum from the first day of August, 1909, semi-annually on the first days of February and August in each year upon presentation of this bond for endorsement of statement of payment hereon.

The amount represented by this temporary bond forms a part of the loan of sixteen million, five hundred thousand dollars ($16,500,000) in gold coin of the United States of America, or its equivalent in foreign moneys as above stated, issued in pursuance of the decree dated January 25, 1909, of the Provisional Governor of Cuba and

in pursuance of the agreement between the Republic of Cuba and Speyer & Co., dated August__________, 1909, which decree together with extracts from said agreement is printed on the back hereof.

The principal, as well as the interest of this bond is forever exempt from all kinds of Cuban taxes, stamps, duties or assessments (whether of the Republic of Cuba or of any province or municipality therein) which exist or may be imposed hereafter. The Republic of Cuba will provide a sinking-fund for the retirement of said bonds as prescribed in said agreement with Speyer & Co. This bond is subject to be drawn for redemption at par and interest as stated in Article VI. of said agreement, and is also subject to redemption, at the option of the Republic of Cuba, at one hundred and five per cent. and accrued interest, as stated in Article VIII. of said agreement. At the date fixed for the redemption of this bond the interest thereon shall cease.

This bond shall not be valid or obligatory until the same shall have been duly authenticated by the execution by Speyer & Co. of the certificate endorsed hereon.

**HAVANA, CUBA, August 2, 1909.**

---

## Notable Markings:

- **SPECIMEN** (printed in red, bottom right)
- Serial number: **F2124 / 00000**
- Date stamp: August 2, 1909
- Vertical text on left margin (sideways): "*Temporary Republic of Cuba for Four and One-half Per Cent. Gold Bond dues 1949. [External Loan] Exchange for a like amount of Engraved Bonds.*"

title: Czechoslovak State 8% Bond 1922, $100, CANCELLED
description: US Dollar-denominated bearer bond of the Czechoslovak State (or a Czechoslovak state enterprise), 8% interest, 1922, face value $100. The bond is stamped CANCELLED (annulled) with a large red overprint. The bond's legal basis and conditions are printed in English (and possibly Czech). The Czechoslovak State issued dollar-denominated bonds on the US market in the early 1920s to raise foreign currency capital for post-WWI reconstruction. The cancellation suggests this bond was redeemed or annulled during a debt restructuring.
creator: Czechoslovak State

title: Egyptian Credit Foncier / Crédit Foncier Egyptien, Bearer Share £20 or Frs. 500, No. 5,000,246, Cairo, January 1904
description: Bilingual English/Arabic/French bearer share No. 5,000,246 of the Egyptian Credit Foncier (Crédit Foncier Egyptien / البنك العقاري المصري), created by decree of H.H. the Khedive bearing date February 15, 1880. Société Anonyme. Capital: £4,000,000 or Frs. 100,000,000. Share value: £20 or Frs. 500 to bearer (Action au Porteur). Presented in both English and French with Arabic title text and decorative Moorish/Islamic arch motif framing the certificate. Cairo, January 1904. Has endorsement and transfer stamps visible at bottom. The Egyptian Credit Foncier provided real estate and agricultural mortgage credit in Egypt under the Khedival era.

title: Bank of England, Consolidated £3 per Cent. Annuities, Letter of Attorney for Acceptance and Dividends, Joint Account, London, ca. 1831
description: Bank of England printed letter of attorney for Consolidated £3 per Cent. Annuities (Joint Account), dated ca. 1831. The document authorizes named attorneys to accept transfers of Three per Cent. Annuities at the Bank of England and to receive dividends on behalf of the principals. Printed by the Bank of England. London seal affixed. Signed, sealed, and delivered in the presence of witnesses. Standard printed form with manuscript insertions.
creator: Bank of England
notes: Joint Account No. 17601, Letter of Attorney for Consolidated 3% Annuities; issued to James Goldson and others, ca. 1831. Names multiple attorneys for dividend receipt and transfers at Bank of England.
transcription:
# Transcription

**JOINT ACCOUNT** | Consolidated £3 *per Cent.* Annuities.
**ACCEPTANCE AND DIVIDENDS.**

*[Dated]* 25th 1831

Know all Men by these Presents, That We *James Woldswyn of Eyelt Brokham Road Edwarg Palombes Clarke of Leyningfiam Lodgh in Norfolk Solicitors* 

do jointly and each of us doth separately for ourselves, and for the Survivor of us, make, constitute, and appoint *David Brown Wolseley Barclay Chefter Lee Benan Henry Turchell Barclay Simon and [unclear] Sutton James all of London Bankers*

our true and lawful Attorney, *[manuscript insertion - illegible]* for us and in our Names and on our behalf, and also for and in the Name and on the behalf of the Survivor of us, to accept all such Transfers, as are or may hereafter be made unto us, of any Interest on Share in the Capital or Joint Stock of the £3 *per Cent. Annuities,* erected by an Act of Parliament of the Twenty-fifth Year of the Reign of His Majesty King George II. entitled, *[text continues]* transferable at the BANK OF ENGLAND, to be charged on the Sinking Fund, &c. and by several subsequent Acts. Also to receive and give Receipts for all DIVIDENDS that are now due, and that shall hereafter become due and payable for the same, for the Time being.

And to do all lawful Acts requisite for effecting the Premises; hereby ratifying and confirming all that *[manuscript insertion - illegible]* shall do therein by Virtue hereof. And in Case of our Deaths, *Attorney* *[manuscript insertion - illegible]* by virtue of, or under Colour, or in pursuance thereof; shall so far as the Governor and Company of the Bank of England are interested or concerned, be as binding upon our respective Executors and Administrators, as the same would have been upon us if living, unless notice in writing of our Deaths, shall have been given previously to the said Governor and Company of the Bank of England: and unless such Notice be given, we hereby severally covenant promise and engage and bind ourselves and our respective Executors and Administrators, to and with the said Governor and Company of the Bank of England, that our respective Executors and Administrators, shall and do allow, ratify and confirm, as good, valid and effectual, Attorney *[manuscript insertion - illegible]* after our decease, so far as the said Governor and Company of the Bank of England, shall or may be in any way or manner interested therein. In Witness whereof we have hereunto set our Hands and Seals the

Day of *[blank]* in the Year of our Lord One Thousand Eight Hundred and Thirty *[illegible]*

*Signed, sealed, and delivered, in the Presence of us by the above-named.*

---

**N.B.** If executed out of Great Britain to be attested by His Majesty's Consul, or Vice Consul, a Magistrate, or Notary Public, in addition to the usual Witnesses.

You are desired to attend to the Instructions for executing Letters of Attorney which are on the other side.

*Printed at the Bank of England, J. Oldham Superintendent.*

---

# Notable markings:

- London seal (circular stamp, upper left)
- Two wax seals or embossed seals (lower right)
- Serial/reference number at top: "11690" and "N"
- Decorative flourish/rule in the center of the document
- Red printed text at bottom (instructions line)
- Handwritten manuscript insertions throughout (dates and names)

**Main Document:**

STADT BERLIN

Schuldverschreibung

Fünfzigtausend Mark
deutsche Reichswährung

Ausgeüertigt mit Grund der mit Ermächtigung des preußischen Staatsministeriums erteilten Genehmigung nach Maßgabe des Beschlusses vom 30. Juni 1923 (Reichsbl. Anlage- und Veneralsamtsblatt) vom 5. Juli 1923).

Auf Grund der von dem Oberpräsidenten von Berlin genehmigten Beschlüsse der städtischen Behörden vom 11., 26. April 1923 wegen Aufnahme einer Anleihe von 10. Milliarden Mark bekundet hierdurch die Magistratur der Stadt Berlin namens der Stadt durch diese, für jeden Inhaber gültige Schuldverschreibung die Gewährung einer tiefsten des Gläubigers unhinderbar Darlehnsschuld von

50 000 Mark.

Die Bedingungen dieser Anleihe sind umfältig abgedruckt.

Zur Sicherheit der hierdurch eingegangenen Verpflichtung haftet die Stadt Berlin mit ihrem Vermögen und mit ihrer Steuerkraft.

Dafern unr Urkunde haben wir diese Aussfertigung unter unserer Unterschrift erteilt.

Berlin, den 31. Juli 1923.

Magistrat der Reichshauptstadt.

[Signatures]
Oberbürgermeister [illegible signature]
Kämmerer [illegible signature]

Aussfertigt [illegible] Seite 156

[Illegible signature]
Kämmerermeister.

---

## English Translation:

**Header:**
1923
January/July

8-18% Berlin Municipal Loan over 3¾ Billion Marks 1923
M. 50000

---

**Main Document:**

CITY OF BERLIN

Debenture (Debt Certificate)

Volume S, No: 15581

Fifty Thousand Marks
German Reich Currency

Issued on the basis of authorization from the Prussian State Ministry, issued in accordance with the resolution of 30 June 1923 (Reichsbl. Loan and General Administrative Gazette) of 5 July 1923.

On the basis of the resolutions of the municipal authorities of Berlin approved by the Superior President of Berlin from 11 and 26 April 1923 regarding the raising of a loan of 10 billion marks, the Magistracy of the City of Berlin hereby declares to every bearer of this debenture the granting of a debt obligation from the creditor of

50,000 Marks.

The conditions of this loan are printed on the reverse.

For the security of the obligations hereby incurred, the City of Berlin is liable with its assets and with its tax revenues.

We have issued this document under our signature in confirmation thereof.

Berlin, 31 July 1923.

Magistrate of the Imperial Capital.

[Signatures]
Lord Mayor [illegible]
Municipal Treasurer [illegible]

Issued [illegible] Page 156

[Illegible signature]
Municipal Treasury Officer.

---

**English text on reverse (partial):**

By virtue of the resolutions of the municipal authorities of April 11 & 26 1923 concerning the issue of a loan of not more than 10000 million marks approved by the administrative President of Berlin, the municipality of the City of Berlin binds itself on the part of the City to each and every bearer of this Bond in the sum, non-callable by the creditor, of

50 000 marks.

The conditions of this Loan are printed on the reverse side.

The City of Berlin is liable with all its assets and revenues for the fulfilment of the obligations resulting from this Bond.

In witness whereof we have hereunto affixed our hand and seal.

---

## Notable Markings:

- **Serial Number:** 15581
- **Series:** Buchtabe (Volume) S
- **Denomination:** 50,000 Mark (Fünfzigtausend Mark)
- **Date:** Berlin, 31 July 1923 (Berlin, den 31. Juli 1923)
- **Signatures:** Three signatures by municipal officials (Oberbürgermeister/Lord Mayor, Kämmerer/Treasurer, Kämmerermeister/Treasury Officer) - signatures are illegible
- **Seal:** Official seal of the Magistrat der Reichshauptstadt (Municipal Council of the Imperial Capital) visible at bottom center
- **Border Design:** Ornate decorative border throughout
- **Denomination Display:** M. 50000 in top right corner
- **Document Reference:** Aussfertigt (Issued) Page 156
- **Interest Rate:** 8-18% (visible in header)

# KINGDOM OF GREECE - 2.5% GUARANTEED GOLD LOAN OF 1898

## Original Text (Greek and French):

**Header (Greek):**
ΒΑΣΙΛΕΙΟΝ ΤΗΣ ΕΛΛΑΔΟΣ
(Kingdom of Greece)

**Title (Greek):**
ΔΑΝΕΙΟΝ ΕΙΣ ΧΡΥΣΕΟΝ ΗΤΙΗΜΕΝΟΝ 2 1/2 0/0 1898
Τῆ χώρᾳ ἀγνήσαι τῆς Γαλλίας, Μεγάλης Βρετανίας καὶ Ρωσσίας
ΟΜΟΛΟΓΙΑ ΑΝΩΝΥΜΟΣ 2,500 ΔΡΑΧΜΩΝ

To the honour of the Peoples of France, Great Britain and Russia

No. 43,842)

**Center Section (Bilingual - French and English):**

ROYAUME DE GRÈCE / KINGDOM OF GREECE

EMPRUNT DE GARANTIE 2 1/2 0/0 DE 1898 / GUARANTEED 2 1/2 0/0 PER CENT LOAN OF 1898

Cet emprunt est garanti par la France, la Grande-Bretagne et la Russie. / This loan is guaranteed by France, Great Britain and Russia.

Obligation au Porteur: 2,500 fr.

**Bond Details (English):**

**Interest Terms:**
Les intérêts sont payables le 15 avril et le 15 octobre de chaque année, à [location details].
(Interest is payable on the 15th April and 15th October of each year...)

**Extended Terms (Multi-language section with detailed conditions in French, English, and Russian):**

[Lengthy paragraph describing bond terms, including:]
- Interest payment dates and locations (Bank of England and other European centers)

- Conversion options
- Terms of redemption

The document contains extensive fine print conditions in three languages discussing:

"KINGDOM OF GREECE
GOLD LOAN GUARANTEED AT 2½% OF 1898
To the honor of the Peoples of France, Great Britain and Russia
ANONYMOUS BEARER BOND 2,500 DRACHMAS
No. 43,842"

**Terms Summary:**
"This loan is guaranteed by France, Great Britain and Russia.

The capital and interest of this loan will be paid in Gold at the Bank of England and other European financial centers.

Interest is payable on the 15th of April and 15th of October of each year.

The bond will be redeemed by drawings by lot. The first drawing will take place within thirty years of the issue and will be finished by 1928.

If resources permit, by option of the Greek Government, a portion of the debt may be redeemed before the dates fixed by the drawing..."

description: Conversion prize bond (Konverzios Nyeremeny Kotveny / Pramien-Obligation / Obligation Loterie) issued by A Magyar Jelzalog-Hitelbank (Ungarische Hypothekenbank / Hungarian Mortgage Credit Bank), Series 881 (Coupure A), No. 77. creator: Magyar Jelzalog-Hitelbank (Hungarian Mortgage Credit Bank)
notes: Littera A, Serie 881, No. 77, 100 Korona. Budapest, 1894. Total issue: 60,000,000 korona. Trilingual Hungarian/French/German. Signed by Bank President and Director.
transcription:
# Magyar Jelzalog-Hitelbank Conversion Prize Bond - 100 Korona

## Original Text:

**Left Column (French):**

SOCIÉTÉ DE CRÉDIT FONCIER DU ROYAUME DE HONGRIE.

COUPURE A

DE 100 COURONNES

DE OBLIGATION AUXOIS

CONVERTIE

Émise en vertu de la loi IX de l'an 1889 et une ukasse de résolution royale No 8866/1905, 14193/1906 et 26351/1906 du Ministère des Finances pour la conversion des 88,320,000 Cour. nominn, encore non amortis des Obligations émises 50 à priori du décret de Crédil Foncier du Royaume de Hongrie émises en 1894 pour un nominal de 70,000,000 de Cour. nominn.

La présente Coupure A, avec la Coupure B même Série et le même Numéro, représente l'Obligation originale 50 à lots, de laquelle l'Obligation originale 50 à lots, de laquelle chacune de ces Coupures pour sa moitié, donne droit à la jouissance invariable des lots de l'Obligation originale 50 à lots, conformément au plan II imprimes ci-après.

Pour suite de la conversion, l'intérêt original de ces Obligations à cessé après paye. Par contre, le porteur de l'Obligation vspait les droit de participer aux tirages conformément au plan II, même pour chaque Coupure individuellemmnt et entiérement.

Chaqui porteur de ces Coupures au tirage pour chaque Coupure, suit aux 200 Cour. originairs pour la dite Coupure, à lieu, conformément au plan II imprimé au verso, dans la même année durant jusqu'à court de sa durée d'amortissement. Jusq l'originaire de 100 Cour. pour chaque Coupure.

Ces Obligations sont garanties par les prêts hypothécaires inscrites au-dessus des prêts hypothécaires, tillers, communes et autres personnes morales après approbation de l'autorité compétente.

Le raciail des Obligations sorties aux tirages et le paiement des primes et des lots, conformément à un plan II imprimé au verso, etc. d'ailleurs préfracé du Conseil de Surveillance.

**Center (Hungarian):**

A Magyar Jelzalog-Hitelbank

Konverzios

Nyeremeny Kotveny

RISZKÖTVÉNYE 100 KORONÁRÓL

Jelen A. riszkötvény az azonos sorozati és számi. R. riszköltványnyel együtt az ugyanityen sorozati es szamú eredeti 200 koronas 50-es szerencsekölcsöny halyébe lépnek, ezen reszkötvénnyel pedig az R. jelzett sorozati es szamú riszkötvénnyel teljes részvételységi halyét viselik az eredeti 200 koronás 50-es szerencekölcsönyöket, a tioldalan i. sorsodsás terv értelmében.

A konverzie foldinn es eredeti 50-os Kötöénye nyereségeiben nincs többé részvétele az után ic és osztaloni és egveszítesen — a II. általa sorosdt terv szerinti nyereszóbeejsitésben való részuételének vá leszürgalmiét.

A riszkötvénynek 100 korondi, mindenbdl riszköltványnak nián lohol az eredeti 200 koronód trős riszkötvénynek miszigaziatása a II. alatt egy szeritu a körömódall öaszesen egyenlően osztzat s szerint, mely szempont lehet az alap a petgy így, hogy minden riszkötvény 100 koronáár [illegible] szomit egész azon öszzegen meg igen megfelelőet terjedelmű, ugy hogy a riszközsimékezetikség, a a [illegible] kölcsönyst jelentő, valamint mi ágon jogi szempont könelékessen kiösztódik és kölcsönbsé vét [illegible].

Midvolt kihasitott kölcsönyek között, a már is nyomtatmények hivaáctse a lálolsatosm levú háiorosa ruszsiletéshez törtendk.

Budapest, 1906. október 1.

MAGYAR JELZALOG-HITELBANK

[Signatures present but not clearly legible]

**Right Column (German):**

UNGARISCHE HYPOTHEKENBANK
TEILOBLIGATION LIT. A
ÜBER 100 KRONEN DER
KONVERSIONS-
PRÄMIEN-OBLIGATION

[Long German text describing the bond terms and conditions, including references to conversion regulations and legal framework]

Budapest, 1. Oktober 1906.

UNGARISCHE HYPOTHEKEN-BANK,

Der behördliche Kommissär:
Der Präsident des Auffsichtsrats:
Für die Kontrolle:

- **Ornamental elements:** Decorative "A" medallions in corners and elaborate typographic headers
- **Text languages:** Hungarian (center), French (left), German (right)

**ИМПЕРАТОРСКОЕ РОССИЙСКОЕ ПРАВИТЕЛЬСТВО**

**РОССИЙСКИЙ 4% ГОСУДАРСТВЕННЫЙ ЗАЕМЪ 1902 Г.,**

выпущенный для реализации привлекаемого России от Китая вознаграждения, полученного высочайшим Указом 1 Марта 1902 г.

= 983,000,000 англ. фунтов, вар. = 281,570,000 тол. гульд. = 19,357,000 франков.

**ОБЛИГАЦИЯ № 196422**

**В ТЫСЯЧУ ИМПЕРАТОРСКИХ ГЕРМАНСКИХ МАРОК**
= 463 руб. = 590 голд. гульд. = 49 фунт. стерл.,

выданная от Государственному Долговому Банку, ин. у отпр. _____

**IMPERIAL RUSSIAN GOVERNMENT**

**RUSSIAN 4% STATE LOAN OF 1902,**

issued for the realization of compensation received by Russia from China, pursuant to the Imperial Decree of March 1, 1902.

From the original capital of 181,950,000 rubles = 983,000,000 English pounds, var. = 281,570,000 guilders = 19,357,000 francs.

In the quantity of 22,000 sheets of 5,000 marks, nos. 000001 to 022000; 75,000 sheets of 2,000 marks, nos. 022501 to 097000; 75,000 sheets of 1,000 marks, nos. 097001 to 207000; 125,000 sheets of 500 marks, nos. 207001 to 253000.

**FOR ONE THOUSAND IMPERIAL GERMAN MARKS**

Issued by the State Debt Bank, for circulation.

## Original (Russian):

**Header:**
ИМПЕРАТОРСКОЕ РОССИЙСКОЕ ПРАВИТЕЛЬСТВО.
НИКОЛАЕВСКАЯ ЖЕЛЕЗНАЯ ДОРОГА.

**Main Title Section:**
ВЫПУСК:
600,000 ОБЛИГАЦИЙ,
в каждой ПО СТО ДВАДЦАТЬ ПЯТЬ РУБЛЕЙ
или ПЯТЬСОТ ФРАНКОВ или ДВАДЦАТЬ ФУНТОВ СТЕРЛИНГ

и в доходе грядущих шесть голландских гульденов;
их бумаг 400,000 облигаций по одном в листе, за № 1—400,000,
и 200,000 по пяти в одном листе, за № 400,001—600,000,
по распоряжению Высочайшего Указа.

75,000,000 РУБ. или 300,000,000 ФР. или 12,000,000 Ф. СТ. или 141,600,000 ГОЛЛ. ГУЛЬД.
Высочайший Указ от 15 Июня 1867 г. (Д.)

**ПЯТЬ ОБЛИГАЦИЙ**
(Five Bonds)

с №579536 по №579540
в 625 рублей, 2500 франк., 100 фунт. стерл., 1180 голл. гульд.

**Body Text:**
Облигация сия дает право на получение до указ ных пор, пока они ещё не будут поданы попросенно по тарифу, ежегодно доходов в 4%, т.е. его подлежит, или эмисий дороги...

[The document contains detailed terms regarding interest payment schedules, semi-annual payments on January 2/14 and July 2/14, and payment locations]

**Payment Locations:**
В Париже: у Четной Конторы у Господ Hottinger & Co.
В Лондоне: у Уполномоченного и у братьях Берингов и Co.
В Амстердаме: y Т.П. Козе и Сыне.

**Signatures and Authority:**
[Signature line for Bursmaster]
[Signature line for Upolnomochenniy ot Pravitelstva - Government Representative]

At bottom, official seal and notation:
О Государственном Министерстве Финансов.

IMPERIAL RUSSIAN GOVERNMENT.
NIKOLAEVSKY RAILWAY.

ISSUE:
600,000 OBLIGATIONS
each of ONE HUNDRED TWENTY-FIVE RUBLES
or FIVE HUNDRED FRANCS or TWENTY POUNDS STERLING

and in the income of forthcoming six Dutch guilders;
of these 400,000 obligations one per sheet, numbered 1—400,000,
and 200,000 five per sheet, numbered 400,001—600,000,
by order of the Imperial Ukase.

75,000,000 RUBLES or 300,000,000 FRANCS or 12,000,000 POUNDS STERLING or 141,600,000 DUTCH GUILDERS.
Imperial Ukase of 15 June 1867 (D.)

**FIVE OBLIGATIONS (BONDS)**

from No. 579536 to No. 579540
at 625 rubles, 2500 francs, 100 pounds sterling, 1180 Dutch guilders.

This obligation grants the right to receive, until the indicated time, while they have not yet been submitted to tariff request, annual income of 4%, that is, it is subject to, or the emission of the road [details regarding terms]...

In London: At the Representatives and Brothers Berenghs and Co.
In Amsterdam: At T.P. Kozе and Son.

transcription:
# Russian Liberty Loan Bond - 1917

## Original Text (Russian):

**Header:**
№0643022                                                                                                №0643022
IV серія.                                                                                                IV серія.

**Title:** ЗАЕМЪ СВОБОДЫ

**Subtitle:** ГОСУДАРСТВЕННАЯ ДУМА

**Main Denomination:** 5% ОБЛИГАЦІЯ
въ СТО РУБЛЕЙ нарицательныхъ.

**Body Text:**
Изъ имени граждане великой свободной России, изъ тѣмъ изъ Васъ, кому дорого будущее нашей Родины, съ — вѣрой мы нашь горячо призыв.

Сильный враг гибнуло втцрого въ нашь предѣлы, грозит сломать часть и вернуть страну къ старому, иынь мертвому строю.

Только напряженіе всѣхъ нашихъ силъ можетъ дать намъ желанную побѣду. Нужна затрата многихъ миллиардовъ, чтобы спасти страну и завершить строеніе свободной России на началахъ равенства и правды.

Не мертвы требуетъ отъ насъ Родина, а исполненія долга.

Ободримъ денеги Государству, помѣстимъ ихъ въ новый заемъ, и спасемъ отъмъ отъ гибли нашу свободу и достоеніе.

**Location and Date:** Петроградъ, 27 марта 1917 года.

---

## English Translation:

**Title:** LOAN OF FREEDOM

**Subtitle:** STATE DUMA

**Main Denomination:** 5% BOND
in ONE HUNDRED RUBLES nominal value.

**Body Text:**
In the name of citizens of the great, free Russia, by those to whom the future of our Motherland is dear, we make this ardent appeal.

A strong enemy, broken in the second [offensive] within our borders, threatens to break our strength and return the country to the old, now dead system.

Only the strain of all our forces can give us the desired victory. A vast expenditure of millions is needed to save the country and complete the building of free Russia on the principles of equality and justice.

Our Homeland does not require the dead from us, but the fulfillment of duty.

Let us encourage [people to give] money to the State, place it in this new loan, and thus save our freedom and wellbeing from ruin.

**Location and Date:** Petrograd, 27 March 1917.

---

- **Serial Number:** № 0643022 (appears twice, top left and right)
- **Series:** IV (appears twice, top left and right)

# TRANSCRIPTION

## REPUBLIC OF PERU.

**THE GUARANTEED LOAN**

**OF**

**THE NATIONAL PISCO TO YCA RAILWAY COMPANY**

**SERIES B.                                                                                                              SERIES B.**

WITH THE AID OF & UNCONDITIONAL GUARANTEE OF THE SUPREME GOVERNMENT OF THE REPUBLIC OF PERU FOR 5 PER CENT PER ANNUM FOR 2 OF & 2 PER CENT FOR REDEMPTION AND 1 PER CENT FOR CUMULATIVE SINKING FUND FOR REDEMPTION INTEREST

**£290,000**                                                                                                        **SOLES 1,450,000**

**STERLING STOCK**                                                                                          **PERUVIAN CURRENCY**

IN BONDS OF £500 STERLING, 500 SOLES EACH AND OF £20 STERLING, 100 SOLES EACH AND IN SERIES AS FOLLOWS:

SERIES A BONDS OF £20 £100 EACH No 1 TO 3,000 £60,000 £300,000
SERIES B BONDS OF £100 £500 EACH No 3001 TO 5,300 £230,000 £1,150,000

INTEREST PAYABLE HALF-YEARLY 1st JANUARY AND 1st JULY CAPITAL REDEEMABLE IN CASH AFTER SIX MONTHS NOTICE OR BY DRAWING BY LOT AT PAR SEMI ANNUALLY OF NOVEMBER OR THE YEAR IN CASE OF NOT SUCCEEDING IN JANUARY OF JULY

**No 3395    BOND FOR £100 STERLING SOLES 500 PERUVIAN CURRENCY**

---

## BODY TEXT (Left Column - English Version):

THE BEARER OF THIS BOND for £100 is entitled to an annual interest of Five Pounds Sterling (or Five Hundred Soles Peruvian Currency) payable on the 1st January and 1st July in each year during the currency of this Loan. The Principal is redeemable in Cash after Six Months' notice or by Drawing by Lot at Par Semi-Annually on or before November or the Year in case of not succeeding in January or July.

[Lengthy terms and conditions text follows regarding the guarantee of the Supreme Government of Peru, sinking fund provisions, and redemption terms - text becomes increasingly small and difficult to read clearly]

---

## BODY TEXT (Right Column - Spanish Version):

**Original:**

EL PORTADOR DE ESTE BONO por cien Libras Esterlinas (£100) tendrá derecho a un interés anual de Cinco Libras Esterlinas (o Quinientos Soles Moneda Peruana) pagadero el 1° de Enero y 1° de Julio de cada año durante la vigencia de este Empréstito. El Capital será reembolsable en Efectivo después de seis meses de aviso previo o por sorteo al Par semestralmente antes del 30 de Noviembre o en caso de no resultar sorteado antes de esa fecha en Enero del año siguiente.

[Extended terms and conditions in Spanish follow - text becomes increasingly difficult to read clearly at smaller sizes]

**English translation:**

THE BEARER OF THIS BOND for One Hundred Pounds Sterling (£100) shall be entitled to annual interest of Five Pounds Sterling (or Five Hundred Soles Peruvian Currency) payable on the 1st of January and 1st of July of each year during the currency of this Loan. The Principal shall be redeemable in Cash after six months prior notice or by drawing at Par semi-annually before November 30th or in case of not being drawn before that date in January of the following year.

[Extended terms regarding the guarantee of the Supreme Government of Peru, sinking fund provisions, and other conditions follow]

---

- **Series A Bonds:** £20 / £100 each, No. 1 to 3,000 (£60,000 / £300,000)

- **Total Loan:** £290,000 Sterling / Soles 1,450,000 Peruvian Currency

- **This Bond:** No. 3395, £100 Sterling / Soles 500 Peruvian Currency

This is a five-column lottery draw table document with accompanying text panels, presented in three languages: French, German, and Serbian.

- AMORTIZATION TABLE / AMORTIZATION PLAN / REDEMPTION PLAN

## TABLE CONTENTS

The document contains five columns of numerical data listing bond numbers designated for redemption in successive lottery draws. Each column contains multiple rows of numbers in the format:
- 000/XXX/XXX (bond reference numbers)
- Organized chronologically by draw dates
- References to "Tirage No." (Draw Number) and corresponding dates

[Due to the image quality and density of numerical data, individual bond numbers are difficult to read with complete accuracy. The tables appear to contain hundreds of entries organized in systematic columns.]

Original text begins: "Bul Damicourt de 1873 lors dates hereto..."
[The text is partially legible but heavily degraded. It contains conditions for the lottery drawing and appears to reference government authorization.]

**Right Panel (German):**
Original text begins: "Die Gesellschaft Amortisations-..." 
[Similarly degraded, containing conditions and administrative details for the redemption lottery scheme.]

---

## NOTABLE MARKINGS

title: Debito Unificato 5% della Citta di Napoli (Unified 5% Debt of the City of Naples), Serie D, No. 20027, 1,000 Lire, 1881
description: Bearer bond (Cartella al Portatore / Titre au Porteur) of the Unified 5% Debt of the City of Naples (Debito Unificato 5% della Citta di Napoli), Serie D, No. 20027. Nominal capital: 1,000 lire (Capitale Nominale Lire Mille). Total emission: 80,053,310 lire. Issued under the Royal Law of 14 May 1881 (Serie 3) and Regulation approved by Decree of 15 October 1881 (Serie 3), per decision of the Consiglio Comunale di Napoli of 20-23 November 1880. Naples, 20 November 1881. Signed by Il Sindaco and Il Delegato Governativo. A new coupon sheet of 98 cedole covering 1 July 1931 to 31 December 1979 is attached, marked "Debito Nuovo Foglio Cedole 1931-1979."
