Charles Laffitte was a French-born banker operating in London, associated with the distinguished French banking family of Jacques Laffitte. Charles Laffitte & Company was an Anglo-French banking and trading house involved in international finance during the mid-Victorian era of rapidly expanding global capital markets.

title: Chicago, Rock Island & Pacific Rail Road Company, $5,000 Gold Mortgage Bond
description: This engraved bond certificate represents a $5,000 Gold Mortgage Bond of the Chicago, Rock Island & Pacific Rail Road Company, Bond No. 1381. The Chicago, Rock Island and Pacific—known as the Rock Island Railroad—was one of the major Midwestern trunk lines, running from Chicago westward across the Great Plains. Gold mortgage bonds were secured by the railroad's real property and equipment and payable in gold coin, offering investors protection against currency inflation during periods of financial uncertainty.

title: Chicago, Rock Island and Pacific Railroad Company, $1,000 Gold Bond of 2002
description: This bond certificate represents a $1,000 Gold Bond of the Chicago, Rock Island and Pacific Railroad Company, due November 1, 2002, No. 530035. The Rock Island Railroad issued these exceptionally long-dated bonds in the mid-twentieth century as part of a major refinancing. The company, which had operated since 1847, went bankrupt in 1975 and was liquidated in 1980—more than two decades before the bonds were due to mature—making them ultimately worthless. The Rock Island's prolonged bankruptcy (1975–1984) became one of the most complex railroad reorganizations in US history.
creator: Chicago, Rock Island and Pacific Railroad Company
notes: Chicago, Rock Island and Pacific Railroad, $1,000 Gold Bond of 2002, due November 1, 2002, No. 530035

title: Republic of China Construction Gold Bonds, Year 29 (1940), $5 US Dollar Bond
description: This bond certificate represents a $5 US Dollar denomination bond from the Republic of China's Year 29 Construction Gold Bonds (建設公債), issued in 1940 during the Second Sino-Japanese War. Denominated in US dollars to attract overseas Chinese investors and foreign capital, these bonds were issued by the Nationalist Government (Kuomintang) to finance wartime infrastructure and military operations while much of coastal China was under Japanese occupation. Dollar-denominated Chinese bonds of this era reflect the internationalization of Nationalist government borrowing.

title: Gouvernement Général de l'Indo-Chine, 3.5% Obligation
description: This engraved bond certificate represents a 3.5% obligation of 500 Francs, No. 153,376, of the Gouvernement Général de l'Indo-Chine (General Government of French Indochina), issued on August 5, 1902. France issued obligations backed by the Indochina colonial government to finance railway construction, port development, and administrative infrastructure across Vietnam, Cambodia, and Laos. These bonds were secured by colonial revenues including customs duties and monopolies on opium, salt, and alcohol.
creator: Gouvernement Général de l'Indo-Chine
notes: Gouvernement Général de l'Indo-Chine, 500 Franc 3.5% obligation No. 153,376, August 5, 1902
transcription:
# Document Transcription

**Original (French):**

RÉPUBLIQUE FRANÇAISE

GOUVERNEMENT GÉNÉRAL DE L'INDO-CHINE

Emprunt de 200 Millions de Francs
dont la réalisation est autorisée par la Loi du 15 décembre 1898, affectée spécialement à la construction de chemins de fer en Indo-Chine. L'intérêt versé pour assurer le service des intérêts et de l'amortissement sera inscrit obligatoirement au budget annexé de l'Indo-chine.

DEUXIÈME ÉMISSION DE 155,000 OBLIGATIONS 3⁰/₀ DE 500 FRANCS
Effectuée en vertu du décret en date du 25 Juillet 1902 et suivant arrêté du Gouverneur général en date du 28 Juillet 1902.
Remboursables par tirage semestriel, qui aura lieu à Paris les 15 avril et 15 octobre de chaque année, à partir du 1er Avril 1903.

INTÉRÊT ANNUEL: 15 FRANCS PAYABLES PAR SEMESTRES LES 1er MAI ET 1er NOVEMBRE DE CHAQUE ANNÉE

Ces obligations sont affectées au service de la Banque de l'Indo-Chine. Elles peuvent être libellées en or ou en titres mobiliers à l'Indochine. Elles ne sont mobiliers à l'Indochine. Elles en sont admises aux droits de transmission sur les Valeurs mobilisés à l'Indochine le revenu, tell qu'il serait actuellement durable en France.

Le paiement des coupons semestriels sera tiré à Paris, à la Banque de France et à la Banque de l'Indo-Chine; à Saïgon, à la Banque de l'Indo-Chine et à Hanoï, à la même Banque. Les obligations seront également payables à Paris.

Le Gouvernement général de l'Indo-Chine reconnait à la faculté d'amortir l'emprunt par anticipation au cours du pair.

Obligation de 500 Francs au Porteur N°153,376

LE DÉLÉGUÉ DES SOCIÉTÉS CONTRACTANTES,     LE DÉLÉGUÉ DU GOUVERNEMENT GÉNÉRAL DE L'INDO-CHINE,

Paris, le 5 Août 1902.

---

**English Translation:**

FRENCH REPUBLIC

GENERAL GOVERNMENT OF INDO-CHINA

Loan of 200 Million Francs
the implementation of which is authorized by the Law of 15 December 1898, specifically allocated to the construction of railways in Indochina. The interest paid to ensure the service of interests and amortization shall be inscribed obligatorily in the annex budget of Indochina.

SECOND ISSUE OF 155,000 OBLIGATIONS 3% OF 500 FRANCS
Effected by virtue of the decree dated 25 July 1902 and pursuant to the order of the Governor General dated 28 July 1902.
Redeemable by semi-annual drawing, which shall take place in Paris on 15 April and 15 October of each year, starting from 1 April 1903.

ANNUAL INTEREST: 15 FRANCS PAYABLE BY SEMESTERS ON THE 1ST OF MAY AND 1ST OF NOVEMBER OF EACH YEAR

These obligations are allocated to the service of the Bank of Indochina. They may be denominated in gold or in securities in Indochina... [text continues regarding transmission rights and payment arrangements]

Bearer Obligation of 500 Francs No. 153,376

THE DELEGATE OF THE CONTRACTING SOCIETIES,     THE DELEGATE OF THE GENERAL GOVERNMENT OF INDOCHINA,

Paris, 5 August 1902.

---

- Serial Number: **153,376**
- Issue Date: **5 August 1902**
- Value: **500 Francs**
- Interest Rate: **3%**
- Two signature lines present (one appears to have a signature)
- Purple revenue stamp/cachet visible on left side
- Circular date stamp at bottom (partially legible: appears to show "1902" or similar)

title: Chinese Government Loan, £10 Bond at 4½%
description: This bond certificate represents a £10 bond at 4½% from the Chinese Government Loan of £6,866,046 10/10, signed by Li Sihao as Minister of Finance. This was one of the reorganization or consolidation loans issued by Republican China to restructure the foreign debt obligations inherited from the Qing Dynasty. British and European banks organized syndicates to place Chinese government bonds with international investors; such bonds were typically secured by Chinese customs revenues administered by the foreign-controlled Imperial Maritime Customs Service.
creator: Republic of China, Ministry of Finance
notes: Chinese Government Loan £6,866,046 10/10, £10 Bond at 4½%, signed by Li Sihao, Minister of Finance

of £ 6,866,046.10/10

2. In each year, in accordance with the Loan Agreement, a certain number of the Bonds of this series shall be drawn by lot and redeemed at par on the 1st day of December in the manner following:—

On the 1st day of December [followed by detailed redemption schedule with bond numbers and dates from 1913-1932]

title: Chinese Rent Receipt (收租票), Guangxu Period
description: This handwritten Chinese rent receipt (收租票) dates from the Guangxu period of the Qing Dynasty (1875–1908), issued by Bao Shan Hall (寶善堂 or similar). It records a rental payment for land or property using traditional Chinese financial accounting conventions and classical Chinese script. Such documents are primary evidence of land tenure and rental practices in late imperial China—a period of significant economic transformation as China confronted Western commercial and legal models while maintaining traditional agrarian financial customs.
creator: Bao Shan Hall
notes: Chinese rent receipt (收租票), Guangxu period (Qing Dynasty), ca. 1875-1908
transcription:
# Chinese Rent Receipt Transcription

## Original (Classical Chinese):

**Header (top of box):**
票租收

**Main text (reading right to left, top to bottom in columns):**

光緒三十二年十月初二日

周宅為給與農莊執掌本收到

秋租錢壹千文年

足錢現租該戶準于十月將租錢貼文

清不準秋帶欠如是日租錢不到

撤出另租此照舊租錢不到

將地攤出另租

寶善堂管字第

[Several red stamps/seals visible]

## English Translation:

**Header:**
Rent Receipt (票)

**Main text (logical reading order):**

In the thirty-second year of the Guangxu period [1906], the tenth lunar month, second day:

The Zhou household, acting as steward of the farmland, has received herewith the autumn rent payment of one thousand cash.

If the full rent is paid by the tenth month, the rent payment will be recorded as agreed. If the rent is not paid by the deadline in accordance with the terms and if there are outstanding debts, this arrangement shall be voided and the land shall be re-leased to another tenant.

If the rent payment is not received, the land shall be taken back and leased to another party.

Bao Shan Hall
[Management record number]

## Notable Markings:

- **Red stamps/seals:** Multiple red seals and stamps throughout the document, indicating official verification and ownership marks
- **Title seal/stamp:** Upper left corner contains an official rectangular seal
- **Handwritten annotations:** Various red ink markings and corrections visible throughout
- **Institutional mark:** "寶善堂" (Bao Shan Hall) appears as the issuing entity

title: 遂億換揭貸倉有限公司 (Sui Yi Mortgage Warehouse Co.), Share Certificate
description: This Chinese share certificate (股份憑票) is from the 遂億換揭貸倉有限公司 (Sui Yi Huan Jie Dai Cang Youxian Gongsi / Sui Yi Mortgage Warehouse Limited Company), issued in Guangxu Year 29 (1903). The company combined mortgage lending (揭貸) with warehouse operations (倉), issuing receipts against stored goods as a form of commodity-backed credit—a hybrid of traditional Chinese pawnshop and Western joint-stock company forms. This represents the institutional experimentation of the late Qing reform era, when Chinese entrepreneurs adapted Western corporate structures to indigenous financial practices.

title: 筒碧鐵路公司 (Tong Bi Railway Company), Share Certificate
description: This Chinese railway share certificate (股票) is from the 筒碧鐵路公司 (Tong Bi Railway Company), issued in Republic of China Year 20 (1931), and features a printed map showing the company's railway route. The inclusion of a route map served both as corporate branding and as documentary evidence of the planned physical infrastructure. The Tong Bi Railway was a provincial or private railway project of the Nanjing Decade (1927–1937), reflecting the Nationalist government's efforts to expand China's railway network beyond the coastal treaty port regions.

title: 日本勤業銀行 (Japan Industrial Bank) Discount Bond, 3,000 Yen
description: This Japanese bond certificate is a 第十面割引勤業債券 (10th Issue Discount Industrial Bond) of the 日本勤業銀行 (Nippon Kangyo Ginko / Japan Industrial Bank), denomination 3,000 yen, with a sale period of July 15 to July 18. The Japan Industrial Bank, established in 1897, was a government-chartered special bank that issued debentures (勤業債券) to fund agricultural improvement, land development, and rural credit throughout the empire. Discount bonds were sold below face value and redeemed at par, with no periodic coupon payments.

title: Republic of China Third Lottery Bond (第叁次有獎公債), 1927
description: This Chinese government bond is the Third Prize Lottery Bond (第叁次有獎公債) issued by the National Government Ministry of Finance (國民政府財政部), Republic of China Year 16 (August 1, 1927), denomination 5 yuan, No. 0675928. The bond features a portrait of Sun Yat-sen, founding father of the Republic. Lottery bonds—public debt instruments with periodic prize drawings instead of ordinary interest—were popular Nationalist fundraising devices; this third issue was among the earliest such bonds issued by the newly established Nanjing government after its 1927 victory in the Northern Expedition.

title: Cinco Por Ciento Español (Spanish 5% Perpetual Rente), Primera Serie
description: This Spanish government bond certificate represents a share of the Cinco Por Ciento Español (Spanish 5% Perpetual Rente), Primera Serie, issued at Oñate on February 6, 1860, with interest payable in Madrid, London, Paris, Brussels, and Turin. The certificate has attached coupon strips for the periodic interest payments. The Spanish 5% perpetual rente was a consolidated national debt instrument traded on multiple European exchanges to attract international investors; it was serviced at financial centers across Europe, reflecting Spain's integration into international capital markets in the mid-nineteenth century.
creator: Government of Spain
notes: Cinco Por Ciento Español (5% Spanish Perpetual Rente), Primera Serie, Oñate, February 6, 1860, payable in Madrid/London/Paris/Brussels/Turin, with coupon strips
transcription:
# Transcription: Cinco Por Ciento Español (Spanish 5% Perpetual Rente)

## Original Text:

**HEADER:**

Cinco Por Ciento Español
RENTAS PERPETUAS,
PAGADERAS

EN MADRID, á la eleccion del Portador,

EN AMSTERDAM, LONDRES, PARIS, BRUSELAS Ó TURIN.

PRIMERA SERIE.

No. 2460

**CAPITAL AND RENTA SECTIONS:**

Capital:
- Nros Flor. 2,400
- Nro. 200
- Nro. 5,076
- Total: 8,940

Renta Anual:
- Nro. Flor. 120 en Amst.
- Nro. £ 10 en Londres
- Nro. [amount] en París
- Nro. 55.50 en Florines

**MAIN TEXT:**

"El presente Certificado, desde que los pagos que se especifican á continuacion, que han sido estipulados por el Real Decreto dado este día en Oñate á S. M. C. el Señor Don CARLOS V. representará una Renta anual y perpetua de CIENTO Y SIETE PESOS FUERTES de ocho Reales de Vellon cada uno, pagaderos en la Tesorería Real de Madrid, en especies de Oro ó Plata, y se recibirá en las inscripciones activa, que se establecerá, con preferencia á todas las demás obligaciones en el erario de la Deuda Pública de España, en virtud del mencionado Decreto de este día.

Esta renta anual y perpetua imprescriptible á correr desde primero de 1856, y después de la entrada de S. M. C. el Señor Don CARLOS V. en Madrid, á 4 de eleccion del Portador, en Amsterdam, Londres, París, Bruselas ó Turin, las cuales Bolsas y en los casos indicados al pie del presente Certificado, en virtud de las entregas de los adjuntos pagos.

El Contratante;
[Signatures present but largely illegible]

Oñate, 6 de Febrero de 1856."

**COUPON SECTION (Right side):**

Multiple coupon strips listing:
- "CINCO POR CIENTO ESPAÑOL"
- "Un Ceros en VEINTE Y TRES PESOS FUERTES Y MEDIO, pagadero en Madrid ó en Amsterdam, Londres, Paris, Bruselas ó Turin,"
- Various dates and serial numbers (Nº. 2460, 2459, 2458, etc.)
- Recurring text about payment locations: "en Amsterdam, Londres, París, Bruselas ó Turin, en las mismas Bolsas y en los casos indicados al pie del adjunto Certificado."

**FOOTER NOTES:**

Payment instructions for various European cities:
- En Amsterdam, a razon de 120 florines por $ 7
- En Londres, en cada de los Banqueros de S.M.C., a razon de 10 libras esterlinas por $ 7
- En París,... á razon de...francos per $ 7
- En Bruselas,... á razon de... francos, per $ 47
- En Turin,... á razon de... liras, igual de valor per $ 7

---

## English Translation:

**HEADER:**

Spanish 5% [Perpetual Rente]
PERPETUAL INCOMES,
PAYABLE

IN MADRID, at the election of the Bearer,

IN AMSTERDAM, LONDON, PARIS, BRUSSELS OR TURIN.

FIRST SERIES.

No. 2460

**MAIN TEXT:**

"The present Certificate, from the time that the payments specified in continuation have been stipulated by the Royal Decree issued this day in Oñate to H.M.C. the Lord Don CARLOS V., shall represent an annual and perpetual income of ONE HUNDRED AND SEVEN STRONG PESOS of eight Reales of Vellon each, payable in the Royal Treasury of Madrid, in species of Gold or Silver, and shall be received in the active inscriptions that shall be established, with preference to all other obligations in the Treasury of the Public Debt of Spain, by virtue of the aforementioned Decree of this day.

This annual and perpetual, non-prescriptible income to run from the first [of the year] 1856, and after the entry of H.M.C. the Lord Don CARLOS V. in Madrid, on the 4th, at the election of the Bearer, in Amsterdam, London, Paris, Brussels or Turin, which Stock Exchanges and in the cases indicated at the foot of the present Certificate, by virtue of the delivery of the attached payments.

The Contractor;
[Signatures]

Oñate, 6th of February, 1856."

**COUPON SECTION:**

SPANISH 5% [RENTE]
"Twenty-three Pesos and One-Half Strong, payable in Madrid or in Amsterdam, London, Paris, Brussels or Turin, in the same Stock Exchanges and in the cases indicated at the foot of the attached Certificate."

---

## Notable Markings:

- **Serial Number:** No. 2460 (main certificate)
- **Date:** Oñate, 6 de Febrero de 1856 (February 6, 1856)
- **Signatures:** Present but largely illegible due to image quality
- **Royal Seal:** Visible in upper right ornamental border
- **Coupon Numbers:** Sequential (2460, 2459, 2458, etc.)
- **Ornamental Borders:** Decorative spiral patterns in left and right margins
- **Print Quality:** Period engraving with fine detail work typical of 19th-century bond certificates

title: Citrus Belt Land Company, Stock Certificate
description: This stock certificate represents 50 shares in the Citrus Belt Land Company of California, Certificate No. 426, issued to S.J. White on January 26, 1911. The Citrus Belt Land Company operated in Southern California's "Citrus Belt"—the citrus-growing regions of San Bernardino and Riverside counties, developed through irrigation projects and the Southern Pacific and Santa Fe railroads in the late nineteenth century. Land companies of this type subdivided and marketed agricultural land to settlers as part of the boosterism that transformed Southern California from semi-arid ranchland into densely settled citrus groves and suburbs.
creator: Citrus Belt Land Company
notes: Citrus Belt Land Company, California, 50 shares, No. 426, S.J. White, January 26, 1911

title: Clădirea Românească (Romanian Building Society), Share Certificate
description: This ornately printed share certificate represents 50 nominative shares (acțiuni nominative) of 500 lei each—totaling lei 25,000—in Clădirea Românească S.A. (Romanian Building Society), Emission VI-a, Nos. 1095201–1095250, Capital Social 600,000,000 lei, issued in Bucharest in January 1946. Clădirea Românească was a Romanian construction and real estate company. Its January 1946 issuance falls in the turbulent early postwar period, as Romania transitioned from wartime German occupation toward Soviet-aligned communist rule; such private companies were nationalized under the communist government that consolidated power in 1947–1948.

title: Colombian India-Rubber Exploration Company Limited, Share Warrant
description: This share warrant certifies the bearer's ownership of 25 shares (Nos. 188,476–188,500) in the Colombian India-Rubber Exploration Company Limited, issued on March 4, 1907. The company was a British enterprise formed to exploit natural rubber concessions in Colombia during the Edwardian rubber boom, when wild rubber from South America commanded premium prices before East Asian plantation rubber came to dominate the market. The company participated in the global scramble for tropical rubber resources that also drove the atrocities of the Congo Free State and the Peruvian Amazon.

title: Compagnie d'Inguaran (Mexique), Part de Bénéfices
description: This profit-sharing certificate, No. 07,399, is a Part de Bénéfices (profit participation share) of the Compagnie d'Inguaran, a French company with interests in Mexico, issued in Paris on January 15, 1898. The Compagnie d'Inguaran held concessions in the Inguaran district of Michoacán—historically important for copper and silver mining—during the Porfiriato era, when the Mexican government under Porfirio Díaz actively courted foreign capital. Parts de bénéfices were a French corporate form entitling holders to a share of profits without a fixed par value or voting rights.

title: Compagnie Occidentale de Madagascar, 100 Franc Share
description: This engraved share certificate represents one action of 100 Francs in the Compagnie Occidentale de Madagascar (Western Madagascar Company), No. 16,102, issued in Paris in the early twentieth century. The Compagnie Occidentale de Madagascar was a French colonial company operating in western Madagascar, France's large island possession in the Indian Ocean (colonized 1896). Such companies typically pursued agricultural production, stock ranching, or mineral extraction; western Madagascar's grasslands were suited to cattle ranching and the export of hides and other livestock products.

title: Compañía Salitrera de Tarapacá y Antofagasta, 10 Shares
description: This stock certificate represents 10 shares at 50 pesos each (Certificate No. C30095) in the Compañía Salitrera de Tarapacá y Antofagasta, issued in Santiago on April 30, 1937, with a large dividend coupon sheet attached. The company operated in the nitrate (salitre) mining districts of Tarapacá and Antofagasta in northern Chile, territories wrested from Peru and Bolivia in the War of the Pacific (1879–1884). Chilean nitrate, used as fertilizer and in explosives, had dominated world markets before the Haber-Bosch synthetic nitrogen process (1913) began displacing natural nitrates, shrinking the industry dramatically by the 1930s.
creator: Compañía Salitrera de Tarapacá y Antofagasta

title: Compañía Anónima Minas de Guanandi, 500 Pesos Share
description: This stock certificate represents one share of 500 Pesos ¾ Oro in the Compañía Anónima Minas de Guanandi, No. 2980, Capital 2,000,000 Pesos Oro (4,000 shares). Issued in Montevideo, 1887. The company was authorized by Decree of October 19, 1887 of the Government of Uruguay to exploit 50 mineral concessions in the municipality of Pocone, province of Matto Grosso (Brazil), granted by Brazilian Imperial Decree No. 9239 of June 28, 1884. This certificate illustrates the cross-border corporate investment of the late nineteenth century, with a Uruguayan company holding concessions to mine precious metals in Brazilian territory.
creator: Compañía Anónima Minas de Guanandi

title: Compañía de los Ferro-Carriles de Madrid a Zaragoza y a Alicante (MZA), 500 Franc Share
description: This engraved stock certificate represents one action of 500 Francs (or 1,900 Reales Vellón) in the Compañía de los Ferro-Carriles de Madrid a Zaragoza y a Alicante, also known as the MZA (Madrid–Zaragoza–Alicante), No. 233055, issued in Madrid on July 1, 1859. The certificate bears the notation "ESTE TITULO SUBSTITUYE AL PRIMITIVO DE IGUAL NUMERO Y FECHA" (This title replaces the original of the same number and date), indicating it was a replacement certificate. The MZA, formed in 1856, was one of Spain's two dominant railway companies, connecting Madrid to the Mediterranean coast; together with the Norte railway, it dominated Spanish rail transport until nationalization in 1941.
creator: Compañía de los Ferro-Carriles de Madrid a Zaragoza y a Alicante

title: Compañía La Petrolífera Nacional, 500 Peseta Share
description: This engraved stock certificate represents one Series A share of 500 Pesetas in the Compañía La Petrolífera Nacional (National Petroleum Company), No. 17341, issued in San Sebastián on May 15, 1928. The company, with a domicile at Plaza del Centenario 5 in San Sebastián, had a capital of 100,000,000 Pesetas divided into 140,000 Series A shares at 500 Pesetas and 800,000 Series B shares at 50 Pesetas. The certificate features a vignette of an oil derrick over an industrial landscape. The Petrolífera Nacional represents Spain's ambitions in petroleum exploration during the 1920s, the decade of international oil company formation following World War I.

title: Compañía Petrolera Mexicana "Faros de Aztlán", 50 Shares
description: This stock certificate represents 50 shares at 50 pesos gold each (total 2,500 pesos) in the Compañía Petrolera Mexicana "Faros de Aztlán", S.A., Certificate No. 0245 (Nos. 012201–012250), Capital 250,000 pesos gold, duration 50 years, domicile Ciudad de México. Issued Mexico City, October 1, 1916. The certificate is decorated with a prominent Aztec warrior figure. "Faros de Aztlán" (Lighthouses of Aztlán) invokes the mythical Aztec homeland, reflecting nationalist aesthetics during the Mexican Revolution. The company was constituted before Notary Don Francisco Merino Ortiz on June 24, 1916, during the revolutionary turbulence of the Carranza presidency.
creator: Compañía Petrolera Mexicana "Faros de Aztlán"

title: Compañía de las Hulleras de Ujo-Mieres, Five-Share Certificate
description: This bilingual (Spanish/French) stock certificate represents five shares (Nos. 370241–370245) at 25 Pesetas Oro each (completamente liberadas / entièrement libérées) in the Compañía de las Hulleras de Ujo-Mieres, Concesiones Reunidas de Ujo-Mieres y Riosa, No. 72049. The company, with a capital of 15,000,000 Pesetas Oro, was constituted on November 3, 1893, before Notary Don José Moreno de la Cruz, based in Madrid. The Ujo-Mieres mines operated in the Asturian coalfields of northern Spain—the most important coal mining region in the country—which fueled Spanish industrialization and were the scene of the 1934 Asturian miners' uprising.

title: Confederate States of America, $1,000 Cotton Loan Bond (1863)
description: This bond certificate represents a $1,000 bond of the Confederate States of America, Loan Authorized by Act of Congress Approved April 30, 1863 (the Cotton Loan). On the first day of June 1865 (or later date), the Confederate States promised to pay the bearer One Thousand Dollars at the seat of Government. Signed by C.G. Memminger, Secretary of the Treasury of the Confederacy. With attached cotton coupon strips labeled "C.S. Cotton Coupon Loan April 30, 1863." The 1863 Confederate Cotton Loan was a bond issue backed by cotton pledged as collateral, primarily aimed at European investors, representing the Confederacy's attempt to leverage its cotton reserves to secure foreign financing during the Civil War.
creator: Confederate States of America
notes: Confederate States of America, $1,000 Cotton Loan Bond, Act of Congress April 30, 1863, C.G. Memminger, Register signed
transcription:
# Transcription of Confederate States of America $1,000 Cotton Loan Bond (1863)

**CONFEDERATE STATES OF AMERICA**

**1000**

**LOAN AUTHORIZED BY ACT OF CONGRESS APPROVED APRIL 30, 1863.**

On the first day of June 1865, the Confederate States of America will pay to the Bearer of this BOND, at the Seat of Government, or at such place of deposit, as may be appointed by the Secretary of the Treasury, the Sum of ONE THOUSAND DOLLARS with interest thereon from the date hereof at seven per centum per annum, to be paid at the pleasure of the Government, at Richmond, or at the office of the Financial Agent of the Government in London at other places as the Secretary of the Treasury may direct. Coupons will be paid semi-annually at the places named above.

Interest Coupons attached hereto dated April 30, 1863, and numbered 1-4, or such supplementary [text unclear] to any Act of Congress approved April 30, 1863, entitled an "Act supplementary to an Act to provide for the funding and further issue of Treasury Notes."

In Witness whereof, the Registers of the Treasury in pursuance of the said Act of Congress hath hereunto set his hand and affixed the Seal of the Treasury at Richmond, this First day of June 1863.

[Signature] C.G. Memminger
Entered [illegible] N.M.
Recorded [illegible]

**REGISTER OF THE TREASURY**

---

**Bottom coupons (repeated four times):**

C.S. COTTON COUPON LOAN, APRIL 30, 1863.

The Confederate States of America will pay to bearer at New Orleans, Mobile, Savannah, Charleston, Wilmington, N.C. or Norfolk, SIXTY DOLLARS, for this year's interest due June 1, 1863, on a Bond for One Thousand Dollars of the Cotton Loan authorized by Act of Congress approved April 30, 1863.

---

**Notable markings:**
- Ornamental borders with wave patterns
- Portrait engraving (center top, likely a Confederate official)
- "1000" printed in four corners
- "ONE THOUSAND DOLLARS" on left panel
- Serial numbering areas (partially visible/illegible)
- Signature of C.G. Memminger, Secretary of the Treasury
- Four cotton coupon strips attached at bottom
- Official seals and decorative scrollwork throughout

title: Confederate States of America, $500 Bond, Act of August 19, 1861
description: This bond certificate represents a $500 bond of the Confederate States of America, authorized by Act of Congress of August 19, 1861. The bond promised payment on July 1, 1869 with interest at 8% per annum, payable semi-annually, backed by an export tax on cotton. Signed by C.G. Memminger, Secretary of the Confederate Treasury. With attached coupon strips at the bottom. This was one of the earliest Confederate bond issues, made during the first months of the Civil War, when the Confederate government was still optimistic about quick recognition by European powers and a short conflict.
creator: Confederate States of America
notes: Confederate States of America, $500 Bond, 8% per annum, Act of August 19, 1861, C.G. Memminger, with coupon strips
transcription:
# Confederate States of America $500 Bond - Transcription

**FIVE HUNDRED**

**CONFEDERATE STATES OF AMERICA**
**LOAN.**

**No. [blank]                                                                                                    No. [blank]**

AUTHORIZED BY THE ACT OF CONGRESS, C.S.A., AUGUST 19, 1861

On the first day of July, 1869, the Confederate States of America will pay to the bearer or registered owner thereof, or his legal representatives, the sum of **FIVE HUNDRED DOLLARS** with Interest thereon from this date, payable at the same place, semi-annually, at the rate of Eight per Cent, per annum, on surrender of the annexed Warrants or Coupons. This debt is authorized by an Act of Congress approved August 19, 1861, entitled "An Act to authorize the issue of Treasury Notes and to provide a War Tax for their redemption."

In Witness whereof His Majesty of the Treasury, in pursuance of the said Act of Congress hath hereunto set his hand and official seal of the Treasury at **RICHMOND**, this 31 day of July, 1862.

Entered A.T.L.                                                                    C.G. Memminger
Recorded, C.S.T.                                                                  Reg'r Register of the Treasury

[At bottom: Five coupon strips for "C.S. LOAN OF THE CONFEDERATE STATES OF AMERICA" dated "AUG 19 1862" and "Will pay to Bearer"]

title: Confederate States of America, $500 Bond, Act of February 20, 1863
description: This bond certificate represents a $500 bond of the Confederate States of America at 7% per annum, authorized by the Act of Congress of February 20, 1863, dated at Richmond on March 2, 1863. The bond promised payment on July 1, 1868, with semi-annual interest coupons, and contained the unusual provision that the Confederate States could defer payment for up to thirty years at the same interest rate upon surrender of the coupons. Signed by the Register of the Treasury. Issued to fund Confederate Treasury notes in the increasingly desperate final phase of the Civil War, when Southern financial resources were rapidly depleting.
creator: Confederate States of America
notes: Confederate States of America, $500 Bond, 7% per annum, Act of February 20, 1863, dated Richmond March 2, 1863
transcription:
# Transcription

**Seven Per Cent. February 20th, 1863.**

**Confederate States of America Bond**

**Authorized by Act of Congress**                    **C.S.A. of February 20th, 1863.**

No. _______________                    No. _______________

On the first day of July, 1868, the Confederate States of America will pay to the Owner of his bond at the seat of Government, or at such place as may be appointed by the Secretary of the Treasury, the Sum of **Five Hundred Dollars** with interest thereon from date at the rate of Seven per Cent. per annum, payable semi-annually on the maturities of the accrued Coupons. This Contract is authorized by An Act of Congress approved February 20th, 1863, Entitled "An Act to authorize the issue of Bonds for funding Treasury Notes, and to open the Express Condition that said Confederate States may from time to time extend the time of payment for any period not exceeding thirty years from this date at the same rate of interest upon the surrender of the Bond."

**In Witness Whereof** the Register of the Treasury in pursuance of said Act of Congress hath here under set his hand and affixed the Seal of the Treasury at Richmond this second day of March 1863.

Entered                                                     Register of the Treasury
Manifold

title: Conseil de la Dette Publique de l'Ancien Empire Ottoman, Provisional Receipt No. 0,288,837
description: This provisional receipt (Récépissé Provisoire au Porteur), No. 0,288,837, was issued by the Conseil de la Dette Publique Répartie de l'Ancien Empire Ottoman in Paris on October 9, 1933. It was delivered in exchange for one named obligation of nominal capital Frs. 500 (or $19.37 or £22) from the Ottoman 5% Emprunt of 1914. Following the dissolution of the Ottoman Empire, the 1923 Treaty of Lausanne allocated portions of the Ottoman public debt among successor states (Turkey, Greece, Bulgaria, and others). The Conseil de la Dette managed this redistribution; these provisional receipts replaced the original Ottoman bonds with new instruments recognizing the holders' claims. With coupon sheet (coupons 1–10) attached.
creator: Conseil de la Dette Publique Répartie de l'Ancien Empire Ottoman
notes: Conseil de la Dette Publique de l'Ancien Empire Ottoman, Provisional Receipt No. 0,288,837, Ottoman 5% Emprunt of 1914, Paris October 9, 1933, with coupons 1-10
transcription:
# Transcription

**Original (French):**

CONSEIL DE LA DETTE PUBLIQUE RÉPARTIE DE L'ANCIEN EMPIRE OTTOMAN
RÉCÉPISSÉ PROVISOIRE AU PORTEUR N° 0,288,837
délivré contre retrait d'une obligation unitaire d'un capital nominal de Frs. 500 ou £ 19,17 ou Liqs. 22
DE L'EMPRUNT OTTOMAN 5% 1914.

Conformément à la décision en date du 9 Juin -1933 de la Commission pour la répartition du capital nominal de la Dette Publique Ottomane, instituée par l'Article 49 du Traité de Lausanne, le présent récépissé a été délivré contre retrait de l'obligation susvisée, pour constater :

a) que la quote-part de la Turquie a fait l'objet d'une émission spéciale ; que les quotes-parts de l'Italie (Dodécanèse), de la Palestine, de l'Irak et des États du Levant sous Mandat français ont été entièrement amorties ;

b) et que, pour celle ci cent régiments, à la date d'extinction du présent récépissé, le capital nominal reste seul engagé auprès de l'emprunteur ; que les montants indiqués dans le tableau ci-dessous, incarnant comptés États qui en sont les débiteurs à raison des montants conces en regard de chacun d'eux.

[TABLE showing debtor states and capital amounts for: GRÈCE, BULGARIE, TRANSJORDANIE (à compter de la Caisse de Masque), HONGRIE, ITALIE, NEDJED, with proportions and capital amounts in Frs., en £, en Liqs.]

TOTAL: 100,- | 19,833,296 | 797,418 | 872,705 | 3.93 | 0.98 | 1.09

Le présent récépissé provisoire est exclusivement seuls pour constater les droits des porteurs des titres retirés de la circulation sur les États débiteurs. Ottomans, indiqués ci-après. Ce titre provisoire ne pourra, par le moyen de cession de droits effectifs au moyen du présent récépissé provisoire, qui pourrait être effectuées au moyen du présent récépissé provisoire, tous aussi qualifié pour procéder aux placements ou autres opérations quotés-parts dans la Dette Ottomane. Les comptes conclus entre les États pour le règlement de leurs parts respectives des opérations, dont les modalités seront portées, en temps utile, à la connaissance des porteurs, par les publications du Conseil de la Dette.

POUR CONTRÔLE:
Par Déclaration du Conseil:              POUR LE CONSEIL DE LA DETTE PUBLIQUE RÉPARTIE
                                         DE L'ANCIEN EMPIRE OTTOMAN.

[Signatures present]                      Le Président:

Paris, le 16 Octobre 1933.

[Document continues with 10 numbered coupons below, each labeled "COUPON" with identical header text and reference number 0,288,837]

---

**English Translation:**

COUNCIL OF THE PUBLIC DEBT APPORTIONED FROM THE FORMER OTTOMAN EMPIRE
PROVISIONAL RECEIPT TO BEARER NO. 0,288,837
issued in exchange for the withdrawal of a unit obligation with nominal capital of Frs. 500 or £ 19.17 or Liqs. 22
FROM THE 5% 1914 OTTOMAN LOAN.

In accordance with the decision dated June 9, 1933 of the Commission for the apportionment of the nominal capital of the Ottoman Public Debt, established by Article 49 of the Treaty of Lausanne, the present receipt has been issued in exchange for the withdrawal of the aforementioned obligation, to establish:

a) that Turkey's share was the subject of a special issue; that the shares of Italy (Dodecanese), Palestine, Iraq and States under French Mandate were entirely amortized;

b) and that, as far as the others are concerned, as of the date of extinction of the present receipt, the nominal capital remains solely engaged with the borrower; that the amounts indicated in the table below, representing the States which are the debtors, in proportion to the amounts shown opposite each of them.

[TABLE of debtor states and amounts]

The present provisional receipt is exclusively for the purpose of establishing the rights of holders of securities withdrawn from circulation on the debtor States. Ottoman, indicated below. This provisional title may not, by means of assignment of rights, be effective by means of the present provisional receipt, which could be used as a means for the placement or other operations of shares in the Ottoman Debt. The accounts concluded between the States for the settlement of their respective shares of operations, the modalities of which will be made known, in due course, to the holders, by publications of the Debt Council.

FOR CONTROL:
By Declaration of the Council:           FOR THE COUNCIL OF THE PUBLIC DEBT APPORTIONED
                                         FROM THE FORMER OTTOMAN EMPIRE.

The President:

Paris, October 16, 1933.

---

**Notable Markings:**

- Official red circular stamp of the "Conseil de la Dette Publique" (appears multiple times)
- Serial number: **0,288,837** (consistent across all coupons)
- Date: **16 Octobre 1933** (October 16, 1933)
- Handwritten signatures present (appears to be initials/approval marks)
- Ten numbered coupons (1-10) attached below the main certificate, each numbered identically
- Ornamental border framing the entire document
- Text printed in elegant serif typeface typical of 1930s financial documents

title: State of South Carolina, Six Per Cent Consolidation Bond, $500
description: This bond certificate represents a $500 Six Per Cent Consolidation Bond of the State of South Carolina, No. DP 1602, secured by an annual tax, with interest payable each January and July in New York and Columbia, S.C., and principal due July 1876. South Carolina's Reconstruction-era government issued Consolidation Bonds to restructure the state's debt accumulated during the Republican Reconstruction administration. After the Democratic "Redeemer" government regained power in 1876, South Carolina repudiated substantial portions of its Reconstruction debt, making such bonds a flashpoint in the political and financial controversies of the post-Civil War South.
creator: State of South Carolina
notes: State of South Carolina, Six Per Cent Consolidation Bond, $500, No. DP 1602, secured by annual tax, principal due July 1876

title: Dutch Coupons for American Land Shares, C.J.M. De Wolf, Antwerp (1795–1796)
description: This document contains two interest coupons (No. 827) of fifty Gulden each, payable as exchange money (wisselgeld) at the counting-house of C.J.M. De Wolf in Antwerp, in June 1795 and June 1796 respectively. Each coupon is attached to a share (Acte van Aendeel) of one thousand Gulden in lands in America, payable pursuant to Articles 2 and 3 of the original deed. These coupons document Dutch investment in American land companies during the 1790s—a period when Dutch financiers were among the largest foreign investors in the young United States, purchasing vast land tracts through companies like the Holland Land Company.
creator: C.J.M. De Wolf
notes: Two Dutch coupons No. 827, 50 Gulden each for f.1000 American land shares, payable at C.J.M. De Wolf, Antwerp, June 1795 and June 1796

title: Crédit Foncier Agricole du Maroc, 100 Franc Share
description: This engraved share certificate represents one action of 100 Francs au Porteur in the Crédit Foncier Agricole du Maroc (Agricultural Land Credit Bank of Morocco), No. 8497. The company, constituted by deed before Notaire Brocquei in Banque on March 17, 1920, had a capital of 1,000,000 Francs (10,000 shares at 100 Francs), with siège social at 92, Rue de Richelieu, Paris. The certificate is decorated with a Moorish arch and North African landscape with camels and palm trees. The Crédit Foncier Agricole du Maroc provided mortgage and agricultural financing in the French Protectorate of Morocco, established in 1912, supporting French settler agriculture and land development.

title: Connecticut Comptroller's Office Interest Certificate, 1792
description: This printed interest certificate, No. 13,729, from the Connecticut Comptroller's Office is dated February 23, 1792, and certifies that Solomon Ensign Jr. is entitled to receive Thirteen Shillings and Six Pence (£0.13.6) Lawful Money out of any funds appropriated for the payment of interest on the liquidated Debt of the State of Connecticut. Signed by A. Kingsbury, Comptroller. Printed by Hudson and Goodwin, Hartford. This document reflects the post-Revolutionary process of settling state war debts, part of the larger debt consolidation that Alexander Hamilton's funding program sought to address at the federal level.
creator: State of Connecticut, Comptroller's Office
notes: Connecticut Comptroller's Office, Interest Certificate No. 13,729, £0.13.6 for Solomon Ensign Jr., February 23, 1792; printed by Hudson and Goodwin, Hartford

title: The Cuba Company, Common Stock Specimen Certificate
description: This is a specimen (unissued) Common Stock certificate of The Cuba Company, incorporated under the laws of the State of New Jersey, stamped "SPECIMEN" in red at the bottom corners. The certificate shows agricultural and harvest vignette imagery at the top. The Cuba Company was organized in 1900 by William Van Horne and associates to build the Cuba Railroad, a 350-mile line connecting Santiago de Cuba to the western provinces, which opened up eastern Cuba's vast undeveloped territory to sugar cultivation. As a specimen, this certificate was retained by the printer or company as a reference copy.
creator: The Cuba Company
notes: The Cuba Company, common stock specimen certificate, State of New Jersey; company built the Cuba Railroad ca. 1900

title: Dale Land & Cattle Co., Stock Certificate
description: This engraved stock certificate represents fifty shares (Certificate No. 8) in the Dale Land & Cattle Co., incorporated under the laws of the State of Texas, Capital Stock $300,000 (3,000 shares at $100 each), issued to J.B. Dale Sr. on March 20, 1895. Places of business: Bonham and Henrietta, Texas; Headquarters: Clay County, Texas. Signed by B.A. McKinney (Secretary) and J.B. Dale (President). The Dale Land & Cattle Co. represented the transition of Texas's cattle ranching economy in the post-frontier era, as large operators like the Dale family shifted from open-range ranching to organized land and livestock companies operating in the Cross Timbers and Red River prairies.

title: Ostend Company (Generale Keijserlijcke Indische Compagnie), Share Subscription Receipt
description: This manuscript document bears the seal of the Generale Keijserlijcke Indische Compagnie (General Imperial East India Company / Ostend Company) and orders cashier Jan Baptist Cogels Jr. to receive from Anna Rosalia de Bruijn van Holst the sum of 250 Gulden exchange money (wisselgeld) as the first installment payment (25%) on one share of 1,000 Gulden in the capital of the said Company, per the conditions stated in the Company's charter (Octroy). Antwerp, August 16, 1723. The Ostend Company was chartered by Habsburg Emperor Charles VI in 1722 to compete in the East India and China trade from the Austrian Netherlands; it was suspended in 1727 under diplomatic pressure from Britain and the Dutch Republic, who viewed it as a threat to their trading monopolies.
creator: Generale Keijserlijcke Indische Compagnie (Ostend Company)

title: Grand Society of Russian Railways (Главное Общество Российских Железных Дорог), 125 Ruble Silver Bond
description: This bond certificate represents one obligation of 125 Silver Metallic Rubles (equivalent to 500 Francs, 20 British Pounds, 402 German Marks, or 238 Dutch Gulden) at 3% from the Third Issue of the Grand Society of Russian Railways (Главное Общество Россiйскихъ Желѣзныхъ Дорогъ), No. 220757. The Grand Society, chartered in 1857, was the principal contractor for building Russia's main trunk railway network. Its bonds were placed across European markets; the multi-currency equivalency on the certificate reflects systematic marketing to French, British, German, and Dutch investors. The Third Issue totaled 105,176 bonds for 13,147,000 rubles, issued circa November 3, 1861.
creator: Grand Society of Russian Railways (Главное Общество Российских Железных Дорог)

Годовый доход составляетъ: 3 р. 75 к. (по-полугодню: 1 р. 87 к.); в-ъ гoдъ и уплачивается по-полугодню, 1/3 июня и 1/3 декабря.

title: N.V. De Woning Maatschappij (Housing Company), Dividend Talon
description: This document is a dividend talon for share No. 1499 of the Naamlooze Vennootschap De Woning Maatschappij (Housing Company), established in Djakarta (Batavia / Jakarta), Dutch East Indies (now Indonesia). The talon—a stub used to obtain a new coupon sheet when the current one was exhausted—is accompanied by dividend coupons Nos. 46–56 for the share. The De Woning Maatschappij provided housing in the Dutch colonial capital; the high coupon numbers indicate a company that had been paying dividends for many years, with the talon issued when earlier coupon sheets had been fully utilized.

title: Debito Pubblico del Regno d'Italia (Italian Public Debt), 5% Rendita Certificate
description: This certificate from the Gran Libro del Debito Pubblico del Regno d'Italia (Grand Book of the Public Debt of the Kingdom of Italy) represents a 5% Rendita (perpetual government annuity) with an annual income of 20 lire, No. [001,300], issued from Florence, the provisional capital of the Kingdom of Italy before 1871. The certificate bears a portrait vignette of King Victor Emmanuel II and multiple cancellation and transfer stamps. The Italian 5% Rendita was the primary consolidated sovereign debt instrument of the young Kingdom of Italy (unified 1861), modeled on French and British perpetual government debt instruments.
creator: Kingdom of Italy, Ministry of Finance
notes: Debito Pubblico del Regno d'Italia, 5% Rendita, annual income 20 lire, issued from Florence; portrait of Victor Emmanuel II

title: Deli-Spoorweg-Maatschappij (Deli Railway Company), 1,000 Gulden Share
description: This share certificate, No. 7390, represents one share of 1,000 Gulden in the Naamlooze Vennootschap Deli-Spoorweg-Maatschappij (Deli Railway Company), Capital 10,000,000 Gulden, established by deed of June 28, 1888, last amended by Royal Decree of September 5, 1913. Amsterdam, May 5, 1914. Signed by the Commission-Chairman and Director. The Deli Railway Company operated a railway network in the Deli region of East Sumatra (Dutch East Indies, now Indonesia), serving the legendary Deli tobacco and rubber plantations whose products—particularly the thin-leafed Deli tobacco used as cigar wrappers—commanded premium prices in European and American markets.

title: Société Belge d'Exportation (Jules Van der Laat & Cie), Action de Fondation
description: This founders' share (Action de Fondation No. 021, Coupure No. 08) represents 1/10 of a Founder's Share of the Société Belge d'Exportation, a commandite par actions (limited partnership by shares) with raison sociale Jules Van der Laat & Cie, Capital Social 5,000,000 Francs (10,000 shares at 500 francs each). Constituted by authentic act on July 29, 1901, for thirty years. Siège Social: Anvers (Antwerp). Gérant: Jules Van der Laat; two Commissaires. Actions de Fondation—founders' shares without fixed par value, entitling holders to a portion of surplus profits—were a distinctive feature of Belgian and French corporate law that rewarded those who organized or contributed non-monetary assets to a company.

title: Banque Royale (John Law), 10 Livres Tournois Banknote, 1720
description: This printed banknote reads: "LA BANQUE promet payer au Porteur à vüe Dix livres Tournois en Especes d'Argent, valeur reçeüe. A Paris le premier Juillet mil sept cens vingt." No. 495,360. Labeled "Division." Witnessed by Fenelon/Giraudeau, signed by Bourgeois/Delanauze, and controlled by Durevest/Granet. John Law's Banque Royale (previously the Banque Générale, established 1716) issued paper notes convertible to silver, underpinning the Mississippi Company stock bubble. This note, dated July 1, 1720, was issued as the bubble was catastrophically collapsing—the bank suspended silver payments in May 1720, and was liquidated before year's end. It is a primary artifact of one of history's most famous financial crises and the first large-scale paper money experiment in Europe.
creator: Banque Royale
notes: Banque Royale (John Law), 10 livres Tournois "Division" banknote No. 495,360, Paris July 1, 1720; issued during collapse of Mississippi bubble

title: Banco Giro di Venezia (Venice Giro Bank), 100 Ducat Cedula
description: This document is a printed cedula (banknote) of the Banco Giro di Venezia (Venice Giro Bank / Bank of Transfer), stating it is to be received as effective money by all Royal and Public Cash Offices in payment of customs, land, and all other contributions, and by the Giro Bank's own cashier for exchange in account (in partita). Issued pursuant to the proclamation of October 1, 1798. Per Ducati Cento (100 Ducats) = £8. No. 1175. Signed by Giovannelli and Schiantavello. The Banco del Giro, founded in Venice in 1619, was one of Europe's earliest public giro banks; this note was issued in 1798 under Austrian Habsburg administration of Venice following Napoleon's 1797 conquest.

title: Overend, Gurney and Company Limited, £50 Share
description: This share certificate, No. 72249, certifies that John Metcalfe Smith, Esq., of Leeds, is the registered proprietor of one share of £50 (with £15 paid up) in the Capital Stock of Overend, Gurney and Company Limited, registered and incorporated on July 12, 1865, under the Companies Act 1862. Secretary: Wm. Bos. November 15, 1865. Overend, Gurney & Company—known as "the bankers' bank" and the largest discount house in London—converted to a limited liability company in 1865 to raise fresh capital. On May 10, 1866, the company suspended payments, triggering "Black Friday"—the worst financial panic in British history since 1825—and leading to thousands of investor losses across the country.
creator: Overend, Gurney and Company Limited
notes: Overend, Gurney and Company Limited, £50 share No. 72249 (£15 paid), John Metcalfe Smith, Leeds, November 15, 1865; company failed May 1866 causing "Black Friday"

title: Éclairage Électrique de St. Pétersbourg, 250 Franc Preference Share
description: This engraved share certificate represents one Preference Share (Action Privilégiée) of 250 Francs, entièrement libérée, No. 38859, in the Société Anonyme de l'Éclairage Électrique de St. Pétersbourg (Electric Lighting of St. Petersburg), constituted by notarial deed in Brussels and published in the Moniteur Belge of June 11, 1891, No. 1288. Siège Social: Bruxelles. Capital: 10,000,000 Francs (40,000 preference shares at 250 Francs + 20,000 ordinary shares). The certificate features an Art Nouveau electric lamp arch and a panoramic view of the St. Petersburg waterfront, illustrating how Belgian capital and engineering financed urban electrification in the Russian Empire.

title: Magyar Jelzálog-Hitelbank (Hungarian Mortgage Credit Bank), 100 Florin Prize Bond
description: This trilingual (Hungarian/French/German) bond certificate represents a 4½% Nyeremény-Kötvény / Obligation à Primes / Prämien-Obligation (Prize Bond) of one hundred Florins (Egyszáz forintról / Cent Florins / Hundert Gulden) in the Magyar Jelzálog-Hitelbank (Société de Crédit Hypothécaire du Royaume de Hongrie / Ungarische Hypotheken-Bank), Series 2884, No. 061, Budapest, July 1, 1880. The Hungarian Mortgage Credit Bank, established in 1870, was Hungary's principal institution for long-term mortgage financing. Prize bonds (nyeremény-kötvények) were widely popular in Central Europe, combining regular interest payments with periodic lottery-style prize drawings.
creator: Magyar Jelzálog-Hitelbank
notes: Magyar Jelzálog-Hitelbank, 4½% 100 Florin Prize Bond, Series 2884, No. 061, Budapest July 1, 1880; trilingual Hungarian/French/German
transcription:
# Magyar Jelzálog-Hitelbank Prize Bond Transcription

## Original Text:

**Header Section:**
SÉRIE 2884 SOROZAT | NUMMER 061 SZÁM

SOCIÉTÉ DE CRÉDIT FONCIER DU ROYAUME D'HONGRIE | MAGYAR JELZÁLOG-HITELBANK | UNGARISCHE HYPOTHEKEN BANK

**Main Title (Trilingual):**
Obligation à Primes | Nyeremény-Kötvény | Prämien-Obligation
au porteur de | bemutatóra | auf Ueberbringer über

CENT FLORINS | 100 | EGYSZÁZ FORINTRÓL | 100 | HUNDERT GULDEN

**Left Column (French):**
Cette obligation forme une part de l'émission de Obligations à 4½ % à primes, effectuée par la Société de Crédit Foncier du Royaume de Hongrie pour faire face aux prêts consentis par elle, conformément à ses statuts, aux Municipalités, Villes, Communes et Personnes légales en tant qu'elles sont autorisées par la loi à contracter des emprunts.

Cette obligation rapporte 4½ % normes val. annuels. L'intérêt est payable deux fois par an au remboursement de l'intérêt. Ces intérêts sont payables chaque semestre le 1. Juin et le 1. Décembre à charge d'une raison à Buda-Pesth et à Vienne conformément à la teneur des conditions énoncées d'autre part.

Le Royaume de Hongrie à Budapest et aux placements qui seront désignés à cet effet.

La Société de Crédit Foncier du Royaume de Hongrie s'engage à rembourser en cinquante années au plus tard le capital de cette obligation que les primes envisagées à la concordance au tableau.

Le remboursement des obligations sorties au tirage aura lieu le paiement des taux se fait dans les conditions énoncées d'autre part.
Budapest, le 1. Juillet 1884.

Le président. | Le directeur.
[Signatures]
Pour le contrôle.
Le président du conseil de surveillance.
[Signature]

**Center Column (Hungarian):**
Ezen kötvény részét képezi azon osztr. ért. 40 millió forinyi összegben kibocsátandó 4½%-os nyeremény-kötvényeknek, melyek a Magyar Jelzálog-Hitelbank által felvételezett, az alapszabályokban előírbozottságának, Országoknak és más jogi személyeknek — a mennyiben ezek hitoktatók hitoktatásnak nyeremény számtani vonatkozásában engedélyeztetik — közösolok alapján alatnak ki.

Ezen kötvény a negyede évente történő kamatszakaszok és kamatlevékvénti minden év junius 1-én és december 1-én ósszr. ért. 2—2 fitat a Magyar Jelzalog-Hitelbank pénztárán allal Budapeśten vagy más kijelölt helyen fizétetik ki.

A Magyar Jelzalog-Hitelbank kötelezi magát, hogy őzen részletkötvény törlesztendo és az inom nev azt nyeremenyek 50 év alatt a magz. kir. pénzigminisztérium által javáhagyott, a hatlapou foglalt sorolast térület fogju hitelni.

A kihuzott kötvények bevallása és a nyeremények kifizetése a följófátok leru iratazandelésenel eltartoitt.

Budapest, 1884. július 1-én.

A Jelen kötvény alapszábayzean föde-
fozottságának biztosítására.

[Signatures]
Igàszán.

**Right Column (German):**
Diese Schuldverschreibung bildet einen Theil der Emissionen von Främlien-Obligationen, welche von der Ungarischen Hypotheken-Bank im Budapest gemäß ihrer Statuten gezogeneu Darlehnen an Muntzipien, Städte, Gemeinden und andere juristische Personen — soweit diese selbigen zu deren Anlahen befugniset werden — entgegent zu werden.

Diese Schuldverschreibung wird bis zur Fälligkeit bezinst und zwar jährlich vier v.H. Die Zinsen halbigjahn am 1. Juni und am 1. Dezember gezahlt werden sollen zu dem Schaltrage der Ungarischen Hypotheken-Bank in Budapest und bei sonstigen hiertigen Lottung fallenderweise zahlbar.

Die Einlösung der gesprochen Schuldverschreibungen, sowie die Auszahlung der Gewinste erfolgt in Gemässheit nachstehender Bedingungen.

Budapest, am 1. Juni 1884.

Der Präsident. | Der Director.
[Signatures]

Für die Controle.
Der Präsident des Ausschusses.
[Signature]

---

## Notable Markings:

- **Series/Number:** SÉRIE 2884, NUMMER 061
- **Dates:** Budapest, July 1, 1884 (1. Július 1884)
- **Denomination:** 100 Florins (Cent Florins / Egyszáz forintról / Hundert Gulden)
- **Interest Rate:** 4½% (4½ %)

- **Official seal/stamp** in center of document
- **Ornamental border** with decorative elements throughout

title: El Callao Consolidated Gold Mining Company Limited, Share Warrant for Five Shares
description: This share warrant, Warrant No. 020887, certifies the bearer's ownership of five shares (Nos. 213621–213625) in the El Callao Consolidated Gold Mining Company Limited, Capital £800,000 (800,000 shares of £1 each). London, August 22, 1903. El Callao, in the Bolívar state of Venezuela, was one of the world's richest gold mining districts in the late nineteenth century; its mines, discovered around 1871, produced remarkable yields and attracted major British investment. The company was organized to consolidate mining operations in the district and continued operating through the early twentieth century.

title: Société Anonyme d'Exploitation de Phu-Quoc, 100 Franc Share
description: This elegantly designed share certificate represents one action of 100 Francs au Porteur, entièrement libérée, No. 032,638, in the Société Anonyme d'Exploitation de Phu-Quoc, with siège social at 55, Rue Lafayette, Paris. Statuts received before Notaire Nobert Dupont, Paris. The certificate features a striking design: a map silhouette of Phu Quoc island at center, flanked by a tropical panorama with sea views and dense vegetation. Phu Quoc is a large island in the Gulf of Thailand, then part of French Cochinchina (now southern Vietnam), known for pepper cultivation and fish sauce production. The company exploited the island's agricultural and natural resources as a concession under French colonial authority.

title: Imperial Russian Government: 6% Perpetual Bond, 5,000 Rubles (1859)
description: This large-denomination bearer bond of 5,000 rubles was issued by the State Commission for the Repayment of State Debts of the Imperial Russian Government, bearing 6% perpetual interest. Dated 1859. Perpetual bonds (often called "consols" after the British model) paid interest indefinitely without a fixed redemption date. This bond was part of Russia's substantial domestic funded debt in the mid-nineteenth century. The 5,000-ruble denomination indicates this was a wholesale instrument likely held by institutional investors or wealthy individuals.

Santiago de Cuba, April 20, 1861. Signed by J.M. de Llaguno (President), Lino [?] (Contador), and J.S. Lozano (Secretary). The company combined railway operations with storage warehouses (almacenes de depósito), a common structure in colonial Cuban enterprise that served the export economy for sugar, coffee, and other agricultural products shipped through Santiago de Cuba, the island's second city.

title: Commonwealth of Pennsylvania, Five Per Cent Stock of 1825, $100
description: This printed bond certificate, No. 71, certifies that there is due from the Commonwealth of Pennsylvania unto James P. Parker in trust of Philadelphia, or his assigns, the sum of one hundred dollars bearing interest at five per centum per annum from August 1, 1829, payable semi-annually at the Bank of Pennsylvania, being stock created pursuant to an Act of Assembly of April 11, 1825, "An act authorizing a loan"—principal reimbursable fifteen years after January 1, 1825. Treasury Department of Pennsylvania, November 18, 1829. Countersigned by State Treasurer Mahon and Auditor General David Mason. Printed by Mowry & Amnon, Harrisburg. Pennsylvania issued bonds like this to finance canal improvements, including the Pennsylvania Main Line of Public Works.

title: Fosses à Charbon de M. le Marquis de Traisnel, Interest Share
description: This manuscript share document, No. 263, from the Compagnie des Fosses à Charbon de M. le Marquis de Traisnel (Company of the Coal Mines of the Marquis de Traisnel), is signed by Directors La Marsche and Dehault and dated June 13, 1781. It certifies that the holder is the proprietor of one "intérêt" (interest/participation share) in the enterprise of the said coal mines, and will participate proportionally in the profits and losses of the Company, subject to assessments for loans (emprunts) and contributions already made or to be made. Transfer requires Company approval. The company operated in the Aniéni region of France, representing an early form of corporate share in a French pre-revolutionary coal mining enterprise.
creator: Compagnie des Fosses à Charbon de M. le Marquis de Traisnel
notes: Fosses à Charbon de M. le Marquis de Traisnel, Interest Share No. 263, signed by La Marsche and Dehault, June 13, 1781; pre-revolutionary French coal mining share
transcription:
# Document Transcription

## Original (French):

**Fosses à Charbon de M. le Marquis de Craisnel; Action d'un Denier dans vingt cinq sols d'intérêt. N°. 263**

Nous Soussignés Directeur, associés de la Compagnie des Fosses à Charbon de M. Le Marquis de Traisnel, reconnaissons que Monsieur D'. Montmoreuil Pelletier D'en Suanne D. est propriétaire d'in Denier d'intérêt dans l'entreprise de dites Fosses et, qu'en consequence, il participera dans la proportion audit Denier aux benéfices et aux pertes de la D. Compagnie, a condition qu'il sera soumis aux emprunts et aux mises faites à faire, et, qui ont de ou seront cy après délibérés, à charge, en outre de ne pouvoir vendre allener, ni céder la présente action dans l'agrément de la Compagnie, laquelle en conformité de son acte de societé, sera libre de retraire ladite action à un profit à en cas de vente ou de trasport. Le nouveau propriétaire devra se presenter dans l'année pour obtenir une nouvelle reconnoissance sous son nom, après avoir Donné, ou hypotheque, ou caution à l'appaisement des Directeurs associés, pour la surete des emprunts faits et à faire, fait à l'assemblée deD. Fosses, à Anniéd ___ Le 13 Juin 1781

[Signatures: La Marsche and Dehault]

---

## English Translation:

**Coal Mines (Fosses) of the Marquis de Craisnel; Share of One Denier in Twenty-five Sols of Interest. No. 263**

We, the undersigned Directors and associates of the Company of Coal Mines of the Marquis de Traisnel, acknowledge that Monsieur D'. Montmoreuil Pelletier D'en Suanne D. is the proprietor of one Denier of interest in the enterprise of said Coal Mines and, consequently, he shall participate in the proportion of said Denier in the profits and losses of the said Company, on the condition that he shall be subject to the loans and contributions made or to be made, and which have been or shall hereafter be deliberated, charged furthermore that he shall be unable to sell, transfer, or cede the present share without the approval of the Company, which in conformity with its deed of association, shall be free to redeem said share at a profit in case of sale or transfer. The new proprietor shall present himself within the year to obtain a new acknowledgment under his name, after having provided, mortgaged, or pledged security to the satisfaction of the Associated Directors, for the security of the loans made and to be made, executed at the assembly of the said Fosses, at Anniéd ___ June 13, 1781

[Signatures: La Marsche and Dehault]

---

## Notable Markings:

- **Serial number:** N°. 263
- **Coat of arms:** Ornate heraldic shield at top center (fleur-de-lis design, indicating French nobility)
- **Signatures:** Two signatures at bottom - "La Marsche" and "Dehault" (Directors)
- **Date:** Le 13 Juin 1781 (June 13, 1781)
- **Reference mark:** "FB" in top left corner
- **Archival mark:** Small notation in top right corner

title: Fukuyama-Han (Bingo Province) Silver Note, 1 Mon-me (1730)
description: This narrow printed paper currency note is a silver note (銀札 / ginsatsu) issued by the Fukuyama-Han (Fukuyama Domain) in Bingo Province (now part of Hiroshima Prefecture, Japan), denominated in 1 Mon-me (一匁), dated 1730. The note displays a deity figure (likely Ebisu, god of fortune and commerce) and is printed in the characteristic vertical format of Japanese Edo-period domain currency. Domain currencies (hansatsu) were paper money issued by individual feudal lords (daimyo) throughout the Edo period (1603–1868), circulating only within the issuing domain. The Fukuyama Domain was a prominent coastal fief on the Seto Inland Sea.

title: General Investment Corporation, Stock Purchase Warrant
description: This stock purchase warrant (No. NCW5418) of the General Investment Corporation, incorporated under the laws of the State of Delaware, certifies that the bearer is entitled to purchase, at any time on or before September 14, 1928 (or a later date), fully paid and non-assessable shares of Common Stock of General Investment Corporation at $8 per share. Signed by Boston, Massachusetts. Issued June 13, 1938 (date visible on certificate). Stock purchase warrants were issued as sweeteners attached to bond offerings or as stand-alone instruments, giving holders the right—but not the obligation—to buy shares at a fixed price, functioning as early financial options.
creator: General Investment Corporation
notes: General Investment Corporation (Delaware), Stock Purchase Warrant No. NCW5418 to purchase common stock, Boston, June 13, 1938
transcription:
# Stock Purchase Warrant Transcription

**No NCW5418**

---

**STOCK PURCHASE WARRANT**

**TO PURCHASE COMMON STOCK OF**

# GENERAL INVESTMENT CORPORATION

*Incorporated under the Laws of the State of Delaware*

**WARRANT TO PURCHASE ___/___ SHARES**

---

THIS CERTIFIES that the bearer is entitled to purchase at any time on or after September 14, 1929 (without limit) all or any part of

_____ SHARES

full-paid and non-assessable shares of COMMON STOCK of GENERAL INVESTMENT CORPORATION, a corporation of the State of Delaware (herein called the "corporation"), as such stock may be constituted at the time of purchase, at the price of $8 per share, upon surrender of this warrant accompanied by full payment of the purchase price in cash at the principal office of the corporation in Boston, Massachusetts. This warrant does not entitle the holder to any rights whatsoever as a stockholder of the corporation. It is expressly agreed that the shares of common stock of the corporation agreed to be sold hereunder are shares of the common stock of the corporation as such stock may be constituted at the time of purchase and that delivery of the number of shares of common stock called for herein, in good delivery hereunder whether or not the claim is made, shall constitute full performance of all the obligations of the corporation hereunder with respect to the exercise of the rights or interests represented by the shares of common stock of the corporation authorized and outstanding at the date hereof or at any other time prior to such purchase and irrespective of any dividends, sales, exchanges or other disposition of stock of the corporation of any class, any change of authorized capital or reclassification thereof, the sale or lease by the corporation of all or any part of its property, or any change whatsoever; provided, however, that if while this warrant is in effect the corporation shall have recapitalized by subdivision or combination of its outstanding shares of common stock into a greater or lesser number of shares, then, in each such case, the number of shares of common stock purchasable upon exercise of this warrant shall be increased or decreased (as the case may be) in proportion to the change through such subdivision or combination in the number of outstanding shares of common stock of the corporation, and provided further, that if while this warrant is in effect the corporation shall consolidate or merge with or into any other corporation, then, as a condition of such consolidation or merger, adequate provision shall be made whereby the holder of this warrant shall thereafter be entitled to purchase, in lieu of the shares of common stock of the corporation theretofore purchasable upon exercise of this warrant, such shares of stock, securities or assets as would have been purchasable upon the exercise of this warrant had such consolidation or merger not taken place. The existence of this warrant shall not prevent the lawful dissolution of the corporation. Whenever in this warrant the corporation is referred to, it shall be deemed to include any corporation into or to which the corporation may have been merged or consolidated. This warrant is exchangeable at the option of the bearer at the principal office of the corporation in Boston, Massachusetts for other similar warrants of different denominations entitling the bearer thereof to purchase an equal aggregate number of shares of common stock of the corporation as specified in this warrant. Every holder of this warrant, by accepting the same, consents and agrees with the corporation and with every subsequent holder of this warrant as follows: Title to this warrant shall be registered by the absolute owner of this warrant and is hereby granted power to transfer absolute title thereto by delivery thereof to a purchaser thereof for value (present or accrued); without notice of prior defenses or equities or claims enforceable against his transferor; every bearer of this warrant shall be presumed to be such a purchaser thereunder unless the contrary be established; every bearer of this warrant waives and renounces all his equities and rights herein in favor of every such purchaser and every such purchaser shall acquire title hereto and to all rights represented hereby; the corporation and any warrant agent shall treat any bearer who is a purchaser hereof of the character above described, as the absolute owner thereof for all purposes without being affected by any notice to the contrary; the corporation and any warrant agent shall be entitled to accept the surrender of this warrant from the bearer hereof and such surrender shall constitute full and complete discharge of all liability of the corporation and any warrant agent hereunder; the corporation does not make any contract with any bearer hereof except as contained in this warrant and any performance by the corporation with respect to the bearer hereof of any agreement contained with respect to this warrant, shall be due performance of such agreement. On the back of this warrant is a form of subscription which must be used upon exercise thereof.

IN WITNESS WHEREOF the corporation has caused this warrant to be signed in its behalf by the undersigned warrant agents.

**GENERAL INVESTMENT CORPORATION**
*(Boston, Mass.)*

Dated **JUN 14 1928** [handwritten]

By _____________________________ [signature]

Warrant Agent _____________________________ [signature]

---

**CENTRAL BANKNOTE COMPANY** [printer's mark at bottom]

---

title: Gouvernement Impérial du Japon, 4½% Loan of 1910, 500 Franc Bond
description: This green-printed bond certificate represents a 500 Franc bond from the 4½% Imperial Japanese Government Loan of 1910 (Emprunt de Quatre Cent Cinquante Millions de Francs / 450,000,000 Franc Loan), Series 30, No. 403578. Dated May 15, 1911. The certificate bears a vignette of Mount Fuji. Japanese government bonds were extensively placed in European capital markets—particularly Paris and London—in the early twentieth century to finance Japanese industrialization and military expenditure following the victories in the Sino-Japanese (1894–95) and Russo-Japanese (1904–05) Wars. France was a major holder of Japanese sovereign debt in this period.

title: Gouvernement de la Nouvelle-Calédonie et Dépendances, 4% Loan of 1931, 1,000 Franc Bond
description: This bond certificate represents a 4% obligation of 1,000 Francs au Porteur, No. 008,679, from the Gouvernement de la Nouvelle-Calédonie et Dépendances (Government of New Caledonia and Dependencies), Emprunt 4% 1931, Net d'Impôts Présents et Futurs. Total issue: 34,500 bonds of 1,000 Francs each (34,500,000 Francs). Paris, August 31, 1931. With attached coupon sheet. New Caledonia, a French Pacific territory, issued bonds to finance colonial infrastructure at the onset of the Great Depression, when global nickel prices—the territory's chief export—had already begun to collapse, foreshadowing the financial strains of the 1930s colonial economies.

title: Republic of Texas Government Bond, $100
description: This bond certificate of the Republic of Texas promises to pay the bearer One Hundred Dollars with interest at eight percent per annum, payable semi-annually from date, redeemable from the Sinking Fund of the Republic at the Treasury at Houston. "Government Bond, payable to holder. Receivable for all Government Duties." Signed by John F. Chalmers (Secretary) and David G. Burnet (or another authorized signatory). With ten semi-annual interest coupon strips at the bottom. The Republic of Texas (1836–1846) issued bonds to finance its government and military operations as an independent republic between its victory at San Jacinto and its annexation by the United States. Texas bonds were a significant form of frontier-era sovereign debt.

title: Hungarian Fund: $1 Bond (New York, February 2, 1852)
description: This small printed certificate is a $1 bearer bond of the Hungarian Fund, issued in New York on February 2, 1852, during the American tour of Lajos Kossuth, the exiled leader of the 1848–49 Hungarian Revolution. Following the crushing of the Hungarian independence movement by Austrian and Russian forces, Kossuth traveled to the United States in 1851–52 to raise money and political support for a renewed Hungarian bid for independence. The Hungarian Fund bonds were sold to American supporters of the Hungarian cause, predominantly Hungarian and German immigrants, at face values as low as $1 to enable broad participation. Though the hoped-for second revolution never materialized, these bonds represent a significant early episode in the history of diaspora finance and international political fundraising.
creator: Hungarian Fund (Kossuth exile government)
notes: Hungarian Fund $1 bearer bond. New York, February 2, 1852. Issued during Lajos Kossuth's American fundraising tour for Hungarian independence.

description: This is a bearer bond of £100 sterling in the Imperial Russian Government 3% State Loan of 1859. Certificate No. 31016. Issued as part of an external British-market loan denominated in pounds sterling, this bond formed part of a series of Russian government borrowings in London during the mid-nineteenth century to finance infrastructure and state expenditures. Interest was payable in London at 3% per annum. The bond is an example of Russian sovereign debt instruments designed to attract British capital.

# Original:

**БИЛЕТЫ**

**ГОСУДАРСТВЕННОГО ПОГАШЕНИЯ КОМИССИИ ДОЛГОВЪ.**

№31016                                                                                                №31016

РОССИЙСКАГО ТРЁХПРОЦЕНТНАГО ЗАЙМА 1859 г.
НА КАПИТАЛЪ СТО ФУНТ. СТЕРЛ.

записанный въ Государственную долговую книгу.

Книга          стр. 122.

Владѣлецъ сего билета получаетъ непрерывный доходъ по ТРИ шт СТО въ годъ, платежные по полугодіо ш Лондонъ, а именно, одинъ фунт стерл. десять шл. на сроки и одинъ фунт стерл.десять шл. на сроки [в АПРЕЛЯ] в банкиръ П. Томсонъ, Т. Бонарь и К°, щц., по желанию, въ Берлинъ, у банкира Ф. Март. Магнусъ, Прусcъ. курантъ. десять талеръ. три сер. гросъ. девять пфен. въ тѣ же самые сроки.

При семь билетъ находятся полугодовые купоны, для получения трёчентовъ, по выданы новые купоны, по предстанія приложенаго къ сему билету талона.

Выписка изъ Уставъ Комиссіи Погашения Долговъ (Сб. Зак. Т. XI издъ. 1857 г.)

Ст. 89. Безвозмездный непрерывный доходъ, равно какъ и напасяю сроочныхъ должниковъ, производятся и въ мѣрное и въ ночное время, безъ всякаго разраныя, къ дружественной ли или непрерывной казнѐ.

— 90. Въ случаѣ смерти иностранного вексельнаго бѣдт защищается, бережение какъ защищение содержатѣ его наслѣдникамъ, по порядку и правамъ наслѣдия согласно закону.

— 91. Капиталъ, въ бѣдосрочной домѣ номинальные, составляя неозгрушимую обѣстъанность вексельнаго, ни въ какомъ случаѣ не подлежатъ налогомъ.

Выписка изъ Высочайшаго Указа, данного 24 Марта 1859 г. на имя Министра Финансовъ.

На погашение сего займа, съ 1859 г., назначается особый, независимо другихъ займовъ, сумъ. Фондъ погашения создался ежегодно тотарѓ процента прежнего изъ расходалального капитала займа. Фондъ сей, который будутъ уважаемости ежегодно капитализируемыхъ по послѣдствия прогнозовъ, какія будутъ причисляны на погашающие билеты, имѣетъ быть обогащенъ на счетъ вынутёе и барчисанное их курсъ. Если его какъ обстоятельствъ облегчилъ, сп с предварительнымъ благоговѣйнымъ сознаніемъ на российскыхъ долго, то для сего развития идеально цѣна мѣсть этихъ сроскъ рублей за его фунтъ стеръ., и билетъ съ тѣмъ ясгіти далежить быть обращены. по цѣнѣ, опрѐдѣляемой ежегодно [illegible], и прочніеся по числу выеда будетъ выдѣляемые сроки.

Управляющій [signature]                    Бухгалтеръ [signature]

1859
