title: Reconstitution de Rentes à 3 Pour Cent: Royal French Annuity Certificate (Paris, July 20, 1763)
description: This handwritten and witnessed notarial certificate reconstitutes (replaces lost or damaged originals of) 3% royal rentes (annual interest bonds) created by the Edict of May 1760, secured on the royal leather tax revenues (Denier sur les Cuirs, established by the Edict of April 1759) and other royal revenues. Executed before royal notaries at the Châtelet de Paris, July 20, 1763. Witnessed by several prominent Parisian financiers. The French crown issued these reconstitution certificates to replace rente holders whose original documents had been lost or destroyed, maintaining their claim to the annual income stream. Issued during the financially ruinous Seven Years' War (1756–63), these rentes were part of the enormous debt burden that would ultimately contribute to the fiscal crisis leading to the French Revolution.
creator: France. Trésor Royal (Royal Treasury)
notes: Reconstitution de Rentes à 3 Pour Cent. Royal French annuity certificate. Paris, July 20, 1763. Secured on royal leather tax (Denier sur les Cuirs, Edit d'Avril 1759) and other royal revenues. Executed before notaries at the Châtelet de Paris. Seven Years' War era fiscal debt.
transcription:
# RECONSTITUTION DE RENTES À 3 POUR CENT

**Original:**

20; Juillet, 1763.

[Coat of Arms - Royal Seal]

RECONSTITUTION DE RENTES À 3 POUR CENT, créées par Édit du mois de Mai 1760, sur les deniers provenans du Droit sur les Cuirs, établi par autre Édit du mois d'Avril 1759, & sur les autres Revenus du Roi.

N° [number unclear] 4283

PARDEVANT les Confeilers du Roi, Notaires, Garde-notes & Gardes-scel de Sa Majesté au Châtelet de Paris, soulsignés, furent prêsens M. [several handwritten names - partially illegible: "Janneau? d'Orles Vendaux?" and others]

Tous Commisaires nommés par Sa Majesté, par ses Lettres patentes données à Versailles le pour l'exécution de l'Édit du mois de mai 1760, ci-après énoncé.

LESQUELS ont dit que le Roi, par son Édit du mois de mai 1760, enregistré au Parlement le 23 [handwritten: "Jambarde? pou"] du même mois, a créé & émis trois millions livres de rentes effectives & héréditaires à trois pour cent, [handwritten notation], à les avoir à prendre par privilège & préférence sur les deniers provenans du Droit sur Sa Majesté [handwritten] sur les Cuirs établi par l'Édit d'Avril 1759, enregistré au Parlement le 11.2 [handwritten: "payms sim Penfre"] septembre suivant, & qu'Elle s'pécialement & par privilège affectés, obligés & hypothéqués, ensembles [handwritten] tous les autres revenus, tant au payement des arrrérages desdites rentes qu'au remboursement des capitaux [handwritten notations] de la manière ci-après énoncée, après notamment fait la facilité dudit droit formé & delicences au payement des arrérages & au rembourcement des capitaux des liquidations des Offices supprimés par ledit Édit d'aoît, conformément à l'article 111 dudit Édit qui seroit executé de la forme & teneur.

Que lesdites rentes feroient vendues & aliénées par lesdits feigneurs Commisaires, sur les quitances [handwritten] du leur Garde du Trésor royal, & les contrats de constitution desdites rentes conséquence par les Notaires que les Acquéreurs voudroient choisir, lesquelles rentes feroient exemptes de la retenue du [handwritten: "trait dit pours"]; Deux tiers pour livre de Droitun, & de toutes autres impositions générallement quelconques, sous quelque dénomination que pu être.

Que chaque constitution particulière desdites rentes ne pourroit être moindre de mille livres de capital, pour faire levées livres de rente, dont les arrérages commenceroient à courir du jour dudit Acquereurs, du premier jour du quartier courant de la date desdites quitances de finance, & feroient payés de six en six mois sur les premiers jours des mois de Janvier & de Juillet de chaque année, par les simples quitances des Rentiers, à commencée du premier Janvier 1761.

Qu'il faudroit annuellement fait fondés dans l'État de la régie dudit Droit sur les Cuirs & des autres revenus de Sa Majesté, tant que lesdites rentes subsisteroient en tout ou en partie, de la somme de trois millions de livres, qui seroit annuellement remise par les Régisseurs ou Fermiers desdits droits, entre les mains des Gardes du Trésor royal, chacun en l'année de son exercice, pour etre employée au payement des arrérages & au remboursement des capitaux desdites rentes, sans que lesdits payts puissent être distraits ou entranchés au l'entendre reimbourse desdites rentes, sans précédent du prélèvement ci-dffus mentionné.

Que les capitaux desdites rentes feroient remboursés en derniers compans, à raison de la forme de douce cents mille livres pour la première année, de pareille forme pour les années suivantes, & par augmentation de la forme à laquelle se trouveroient monter les arrérages des capitaux qui auroient été remboursés durant l'année, en forte qu'il seroit employé tous les trois millions de fonds jusqu'à parfait reimbourrement desdites rentes, sans pouvoir difiaire aucune partie de ce fonds, sous quelque prétexte que ce fût.

Que ce remboursement seroit fait par le Garde du Trésor royal en exercice, aux premiers jours de Janvier & de Juillet de chaque année, par la voie du fort en forme de Loterie, à commencer du premier Janvier 1762, & ainsi de suite de six en six mois, à l'effet de quoi les contrats de constitution desdites rentes feroient numérotés, & les numéros mis publiquement, dans les derniers jours de Juin & de Décembre de chaque année, dans une roue en la grande salle de l'Hôtel de ville de Paris.

---

**English Translation:**

**20 July, 1763**

[Royal Coat of Arms]

**RECONSTITUTION OF 3% ANNUITIES**, created by Edict of the month of May 1760, on the monies derived from the Right on Hides [leather tax], established by another Edict of the month of April 1759, & on the other Revenues of the King.

No. 4283

BEFORE the Councillors of the King, Notaries, Keeper of Records & Keeper of Seals of His Majesty at the Châtelet of Paris, undersigned, appeared M. [names partially illegible]

All Commissioners appointed by His Majesty, by his Letters Patent given at Versailles for the execution of the Edict of the month of May 1760, hereinafter stated.

WHICH [Commissioners] have declared that the King, by his Edict of the month of May 1760, registered in Parliament on the 23rd of the same month, has created & issued three million livres in effective & hereditary annuities at three per cent, to be drawn by privilege & preference on the monies derived from the Right on Hides established by the Edict of April 1759, registered in Parliament on the 12th of the following September, & that it [the Crown] particularly & by privilege affected, obliged & mortgaged, together with all other revenues, both for the payment of arrears of the said annuities & for the reimbursement of the capital, in the manner hereinafter stated, after having made provision for the facility of said right formed of delicts in the payment of arrears & the reimbursement of capitals of the liquidations of Offices suppressed by said Edict of August, in conformity with article 111 of said Edict which would be executed in the form & tenor.

That the said annuities would be sold & alienated by the said Commissioners, on the receipts of their Treasurer of the Royal Treasury, & the contracts of constitution of said annuities by consequence of the Notaries which the Purchasers would wish to choose, which annuities would be exempt from the retention of [amount]; Two thirds per pound of Rights, & from all other impositions generally whatsoever, under whatever denomination it might be.

That each particular constitution of said annuities could not be less than one thousand livres of capital, to produce five livres of income, of which the arrears would commence to run from the day of said Purchase, from the first day of the quarter current of the date of said quitances of finance, & would be paid every six months on the first days of the months of January & July of each year, by simple receipts of the Rentiers, to commence from the first of January 1761.

That it would be necessary to annually establish in the Account of the management of said Right on Hides & of the other revenues of His

title: Deutsches Reich: Reichsbanknote, Ein Tausend Mark (No. 6125588N, Berlin, April 21, 1910)
description: This printed note is a one-thousand mark (Ein Tausend Mark) Reichsbanknote. Serial No. 6,125,588N. Berlin, April 21, 1910. Issued by the Reichsbankdirektorium (Imperial Bank Directorate) under signatures of the Bank directors and deputy directors. The note states: "zahlt die Reichsbankhauptkasse in Berlin ohne Legitimationsprüfung dem Einlieferer dieser Banknote" (the Reichsbank main cashier in Berlin will pay the bearer of this banknote without identity verification). This pre-World War I German 1,000-mark note was one of the largest-denomination banknotes in regular circulation before 1914. The Reichsbank was Germany's central bank, established in 1876 upon German unification. This note was printed in the era of the classical gold standard, when the Reichsmark was fully convertible to gold.

title: Weimar Republic: Reichsbanknote, Tausend Mark (Pa 688969, Berlin, September 15, 1922)
description: This printed note is a one-thousand mark (Tausend Mark) Reichsbanknote of the Weimar Republic. Serial No. Pa 688969, Series KH. Berlin, September 15, 1922. Issued by the Reichsbankdirektorium. The note carries the notice: "Vom 1. Januar 1923 ab kann diese Banknote aufgerufen und unter Umtausch gegen andere gesetzliche Zahlungsmittel eingezogen werden" (From January 1, 1923, this banknote may be called in and exchanged for other legal tender). This note was issued during the onset of the great German hyperinflation of 1921–23, during which the value of the mark collapsed catastrophically—by November 1923 the exchange rate reached 4.2 trillion marks per US dollar. The 1,000-mark note, a large denomination in 1910, had become almost worthless by the date of this issue. This note illustrates the extreme paper money production of the Weimar inflationary period.
creator: Reichsbank (German Weimar Republic Bank)
notes: Weimar Republic Reichsbanknote. 1,000 Marks (Tausend Mark). Pa 688969, KH. Berlin, September 15, 1922. Hyperinflation era. Note callable from January 1, 1923.

title: République Chinoise: 5% Gold Bond of 1925, $50 U.S. Gold Dollars (No. 350,356, London, May 27, 1925)
description: This bilingual (French/English) printed bearer bond (bon au porteur / to bearer) of $50 U.S. gold dollars at 5% annual interest is No. 350,356 of the Republic of China 5% Gold Bond of 1925. London, May 27, 1925. Issued as part of the international borrowings of the Republic of China in the mid-1920s to fund railway development and other national projects. Denominated in U.S. gold dollars and issued through London, this bond was designed to attract international investors seeking security in gold-standard currency. Chinese sovereign bonds of the Republic era were subject to significant default risk given the political instability of the warlord period.
creator: Government of the Republic of China

transcription:
# Transcription of Republic of China 5% Gold Bond of 1925

## Header Text

**PAYMENT OF COUPONS AND OF DRAWN BONDS SHALL ALSO BE MET IN LONDON**
**AT THE COUNTING-HOUSE OF J. HENRY SCHRODER & CO.**

---

## Main Bond Text

**RÉPUBLIQUE CHINOISE**

**BON 5% OR 1925**
**5% GOLD BOND 1925**

**DE CINQUANTE DOLLARS OU FIFTY**
**FIFTY U.S.A GOLD DOLLARS**

**AU PORTEUR / TO BEARER**

No. 350,356

---

## French Text (Left Column)

**Original:**

Faisant partie d'un maximum de 877,878 Bons
Émis en vertu des Protocoles conclus:
1. Protocole final du 7 Septembre 1901
2. Arrangement du 1 Juillet 1902
3. Obligations de l'Indemnité Chinoise à 5% Or 1901,
signés respectivement par le Ministre des Affaires Étrangères et le Ministre des Finances de la République Chinoise, d'une part, et le Baron de Rothschild, Président de la Banque Industrielle de Chine, d'autre part.

4. Accord du 1 Février 1925
5. Lot Française du 8 Février 1925
6. Accord du 15 Avril 1925

Le présent Bon jouit d'un droit de priorité absolue sur les revenus affectés à l'Indemnité Chinoise, et il produit un intérêt de 5% l'an, payable semestriellement, les 15 Décembre de chaque année et le 15 Janvier suivant.

Le porteur du présent Bon aura droit:
1. Au remboursement de la valeur nominale à raison de Dollars U.S.A., lors de l'amortissement, chaque année, à partir du Janvier 1926 jusqu'à celui de Décembre 1953, ou au jour du tirage s'il est antérieur.

2. À la jouissance d'un intérêt semestriel de 5% l'an.

Le remboursement annuel lieu le 15 Janvier selon le premier tirage se fera le Décembre 1925.

Le présent Bon est transférable.

À New York, en Dollars Or des États-Unis d'Amérique, et à Londres, par l'intermédiaire de la Banque Industrielle de Chine (Établissement Franco-Chinois) et à Shanghai, Saïgon, aux guichets de la Société Générale (Établissement Franco-Chinois) et sur toutes autres places qui pourraient être désignées par cette Société ou du Gouvernement Chinois, les intérêts et le capital seront payés en or ou son équivalent.

La présentation au guichet payeur.

Conformément à l'accord à et suite à l'accord intervenu entre le Gouvernement Français et le Gouvernement Chinois, l'inspecteur Général de la Douane Chinoise, Le Ministre des Affaires Étrangères et le Directeur Général de la Banque Industrielle de Chine (Établissement Franco-Chinois) sont investis des pouvoirs pour la présentation du présent Bon.

Conformément à l'article 3 de l'Arrangement de 1902, le Gouvernement Français a accepté par le Ministre des Affaires Étrangères la responsabilité de la présentation de ce Bon et de la mise en œuvre des mesures nécessaires pour l'encaissement.

Au cas où les revenus affectés à l'Indemnité Chinoise seraient insuffisants pour le paiement des coupons, le Gouvernement Français s'engage à effectuer sur ses revenus affectés à l'Indemnité de 1901 un prélèvement supplémentaire de la difference.

**LONDON, MAY 27, 1925**

---

## English Text (Right Column)

**Translation:**

Being one of a total issue of 877,878 Bonds created in accordance with the following Protocols and Agreements:
1. Final Protocol of September 7th, 1901
2. Agreement of July 1st, 1902
3. Chinese Indemnity 5% Gold Bonds of 1901, signed respectively by the Minister for Foreign Affairs and the Minister of Finance of the Chinese Republic, on the one hand, and by Baron de Rothschild, President of the Banque Industrielle de Chine, on the other hand.

4. Agreement of February 1st, 1925
5. French Law of February 8th, 1925
6. Agreement of April 15th, 1925

The bearer of this Bond is entitled to receive interest at 5% per annum payable half-yearly on December 15th and January 15th of each year, the first coupon falling due on July 15th, 1925.

The bearer shall be entitled to:
1. Repayment of the face value in U.S.A. Gold Dollars at the time of redemption, which shall take place on December 15th of each year, commencing December 15th, 1925, and ending December 15th, 1953, or on the date of drawing should that date be earlier.

2. Receipt of interest at 5% per annum payable half-yearly.

The annual redemption shall commence on December 15th, 1925.

This Bond is transferable.

In New York, in U.S.A. Gold Dollars, at any financial institution to be named by the Banque Industrielle de Chine (Franco-Chinese Establishment).

In London, at the office of J. Henry Schroder & Co.

In Peking, Shanghai, Canton, or in Saigon, at the branches of the Société Générale (Franco-Chinese Establishment) or at such other agencies as may be designated by the Société Générale or by the Chinese Government.

Payment of interest and principal shall be made in gold or its equivalent.

In accordance with the Agreement concluded on April 15th, 1925, between the French Government and the Chinese Government, the Inspector General of the Chinese Maritime Customs, the Minister for Foreign Affairs, and the Director General of the Banque Industrielle de Chine (Franco-Chinese Establishment) are hereby vested with full power for presentation and encashment of this Bond.

In accordance with Article 3 of the Agreement of 1902, the French Government has undertaken through its Minister for Foreign Affairs to assume responsibility for presentation and for taking all necessary steps for collection and payment of interest and principal.

In the event that the revenues pledged for the Indemnity should prove insufficient for the payment of the interest and principal on this Bond, the French Government undertakes to make up the deficit from the revenues pledged for the 1901 Indemnity.

**LONDON, MAY 27, 1925**

---

title: Gouvernement de la République Chinoise: 8% Treasury Bond 1921, Lung-Tsing-U-Hai Railway, 500 Francs (No. 012918, Brussels, July 1, 1921)
description: This printed bearer bond (bon du trésor) of 500 francs at 8% annual interest is No. 012918 of the Republic of China 8% Lung-Tsing-U-Hai Railway (隴秦豫海鐵路) Loan of 1921. Total issue: 50,000,000 francs, divided into 100,000 bonds of 500 francs each. Brussels, July 1, 1921. Signed by the Chinese Finance Minister and the Compagnie Générale de Chemins de Fer et Tramways en Chine (General Company of Railways and Tramways in China). The Lung-Tsing-U-Hai Railway (Gansu–Shaanxi–Henan–Jiangsu) was a major planned trans-China line. This Belgian franc issue complemented Dutch guilder (cf. row 395) and other currency issues for the same railway, reflecting the multi-country European capital-raising strategy of the Chinese Republic. The Belgian-based Compagnie Générale de Chemins de Fer et Tramways en Chine was the principal financial intermediary.

title: Royaume de Bulgarie: 4½% Or State Loan of 1909, 500 Gold Francs / Cinq Cents Leva Zlatni (No. 055,199)
description: This bilingual (Bulgarian/French) printed bearer bond (Облигация / Obligation) No. 055,199 is a 500-gold-franc denomination of the amortizable 4½% Gold State Loan of Bulgaria, 1909. Denomination: 500 gold francs = 500 Bulgarian gold leva = 476 Austrian crowns = 405 German marks = 19.163 British pounds sterling = 9.65 Dutch guilders. Total emission represented by 200,000 bonds. Signed by the Bulgarian Finance Ministry. The 1909 loan was issued shortly after Bulgaria declared full independence from the Ottoman Empire (October 5, 1908), requiring repayment of an Ottoman tribute and new international capital for state development. The multiple-currency denomination equivalences reflect the gold-standard international bond market of the Edwardian era.

title: Russian General Oil Corporation (Société Générale Naphthifère Russe) Limited: Share Warrant to Bearer, £1 (No. 1625, London, 1913)

Capital $2,500,000 in 2,500,000 shares of £1 each. No. 1625. Given under the Common Seal of the Company. London, [1913]. An annotation dated April 23, 1954 (possibly a later registration or claim by émigré owners) appears at the bottom. The company was incorporated in England to exploit oil properties in the Russian Empire, part of the wave of British-registered companies active in the Baku and Grozny oil regions of the Caucasus before World War I. The trilingual format reflects the international nature of the ownership and investor base.

title: Imperial Russian Government: State 4% Rente (Государственная 4% Рента), Certificate No. 02756, 1,000 Rubles
description: This printed certificate (свидетельство) No. 02756 represents 1,000 rubles face value in the Imperial Russian Government State 4% Rente (Государственная 4% Рента), yielding 40 rubles annual income. Series 191 (Сто девяносто первая / one hundred and ninety-first), total series value 10,000,000 rubles. Bearer certificate (на предъявителя). Decorated in a striking pink-and-brown Art Nouveau design with the Imperial Russian double-eagle. The State Rente was a perpetual or long-term government bond paying a fixed annual income (renta), modeled on French and British consolidated annuities. Russian state rentes were widely held domestically and abroad as conservative income securities.

title: Russisk-Norsk Skogindustri A/S (Russian-Norwegian Timber Industry Company): Share No. 0349, 1,000 Rubles (Petrograd, 1917)
description: This bilingual (Russian/Norwegian) printed bearer share No. 0349 of 1,000 rubles represents one share in the Русско-Норвежское Лесопромышленное Акционерное Общество (Russian-Norwegian Timber Industry Joint-Stock Company / Russisk-Norsk Skogindustri A/S). Capital 3,000,000 rubles. Charter (Устав) approved December 29, 1916. Issued to Herr Jorgen Bogaland. Petrograd, 1917. The company exploited timber resources in northern Russia and was a product of Norwegian-Russian commercial relations in the timber trade, which had been active since the nineteenth century. This share was issued in 1917—the year of the Russian Revolution—and the company was likely nationalized by the Bolsheviks shortly thereafter.

title: Stadtanleihe Kremnitz (Kremnica Municipal Bond): 5% Bearer Bond, 10,000 Kronen (Serie 000513, No. 011)
description: This printed bearer bond (5%ige Schuldverschreibung) of 10,000 kronen at 5% annual interest is a municipal bond issued by the town of Kremnitz (Kremnica, present-day Slovakia), then part of the Kingdom of Hungary within the Austro-Hungarian Empire. Serie 000513, No. 011. The bond features a decorative Art Nouveau design with a panoramic engraved view of the city. Kremnica (Kremnitz in German, Körmöcbánya in Hungarian) is a historic Slovak mining town, site of one of the oldest continuously operating mints in Europe (the Kremnica Mint, founded ca. 1328), and was an important economic center of Upper Hungary. Municipal bonds (Stadtanleihen) were a common form of financing for Central European towns during the late nineteenth and early twentieth centuries.

title: Walchensee-Anleihe: Schuldverschreibung, 10,000 Mark (Buchstabe E, No. 419133, Munich, February 28, 1922)
description: This printed bearer bond (Schuldverschreibung) of 10,000 marks at minimum 7% annual interest per year was issued jointly by three Bavarian electricity companies—Walchenseewerk A.G., Mittlere Isar A.G., and Bayernwerk A.G.—as the Walchensee-Anleihe (Walchensee Loan). Buchstabe E, No. 419133. Munich, February 28, 1922. The loan financed the construction of the Walchensee hydroelectric power plant (Walchenseekraftwerk) in the Bavarian Alps, which when completed in 1924 was one of the most powerful hydroelectric plants in Europe. The three issuing companies were state-financed utilities instrumental in Bavaria's post-World War I industrialization. This bond was issued during the Weimar hyperinflation, which would eventually render the nominal 10,000-mark denomination nearly worthless.
creator: Walchenseewerk A.G. / Mittlere Isar A.G. / Bayernwerk A.G.
notes: Walchensee-Anleihe. 10,000 marks, min. 7% p.a. Buchstabe E, No. 419133. Munich, February 28, 1922. Issued by Walchenseewerk A.G., Mittlere Isar A.G., Bayernwerk A.G. Finances Walchensee hydroelectric power plant, Bavaria.

# Walchensee-Anleihe: Schuldverschreibung 10,000 Mark

**Original:**

WALCHENSEE-ANLEIHE

Anleihe der Aktiengesellschaften
WALCHENSEEWERK · MITTLERE ISAR · BAYERNWERK
Zum jeweiligen Reichsbank-Diskontsat abzüglich zwei Prozent, jedoch mit höchstens 15 Prozent und mindestens 7 Prozent verzinsliches Anlehen vom Jahre 1923.
Vom Jahre 1928 ab zum Nennwerte rückzahlbar.

10000 M.                                                                                                    10000 M.

SCHULDVERSCHREIBUNG

Buchstabe E                    über                    № 419133

Zehntausend Mark

Die Aktiengesellschaften Walchenseewerk, Mittlere Isar und Bayernwerk in München
haben unter nachverzeichnlicher Haftung dem Inhaber dieser Schuldverschreibung

ZEHNTAUSEND MARK IN REICHSWÄHRUNG.

Sie verpflichten sich, diese Summe zum jeweiligen Reichsbank-Diskontstat abzüglich zwei Prozent für
die Jahre, jedoch mit höchstens 15 und mindestens 7 vom Hundert, in halbjahrlichen – je am 1. März und 1. September –
zu verzinsen und nach Massgabe der nachfolgenden Bestimmungen zu erstatten.

Rückzahlung der Schuld.
1. März 1923. Die Septembereinzahlungen werden am Massgabe des am letzten Tage des vorangegangenen Februars
gelüftet bezahlten Reichsbank-Diskontrates. Die Märzzahlungen unter Zuwägung der zweijährigen Aufwertung
salzes vom 31. August des Vorjahres eingelöst. b) Die Schuldverschreibungen werden vom Jahre 1928 an zum Nennwert in der Weise zur jährlichen Tilgung gelost. c) Die gesamte
Anleihe ist zum Jahre 1923, gegebenenfalls ist. Die Auslosungen erfolgen im Monat März zum 1. September eines
jeden Jahres; und diese Zeit die Verzinsung der ausgelosten Schuldverschreibungen aufhört. Das Tragerisiko des
Käufers beginnt mit dem Tage der Auslosung. Die Rückzahlung erfolgt zum Nennwert. Couponinhaber
können für Schuldner verlässt Auslosungen vornehmen oder auch die einzelheit, noch ausstehende Schuldverschreibungen mit einer Frist von 6 Monaten zur Rückzahlung kündigen. c) Etwaige Rückstände bei
Losten und Kleinbefunde nach der Bayersche Staatsbank München und
der einjährig Bayerisch Staatsbank eingelöst. d) Zur Sicherheit der Inhaber der
Schuldverschreibungen werden Grundpfandrechte eingetragen.

Die Verwaltung.

[Long detailed German text regarding terms, conditions, and administrative details continues...]

MÜNCHEN, den 20. Februar 1923.

Walchenseewerk A.G. · Mittlere Isar A.G. · Bayernwerk A.G.

**English translation:**

WALCHENSEE LOAN

Loan of the Joint Stock Companies
WALCHENSEEWERK · MITTLERE ISAR · BAYERNWERK

Bearing interest at the respective Reichsbank discount rate minus two percent, but with a maximum of 15 percent and a minimum of 7 percent per annum from the year 1923.
From the year 1928 onwards repayable at nominal value.

10,000 M.                                                                                                    10,000 M.

BOND (DEBT INSTRUMENT)

Letter E                    for                    No. 419133

Ten Thousand Mark

The joint stock companies Walchenseewerk, Mittlere Isar, and Bayernwerk in Munich
hereby commit themselves, under the liability noted below, to the bearer of this bond

TEN THOUSAND MARK IN REICHSCURRENCY.

They obligate themselves to pay interest on this sum at the respective Reichsbank discount rate minus two percent for the years, but with a maximum of 15 and a minimum of 7 percent per hundred annually – in semi-annual installments on March 1 and September 1 – and to redeem it according to the following provisions.

Repayment of the Debt.
March 1, 1923. The September payments will be made at the rate of the Reichsbank discount rate prevailing on the last day of the preceding February. The March payments are subject to adjustment according to the revaluation rate applicable as of August 31 of the preceding year. b) The bonds will be drawn for repayment at nominal value annually from 1928 onwards. c) The entire loan may be redeemed ahead of schedule by 1923, and as appropriate. Drawings occur in March for the period ending September 1 of each year; from this date, interest on drawn bonds ceases. The risk of loss passes to the buyer on the date of drawing. Repayment is made at nominal value. Coupon holders may request early repayment for their bonds or, in exceptional circumstances, request redemption of individual outstanding bonds with six months' notice. c) Any outstanding balances at the time of drawing shall be deposited with the Bavarian State Bank in Munich and the Bavarian State Bank respectively for collection. d) Mortgage liens will be registered as security for the bondholders.

[Detailed terms and conditions follow...]

MUNICH, February 20, 1923.

Walchenseewerk A.G. · Mittlere Isar A.G. · Bayernwerk A.G.

title: Compañía del Ferro-Carril de Sevilla–Jerez–Cadiz (Séville–Xérès–Cadix): Action de 500 Francs / 1,900 Reales (No. 40,721)
description: This bilingual (Spanish/French) printed bearer share (acción al portador / action au porteur) No. 40,721 of 500 francs (= 1,900 reales vellón) is a share in the Compañía Anónima de los Ferro-Carriles de Sevilla–Jerez–Cadiz (Compagnie Anonyme des Chemins de Fer de Séville–Xérès–Cadix). Authorized by Royal Decree (Spain) and by the French Crown, May 4, 1857. Capital: 266,000,000 reales = 70,000,000 francs, divided into 140,000 shares. Duration: 99 years. The Seville–Jerez–Cádiz railway was one of the first railways in Andalusia, connecting the great sherry wine-producing region of Jerez de la Frontera (known in French as Xérès, giving its name to the wine "sherry") to the major ports of Seville and Cádiz. The bilingual format reflects French investor participation. The certificate includes an attached coupon sheet at the bottom.
creator: Compañía del Ferro-Carril de Sevilla–Jerez–Cadiz
notes: Compañía del Ferro-Carril de Sevilla–Jerez–Cadiz. Bilingual Spanish/French. 500 francs = 1,900 reales. No. 40,721. Capital 70,000,000 francs / 140,000 shares. Royal Decree May 4, 1857. With coupon sheet.

title: Shanghai-Nanking Railway: Net Profit Sub-Certificate No. E3036 (British & Chinese Corporation Limited, London, December 2, 1904)
description: This printed certificate is a Net Profit Sub-Certificate No. E3036 issued by the British & Chinese Corporation Limited, entitling the bearer to participate in the net profits of the Shanghai-Nanking Railway (今上海-南京铁路) as secured by a Declaration of Trust dated July 10, 1904, made between the British and Chinese Corporation Limited (of one part) and the Hongkong and Shanghai Banking Corporation (of the other part). One of not more than 32,500 such sub-certificates. London, December 2, 1904. The Shanghai-Nanking Railway was a key trunk line in the Yangtze Delta region of China, running between Shanghai and Nanjing; construction was largely British-financed. The British & Chinese Corporation Limited was a joint venture of the Hongkong and Shanghai Banking Corporation and Jardine, Matheson & Co. formed to underwrite Chinese railway and development loans.
creator: British & Chinese Corporation Limited
notes: Shanghai-Nanking Railway Net Profit Sub-Certificate. No. E3036. British & Chinese Corporation Limited. Declaration of Trust, July 10, 1904 (with Hongkong and Shanghai Banking Corporation). London, December 2, 1904. One of max. 32,500 sub-certificates.

title: Sherman & Barnsdall Oil Company: Stock Certificate (New York, May 1865)
description: This engraved stock certificate in the Sherman & Barnsdall Oil Company, Capital $750,000, was issued in New York in May 1865. Bearing a U.S. Internal Revenue Civil War revenue stamp. The certificate records a transfer to James H. Arbona from James Irving, New York, June 27, 1868, by H.C. Brolasky. Sherman & Barnsdall was an early Pennsylvania petroleum company active in the oil regions of western Pennsylvania during the first decade of the oil industry's explosive growth following Edwin Drake's 1859 discovery at Titusville. The period 1863–69 saw enormous speculative activity in oil company stocks, with dozens of companies incorporated to exploit the Pennsylvania oil fields. This certificate, issued in the immediate aftermath of the Civil War, represents the intersection of oil speculation and wartime economic expansion.
creator: Sherman & Barnsdall Oil Company
notes: Sherman & Barnsdall Oil Company. Capital $750,000. New York, May 1865. With U.S. Civil War revenue stamp. Transfer from James Irving to James H. Arbona, June 27, 1868, by H.C. Brolasky. Pennsylvania oil boom era.

title: Сибирский Торговый Банк (Commercial Bank of Siberia / Banque de Commerce de Sibérie): Share No. 10691, 250 Rubles (St. Petersburg, 1906)
description: This printed quadrilingual (Russian/German/French/English) bearer share No. 10691 of 250 rubles is a share in the Сибирский Торговый Банк (Sibirische Handels-Bank / Banque de Commerce de Sibérie / Commercial Bank of Siberia). Charter (Устав) approved June 28, 1872. St. Petersburg, 1906. The four languages used in the border text—Russian, German, French, and English—reflect the international investor base this bank sought to attract. The Commercial Bank of Siberia was one of the major commercial banks of the Russian Empire, with its operational center in Siberia and Trans-Baikal region, and its administrative headquarters in St. Petersburg. It financed trade and industrial development in Siberia, including the growing Trans-Siberian Railway commerce.

title: Société Anonyme des Verreries d'Extrême Orient: Action No. 8339 (Capital ¥1,500,000, Japan, 1927)
description: This bilingual (French/Japanese) printed bearer share No. 8339 is a share in the Société Anonyme des Verreries d'Extrême Orient (Far East Glassworks Company). Capital ¥1,500,000. Incorporated under Japanese law, the society was constituted in March 1927 before a Japanese commercial court as stated on the certificate. The company manufactured glass in Japan or the Japanese empire, and its French corporate form reflects the involvement of French investors and management. The Verreries d'Extrême Orient operated in the Far East glass manufacturing market during the interwar period, supplying glass products to the rapidly industrializing economies of East Asia.
creator: Société Anonyme des Verreries d'Extrême Orient

title: Société Anonyme Minière des Aimaks de Touchetoukhan et de Tsetsenkhan en Mongolie: Action, 50 Rubles (No. 07520, St. Petersburg, 1911)
description: This trilingual (Russian/French/Chinese) printed bearer share No. 07520 of 50 rubles is a share in the Акционерное Общество Рудного Дела Тушетухановского и Цэцэнхановского Аймаков в Монголии (Société Anonyme Minière des Aimaks de Touchetoukhan et de Tsetsenkhan en Mongolie / Mining Company of the Tushetu Khan and Tsetsenkhan Aimaks in Mongolia). The Chinese border text reads 蒙古郭爾河等處礦務股票 (Mongolia Goer River Area Mining Company Share). St. Petersburg, 1911. The company exploited mineral deposits in the Khalkha Mongolian aimaks (provinces) of Tushetu Khan and Tsetsenkhan, areas of Inner/Outer Mongolia then under nominal Qing Chinese suzerainty and Russian commercial penetration. The trilingual format reflects the overlapping Russian, French, and Chinese interests in Mongolian mining.

title: Société Atlantique de Réassurances: Action de 1,000 Francs au Porteur (No. 69141, Tanger, ca. 1952)
description: This printed bearer share (action de mille francs au porteur, entièrement libérée) No. 69141 is a share in the Société Atlantique de Réassurances (Atlantic Reinsurance Society), Société Anonyme, registered at 17, Rue Goya, Tanger (the International Zone of Tangier, Morocco). Capital 270,000,000 francs divided into 270,000 shares of 1,000 francs each. With a large coupon sheet of 18 coupons attached for annual dividend payments. The International Zone of Tangier (1923–56) had a unique political status—governed jointly by several European powers and Morocco—and was a center of international business, finance, and speculation, particularly attractive for offshore companies and reinsurance operations.
creator: Société Atlantique de Réassurances
notes: Société Atlantique de Réassurances. Action au porteur, 1,000 francs. No. 69141. Capital 270,000,000 francs / 270,000 shares. Siège social: 17 Rue Goya, Tanger (International Zone). With 18-coupon sheet. Ca. 1952.

title: Société Belge d'Entreprises en Chine: Action de 500 Francs au Porteur (No. 0628, Brussels, March 19, 1924)
description: This printed bearer share (action de 500 francs au porteur) No. 0628 is a share in the Société Belge d'Entreprises en Chine (Belgian Company for Enterprises in China), Société Anonyme, with registered office in Brussels. Capital social: 3,000,000 francs represented by 6,000 shares of 500 francs each. Brussels, March 19, 1924. Belgium had significant commercial and financial interests in China during the early twentieth century, including concessions in Tianjin and involvement in Chinese railway finance. The Société Belge d'Entreprises en Chine was a holding or operating company engaged in Belgian commercial activities in China during the Republican period.

title: Bulgarian Red Cross Society (Дружество "Червен Кръст"): Lottery Bond, 20 Gold Leva (No. 05331)
description: This trilingual (Bulgarian/French/Russian) printed lottery bond (Облигация / Obligation / Облигация) No. 05331 of 20 gold leva was issued by the Българско дружество "Червен Кръст" (Société Bulgare de la Croix Rouge / Bulgarian Red Cross Society) as part of a 4,000,000 gold-leva prize loan (задоженъ заем по именни / emprunt à termes à lots). With coupon sheet on the right side. Red Cross and humanitarian organizations frequently issued prize lottery bonds in the Balkan states during the late nineteenth and early twentieth centuries as a fundraising mechanism, combining charitable appeal with the attraction of lottery prizes. The trilingual format (Bulgarian, French, Russian) reflects Bulgaria's diplomatic alignment and the international Red Cross movement.

Capital social: 600,000 rubles. 1st emission. Charter approved by H.I.M. (His Imperial Majesty, Tsar Alexander III) on June 9, 1895. Warsaw (Варшава), 1895. Łowicz (Russian: Lovitch) is a town in central Poland, then part of Russian-controlled Congress Poland. The company produced chemical fertilizers and agricultural chemicals, reflecting the late nineteenth-century expansion of industrial agriculture in Central Europe.

title: Общество Столичнаго Освещения (Société d'Eclairage de la Capitale): Share No. 39419, 100 Silver Rubles (St. Petersburg, ca. 1858)
description: This bilingual (Russian/French) printed bearer share No. 39419 of 100 silver rubles is a share in the Общество Столичнаго Освещения (Société d'Eclairage de la Capitale / Capital Lighting Company). Capital: 4,000,000 silver rubles divided into 40,000 shares of 100 silver rubles each. Charter (Высочайше утвержденное) highest-approved October 10, 1858. Issued to Byron Williams John Plytho (?) / Johnston Robinson Esqre. The Общество Столичнаго Освещения was chartered in 1858 to provide gas lighting for St. Petersburg, marking the beginning of organized municipal gas lighting in the Russian imperial capital. It was among the pioneering utility companies in Russia and one of the first public utilities to be organized as a joint-stock company in the Empire.

title: Société des Mines d'Or de Kilo-Moto: Part Bénéficiaire No. 1191340 (Belgian Congo, ca. 1944)
description: This printed profit-participation share (part bénéficiaire sans désignation de valeur, "without designation of value") No. 1191340 was issued by the Société des Mines d'Or de Kilo-Moto, Société Congolaise à Responsabilité Limitée (Belgian Congo limited liability company). Siège Social: Kilo, Belgian Congo; Siège Administratif: Brussels. Capital social: 230,000,000 francs. "Titre créé après le 6-10-1944" (certificate created after October 6, 1944). The Kilo-Moto mines are located in what is now the Ituri and Haut-Uele provinces of the northeastern Democratic Republic of Congo and were among the most productive gold mines in sub-Saharan Africa throughout the twentieth century. The mines were operated under Belgian colonial concession until independence in 1960 and subsequently under Zairian/Congolese state control.

title: Société Franco-Péruvienne des Mines de la Province de Castro-Virreyna: Action de 500 Francs (No. 1,735, Paris, January 9, 1852) [Liquidated]
description: This printed share (action) of 500 francs is No. 1,735 of the Société Franco-Péruvienne des Mines de la Province de Castro-Virreyna, en commandite sous la raison sociale Crosnier & Cie. Capital: 1,000,000 francs (2,000 shares of 500 francs). Issued to Monsieur Blaryez François Charles Bouguier. Paris, January 9, 1852. Stamped "Remboursé par le Liquidateur" (repaid through liquidation). The Castro-Virreyna region of central Peru was historically one of the richest silver and gold mining areas in South America, home to mines exploited since the early colonial period. This French mining venture in Peru represented the broader wave of European mining investment in Latin America following the independence movements. The liquidation stamp indicates the company was wound up—a common fate for many foreign mining ventures in Peru, where complex land rights, geological difficulties, and political instability frustrated investors.
creator: Société Franco-Péruvienne des Mines de la Province de Castro-Virreyna (Crosnier & Cie.)

title: Société Générale Égyptienne pour l'Agriculture & le Commerce: Dixième de Part de Fondateur, No. 02524
description: This printed bearer certificate represents one-tenth of a founder's share (dixième de part de fondateur au porteur, sans désignation de valeur) No. 02524 in the Société Générale Égyptienne pour l'Agriculture & le Commerce (Egyptian General Society for Agriculture and Commerce), Société Anonyme. Capital initially 12,500,000 francs, subsequently increased to 15,000,000 francs (60,000 shares of 250 francs each, plus founder's parts divided into tenths). The company was constituted by notarial act before M. Amenosse-Louis-Jean-Collin, notary in Antwerp, Belgium. The Société Générale Égyptienne operated in Egypt during the Khedivial period (likely 1860s–1870s) in agricultural development and commerce. "Parts de fondateur" (founder's parts) without par value entitled holders to a share of surplus profits above the ordinary dividend.
creator: Société Générale Égyptienne pour l'Agriculture & le Commerce

title: The Spassky Copper Mine Limited: Share Warrant to Bearer, £1 (No. 68868, London, May 10, 1917)
description: This bilingual (English/French) printed share warrant to bearer entitles the holder to one fully paid share of £1 in The Spassky Copper Mine Limited. Capital £1,250,000 in 1,250,000 shares of £1 each. No. 68868. Given under the Common Seal of the Company. London, May 10, 1917. The Spassky copper mine was located in the Karaganda region of the Kazakh steppe (present-day central Kazakhstan), one of the richest copper deposits in Central Asia. Incorporated in the United Kingdom, the company was part of the wave of British investment in Russian and Central Asian mining ventures during the late imperial period. Issued in May 1917—two months after the February Revolution that overthrew the Tsar—the company was subsequently nationalized by the Bolsheviks.
creator: The Spassky Copper Mine Limited
notes: The Spassky Copper Mine Limited. Share warrant to bearer, £1. No. 68868. Capital £1,250,000. London, May 10, 1917. Bilingual English/French. Spassky mine, Kazakhstan.

title: Sechste Österreichische Kriegsanleihe (Sixth Austrian War Loan): 5½% Staatsanleihe, 1,000 Kronen (Serie 247, No. 007886, Vienna, April 1, 1917)
description: This printed bearer bond of 1,000 kronen at 5½% annual tax-free (steuerfreie) amortizable interest is one of the denominations of the Sixth Austrian War Loan (Sechste Österreichische Kriegsanleihe). Serie 247, No. 007,886. Vienna, April 1, 1917. Art Nouveau decorative design with flanking figures, cityscape oval, and Austrian imperial arms. This was the sixth of eight successive war loans issued by Austria-Hungary to finance World War I. By 1917 the financial situation was increasingly desperate, and these loans were heavily promoted through patriotic advertising campaigns. Along with the seventh and eighth loans, the sixth issue was subscribed largely by patriotic compulsion rather than investor confidence, and the collapse of the Habsburg Empire in 1918 rendered these bonds worthless.
creator: Austro-Hungarian Government
notes: Sechste Österreichische Kriegsanleihe (6th Austrian War Loan). 5½% tax-free amortizable Staatsanleihe. 1,000 kronen. Serie 247, No. 007,886. Vienna, April 1, 1917. Art Nouveau design.
transcription:
# Transcription

**Original:**

1000
KRONEN
1000

SERIE 247
SECHSTE ÖSTERREICHISCHE KRIEGSANLEIHE
STEUERFREIE 5½% AMORTISABLE

STAATSANLEIHE
EINTAUSEND KRONEN

Diese Staatsschuldverschreibung bildet einen Bestandteil der auf Grund der kaiserlischen Verordnung vom 4. August 1914, R.G.Bl. Nr. 209, ausgegebenen steuerfreien 5½% amortisablen Staatsanleihe (VI. Kriegsanleihe). Die Anleihe ist in Serien à 50,000,000 Kronen eingeteilt und in Abschnitten zu 50, 100, 500, 1000, 5000, 10.000 und 50.000 Kronen zahlbar. Die Coupons und der Talon versehen mit einem Talon versehen, gegen welchen die weiteren Coupons ohne Anrechnung vom Kronen oder derselben vom k. k. Staatszentralkasse in Wien zeitgerecht ausgelöst werden. Die Schuldverschreibungen dieser Anleihe werden vom 1. April 1917 ab mit 5½% jährlich verzinst. Die jährliche Verzinsung erfolgt am Ende eines jeden Jahres, nachhinein verlangt. Die Anleihe wird zum Nennwerte zurückgezahlt und unter Einhaltung eines anstehend gleichen Zinstermin und Kapitalzahlung umfassenden Annuitätsaufwandes in den Jahren 1928 bis 1957 auf Grund von Auslosungen getilgt. Die Auslosung wird nach Serien (zu 5,000,000 l) vorgenommen und die zurückgezahlten Schuldverschreibungen werden von der k. k. Staatszentralkasse in Wien vernichtet. Die Zahlung erfolgt an dem der Auslosung folgenden 1. April. Die ausgelosten Serien werden alsdann aufgelöst nach Zeichnung nehst einer Liste der Serien, aus welchen noch Restanten ausheilen, in der amtlichen „Wiener Zeitung" verlautbart. Der k. k. Staatsrat verwahrt ist das Recht vorbehalten, vom 1. Januar 1927 an bei einer Kündigung eines Jahres von vorne herein ohne Auslosung unter Einhaltung einer dreimonatigen Kündigungsfrist eine Nenn-werte zurückzuzahlen. In dieser Kündigung wird in der amtlichen Wiener Zeitung verlautbart werden. Die Ausschließung der Zinsen sowie die Rückzahlung des Kapitals der Staatsschuldverschreibungen erfolgt ohne Steuer-, Gebühren- oder sonstigen Abzug. Ein allfälliger Wertverlust der Coupons wird vom Kapitalsbetrag in Abzug gebracht. Die Verschreibungen dieser Anleihe werden bei der k. k. Staatszentralkasse in Wien während der Gültigkeitstage des Kapitalstitels ausgegeben und derselb alle bis dahin noch nicht fällig gewordenen Coupons nebst Talon zurückzustellen. Der Betrag der fälligen Coupons wird vom Kapitalsbetrag in Abzug gebracht. Die Dauer des Kapitals binnen 30 Jahren, in Ansehung der Zinsen binnen 8 Jahren.

Wien, am 1. April 1917.

Der k. k. Finanzminister:
[Signature]

Diese Schuldverschreibung ist in dem Hauptbuche der österreichischen Staatsschuld und [illegible] des Reichsrates eingetragen.

[Two signatures below]

---

**English Translation:**

1000
KRONEN
1000

SERIES 247
SIXTH AUSTRIAN WAR LOAN
TAX-FREE 5½% AMORTIZABLE

STATE BOND
ONE THOUSAND KRONEN

This state debt certificate forms part of the tax-free 5½% amortizable state loan (VI. War Loan) issued on the basis of the Imperial Decree of August 4, 1914, R.G.Bl. No. 209. The loan is divided into series of 50,000,000 kronen each and is payable in denominations of 50, 100, 500, 1,000, 5,000, 10,000, and 50,000 kronen. The coupons and the talon are provided with a talon, against which further coupons without set-off will be timely redeemed by the k.k. State Central Treasury in Vienna. The debt certificates of this loan will be bearing interest at 5½% per annum from April 1, 1917 onwards. Annual interest is paid at the end of each year; capital repayment follows at the end of the year in which the drawing occurs. The loan will be repaid at par value and will be amortized in equal annual installments (annuities) encompassing both interest and capital repayment during the years 1928 to 1957 on the basis of drawings. Redemption is carried out by series (of 5,000,000 l) and the repaid debt certificates are destroyed by the k.k. State Central Treasury in Vienna. Payment occurs on April 1 following the drawing. The drawn series will subsequently be dissolved according to the drawing with a list of the series from which remainders are still outstanding, published in the official "Wiener Zeitung." The k.k. State retains the right to terminate from January 1, 1927 onwards with notice of one year, without drawing, with observance of a three-month notice period, to repay at par value. This termination will be published in the official Wiener Zeitung. The exclusion of interest as well as the repayment of the capital of the state debt certificates occurs without tax, fees, or any other deductions. Any incidental loss of value of the coupons will be deducted from the capital amount. The securities of this loan are issued by the k.k. State Central Treasury in Vienna during the validity period of the capital title, and the same must return all coupons not yet due together with the talon. The amount of due coupons will be deducted from the capital amount. The duration of the capital is 30 years, for interest 8 years.

Vienna, April 1, 1917.

The k.k. Finance Minister:
[Signature]

This debt certificate is entered in the main book of Austrian state debt and [illegible] of the Reich Council.

[Two signatures]

---

**Notable Markings:**

- Serial number: No. 007,886
- Series: 247
- Date: Vienna (Wien), April 1, 1917
- Denomination: 1,000 Kronen at 5½% interest
- Two official signatures (Finance Minister and another official)
- Handwritten annotation "u/115" in red at top left
- Ornamental Art Nouveau border design with flanking allegorical figures
- Austrian Imperial Double-Headed Eagle coat of arms at bottom center
- Purple/mauve and cream colored decorative elements throughout

title: Achte Österreichische Kriegsanleihe (Eighth Austrian War Loan): 5½% Staatsanleihe, 100 Kronen (Serie 117, No. 128924, Vienna, June 1, 1918)
description: This printed bearer bond of 100 kronen at 5½% annual tax-free amortizable interest is a small-denomination note of the Eighth Austrian War Loan (Achte Österreichische Kriegsanleihe). Serie 117, No. 128,924. Vienna, June 1, 1918. Art Nouveau decorative design with the Austrian imperial coat of arms flanked by allegorical figures. The eighth and final Austrian war loan was issued in June 1918, just five months before the armistice that ended World War I and the simultaneous collapse of the Austro-Hungarian Empire. By this point it was clear to most observers that Austria-Hungary was heading for defeat, making this among the most ill-fated of all sovereign bond issues in European history.
creator: Austro-Hungarian Government
notes: Achte Österreichische Kriegsanleihe (8th Austrian War Loan). 5½% tax-free amortizable. 100 kronen. Serie 117, No. 128,924. Vienna, June 1, 1918. Art Nouveau design. Final Austrian war loan, issued five months before armistice.
transcription:
# Transcription

**Original (German):**

SERIE 117                                                    № 128.924

100 K
Achte Österr. Kriegsanleihe :: Steuerfreie 5½°/₀amortisable

STAATSANLEIHE
BLÜHDERT KRONEN

Diese Staatsanleihesverschreibung bildet einen Bestandteil der auf Grund des Gesetzes vom 4. Juni 1918, R. G. Bl. Nr. 151 eröffneten achten Kriegsanleihe (VIII. Kriegsanleihe). Die Anleihe ist in Serien zu 5,000.000 Kronen eingeteilt und in Abschnitte zu 100, 200, 500, 1000 und 50.000 Kronen ausgeführt; jede Staatsanleihesverschreibung ist mit 22 Coupons und mit einem Talon versehen, gegen welchen die weiteren Coupons ohne Unterschrift zu 100 Kronen oder Coupons von 50 Kronen bei Einlösung an den k. k. Staatscentralkasse in Wien zeitgerecht ausgehändigt werden. Die Anleihe ist zum Kurse von par 100 begeben mit 5½°/₀ jährlich, und zwar die Abschnitte zu 50 Kronen zu ganzjährigen Raten vom 1. Juni und zum Kurse von 50 Kronen zu halbjährigen Raten vom 1. Juni und 1. Dezember eines jeden halbjährlich verschwindenden Termin. Die Zinsen vom 1. Juni 1918 bis 31. August 1918 werden in Obligationscoupons verrechnet. Die Anleihe wird zum Nennwerte rückzahlbar und unter Beibehaltung der Couponrechte mit 1914 als amortisierbar erklärt. Im Falle einer etwaigen Rückzahlung vor dem Fälligkeitstage wird die Ausstattung auf auflösendst in den Jahren 1921 bis 1958 auf Grund von Auslosungen getätigt. Die Auslosung wird nach [illegible] Worten entschieden und findet im September jeden Jahres statt; die Rückzahlung erfolgt am dem Auslosung folgenden 1. März. Die aufgelösten Serien werden nach Ziehung gestat nach Ziehung gehört eine Liste der Serie. Verfahren wird publiziert. Kontrolle gegen Verlust.

Der k. k. Staatsverwaltung hat das Recht vorbehalten, vom 1. Januar 1927 angefangen die Aus- lösung unabhängig von Ziehungslosung oder noch ungelisten Anleihesfläches einer dringenden Rückgabefirst zum Nennwerte zurückzuschreiben. Die Kündigung wird in der amtlichen "Wiener Zeitung" verlautbart werden. Die Auswahrung der Zinsen sowie die Rückzahlung der Anleihe erfolgt bei der k. k. Staatscentralkasse in Wien oder sonstigen Abzug gegen Hinterlegung der fälligen Zinsencoupons, beziehungsweise Staatsanleihe-verschreibung. Die Rückzahlung erfolgt am Fälligkeitstage des Kapitalbetrages; es sind demzufolge alle bei dahem Neben Fällig gewordene Coupons nebst Talon zurückzustellen. Der Betrag der Staatsanleihesverschreibung erleichted durch Verjährung in Anschung der Kapitalbeträges; es sind demzufolge alle bei dahem noch Fällig gewordene Coupons plus Talon zurückzusetzen. Die Staatsanleihesverschreibung erleichted durch Verjährung in Anschung des Kapitals binnen dreißig Jahren. Im Anschlung der Zinsen sechs Jahre vom Fälligkeitstermine an.

Wien, am 1. Juni 1918.
Der k. k. Finanzminister

[Signature]

Diese Schuldverschreibung ist in der Hauptbuche der Staatsschuld eingetragen.
Für die Staatsschulden-Kontrollkommission des Reichsrates

[Signature]

---

**English translation:**

SERIES 117                                                    No. 128,924

100 K
Eighth Austrian War Loan :: Tax-Free 5½% Amortizable

STATE LOAN
HUNDRED KRONEN

This state loan certificate constitutes a component of the eighth war loan (VIII. War Loan) established pursuant to the law of June 4, 1918, R. G. Bl. No. 151. The loan is divided into series of 5,000,000 kronen each and is issued in denominations of 100, 200, 500, 1,000, and 50,000 kronen; each state loan certificate is provided with 22 coupons and one talon, against which further coupons without signature for 100 kronen or coupons for 50 kronen shall be issued without delay at the k.k. State Central Treasury in Vienna upon presentation. The loan has been issued at par 100 at 5½% per annum, with the denominations of 50 kronen to be redeemed in full annual installments from June 1st and at the rate of 50 kronen in semi-annual installments from June 1st and December 1st in terms that expire every half-year. Interest from June 1, 1918 to August 31, 1918 shall be settled in obligation coupons. The loan shall be repayable at par value and, with retention of coupon rights, is declared amortizable as of 1914. In the event of any redemption prior to the maturity date, the distribution shall be effected in the years 1921 through 1958 on the basis of drawings. The drawing shall be conducted according to [illegible] principles and shall take place in September of each year; redemption shall be effected on March 1st following the drawing. The drawn series shall be published according to the drawing record. Control against loss is maintained.

The k.k. State Administration reserves the right, commencing January 1, 1927, to effect redemption independent of drawing or of any uncalled loan portions at par value upon urgent notice. The notice shall be published in the official "Wiener Zeitung." Interest payments and redemption of the loan shall be effected at the k.k. State Central Treasury in Vienna or other authorized offices upon presentation of the due interest coupons or, respectively, state loan certificates, less any deductions for unpaid coupons. Redemption shall take place on the maturity date of the principal; accordingly, all due coupons and talons must be surrendered with it. The principal of the state loan certificate is subject to prescription after thirty years; interest coupons are subject to prescription six years from their maturity date.

Vienna, June 1, 1918.
The k.k. Finance Minister

[Signature]

This debt certificate has been recorded in the main register of state debt.
For the State Debt Control Commission of the Reichsrat

[Signature]

---

**Notable markings:**

- **Serial designation:** Serie 117, No. 128,924
- **Denomination:** 100 Kronen
- **Interest rate:** 5½% per annum, tax-free
- **Issue date:** Vienna, June 1, 1918
- **Signatures:** Two official signatures (Finance Minister and representative of the State Debt Control Commission)
- **Decorative elements:** Art Nouveau design with Austrian imperial double-headed eagle coat of arms, flanked by allegorical female figures representing war/strength and prosperity; ornamental borders with spiral and floral motifs in green and pink
- **Stamp:** Green postal/official stamp visible in upper left portion
- **Document condition:** Printed bearer bond with characteristic period typography and engraving

title: k.k. Staatsschuldverschreibung (Austrian Imperial State Bond): 100 Gulden Österreichische Währung (No. 207,407, Vienna, July 1, 1868)
description: This printed Imperial-Royal (k.k.) State Debt Certificate (Staatsschuldverschreibung) represents 100 gulden (Österreichische Währung / Austrian Currency) in the 4½% transfunded Austrian state debt. No. 207,407. Vienna, July 1, 1868. Issued by the k.k. Direction der Staatschulden (Imperial-Royal Directorate of State Debts). The document certifies the bearer's participation in the consolidated state debt resulting from the debt arrangements of 1867 (following Austria's defeat in the Austro-Prussian War of 1866 and the Compromise establishing the Austro-Hungarian dual monarchy). The Austrian gulden (florin) was the standard currency until replaced by the krone in 1892. Bears an Antwerpen (Antwerp) stamp, indicating this bond was registered or deposited there, consistent with Dutch/Belgian investor holding.
creator: k.k. Direction der Staatschulden (Austrian Imperial-Royal Directorate of State Debts)
notes: k.k. Staatsschuldverschreibung (Austrian Imperial State Bond). 100 gulden (Österreichische Währung). No. 207,407. Vienna, July 1, 1868. 4½% transfunded state debt. k.k. Direction der Staatschulden. Antwerpen deposit stamp.
transcription:
# Transcription of k.k. Staatsschuldverschreibung (Austrian Imperial State Bond)

## Original (German):

**Header:**
SLAZ FORINT OSZTRNAK ERTEKE | STO ZLÁTYCH RAKOUSKEHO C.

**Document Number:**
No 207,407 *
ANTWERPEN

**Title:**
Staatsschuldverschreibung

**Main Text:**

Die k.k. Direction der Staatschuldt bestätigt, dass gegenwärtige Staatsschuld-verschreibung über

**Ein Hundert Gulden**

Österreichische Währung

einen Bestandtheil der 5½igen [unclear] Staatsschuld, welche nicht erphofagen Zinsern von 16½ [unclear] bestand. Die [unclear] Schuld habet, welche auf Grund des Gesetzes von 20. Juni 1868 aus der Umwandlung der verschiedenhen Gattingen del mit Ende 1867 bestandenen fundirten allgemeinen Staatsschrift Hervorgewangen ist, zu deren Verzinsung die Länder der ungarischen Krone, gemäss Uebereinkunften, den im Gesetztartikel XV 1867 festgesetzten Jahresbeiträg leisten.

Die k.k. Staatsschiffter-Casse erfolgt halbjährig die Zinsen än den Ueberbringer der zu dieser Staatschuldverschreibung gehörigen Zinsen-Coupons in klingender Münze.

Wien, am 1. Juli 1868.

Für die k. k. Direction der Staatschuldt | Für die k. k. Staatsschuiden-Casse:
[Signature: illegible] | [Signature: illegible]

Diese Staatsschuldverschreibung ist in das Hauptbuch der Staatschuldt eingetragen.

Für die Staatsschuiden-Controlcommission des Reichrathes:
[Signature: illegible] | [Signature: illegible]

---

## English Translation:

The Imperial-Royal Direction of State Debt confirms that the present State Debt Certificate represents

**One Hundred Gulden**

Austrian Currency

constituting a portion of the 5½ percent [unclear] State Debt, which [unclear] interest of 16½ percent [unclear]. The [unclear] debt, which on the basis of the law of June 20, 1868, resulted from the conversion of the various categories that with the end of 1867 constituted the funded general state debt, for the interest payments of which the lands of the Hungarian Crown, according to agreement, render the annual contributions fixed in law article XV 1867.

The Imperial-Royal State Debt Treasury pays the interest half-yearly to the bearer of the interest coupons belonging to this state debt certificate in sound currency.

Vienna, July 1, 1868.

For the k.k. Direction of State Debt | For the k.k. State Debt Treasury:
[Signature] | [Signature]

This State Debt Certificate has been entered in the main register of State Debt.

For the State Debt Control Commission of the Imperial Diet:
[Signature] | [Signature]

---

## Notable Markings:

- **Date:** Vienna (Wien), July 1, 1868
- **Stamps:** Red circular stamps and rectangular stamps (Antwerp/ANTWERPEN marking visible)
- **Denomination:** 100 Gulden Österreichische Währung (Austrian Currency)
- **Interest Rate:** 5½ percent (4½% mentioned in header)

title: Massachusetts Commonwealth Tax Certificate (£3, 1786) and State of Massachusetts-Bay Bill ($5, ca. 1785): Two Documents

The upper document is a Massachusetts Commonwealth Tax Certificate, No. 1582, issued by the Treasurer's Office in Boston, April 1, 1786. Signed by Alex Hodgdon, Treasurer. It certifies that £3.13s.0 is due to P. Seek or Bearer, receivable in payment of one-third of Tax No. 5 (granted by the General Court of Massachusetts in March 1786), equivalent in value to gold and silver—a noteworthy inflation-adjustment clause. The lower document is a State of Massachusetts-Bay Bill, No. 1541 (or similar), for Five Dollars ($5), issued pursuant to an Act of the Legislature of the State of Massachusetts Bay dated the Fifth Day of May, 1785. Payable December 31, 1786, with 5% annual interest, paid by the State of Massachusetts-Bay in Spanish milled dollars. Signed by A. Cranch. These two documents represent the emergency finance instruments of the early American republic in the financially troubled years after the Revolution, when many states issued paper money and short-term certificates to manage fiscal obligations.
creator: Commonwealth of Massachusetts (Treasurer's Office)
notes: Two documents: (1) Massachusetts Commonwealth Tax Certificate No. 1582, £3.13s.0, Boston, April 1, 1786. Signed by Alex Hodgdon, Treasurer. (2) State of Massachusetts-Bay Bill No. 1541(?), $5, ca. 1785. 5% interest; payable Dec 31, 1786. Signed by A. Cranch.

Seek] or Bearer, the Sum of [handwritten: Three Pounds] and this Certificate shall be received in Payment for one third of the Tax (No. 5) granted in *March 1786*, equal to the same Sum in Gold and Silver.

[Signature: handwritten] £9. 13. 0 [handwritten: Alex Hodgdon] Treasurer.

Payable [unclear] 1786.
Annually, 1 60
Monthly, 0 12

[Signature: handwritten] A. Cranch

title: State of New York Canal Stock: $1,000 at 3% Per Annum (Henry Whiting, Albany, May 31, 1842)
description: This printed stock certificate from the New York State Comptroller's Office certifies that the People of the State of New York owe to Henry Whiting or his assigns the sum of $1,000 ("New York State Canal Stock") bearing interest at 3% per annum from May 31, 1842, payable quarterly on the first days of February, April, July, and October. Reimbursable at the pleasure of the State at any time after one year's advance notice. Albany, May 31, 1842. Signed by N.E. [?], Comptroller. Issued in pursuance of Chapter [?] of the Laws of New York, 1842. New York State canal stock was issued to fund the construction and expansion of the Erie Canal and related waterways—the most transformative public works project in American history, which opened the interior to commerce and made New York City the nation's leading commercial center.

title: Texas and German Emigration Company: Certificate of Stock No. 572 (Houston, June 15, 1852)
description: This printed stock certificate, No. 572, Folio 92, certifies that the German Emigration Company is the proprietor of 2 shares (No. 2775 and 2776) at $100 each in the Texas and German Emigration Company, constituted by Indentures dated September 15, 1843. Given at the City of Houston, Texas, this fifteenth day of June, 1852. Issued by authority of Indenture and signed by Henry F. Fisher, Agent of the German Emigration Company. The Memorandum on the certificate describes the joint stock as consisting of lands and tenements in Texas (about 1,200,000 acres plus other Texas properties), all rights and claims under the colonization contract with the Republic/State of Texas, and all debts and claims in favor of the Company. The Texas and German Emigration Company was associated with the Adelsverein (Society for the Protection of German Immigrants in Texas), organized by Prince Carl of Solms-Braunfels and other German noblemen to colonize Texas. Its agents—especially Henry Francis Fisher—negotiated land grants with the Republic of Texas, though the colonization enterprise ultimately failed financially. This certificate and the Adelsverein priority bond (cf. row 424) are companion pieces from the same colonization venture.
creator: Texas and German Emigration Company
notes: Texas and German Emigration Company. Certificate of Stock No. 572, Folio 92. 2 shares (No. 2775–2776) at $100 each. Houston, June 15, 1852. Agent: Henry F. Fisher. German immigrant colonization of Texas (Adelsverein). Related to Adelsverein bond (row 424).
transcription:
# TEXAS AND GERMAN EMIGRATION COMPANY
## CERTIFICATE OF STOCK

**No. 572 FOLIO 92**

SHARES OF ONE HUNDRED DOLLARS EACH.

This is to certify, that the German Emigration Company is proprietor of TWOT shares, No. 2775 and 2776, in the Joint Stock of the Texas and German Emigration Company, constituted by articles of association, incepted and agreement, contained in Indentures, bearing date the fifteenth day of September, A.D. 1843.

This Certificate is transferable by delivery or assignment, vesting in the bearer all and assignee all the rights of the original proprietor, to be subject to the provisions of said Indentures, and to the right of said German Emigration Company, as their assigns, to redeem the Stock hereby represented, by paying to him, as bearer, as aforesaid in any Banking or Commercial House in the City of Galveston, for him, as bearer, the said sum with interest from the first day of January, 1844, at the rate of five per cent per annum, on or before the first day of May, A.D. 1855.

Given at the City of Houston, this fifteenth day of June, 1852.

Issued by authority of said Indenture, and in accordance with its requirements, by

Henry F. Fisher

AGENT OF THE GERMAN EMIGRATION COMPANY.

---

## MEMORANDUM.

The JOINT STOCK of the **Texas and German Emigration Company** consists in the lands and tenements, rights, interests and property vested, conveyed, put in, assigned and transferred by the **German Emigration Company** to the **Texas and German Emigration Company**, as per Indenture of September 15th, 1843, to-wit: 1st.—All their right and claim to about 1,200,000 acres of land in Harris & Matagorda Counties, Grant. 2nd.—All their right and title to their private property in and about New Braunfels, Fredericksburg, Indianola, and lands and tenements elsewhere in the State of Texas. 3rd. All the debts, demands and claims due them in any manner in the State of Texas.

---

- **Serial Number:** No. 572, Folio 92
- **Signature:** Henry F. Fisher (Agent of the German Emigration Company)
- **Date:** June 15, 1852
- **Location:** Houston, Texas
- **Decorative ornamental border** with geometric patterns on left margin
- **Page number:** 24 (upper right corner)

title: The Common Fund Company Limited: Scrip H170, Right to Call for 100 Shares at £20 Each (London, December 20, 1869)
description: This engraved scrip certificate H170 of The Common Fund Company Limited certifies that the holder has the right to call for 100 shares of £20 each in the Capital Stock of the company, and to receive Certificates of Full Stock upon paying the par cost of £20 per share in one sum. Signed by R.W. Millz, Registrar. London, December 20, 1869. "Convertible into stock at par by all the Company's bankers in Europe and America." Offices in Washington, Berlin, Paris, and London. Incorporated under the Companies' Acts of 1862–67. Features a decorative globe showing North and South America. The Common Fund Company was a transatlantic investment company of the late Victorian era, with an unusually broad international corporate presence (offices in four countries) and pan-European convertibility for its scrip. Scrip certificates (as opposed to full shares) were interim instruments representing the right to acquire fully paid shares upon payment of the subscription price.

title: Commonwealth of Pennsylvania: Land Warrant for Survey, Allegheny/Conewango Purchase (Governor Thomas Mifflin, 1792)
description: This printed and handwritten land warrant is issued by the Commonwealth of Pennsylvania, signed by Governor Thomas Mifflin, directing the Surveyor-General, Daniel Brodhead, Esquire, to survey a tract of land in the late purchase made from the Indians, East of the Allegheny River and Conewango Creek, for Robert A. Arres(?), who had paid the full purchase price to the Receiver-General of the Land-Office. The warrant references several statutes: An Act of April 7, 1792, for the purchase and regulation of unappropriated lands; An Act of October 3, 1788, for surveying and disposing of unappropriated lands; and An Act of April 19, 1792, for sale of vacant lands. The document instructs that four hundred [?] acres be surveyed. 1792. This land warrant represents a foundational document of American frontier real estate finance—the mechanism by which the Commonwealth of Pennsylvania allocated and monetized its western lands in the years immediately following independence.
creator: Commonwealth of Pennsylvania (Governor Thomas Mifflin)
notes: Commonwealth of Pennsylvania land warrant. Signed by Governor Thomas Mifflin. For survey of land in the Allegheny River/Conewango Creek purchase (Act of April 7, 1792). Directed to Surveyor-General Daniel Brodhead. For Robert A. Arres(?). 1792.
transcription:

**The Commonwealth of Pennsylvania, ff.**

Whereas Robert A. Arres Esqr.

hath applied for, and paid into the office of the Receiver-General, of the Land-Office, the whole of the purchase money for [illegible] thousand acres of land within the last purchase made of the Indians, East of Allegheny river and Conawango creek, at and after the rate of Five Pounds for every hundred acres of the fame, agreeably to an act of Assembly, of the said Commonwealth, passed the 7th day of April, 1792, entitled "An "Act to provide further regulation, whereby to secure fair and equal proceedings in the Land-Office, and in fur-"veying of lands;" to one other Act of the said Assembly dated 3d day of October 1788, entitled "A Supple-"ment to an Act, entitled "An Act to alter and amend an Act, entitled "An Act for granting and dis-"posing of the unappropriated lands within this state;" also to one passed the 3d April 1792, entitled "An "Act for the sale of the vacant lands, within this commonwealth." The said application and warrant are numbered four thousand five hundred [and] twenty [six]

There are therefore to authorize and require you to survey or cause to be surveyed for the said Robert A. Morris

the said quantity of acres, within the said late purchase, agreeably to said acts.

In Witness whereof Thomas Mifflin, Governor of the said commonwealth, hath herunto set his hand, and caused the lefs seal of the said commonwealth to be affixed, the twenty fourth day of December in the year 1792

To Daniel Brodhead, Esquire, }

title: The Egyptian Enterprise and Development Company: Deferred Share Warrant to Bearer No. 08527 (Cairo, March 5, 1906)
description: This bilingual (English/French) printed deferred share warrant to bearer (Certificat de Part de Dividende au Porteur) No. 08527 entitles the holder to one deferred share in The Egyptian Enterprise and Development Company. Capital: £E. 160,000, increased to £E. 400,000 by resolution of the General Meeting of Shareholders on December 15, 1905, divided into 40,000 ordinary shares of £E. 10 each. Empowered by Khedival Decree of November 26, 1904. Cairo, March 5, 1906. Signed by two Administrators. The Egyptian Enterprise and Development Company was an Edwardian-era commercial enterprise operating in Egypt under Khedivial charter, engaged in development and business activities in the country during the period of British occupation (1882–1952). Deferred shares received dividends only after ordinary shares had received their fixed dividend, making them highly leveraged profit-participation instruments.

title: The New Four per Cent. Annuities (Bank of England): Stock Transfer Receipt for £6 6s. 2d. (London, August 2, 1822)
description: This printed and handwritten transfer receipt records the transfer of £6 6s. 2d. (six pounds, six shillings, two pence) interest (or share) in the Capital Stock of The New Four per Cent. Annuities—the British Government Consols restructured from Five Per Cent. Annuities under an Act of Parliament of the 3rd Year of His Majesty King George IV, entitled "An Act for transferring several Annuities of Five Pounds per Cent. into Annuities of Four Pounds per Cent." Transferable at the Bank of England. Consideration paid: £6 5s. 7d. Transferred to Philip Davis, Abigail Davis, and Jane Catherine Davis. Received August 2, 1822. Witnessed by J. Robinson (1085) and H.J. Cosper. "Account Closed 25th November 1836." Transfer Days: Tuesday, Wednesday, Thursday, Friday (Holidays excepted). Dividends due January 5 and July 5 annually. This document illustrates the secondary market in British government perpetual annuities at the Bank of England—the foundation of the British national debt market that developed from the late seventeenth century and became the model for sovereign bond markets worldwide.
creator: Bank of England (New Four per Cent. Annuities)
