title: Tontine Royale: Subscription Contract, Second Class, First Subdivision (Paris, August 1734)
description: A printed subscription contract for the French royal tontine established by the King's edict of August 1734, drawn up before Notaires du Châtelet de Paris, including Michel Etienne Turgot (Chevalier, Seigneur de Soufflinois), François Poulain de Soissons, and others. The document records participation in the royal tontine under which 14,063,000 livres of life annuities (rentes viagères) were to be sold, organized in divisions (classes and subdivisions) of three livres de capital each, with annuities charged on the revenues of the Aides et Gabelles. Survivors of each subdivision were to receive the annuities of deceased members, concentrating income among the longest-lived participants. The tontine was a leading instrument of 18th-century French royal finance, combining elements of life insurance, speculation, and sovereign borrowing.
creator: Notaires du Châtelet de Paris
notes: French royal tontine, August 1734. Second Class, First Subdivision. 14,063,000 livres of life annuities. Drawn up before Notaires du Châtelet de Paris including Michel Etienne Turgot. Annuities charged on Aides et Gabelles revenues.
transcription:
# Transcription

**Original (French):**

TONTINE ROYALE Aoüst 1734. 2.e Classe.

M. De Raphanneuf
Subdivision

PARDEVANT les Contrôllers du Roy, Notaires au Chastelet de Paris, souffignez, furent presens Messire MICHEL ESTIENNE TURGOT, Chevalier, Seigneur de Souffons, Bons, Uffy, Potigny, Perciers, Brucourt, & autres lieux, Conseiller du Roy en ses Conseils, President au Parlement & en la seconde Chambre des Requestes du Palais, Prevost des Marchands; Claude-François Petit, Ecuyer, Conseiller du Roy, Quartiniers; Jean-Baptiste de Santeul, Ecuyer; Jean-Baptiste Tripart, Ecuyer, Conseiller du Roy & de la Ville; & François Touvenot, Ecuyer, Conseiller du Roy, Notaire au Chastelet, tous Eichevins de cette Ville.

Lesquels ont dit, que le Roy par son Edit du mois d'Aoüst 1734. registré où besoin a esté, auroit, pour les causes y contenués, ordonne qu'il seroit par les Commissaires de Sa Majesté nommeroît, vendu & alienné quatorze cens soixante-trois mille livres actuelles & effectives de rente viagere, sous le titre de Tontine, fusette à accroissement, distribuée en quinze classes, composées de differentes divisions, dont chaque action feroit de trois cens livres de capital; lesdigtes rentes à prendre sur le produit des droits d'Aydes & Gabelles, & des cinq grosses fermes, que Sa Majesté à [speciellement] & par privilege, affectez, obligez & hypothequez au payement & continuation des arrerages desdites rentes, même par préference à la partie du trésor Royal, dont les constitutions seroient faites à ceux qui defereroient les acquerois sous leurs noms, où autres qu'ils nommerioent, avec faculté à chaque personne de prendre tel nombre de parties de rente qu'il lui plairoit dans chaque subdivision de la classe, pour lesquelles il lui seroit expedié des contrats: Que de la rente de chaque rentier qui viendroit à deceder, un quart des arrearges de fadite rente seroit estenu à la defcharge des revenus de Sa Majesté, & les trois autres quarts appartiendieroît par accroissement aux survivans de la subdivision de la classe, entre lesquels ils seroient distribuée d'année en année jusqu'au dernier mourant; en forte que le dernier vivant de chaque subdivision de chacune desdites classes, recueilleeroit feul les trois quarts du revenu des capitaux qui composeroient ladite subdivision, & que lesdites rentes pourroient auffi estre acquises par les estrangers non naturalizez, même ceux demeùrans hors du Royaume; à l'efect de quoy Sa Majesté auroit renoncé à tous droits d'aubaine, confiscations, & autres qui pourroient lui appartenir: pour, des arrearges desdites rentes, seûoit par les acquéreurs leur vie durant, comme de leur propre chose, vray & loyal acquelt, en vertu des contracts qui seroient faits par lesdits Sieurs Brevolt des Marchands & Eichevins, & estre payé des arrearges desdites rentes à bureau ouvert par chacune année, en deux paiemens, sur lesquittances en parchemin, paffez devant Notaires en la forme portée audit Edit, à commencer du premier Octobre 1734. pour ceux qui auroient fourni leurs fonds avant le premier Janvier 1735. fans que lesdites rentes puiffent estre fujettes à aucunes faites pour quelque caufe que ce soit, même pour les dettes & affaires de Sa Majesté, ni estre reduites ni retranchées fous quelque pretexte que ce puiffe estre: en outre, que les rentes qui seroient acquises par les estrangers, seroient exemptes de toutes lettres de marque & de represaille, ainfi qu'il eft plus au long porté audit Edit; en execution duquel a esté faite vente & alienation par Messieurs les Commiffaires du Conseil, Procureurs speciaux de Sa Majesté auxdits Sieurs Brevolt des Marchands & Eichevins, desdites quatorze cens foixante-trois mille livres de rente, par contract paffé en vertu des Lettres patentes y enoncées, pardevant Bourron & ion conferere

---

# English Translation:

ROYAL TONTINE August 1734. Second Class.

M. De Raphanneuf
Subdivision

BEFORE the Controllers of the King, Notaries of the Châtelet of Paris, undersigned, were present Messire MICHEL ESTIENNE TURGOT, Knight, Lord of Souffons, Bons, Uffy, Potigny, Perciers, Brucourt, and other places, Counselor of the King in his Councils, President of Parliament and in the Second Chamber of Petitions of the Palace, Provost of the Merchants; Claude-François Petit, Esquire, Counselor of the King, Quarternier; Jean-Baptiste de Santeul, Esquire; Jean-Baptiste Tripart, Esquire, Counselor of the King and of the City; and François Touvenot, Esquire, Counselor of the King, Notary of the Châtelet, all Aldermen of this City.

Who declared that the King, by his Edict of the month of August 1734, registered where necessary, would have, for the reasons contained therein, ordered that by the Commissioners of His Majesty appointed, fourteen thousand sixty-three thousand livres of actual and effective life annuities would be sold and alienated, under the title of Tontine, subject to increase, distributed into fifteen classes, composed of different divisions, each share consisting of three hundred livres of capital; said annuities to be charged on the proceeds of the rights of Aides and Gabelles, and the five major farms, which His Majesty had specially and by privilege, affected, obliged, and mortgaged to the payment and continuation of the arrears of said annuities, even in preference to the portion of the Royal Treasury, the constitutions of which would be made to those who would purchase the shares under their names or others whom they would name, with the faculty for each person to take such number of annuity shares as they pleased in each subdivision of the class, for which they would be issued contracts: That from the annuity of each annuitant who should come to die, one quarter of the arrears of said annuity would be retained for the discharge of the revenues of His Majesty, and the three other quarters would belong by accretion to the survivors of the subdivision of the class, among whom they would be distributed year by year until the last survivor; so that the last living member of each subdivision of each of said classes would collect alone the three quarters of the revenue of the capital which would compose said subdivision, and that said annuities could also be acquired by foreigners not naturalized, even those residing outside the Kingdom; for which purpose His Majesty would have renounced all rights of escheat, confiscations, and others which might belong to him: for, from the arrears of said annuities, would be paid by the acquirers for their lifetime, as of their own property, true and loyal ownership, by virtue of the contracts which would be made by said Messieurs Brevolt of the Merchants and Aldermen, and be paid of the arrears of said annuities at open bureau by each year, in two payments, upon receipts on parchment, passed before Notaries in the form contained in said Edict, commencing from the first October 1734 for those who would have provided their funds before the first January 1735, provided that said annuities cannot be subject to any proceedings for any cause whatsoever, even for the debts and affairs of His Majesty, nor be reduced or diminished under any pretext whatsoever: furthermore, that the annuities which would be acquired by foreigners would be exempt from all letters of marque and reprisal, as it is more fully contained in said

title: Compagnie Générale des Tramways de Moscou & de Russie: Action Privilégiée, 250 Francs (No. 00274, Brussels, 1885)
description: Preferred share (Action Privilégiée) No. 00274 of the Compagnie Générale des Tramways de Moscou & de Russie (General Company of Moscow and Russia Tramways), a Belgian société anonyme with registered offices in Brussels. The capital of 250 francs is fully paid (au porteur entièrement libérée). The company was established under contracts dated January 17, 1885, confirmed by M. Van Halteren, notary, and published in the Moniteur belge on February 4 and 7, 1885. Signed by two administrators. The company financed and operated urban tramway infrastructure in Moscow and other Russian cities, representing a significant Belgian capital investment in Russian transport.

title: Travaux d'Irrigation du Gouvernement Égyptien (Assouân et Assiout 1898): Mandat de Paiement, £500 Sterling (No. 58/111)
description: A payment warrant (Mandat de Paiement) No. 58 / No. 111, for £500 Sterling, issued by the Egyptian Government for the Aswan and Assiut Irrigation Works of 1898. The Egyptian Government acknowledges owing to MM. John Aird & Cie., or the bearer, the sum of Five Hundred Pounds Sterling, payable in London on January 1, 1932, at the offices of the Banque de l'Afrique, with interest at 3½% per annum. The document relates to the major British-engineered irrigation projects at Aswan (the first Aswan Dam, completed 1902) and Assiut (Asyut Barrage), financed through payment warrants (mandats) issued against future revenue. John Aird & Co. was the principal British contractor for the Aswan Dam.
creator: Egyptian Government, Ministry of Public Works
notes: Mandat de Paiement No. 58/111, £500 Sterling. Travaux d'Irrigation du Gouvernement Égyptien, Assouân et Assiout 1898. Payable to John Aird & Cie. Due London, January 1, 1932, at Banque de l'Afrique. Interest 3½% p.a.
transcription:
# Transcription

**Original (French):**

NUMÉRO DE SÉRIE: 58
NUMÉRO D'ORDRE: 111

TRAVAUX D'IRRIGATION DU GOUVERNEMENT ÉGYPTIEN

ASSOUAN ET ASSIOUT 1898

MANDAT DE PAIEMENT

£500 STERLING | £500 STERLING

Le Gouvernement Égyptien déclare par les présentes, qu'en retour de travaux effectués et de matériaux fournis pour les travaux d'irrigation susmentsionnés, il reconnaît devoir, absolument, et sans condition aucune, à MM. JOHN AIRD et CIE. la somme de Cinq cents livres sterling (£500).

Le Gouvernement s'engage par les présentes à payer à MM. JOHN AIRD et CIE., ou au PORTEUR DE CE MANDAT, le 1er Janvier 1932 la dite somme de Cinq cents livres sterling.

Ce paiement s'effectuera à LONDRES, par l'intermédiaire de la Banque d'Angleterre, contre la remise de ce mandat.

Ce paiement sera effectué à tout événement AU PORTEUR des présentes, en totalité et sans déduction quelconque et indépendamment de toute contestation qui peut être actuellement ou qui pourrait s'élever dans la suite quelconque. [Underlined text in box: "Gouvernement"]

MM. John Aird et Cie., ou de toute autre contestation quelle qu'elle soit, la dette reconnue par les présentes étant pour une somme certaine et déterminée et constituant une créance liquide et reconnue par le Gouvernement Égyptien.

Le présent mandat confère au porteur, jusqu'à son entier désintéressement, une affectation sur les travaux pour assurer le paiement de la somme indiquée dans ce mandat, le dit porteur pourra, d'accord avec les porteurs de tous mandats sans par rapport aux mêmes travaux (dont le maximum ne devra pas toutefois excéder les limites mentionnées dans la table au dos des présentes) ou d'accord avec la majorité de ces porteurs, nommer ou faire nommer par l'autorité compétente un représentant chargé de mettre à exécution de la manière qu'il appartiendra le dit droit d'affectation, au cas où ce mandat n'aura pas été payé à l'échéance.

Aucune prise de possession par le Gouvernement des travaux ni aucun autre acte quelconque ne seront susceptibles de porter atteinte à la dite affectation.

Le présent mandat et tous autres mandats émis ou à émettre dans les limites susmensionnées auront rang égal et jouiront sans aucun droit de préférence ni de priorité à raison de leur numéro, série, date d'émission ou autre circonstance quelconque.

En date du 30 Juillet 1901 | Pour le Gouvernement,
[Signatures and handwriting] | Le Ministre des Travaux Publics.

Contresigné
[Signature]
Ingénieur du Gouvernement Égyptien.

---

# English Translation:

SERIES NUMBER: 58
ORDER NUMBER: 111

IRRIGATION WORKS OF THE EGYPTIAN GOVERNMENT

ASWAN AND ASSIUT 1898

PAYMENT WARRANT

£500 STERLING | £500 STERLING

The Egyptian Government declares by these presents, that in return for work executed and materials supplied for the above-mentioned irrigation works, it acknowledges owing, absolutely and unconditionally, to MM. JOHN AIRD and CO. the sum of Five Hundred Pounds Sterling (£500).

The Government undertakes by these presents to pay to MM. JOHN AIRD and CO., or to the BEARER OF THIS WARRANT, on 1st January 1932 the said sum of Five Hundred Pounds Sterling.

This payment shall be made in LONDON, through the intermediary of the Bank of England, against the surrender of this warrant.

This payment shall be made in any event TO THE BEARER of these presents, in full and without any deduction whatsoever and independently of any contestation which may currently exist or which may subsequently arise in any manner. [Underlined in box: "Government"]

MM. John Aird and Co., or any other contestation whatsoever, the debt acknowledged by these presents being for a certain and determined sum and constituting a liquid claim recognized by the Egyptian Government.

The present warrant confers upon the bearer, until full satisfaction, a charge upon the works to secure payment of the sum indicated in this warrant. The said bearer may, in agreement with the bearers of all other warrants relating to the same works (the maximum of which shall not, however, exceed the limits mentioned in the table on the reverse of these presents) or in agreement with the majority of such bearers, appoint or cause to be appointed by the competent authority a representative charged with executing in the manner which shall be appropriate the said right of charge, in the event that this warrant shall not have been paid at maturity.

No taking into possession by the Government of the works nor any other whatsoever act shall be capable of affecting the said charge.

The present warrant and all other warrants issued or to be issued within the limits aforesaid shall rank equally and shall enjoy without any right of preference or priority by reason of their number, series, date of issue or other circumstance whatsoever.

Dated 30 July 1901 | For the Government,
[Signatures] | The Minister of Public Works.

Countersigned
[Signature]
Engineer of the Egyptian Government.

---

- **Pink/magenta stamp marks:** Rectangular stamp or approval mark visible in the document
- **White circular marks:** Several white circular markings (possibly from document handling or filing)
- **Language:** French (official language for Egyptian Government international financial documents of the period)

title: Court of Chancery Injunction Bond, Madison County, Tennessee: Nathan H. Ballon and John E. Bostick, $1,900 (April 10, 1852)
description: A handwritten surety bond (obligation) for $1,900 Lawful Money, filed in the Court of Chancery at Jackson, for the district of Madison County, Western Division of the State of Tennessee. Nathan H. Ballon and John E. Bostick bind themselves jointly and severally to pay the sum of nineteen hundred dollars, conditioned on Ballon prosecuting his bill of complaint against Thomas Farnell and others with full legal effect; the bond to be void if the suit succeeds, otherwise to remain in force. Dated April 10th, 1852. Security for the bond: property valued over $900, age 50 and 41 respectively. Approved by the Court. An example of 19th-century American legal surety practice in chancery proceedings.
creator: Nathan H. Ballon; John E. Bostick
notes: Chancery Court injunction bond, $1,900. Court of Chancery, Jackson, Madison County, Western Division, Tennessee. Nathan H. Ballon plaintiff; John E. Bostick surety. Dated April 10, 1852. Property security approved by Court.
transcription:
# Transcription

Know all men by these presents, that we, Nathan H. Ballon & Mccoy D. Walmores & Deo E. Bostick, Jas W. Cleaph

are held and firmly bound unto Res. a. Farnell & a. Sauncer, Tably. P. W. & Nathaniel Atcherwerds, Jas S. Hargrood Lowry, Jassella Starges Elyan Thos, Jas Millers, Elyard Mary & M'Leay,

in the just and full sum of Nineteen hundred dollars

dollars, for the payment of which, well and truly to be made, we and each of us, bind ourselves, our heirs, executors and administrators, jointly and severally, firmly, by these presents, sealed with our seals, and dated this 10th day of April 1852

The condition of the above obligation is such, that whereas the above bound

N. Ballon

this day filed a Bill of complaint in the Clerk's office of the Court of Chancery, at Jackson, for the district composed of the county of Madison, for the Western Division of the State of Tennessee, against said

Thos Thos Farnell and others)

and prayed for and obtained an Injunction upon the matters set forth in said Bill. Now should the said,

Haltoone

prosecute his suit on said Bill with effect, or in case he shall fail therein, shall pay to the said

Leaph,

all such costs and charges as may accrue in consequence thereof, and stand to, abide by and perform such order, judgment or decree, as may be made by said Court, touching said Injunction; then the above obligation to be void; otherwise, to remain in full force and virtue.

[Handwritten notes at bottom:]
Security for Bond this [illegible]
Slave [illegible] or 52 yales 900. D.N.
Hiren age 41, value 900 D.
Accepted by Court John E. Bostick Ced

---

**Notable markings:**

- Handwritten annotations throughout in cursive script
- Signatures of Nathan H. Ballon and John E. Bostick
- Court approval notation at bottom
- Property valuation notes indicating security ($900+)
- Ages noted: 50 and 41 years
- Dated: 10th day of April 1852
- Handwritten note in upper left corner: "Two Slaves or Bond 1900"

title: Unilever N.V.: Optiebewijs (Option Certificate) No. 97790 (Rotterdam, July 1937)
description: Option certificate (Optiebewijs) No. 97790 of Unilever N.V., established in Rotterdam. The holder of eight such option certificates has the right to subscribe to eight new Unilever N.V. shares of f. 20 nominal value each (totaling f. 160). Valid until June 10, 1942 (van onwaarde na 10 June 1942). The certificate is linked to the conditions of Van den Bergh's Fabrieken N.V. 3½% Obligatielening (bond loan) of 1937, under which the option right was granted to bondholders. Dated Rotterdam, July 1937. Signed by the Secretary and a member of the Board of Directors. Represents a significant corporate finance instrument from one of the world's earliest modern consumer goods multinationals.
creator: Unilever N.V.
notes: Optiebewijs No. 97790. Unilever N.V., Rotterdam. Eight certificates entitle holder to subscribe to 8 new shares at f.20 each (f.160 total). Valid until June 10, 1942. Related to Van den Bergh's Fabrieken N.V. 3½% Obligatielening, 1937.
transcription:
# Unilever N.V. Option Certificate No. 97790

**Original (Dutch):**

UNILEVER N.V.
GEVESTIGD TE ROTTERDAM.

OPTIEBEWIJS No. 97790

VAN ONWAARDE NA 30 JUNI 1942.

Bij notariële acte dd° 23 November 1937 is de naam der Unilever N.V. veranderd in: „Lever Brothers & Unilever N.V.". De naam der Van den Bergh's Fabrieken N.V. werd bij notariële acte dd° 2 September 1938 gewijzigd in: „Van den Bergh's en Jurgens' Fabrieken N.V.".

De houder van acht dezer optiebewrijzen heeft het recht, tegen inlevering dezer bewijzen van Unilever N.V. een certificaat van een gewoon aandeel in Unilever N.V., groot ƒ 1000.— nominaal, te verwerven tot de prijs van ƒ 1950.— (195%) tot en met 31 December 1938 en van ƒ 2150.— (215%) tot en met 1 Januari 1939 tot en met 30 Juni 1942, op de voorwaarden vervat in de aan de keerzijde van dit bewijs vermelde bepalingen. Overeenkomstig art. 5 dieser bepalingen worden bij de storting 3½%-obligatien, behorende tot de in 1937 ten laste van Van den Bergh's Fabrieken N.V. uitgegeven 3½%-obligatielening, ten hoogste groot ƒ 30.000.000.—, in betaling aangenomen tot haar volle nominale bedrag, vermeererd met opgelopen rente.

ROTTERDAM, Juli 1937.

[Secretary signature]
SECRETARIS.

[Chairman signature]
LID VAN DE RAAD VAN BESTUUR.

JOH. ENSCHEDÉ EN ZONEN - HAARLEM

---

**English Translation:**

UNILEVER N.V.
ESTABLISHED IN ROTTERDAM.

OPTION CERTIFICATE No. 97790

OF NO VALUE AFTER 30 JUNE 1942.

By notarial deed of 23 November 1937, the name of Unilever N.V. was changed to: "Lever Brothers & Unilever N.V.". The name of Van den Bergh's Fabrieken N.V. was changed by notarial deed of 2 September 1938 to: "Van den Bergh's and Jurgens' Fabrieken N.V.".

The holder of eight of these option certificates has the right, upon return of these certificates to Unilever N.V., to acquire a certificate of an ordinary share in Unilever N.V. with a nominal value of ƒ 1,000.— at the price of ƒ 1,950.— (195%) until and including 31 December 1938 and ƒ 2,150.— (215%) until and including 1 January 1939 and until 30 June 1942, under the conditions set forth in the provisions stated on the reverse of this certificate. In accordance with article 5 of these provisions, upon deposit of the 3½%-bonds belonging to the 3½%-bond loan issued in 1937 by Van den Bergh's Fabrieken N.V., up to a maximum amount of ƒ 30,000,000.—, shall be accepted in payment at their full nominal value, plus accrued interest.

ROTTERDAM, July 1937.

---

**Notable Markings:**

- Two handwritten signatures: one labeled "SECRETARIS" (Secretary) and one labeled "LID VAN DE RAAD VAN BESTUUR" (Member of the Board of Directors)
- Orange circular seal in upper right corner with Dutch coat of arms and text "50 C" (denomination marking)
- Serial number: **No. 97790**
- Ornate blue decorative border frame throughout
- Printer mark at bottom: "JOH. ENSCHEDÉ EN ZONEN - HAARLEM" (Johannes Enschedé and Sons, Haarlem - a prominent Dutch security printer)
- Date: Rotterdam, Juli 1937 (July 1937)

title: United States of Brazil: 5% 20-Year Funding Bond of 1931, $100 (No. S2940, Due October 1, 1951)
description: A $100 bearer bond issued by the United States of Brazil under its 5% 20-Year Funding Loan of 1931, due October 1, 1951. Serial No. S2940. Printed in an ornate orange border with allegorical vignettes. Brazil promises to pay the bearer $100 on October 1, 1951, with semi-annual interest coupons at 5% per annum, payable at the City Bank Farmers Trust Company in New York. Authorized by Decree No. 20,251 of August 1931, representing a major Brazilian sovereign debt restructuring during the Great Depression. The Funding Loan consolidated Brazil's short-term obligations and was a key instrument of the country's efforts to maintain international creditworthiness during the global financial crisis.
creator: United States of Brazil
notes: Brazil 5% 20-Year Funding Bond of 1931, $100. No. S2940. Due October 1, 1951. Decree No. 20,251, August 1931. Semi-annual coupons. Payable at City Bank Farmers Trust Company, New York.
transcription:
# Transcription of United States of Brazil 5% 20-Year Funding Bond of 1931

## Main Text Transcription:

**UNITED STATES OF BRAZIL**

**5 PER CENT. 20-YEAR FUNDING BOND OF 1931**

**DUE OCTOBER 1, 1951**

No C
82940                                                                                                82940
No C

United States of Brazil, wheresoever issued the Government for value received, promises to pay to the bearer hereof on the first day of October, 1951 (unless before said date this bond shall have been duly called for redemption), and payment of the accruing upon prior date, pursuant to the principal sum of

**ONE HUNDRED DOLLARS ($100)**

in gold coin of the United States of America of the present standard of weight and fineness existing October 1891, with interest thereon from the first day of October, 1931, at the rate of five per cent. per annum, payable semi-annually on the first and last days of October in each and every year until this bond shall be paid. Principal and semi-annual interest on this bond shall be made only upon presentation and surrender of the attached interest coupons as they severally mature. Both principal and interest shall be paid without exchange or other deduction to any person in London, England, at the office of a paying agent to be nominated by Dalton, Read & Co., in advising of Bankers buying such bonds, or in New York, at the office of the City Bank Farmers Trust Company, or such other paying agent as the United States of Brazil may designate.

[MIDDLE SECTION - SMALLER TEXT - Legal conditions regarding bond terms, coupons, and financial covenants - text is dense and partially illegible in places]

Principal of and interest on this Bond shall also be collectible at the offices of the bearer hereof in London, England, at the office of a paying agent to be nominated by Dalton, Read & Co., in advising of bankers buying such bonds, or in New York, at the office of the City Bank Farmers Trust Company, or such other paying agent as the United States of Brazil may designate.

[EXTENSIVE LEGAL TEXT regarding funding loan details, consolidation of prior debts, and financial covenants - much of the fine print is challenging to read with complete accuracy]

...The Sterling Bonds and the Time Bonds are similarly entitled to the benefits of an Equivalent Gold Coin provision. The Sinking Funds Bonds and the Time Bonds are also jointly and severally subject to the security of an equivalent and participating proportion of said General Gold Sinking Fund and of any Gold Loan Currency Fund that may be constituted for the same annual redemption of Bonds of the principal amount thereof. The Sterling Bonds and the Time Bonds are similarly entitled to the benefits of an Equivalent Gold Coin provision. [text continues with financial and legal terms]

...such purpose until such time decline affords for such purpose.

Dated, October 1,1931.

**United States of Brazil**

---

Two signatures appear at the bottom of the document [signatures present but individual names not clearly legible in reproduction]

description: Scrip certificate No. 262 of the Westchester & Philadelphia Rail Road Co., authorized by resolution of the Board of Directors on September 29th, 1860. Certifies that Washington Hastings (or assigns) is entitled to receive the sum of One Thousand Eight Hundred and Sixty-Five Dollars ($1,865), or its equivalent in Railroad scrip, with interest at the rate of six percent per annum, on the first day of October. Amount of One Thousand Eight Hundred and Sixty-Five Dollars. Dated October 10, 1862. Signed by the Treasurer and bearing the company seal. The Westchester & Philadelphia Rail Road was a Pennsylvania short-line railroad serving the Philadelphia region. Railroad scrip was a common form of corporate obligation used in 19th-century American railroad finance.

title: Y. Rofé & Co. (Cairo): Acte Préliminaire de Vente d'Obligations, Canal de Suez 3%, 2ème Série
description: A preliminary bond sale agreement (Acte Préliminaire de Vente d'Obligations) issued by Y. Rofé & Co., bankers and brokers with offices in Cairo (Le Caire), Port Said, Alexandria (Alexandrie), and Mansoura. The document confirms that Y. Rofé & Co. has agreed to sell Suez Canal Company 3% bonds, 2nd Series (Oblig. Canal de Suez 3% 2ème Série) to the identified purchaser at the stated price and terms. The agreement specifies conditions for payment, delivery of definitive bond certificates, and recourse under Article 238 of the Code Civil Mixte in the event of failure to deliver. Issued from the Cairo head office (Rue d'Ismaïlieh / Rue d'Almaz, telephone 44-67). The Suez Canal Company's bonds were among the most internationally traded securities of the late 19th century.
creator: Y. Rofé & Co., Le Caire
notes: Acte Préliminaire de Vente d'Obligations, Oblig. Canal de Suez 3%, 2ème Série. Y. Rofé & Co., Cairo; offices in Port-Said, Alexandrie, Mansoura. Reference to Code Civil Mixte, Art. 238. Ca. late 19th century.

---

**Y. ROFÉ & CO**

LE CAIRE

5, Sharia El Maghraby – Telephone 44-97

SUCCURSALES:
ALEXANDRIE                    PORT-SAÏD                    MANSOURA
4, rue des 28 Juillet 2282    12, rue Said - Tel. 721      rue Makac

**ACTE PRÉLIMINAIRE DE VENTE D'OBLIGATIONS**

VALABLE APRÈS LA CONFIRMATION DES VENDEURS PAR LE LITRE SÉPARÉE

Entre: _________________ le _________________ 191_

La Maison Sociale Y. ROFÉ & Co (Société Mixte) ayant son siège au Caire

Et: _________________

Demeurant à _________________

profession _________________

Il a été convenu et arrêté ce qui suit:

1° La Raison Sociale Y. ROFÉ & Co. vend au contractant de deuxième part, qui accepte:

**Oblig. Canal de Suez 3% 2ème Série**

(cent les Nos seront désignés à l'este définitif conformément à l'art. 3)

au prix forfaitaire de P.T. _________________
payables de la façon suivante:
P.T. _________________ au comptant, à la signature du présent acte et le solde en _________________
versements de P.T. _________________ chacun dont le dernier de P.T. _________________ échéant le premier de
chaque mois, à partir du mois prochain.

2° Le présent acte préliminaire ne liera la Maison Y. ROFÉ & Co. qu'après confirmation par elle notifiée à l'acheteur par lettre séparée.

Cette lettre concernera les conditions principales du présent contrat.

L'acheteur devra, en cas où il constaterait une différence quelconque entre les conditions du présent contrat et celles qui seront énoncées dans la lettre de confirmation, notifier ses réclamations par lettre recommandée à la Maison Y. ROFÉ & Co., dans les 30 jours qui suivent la date de la lettre de Confirmation. Faute de quel les conditions énoncées dans cette dernière lettre seront irrévocables à l'acheteur.

3° Après paiement du dernier terme du prix convenu, la Maison Y. ROFÉ & Co. remettra à l'acheteur, avec une insertion dûment acquitée qui constituera l'acte définitif de la vente, les titres d'ésisons mentionnés, dont la propriété sera acquise à ce moment seulement et légalement à l'art. 338 du Code Civil Mixte.

La remise des titres sus-dits aura lieu exclusivement au Siège de la Raison Sociale Y. ROFÉ & Co., au Caire, 15 jours après paiement du solde du prix convenu.

L'acheteur devra rendre dans à la Maison Y. ROFÉ & Co. le présent contrat avec toutes les pièces y relatives, accompagnés d'une décharge régulière signée par lui établissant la réception des titres.

payable in the following manner:
P.T. _________________ in cash, upon signature of the present deed and the balance in _________________
installments of P.T. _________________ each, the last of which being P.T. _________________ due on the first of
each month, beginning the following month.

2° The present preliminary deed shall bind the House Y. ROFÉ & Co. only after confirmation by it notified to the purchaser by separate letter.

This letter shall concern the principal conditions of the present contract.

The purchaser shall, in case he should find any difference whatsoever between the conditions of the present contract and those which shall be stated in the confirmation letter, notify his claims by registered letter to the House Y. ROFÉ & Co., within 30 days following the date of the Confirmation letter. In the absence of which the conditions stated in this last letter shall be irrevocable to the purchaser.

title: Deutsches Reich: 8–15% Schatzanweisung, 5,000,000 Mark Reichswährung, Ausgabe II (No. 73723, May 1923)
description: An 8–15% variable-rate Treasury certificate (Schatzanweisung) of the German Reich, Issue II (Ausgabe II), Buchstabe A, No. 73723, for Five Million Mark Reichswährung (5,000,000 M). Issued May 20, 1923, at the height of the German hyperinflation. The certificate was authorized under the Reichsschuldenverwaltung (Imperial Debt Administration). It bears a strip of attached interest coupons, with payment dates marked from 1921 through the early 1930s. The variable interest rate (8–15%) reflected the extreme monetary instability of the Weimar Republic period. By the time of issuance in May 1923, the German mark had already lost the vast majority of its pre-war value; within months, 5,000,000 marks would become essentially worthless, culminating in the hyperinflationary collapse later that year.
creator: Reichsschuldenverwaltung
notes: 8–15% Schatzanweisung des Deutschen Reichs, Ausgabe II, Buchstabe A, No. 73723. 5,000,000 Mark Reichswährung. Issued May 20, 1923. Runs from September 1, 1921; matures December 31, 1930. Attached coupon strip. Weimar hyperinflation era.

Februar bis Ende Juli, in beiden Fällen zinsend auf volle halbe hundertste Teil auf, unter. Der Anspruch hierfür gegen Ausländigung der fälligen Schatzanweisung nach feiner Wahl entwedet bei der Staatschuiden -Tilgungskäffe in Berlin oder außerhalb Berlins durch Vermittlung himländler Reichsbankzentralen oder Reichsbankstellen erfolgt, wenn die Schatzanweisung nicht binnen dreißig Jahren nach dem Fälligkeitstage zur Einlösung vorgelegt wird.

title: Heeren van Zaamslag: Eendracht Polder Bond, £100 Flemish (No. 353, Island of Axel, Zeeland, ca. 18th Century)
description: A handwritten and partly printed bond document No. 353 from the Heeren (Lords) of Zaamslag, as administrators of the newly completed Eendracht Polder reclamation on the island of Axel, Zeeland, Netherlands. The undersigned acknowledge being justly and truly indebted to Pieter Paul van Gelce, or the lawful bearer (wettigen Toonder), in the capital sum of One Hundred Pounds Flemish (£100 Ponden Vlaams), received as a loan for financing the polder and its operations. Interest at 3% per annum (à 3 per Cento vry Geld jaarlyks), payable on demand. Holders of the bond are entitled to participate by lot in the profits of the polder's agricultural produce. An early instrument of Dutch land reclamation finance, reflecting the sophisticated capital markets supporting 18th-century Zeeland water management.
creator: Heeren van Zaamslag
notes: Eendracht Polder bond, No. 353. Heeren van Zaamslag, island of Axel, Zeeland. Capital: £100 Flemish at 3% p.a. Issued to Pieter Paul van Gelce or bearer. Holders entitled to participate in polder produce profits distributed by lot. Dutch land reclamation finance, ca. 18th century.
transcription:
# Transcription

**Original (Dutch):**

[Handwritten at top, partially illegible]
Shat Engel 28 Stanj Gormo July 1779
[Additional handwritten text, unclear]

No. 153.

Wy Ondergeschreven als Heeren van ZAAMSLAG, en Geinteresfecrden in de voltrokken nieuwe Dykagie genaamd *den Eendracht Polder*, in den Eilande van *Axel*, bekennen en verklaren wel en deugdelyk schuldig te wezen aan [handwritten] *Dhr. Pieter Paul van Gelce* — of wettigen Toonder dezes, Eene Capitale Somme van *Een Honderd Ponden Vlaams*, ipruitende ter zake van deugdelyk geleende en op Intrest vertrekkte Penningen, by Ons in voornaemde qualiteit ontfangen en genoten; renunciërende derhalven van de exceptie van onaagetelden gelde; en zulx om die Penningen benevens meerdere Capitaalen tot voltrekking van boveugemelde Bedyking met de dependentien van dien te emploiëeren: Beloovende derhalven het voorschreve Capitaal van £ 100:--:-- met daarenboven den Intrest van dien à 3 per Cento vry Geld jaarlyx en langer loopende na rato des tyds, te zuillen betalen en aflossfen aan voorschreve

*Heere Dhr. Pieter Paul van Gelce* — of zyn Actie verkregen hebbende, by Loting tegen meerdere Capitaalen uit de Producten, welke uit voorschreve Dykagie ftaan te profitueeren, of by ommelagen op de Landen van dien, daar toe fpecitaal van tyd tot tyd te doen. Verbindende Wy ondergeschreven in meergelmelde qualiteit tot verzekering der voldoening en restitutie van voorschreve Capitaal en

---

## English Translation:

We, the undersigned, as Lords of ZAAMSLAG, and interested parties in the completed new Dyke work called *the Eendracht Polder*, in the Island of *Axel*, acknowledge and declare that we are justly and truly indebted to [handwritten] *Mr. Pieter Paul van Gelce* — or the lawful Bearer hereof, in a Capital Sum of *One Hundred Pounds Flemish*, arising from monies justly loaned and furnished on Interest, received and accepted by Us in the aforementioned capacity; renouncing thereby the exception of uncounted money; and so that those Monies together with further Capital may be applied to the completion of the aforementioned Dike works with their dependencies to be employed: Promising thereby that the aforementioned Capital of £100:--:-- together with the Interest thereof at 3 per Cent in proper Currency annually and for a longer term in proportion to the time, shall be paid and redeemed to the aforementioned

*Mr. Pieter Paul van Gelce* — or his assignee, by Lottery against further Capitals from the Products which from the aforementioned Dike work shall be able to profit, or by assessment on the Lands thereof, which shall be done specially from time to time. Binding ourselves, the undersigned, in the aforementioned capacity to the security of the payment and restitution of the aforementioned Capital and

---

## Notable Markings:

- Document number: **No. 153** (printed at top center)
- Handwritten inscription at top (partially illegible, dated July 1779)
- Handwritten name insertion: *Dhr. Pieter Paul van Gelce* (appears twice, indicating the bond holder's name)
- Capital amount in italics: *£ 100*
- Polder name in italics: *den Eendracht Polder*
- Location in italics: *Axel*
- Interest rate clearly stated: **à 3 per Cento vry Geld jaarlyks** (3% per annum in proper Currency annually)

title: Emprunt Russe 4% Or, Sixième Émission 1894: Coupon Sheet (Talon), 187 Roubles 50 Cop. (No. X035911)
description: A large sheet of detachable interest coupons (talon) for the Russian Imperial Government 4% Gold Loan, Sixth Issue of 1894. The obligation denomination is 187 roubles 50 kopeks (equivalent to 500 French francs, £20, 405 Reichsmarks, or $96.25). Serial No. X035911. The sheet contains approximately 20 detachable coupons numbered in sequence (approximately coupons 97–116), each representing a semi-annual interest installment. Text is primarily in Russian, with French headers identifying the loan. Each coupon bears the coupon number, payment amount, and payment date. The 1894 Russian 4% Gold Loan was one of the largest sovereign bond issues of the late 19th century, placed across European financial markets.

title: Gouvernement Impérial de Chine: Emprunt Chinois 5% Or 1903, Obligation de 500 Francs (No. 30659, Brussels, April 12, 1907)
description: A 500-franc bearer bond (Obligation au Porteur) No. 30659 issued by the Imperial Chinese Government under its 5% Gold Loan of 1903 (Emprunt Chinois 5% Or 1903). Issued in Brussels on April 12, 1907. The total loan capital was 60,000,000 francs; interest at 5% per annum, payable semi-annually in gold. The bond bears Chinese imperial characters (seals and authorization signatures) alongside French text, with attached coupon stubs for future interest payments. The loan was placed through European banking syndicates and guaranteed by the revenues of Chinese Imperial Customs. Part of the series of late Qing dynasty foreign loans taken to finance government expenditures, the 1903 Chinese Gold Loan is an important example of early 20th-century Chinese sovereign borrowing in European capital markets.

# Transcription

**Original (French):**

GOUVERNEMENT IMPÉRIAL DE CHINE

EMPRUNT CHINOIS
5% OR 1903

AU CAPITAL DE 25.000.000 DE FRANCS
Représenté par 50.000 Obligations de 500 Francs

Émis en vertu de l'Édit de S.M. l'Empereur de Chine du 20 Octobre 1903.

OBLIGATION DE CINQ CENTS FRANCS
AU PORTEUR

N° 30,659

Reportant les intérêts annuels de 25 francs, payables par semestre le 1er Janvier et le 1er Juillet de chaque année,
remboursable au pair en 40 années par tirage au sort et conversion à la Banque de France et autres banques désignées

LE PREMIER TIRAGE AURA LIEU LE TROISIÈME MERCREDI D'AVRIL 1913

Le présent Emprunt est destiné exclusivement à l'établissement d'une ligne de Chemin de fer entre Kaifong-Fou
et Honan-Fou, villes situées dans la province du Honan.

L'Ordre l'Obligation Générale du Gouvernement Impérial Chinois, en Emprunt, de même que celui dont la création
est prescrite par le Décret Impérial du 20 Octobre 1903, sera organisé en exploitation, à une garantie
spéciale de premier rang sur la ligne de Chemin de fer, matériel roulant et ses et produits.

Le Ministère Plénipotentiaire de Chine à Bruxelles s'est spécialement chargé de signer les titres et de en révéler de
son.

Les Titres sont exempts, à tout jamais, de tout impôt Chinois, présent et à venir, ainsi que de tout autre
prélèvement de la part de l'Etat de Chine.

Le paiement des intérêts et des titres sortis se fera à Bruxelles et à Paris.

Bruxelles, le 12 Avril 1905.

POUR LE GOUVERNEMENT IMPÉRIAL CHINOIS :

LE DIRECTEUR GÉNÉRAL DES CHEMINS DE FER CHINOIS,          LE MINISTRE PLÉNIPOTENTIAIRE DE CHINE À BRUXELLES,

Par délégation de la Compagnie Générale
des Chemins de fer
et du Min. des Finances.

[Signatures and Chinese characters present]

**English Translation:**

IMPERIAL GOVERNMENT OF CHINA

CHINESE LOAN
5% GOLD 1903

WITH A CAPITAL OF 25,000,000 FRANCS
Represented by 50,000 Bonds of 500 Francs

Issued by virtue of the Edict of H.M. the Emperor of China of October 20, 1903.

OBLIGATION OF FIVE HUNDRED FRANCS
TO BEARER

No. 30,659

Bearing annual interest of 25 francs, payable semi-annually on January 1st and July 1st of each year,
redeemable at par over 40 years by drawing and conversion at the Bank of France and other designated banks.

THE FIRST DRAWING SHALL TAKE PLACE ON THE THIRD WEDNESDAY OF APRIL 1913

This Loan is intended exclusively for the establishment of a railway line between Kaifeng and
Honan-Fu, cities located in Honan Province.

The General Obligation of the Imperial Government of China, in Loan, as well as that which the creation
is prescribed by the Imperial Decree of October 20, 1903, shall be organized in operation, with a special
guarantee of first rank on the railway line, rolling stock and its products.

The Minister Plenipotentiary of China in Brussels has been specially charged with signing the titles and verifying them.

The Titles are exempt, for all time, from all Chinese taxes, present and future, as well as from any other
levies on the part of the Chinese State.

Payment of interest and redeemed bonds shall be made in Brussels and Paris.

Brussels, April 12, 1905.

FOR THE IMPERIAL GOVERNMENT OF CHINA:

THE GENERAL DIRECTOR OF CHINESE RAILWAYS,          THE MINISTER PLENIPOTENTIARY OF CHINA IN BRUSSELS,

title: Российское Императорское Правительство: 4% Золотой Заём, 6-й Выпуск 1894 г., Облигация 125 Рублей Золотом (No. 59944)
description: An ornate Russian Imperial Government 4% Gold Loan bearer bond, Sixth Issue of 1894 (Российский Четырехпроцентный Золотой Заём, Шестой Выпуск 1894 года). Obligation No. 59944, denomination 125 Rubles in gold (equivalent to 500 French francs), 6th emission. The bond features the Imperial Russian double-headed eagle at center and an elaborate decorative printed border. Text in Russian. The loan was authorized under the Imperial ukase of February 3, 1894, for a total of 125,000,000 rubles at 4% interest, redeemable by annual drawings over 50 years. Interest payable at the major financial centers of Europe: St. Petersburg, Moscow, Paris (via Crédit Lyonnais), London (Hope and Baring), Amsterdam (Lippmann), Berlin, Frankfurt, Hamburg, and Vienna.

title: Leib-Renten-Lotterie der Stadt Hamburg: Lottery Ticket No. 7027, 200 Mark Banco (Hamburg, April 23, 1773)
description: A printed lottery ticket No. 7027 for the Life Annuity Lottery (Leib-Renten-Lotterey) of the City of Hamburg, published on April 23, 1773. The bearer of this ticket holds one lot in the life annuity lottery, credited under the noted number in the Kämmerei Lotterie-Conto in Banco for the sum of Two Hundred Mark Banco (Zwey Hundert Mark Banco). Printed with the Hamburg city coat of arms (two white towers on a red field) at top. The Hamburg Life Annuity Lottery was a form of municipal finance that combined lottery participation with life annuity-style returns: participants purchased lottery tickets, and winners received life annuities paid from Hamburg's municipal treasury. Such instruments were a common and sophisticated method of city-state borrowing in 18th-century northern Europe.
creator: City of Hamburg (Kämmerei)
notes: Leib-Renten-Lotterey der Stadt Hamburg. No. 7027, 200 Mark Banco. Published April 23, 1773. Credited in Kämmerei Lotterie-Conto in Banco. Life annuity lottery; Hamburg city coat of arms. Municipal finance instrument.

title: Oliver Wolcott, Comptroller of Public Accounts (Connecticut): Interest Receipt No. 2435, £1 15s 3d (Hartford, April 4, 1789)
description: A printed and handwritten interest receipt No. 2435 issued by Oliver Wolcott, Comptroller of the Public Accounts of Connecticut, acknowledging payment of One Pound Fifteen Shillings and Three Pence Lawful Money (£1 15s 3d) to John Douglas. The payment represents interest on four State Notes with a principal of £29.6.4½, computed to the first of February 1789. Issued at the Comptroller's Office, Hartford, Connecticut, April 4th, 1789. Signed by Stephen Crosby (or similar). Oliver Wolcott Sr. served as Comptroller of Connecticut's public accounts during the post-Revolutionary War period and later as U.S. Secretary of the Treasury. This receipt documents Connecticut's efforts to service its Revolutionary War debt through regular interest payments to note holders.
creator: Oliver Wolcott, Comptroller of Public Accounts, Connecticut

title: Negotiatie van de Compagnie Française Lijfrente: Subscription Record, Nominees Nos. 30–59 (Amsterdam, May 1, 1787)
description: A printed document dated May 1, 1787, compiled before Notaries Abraham Fock and Adriaan Schaap as Commissioners, listing nominees Nos. 30 through 59 for the Compagnie Française Lijfrente Negotiatie van Maart 1781. The nominees are predominantly women, registered under numbered entries. The Dutch negotiatie on French life annuities was a popular investment vehicle of the 1780s whereby Dutch investors purchased shares in a pooled fund that in turn bought French royal life annuities (rentes viagères), selecting young female nominees to maximize the expected payment duration. Annuity income accumulated for surviving nominees' benefit, concentrating payments among the longest-lived. The document records the official register of nominees as at May 1, 1787, serving as proof of each nominee's enrollment in the annuity pool.
creator: Abraham Fock; Adriaan Schaap (Commissioners)
notes: Subscription list Nos. 30–59 for the Compagnie Française Lijfrente Negotiatie van Maart 1781. Amsterdam, May 1, 1787. Commissioners: Abraham Fock and Adriaan Schaap. Nominees predominantly women. Dutch negotiatie on French royal life annuities (rentes viagères).

title: Hope & Comp., Ketwich & Voombergh, en Weduwe Willem Borski: Certificaat der Vijfde Reeks, 5% Russische Fondsen in Zilver, 500 Roubles (No. 13, Amsterdam, August 29, 1834)
description: A certificaat (certificate) of the Fifth Series (Vijfde Reeks) of 5% Russian Funds in Silver (Russische Fondsen in Zilver), No. 13, for a capital of 500 Roubles, issued in Amsterdam on August 29, 1834, by the leading Dutch banking houses Hope & Comp., Ketwich & Voombergh, and Weduwe Willem Borski (Widow Willem Borski). Interest at 5% per annum, payable on April 1 and October 1, administered through the Notariskantoor Commelin & Weyland. The last coupon shown is April 1, 1839. These Dutch certificates facilitated retail Dutch investment in Russian government silver bonds without direct ownership of the underlying Russian instruments, a common Amsterdam intermediation practice. Hope & Company was one of Europe's most prominent banking houses and played a central role in placing Russian government debt in Western markets.
creator: Hope & Comp.; Ketwich & Voombergh; Weduwe Willem Borski
notes: Certificaat der Vijfde Reeks, 5% Russische Fondsen in Zilver. No. 13, 500 Roubles. Amsterdam, August 29, 1834. Issued by Hope & Comp., Ketwich & Voombergh, Weduwe Willem Borski. Interest 5% p.a., April 1 and October 1. Last coupon April 1, 1839.
transcription:
# Transcription

**Original (Dutch):**

---

CERTIFICAAT
DER VIJFDE REEKS,
van 5 per cent Russische Fondsen
IN ZILVER.

fl. [handwritten: 1/2]

HOUDER dezes is voor een Kapitaal van VIJF HONDERD ROEBELS, geregistrigd tot den mede-eigendom van oorspronkelijke inschuijvingen van Russische Fondijen in ZILVER, à 5 per cent Interest, betaalbaar alle 2de MAANDEN op 1s April en 1s October, ingevolge Regtsgeltje Uittase in dato 2 Juny 1834, welke door het Kantoor van Administratie onder Directie van

HOPE & COMP.,
KETWICH & VOOMBERGH,
EN
WEDUWE WILLEM BORSKI,

zijn gedeponneerd ten Kantore van de Notarissen COMMELIN & WEYLAND.

De Intereesen verschillende 1 April en 1 October, zullen voor rekening en risico van den Houder ontvangen en uitbetaald worden op COUPONS, overeenkomstig Art. 6 van het Bewust, in dato 30 Juny 1824.

De HOUDER of HOUDERS dozer Certificaten, zullen ten allen tijde geregtigd zijn, de Certificaten, welke zij tegen overdracht, tegen intrekking der CERTIFICATEN met de Ontverschenen Coupons het Subject tot zijne in Maart van [Nieuwe Coupons] daaraoe herhoudende, en tegen betaling van de onkosten in Art. 10 van het BEWUST vermeld, mits de Certificaten wij worden ingeleverd bij partijen van 10 stuks, representeerende een Kapitaal van Z. R. 5000.

Amsterdam den 29 Augustus 1834

[Signatures - illegible handwriting]

Serijn by my
Commelin
Notaris.

[Additional signatures]

Hierbij afgegeven Geldparse Coupons la 1s April 1839 en Talon.

---

## English Translation:

**CERTIFICATE
OF THE FIFTH SERIES,
of 5 per cent Russian Funds
IN SILVER.**

fl. [handwritten: 1/2]

THE HOLDER hereof holds for a Capital of FIVE HUNDRED ROUBLES, registered as joint owner of original subscriptions of Russian Funds in SILVER, at 5 per cent Interest, payable every 2 MONTHS on 1st April and 1st October, according to Legal Deed of 2 June 1834, which has been deposited at the Office of Administration under the Direction of

HOPE & COMP.,
KETWICH & VOOMBERGH,
AND
WIDOW WILLEM BORSKI,

are deposited at the Office of the Notaries COMMELIN & WEYLAND.

The Interest accruing from 1 April and 1 October shall be received and paid out to the Holder at his risk and cost on COUPONS, in accordance with Art. 6 of the Deed, dated 30 June 1824.

The HOLDER or HOLDERS of this Certificate shall at all times be entitled, the Certificates which they have against transfer, against the redemption of CERTIFICATES with unpaid Coupons, the Subject to its right in March of [New Coupons] thereunto binding, and against payment of the costs mentioned in Art. 10 of the DEED, provided the Certificates are delivered by parties of 10 pieces, representing a Capital of Z. R. 5000.

Amsterdam, 29 August 1834

title: Certificaat No. 1475: Dutch Certificate for Swedish Crown Debt Negotiated in Holland (Amsterdam, October 10, 1816)
description: A Dutch certificaat No. 1475 evidencing the holder's position as a creditor in the Swedish Crown (Zweedsche Kroon) debt negotiated in Holland. The holder, W.G. van de Poll, has paid the first three installments toward repayment of debts owed by His Majesty the King of Sweden to Dutch creditors, with an agreed schedule to pay a fixed percentage of the nominal capital per year. Nominal capital of approximately 3,000 Dutch guilders remains partly outstanding. The certificate also records that remaining portions of the Swedish government obligations (Lijfrenten en Obligatiën ten laste de Zweedsche Kroon) are to be distributed among creditors. Issued in Amsterdam on October 10, 1816, and signed by W.G. van de Poll. Reflects the post-Napoleonic restructuring of Swedish sovereign debt held by Dutch investors.
creator: W.G. van de Poll
notes: Certificaat No. 1475. Dutch certificate for Swedish Crown debt in Holland. Holder: W.G. van de Poll. First three installments paid; remaining nominal capital ~3,000 Dutch guilders. Amsterdam, October 10, 1816. Post-Napoleonic Swedish debt restructuring.
transcription:
# Transcription

**Original (Dutch):**

N°. 1475

CERTIFICAAT.

Houder dezes, ten gevolge van het dépôt van de hier onder gespecificeerde [*handwritten: Mtg ahi*], ten lasten der Zweedsche Kroon in Holland genegotieerd, groot [*handwritten: Jielduizen*] [*handwritten: Drie duizend Guilden*] nominal Kapitaal, ontvangen hebbende de, uit de betaling der drie eerste termijnen, van het geen van weegens Zijne Majesteit den Koning van Zweden tot kwijting dezer schulden in Holland is aangenomen te voldoen, bepaalde repartitie van vijftien percent van gemeld nominal Kapitaal, blijft als nu nog verder geregtigd, om, in evenredigheid tot het zelve nominal Kapitaal Zweedsche [*handwritten: Mtg ahi*] te deelen, in het geen, na uitbetaling van weegens het Zweedsche Gouvernement der drie overige termijnen, russchen de houders der Lijfrenten en Obligatiën ten lasten de Zweedsche Kroon zal blijken te kunnen worden verdeeld.

Zijnde dit Certificaat, ook namens de Heer *W. G. van de Poll*, ingevolge door denzelven ten dien einde special verleende qualificatie, geteekend en afgegeven in Amsterdam den 10. [*handwritten: Oktbr*] – 1816, door [*signature*]
[*handwritten: hag. batavus 10.10.bre 10. 10.1816.*]

SPECIFICATIE:

[*handwritten: g 0.M. twee L°° 1199. g223 & 5043.*]

---

## English Translation:

The holder hereof, in consequence of the deposit of the hereby specified [*amount*], charged to the Swedish Crown negotiated in Holland, for the amount of [*Three Thousand Guilders*] nominal Capital, having received, from the payment of the first three installments, of that which His Majesty the King of Sweden has undertaken to pay in Holland for the discharge of these debts, the determined distribution of fifteen percent of said nominal Capital, remains now further entitled to share, in proportion to the same nominal Capital of the Swedish [*amount*], in that which, after payment by the Swedish Government of the three remaining installments, between the holders of Annuities and Obligations charged to the Swedish Crown shall appear to be able to be distributed.

Being this Certificate, also in the name of Mr. *W. G. van de Poll*, pursuant to the special qualification granted by the same for that purpose, signed and issued in Amsterdam on 10th [*October*] – 1816, by [*signature*]
[*The Hague, October 10, 1816.*]

SPECIFICATION:

[*handwritten: g 0.M. two L°° 1199. g223 & 5043.*]

---

- Document number (top left): **N°. 1475**
- **Signatures**: Present at bottom of document (illegible in reproduction)
- **Handwritten annotations**: Multiple insertions in cursive script throughout, indicating specific amounts and dates
- **Handwritten date notation**: "hag. batavus 10.10.bre 10. 10.1816" (The Hague, October 10, 1816)
- **Handwritten specification**: Numerical notation at bottom of document

title: Imperial Chinese Government Gold Loan of 1908: Bond for £20 Sterling / Obligation de £20 Sterling (No. B106822, Paris, March 15, 1908)
description: A bilingual English/French bearer bond No. B106822 for £20 Sterling (Obligation de £20 Sterling) issued by the Imperial Chinese Government under the Gold Loan of 1908 (Emprunt Or de 1908), with a total issue of £5,000,000 Sterling. Issued in Paris on March 15, 1908, through the Banque de l'Indo-Chine. Printed in green with an ornate decorative border and vignette showing Chinese harbor and railway scenes. The loan was backed by the revenues of the Chinese Imperial Maritime Customs and other government revenues. Interest and principal payable in London, Paris, Berlin, and other European financial centers. Part of the series of late Qing dynasty foreign loans; the 1908 Gold Loan financed railway construction and government expenditures during a period of rapid Chinese modernization effort.
creator: Imperial Chinese Government; Banque de l'Indo-Chine
notes: Imperial Chinese Government Gold Loan 1908. £5,000,000 total. Bond No. B106822, £20 Sterling. Paris, March 15, 1908. Banque de l'Indo-Chine. Bilingual English/French. Green ornate border. Backed by Chinese Imperial Customs revenues. Late Qing railway finance.

title: Hollandsche Garantie- & Trust Compagnie: 6% Certificaat van Inschrijving in het Grootboek van het Duitsche Rijk (Reichsschuldbuch), 2000 Reichsmark (No. 0128, Amsterdam, November 1928)
description: A 6% Certificate of Registration in the German National Debt Register (Groot Boek van het Duitsche Rijk / Reichsschuldbuch), No. 0128, for 2,000 Reichsmark, Series per 1943 (maturing March 31, 1943). Issued by the Hollandsche Garantie- & Trust Compagnie in Amsterdam, November 1928. The certificate represents a 6% inscription in the Reichsschuldbuch, registered in the name of the Hollandsche Garantie- & Trust Compagnie as trustee for Dutch investors, with conditions authorized and witnessed by Notary B.H. Grepels. Orange Dutch tax stamp visible. Issued during the Weimar Republic's period of currency stabilization, when the German government attracted foreign capital through long-term bond issues following the hyperinflation crisis. This Dutch-issued certificate intermediated German sovereign debt for retail Dutch investors.
creator: Hollandsche Garantie- & Trust Compagnie
notes: 6% Certificaat van Inschrijving, Grootboek Duitsche Rijk (Reichsschuldbuch). No. 0128, 2,000 Reichsmark, Series per 1943. Amsterdam, November 1928. Hollandsche Garantie- & Trust Compagnie. Notary B.H. Grepels. Dutch investment vehicle for Weimar-era German government bonds.

title: Koninklijke Nederlandsche Maatschappij tot Exploitatie van Petroleumbronnen in Nederlandsch-Indië: Warrant No. 015284 (Series per 1943, 's-Gravenhage, February 1937)
description: A stock warrant (Warrant) No. 015284, Series per 1943, of the Koninklijke Nederlandsche Maatschappij tot Exploitatie van Petroleumbronnen in Nederlandsch-Indië (Royal Dutch Company for the Exploitation of Petroleum Sources in Dutch East Indies), established in The Hague. The holder has the right until March 31, 1940 to purchase one share at f.1,000 nominal value from the above company, or from April 1, 1940 until March 31, 1943 against payment of f.500. Valid until March 31, 1943 (van onwaarde na 31 Maart 1943). Issued 's-Gravenhage, February 1937. Signed by two directors. Royal Dutch was the Dutch predecessor entity of the Royal Dutch/Shell oil company. This warrant was part of the company's equity capital-raising activities in the late 1930s, reflecting the complex financial structure of the early petroleum industry.

title: Compagnie des Indes: Legal Authorization Document for Share Transfer and Lottery Proceeds, Paris (January 13, 1745)
description: A printed and handwritten legal document dated January 13, 1745, Paris, relating to the French East India Company (Compagnie des Indes). The document references Gabriel-Jérôme de Bullion (Chevalier, Maître de Camp de Régiment de Prusse Infanterie, Conseiller du Roy en son Conseil, Prévôt de la Ville, Prévôté et Vicomté de Paris), acting as Directeur de ladite Compagnie. The text references the King's Council edict of February 3, 1724 (re-establishing the reorganized Compagnie des Indes), and concerns the handling of Loterie de Saint-Quentin proceeds and share-related financial arrangements. Multiple named parties are involved, including Dame Juliane Elizabeth de Guise Holler and her husband or agent, regarding the sale, transfer, and remuneration of Company shares and related financial instruments under the Compagnie des Indes charter. A significant document illustrating the legal and financial complexity of French East India Company share transactions in the mid-18th century.
creator: Compagnie des Indes (French East India Company)
notes: Compagnie des Indes legal document, January 13, 1745, Paris. Director: Gabriel-Jérôme de Bullion. Concerns share transfers and Loterie de Saint-Quentin proceeds. References King's Council edict February 3, 1724. Parties include Dame Juliane Elizabeth de Guise Holler.
transcription:
# Transcription of Document

**Original (French):**

13 Janvier 1745.

[Top left seal: "GEN. DE PARIS"]
[Top right seal: ornamental design with text]

À Tous ceux qui ces presentes Lettres verront, Gabriel-Jérôme de Bullion, Chevalier Comte Defchilmont, Maistre de Camp du Regiment de Provence Infanterie, Conseiller du Roy en son Conseil, Prevôt de la Ville, Prevôté & Vicomté de Paris, Sçavoir faisons que pardevant M [illegible] Conseillers du Roy, Notaires, Gardes-notres & Gardes-[unclear] de Sa Majestè au Chastelet de Paris, soullignez, furent presens. [handwritten: unclear names]

Syndie de la Compagnie des Indes, Et [handwritten] à Moucharsard [unclear]

Directeurs de ladite Compagnie; lesquels en vertu de l'Arrêt du Conseil d'État du Roy du 15. Février 1724. par lequel Sa Majestè a accordè à ladite Compagnie le Privilege Exclusif de faire dans l'estendue du Royaume, differentes Loteries; Et en execution de la Declaration du 13. Decembre audit an, prise dans l'Assemblée generale d'Administration de ladite Compagnie, au sujet de la Loterie composée de Lots payables, les uns en Argent, & les autres en Rentes Viageres, qui doivent former un corps de fontine de Vingt-mille sept cens cinquante Numero, chacun d'eux, susceptible d'accroissement, à mesures des decès des Rentiers irreallez à ladite Loterie; favoir, pour leur à profit de ladite Compagnie. Et pour l'autre moitié à ceux des Rentiers survivans, que le sort preferera dans les Loteries qui doivent estre annuellement & successivement. Lesquelles Rentes Viageres ont le ront assignées sur le fonds de ladite Compagnie, lesdits Sieurs Syndic & Directeurs ont pouvoir de continuer en profit des Suites de Sa Majestè, que des Arrangers, auxquels le payement des Arrangess & des Accroissemens d'oit estre fait, quand même ils refideraient hors du Royaume, & même en tems de guerre, fans que lesdites Rentes puissent estre retranchées ou efisgées pour quelque cause que ce soit, pas même pour l'affaires de Sa Majestè, conformement à l'Arrêt du 26. Juin 1724. ains qu'il est plus au long porté par ladite Publication; par laquelle il est permis aux Acqueireurs desdites Rentes de passer les Contrats fous le nom de telles personnes qu'il voudront choisir, pour ou joüir, sans que par eux, que per ceux qu'ils nommeront fur les Quittances, pendant la vie desdites personnes fondées fur lyl desdites Constitutions auront cinq miles. Ont lesdits Sieurs Syndic & Directeurs comparu, en vertu desdites Declarations & Arrests du Conseil d'État & Fevrier & 26 Juin 1724. vendu, creé & constitué, afis & assigné par ces presentes; Et permis pour & au nom de ladite Compagnie des Indes, garantir de tous troubles & autres empechemens generalement quelconques, à [handwritten: unclear]

Royier [handwritten: unclear]
Seigneur &c.

[Handwritten section below - largely illegible due to handwriting and deterioration]

[Lower portion contains more handwritten text regarding transfer of shares and financial arrangements]

Sieurs Quitances, qui ne pourront estre paisées que

---

**Notable markings:**

- **Printed seal (top left):** "GEN. DE PARIS" within ornamental circular frame
- **Printed seal (top right):** Ornamental design with floral/heraldic elements
- **Handwritten annotations:** Throughout document margins and text
- **Date:** "13 Janvier 1745" (13 January 1745) - top left
- **Location:** Paris
- **Multiple signatures:** Present but largely illegible in the handwritten portions
- **Document class marking:** "No. 386." visible on right margin

The document is heavily annotated with 18th-century cursive handwriting, making several portions difficult to read with complete certainty.

title: Liverpool Corn Trade Association, Limited: C. Perpetual Mortgage Debenture No. 291, £100 – Conditions and Transfer Endorsements
description: The reverse side of C. Perpetual Mortgage Debenture No. 291 of the Liverpool Corn Trade Association, Limited, showing the printed conditions (The Conditions within referred to) governing the debenture, and multiple handwritten transfer endorsements recording the ownership history. Transfer records note: transfer dated May 25, 1910 to Ernest Clifford Grasman at 58 Crofton Road, Essex County, registered in the books of the Association in June 1919; a further transfer dated October 9, 1933. The conditions detail the debenture's terms, including interest payment provisions, security over the Association's property, and redemption terms. This reverse side complements the front-face certificate (No. 291), together constituting the complete debenture instrument.

title: Liverpool Corn Trade Association, Limited: C. Perpetual Mortgage Debenture No. 291, £100 at 3½% (Liverpool, June 1897)
description: C. Perpetual Mortgage Debenture No. 291 of the Liverpool Corn Trade Association, Limited, incorporated under the Companies Acts 1862 to 1893. Capital of the Association: £60,000 in 400 Shares of £150 each. Registered Office: 8, Brunswick Street, Liverpool. Issued under authority of Clause 54 of the Articles of Association and a Board resolution dated May 26, 1897, as part of an issue of £100,000 C. Perpetual Mortgage Debentures carrying interest at £3½ per centum per annum, payable on February 1 and August 1. The Association charges all its property, estate, and effects, present and future, as security. The debenture is transferable by endorsement. Given under the Common Seal of the Association, June 1897. Signed by the Chairman, a Director, and the Secretary (Edward Graham). The Liverpool Corn Trade Association was a leading institution in Liverpool's commodities market.
creator: Liverpool Corn Trade Association, Limited
notes: C. Perpetual Mortgage Debenture No. 291, £100 at 3½% p.a. Liverpool Corn Trade Association, Ltd. Issue of £100,000. Capital £60,000 in 400 shares of £150. 8 Brunswick Street, Liverpool. Common Seal, June 1897. Secretary: Edward Graham.
transcription:
# Transcription

THIS DEBENTURE IS ISSUED UNDER THE AUTHORITY OF CLAUSE 54 OF THE ARTICLES OF ASSOCIATION OF THE LIVERPOOL CORN TRADE ASSOCIATION, LIMITED, OF A RESOLUTION OF THE BOARD OF DIRECTORS DATED THE 26TH OF MAY, 1897.

THE

**Liverpool Corn Trade Association,**
LIMITED.

INCORPORATED UNDER THE COMPANIES' ACTS, 1862 TO 1893.

CAPITAL: £60,000, in 400 Shares of £150 Each.

Registered Office:
8, BRUNSWICK STREET, LIVERPOOL.

ISSUE of £100,000 C. PERPETUAL MORTGAGE DEBENTURES, carrying Interest at £3½ per Centum per Annum.

No. 291     C. PERPETUAL MORTGAGE DEBENTURE. £100.

The Liverpool Corn Trade Association, Limited, [acknowledges receipt of] One Hundred Pounds, being part of their Perpetual Mortgage Debenture Stock, and hereby agrees to pay to the bearer or registered holder for the time being the sum of One Hundred Pounds, or other the registered holder for the time being to be made on the 1st day of August.

The Association will, during the continuance of this security, pay to each registered holder interest thereon at the rate of £3½ per centum per annum. By equal half-yearly payments, on the 1st day of February and the 1st day of August, the 1st of such half-yearly payments to be made on the 1st day of August.

The Association doth hereby charge with the said principal money and interest all the present and future property of the Association, both real and personal, including uncalled capital, by way of security to the intent that the "C" Debenture and the other debentures forming part of the above-mentioned authorized issue of £100,000 shall rank, pari passu, as a charge upon the said property and future property of the Association, called only in the event of the Association being unable to pay the 1st and £10,000 in Debentures which are charged upon the said property in priority to the Debentures respectively named.

The Debenture is issued subject to and with the benefit of the conditions endorsed hereon, which are as to hereinafter set out.

**Given** under the Common Seal of the Association,

this 16th day of June, 1897

[Signatures present but largely illegible - appear to be three signatories]

**Notable markings:**

- Official stamp/seal (orange/red colored) in top right corner
- Decorative ornamental border frame around entire document
- Three signatures at bottom (members of Board of Directors)
- Purple circular stamp/seal visible in center-right area
- Serial number "No. 291" at bottom left
- Red pen mark near one signature

title: London Certificatie: Dutch Notarial Certificate for Bank of England 3% Consolidated Annuities (London, ca. late 18th Century)
description: A handwritten Dutch notarial certificate (London Certificatie) by Pieter Hendrik Hoogenbergh, a Dutch public notary authorized and admitted in London. The notary certifies, at the request of the named holder, that the individual is duly registered in the Grand Ledger of Transfers and Accounts of Shares in the Consolidated Capital of Three Per Cent Bank Annuities (Drie Per Cent Bank Annuïteiten) at the Bank of England, in the holder's own name. Authenticated with a red wax seal bearing the notary's heraldic device. Concluded "In Testimonium Veritatis" with the notary's signature. Not. Pub. No. 7. Issued in London, anno Seventien Honderd [?]. This type of certificate was routinely used by Dutch investors in British government consols (3% Consolidated Annuities) to certify and authenticate their holdings for purposes of Dutch probate, tax, or legal proceedings.

title: United States of America: Bill of Exchange No. 57, $60 (300 Livres Tournois) for Interest on Loan Office Certificates (November 21, 1778)
description: A printed and handwritten bill of exchange No. 57, issued by the United States of America on November 21, 1778. At Thirty Days Sight, payable to Jeremiah Green Esq. (or Order) in Three Hundred Livres Tournois (equivalent to Sixty Dollars), for Interest due on Money borrowed by the United States. Countersigned by Nath. Appleton, Commissary of the Loan Office in the State of Massachusetts Bay. Signed by H. Atkinson, Treasurer of Loans. These bills of exchange were issued by the Continental Congress as a mechanism for paying interest to domestic holders of Loan Office Certificates, allowing creditors to receive their interest in the form of bills that could be negotiated in France, reflecting the U.S. government's reliance on French credit during the Revolutionary War.
creator: United States of America, Loan Office, Massachusetts Bay
notes: US Bill of Exchange No. 57, $60 / 300 Livres Tournois. November 21, 1778. Payable to Jeremiah Green Esq. Interest on Loan Office Certificates. Countersigned by Nath. Appleton, Commissary; signed by H. Atkinson, Treasurer of Loans. Massachusetts Bay. Continental Revolutionary War finance.

title: United States of America: Bill of Exchange No. 814, $24 (120 Livres Tournois) for Interest on Loan Office Certificates (November 24, 1778)
description: A printed and handwritten bill of exchange No. 814, issued by the United States of America on November 24, 1778. At Thirty Days Sight, payable to Timothy Newell Esq. (or Order) in One Hundred and Twenty Livres Tournois (equivalent to Twenty-four Dollars), for Interest due on Money borrowed by the United States. Countersigned by Nath. Appleton, Comptroller of the Commissioner of the Great Loan-Office in the State of Massachusetts Bay. Signed by H. Atkinson, Treasurer of Loans. Illustrates the systematic use of Livres Tournois-denominated bills of exchange to service domestic Revolutionary War debt through French currency instruments.

title: State of Massachusetts Bay: Interest-Bearing State Note, £230, Payable to Elisha Curtis (January 1, 1780)
description: An ornately printed state note from the State of Massachusetts Bay, dated the First Day of January, A.D. 1780. Issued in behalf of the State, the subscriber (as Committee member) promises to pay Elisha Curtis, or his or her Order, the Sum of Two Hundred and Thirty Pounds, bearing interest at six per cent per annum, with interest payable on the first Mondays of January, April, July, and October each year. Issued pursuant to a Law of the State titled "An Act to create a suitable Fund" authorizing additional taxation to service state obligations. The principal is recorded as redeemable only by application in person or by attorney. Signed by William [Cranch] and a second Committee member, with a Treasurer's signature. A key instrument of Massachusetts war finance during the Revolutionary period, when the State issued a variety of interest-bearing certificates to fund its military and governmental expenditures.
creator: State of Massachusetts Bay
notes: State of Massachusetts Bay interest-bearing state note, £230. Payable to Elisha Curtis. January 1, 1780. Interest 6% p.a., payable quarterly. Pursuant to Act authorizing additional taxation. Signed by Committee and Treasurer. Revolutionary War Massachusetts state finance.

title: Mexican Five Per Cent Deferred Stock: Bond with Full Coupon Sheet (London, mid-19th Century)
description: A large printed bearer bond certificate for the Mexican Five Per Cent Deferred Stock, featuring an elaborate decorative border and the Mexican national eagle vignette at top center. The bond includes a complete attached coupon sheet on the right side. The Mexican 5% Deferred Stock was one of the principal classes of Mexican external debt created through the conversion and restructuring of Mexico's London-market obligations, administered through the Council of the United Mexican States in London. The "deferred" designation indicates that interest payments were suspended and would recommence upon specified conditions being met. The bond includes an extensive text detailing the terms of the loan, the conditions of payment, and the security offered. The Mexican Deferred Stock was traded on the London Stock Exchange and was the subject of prolonged default, diplomatic disputes, and multiple renegotiations throughout the mid-to-late 19th century.
creator: Mexican Government; Council of the United Mexican States (London)
notes: Mexican 5% Deferred Stock. Large format bearer bond with Mexican eagle vignette, ornate border, and full attached coupon sheet. Part of Mexican external debt restructuring, London market, mid-19th century. Extensive bond indenture text included.

# Transcription

**No.** [number]

**STATE TREASURERS BOND**

*The First Day of JANUARY, A.D. 1780.*

IN behalf of the State of Massachusetts-Bay, I the Subscriber do hereby promise and oblige Myself and Successors in the Office of TREASURER of said STATE, to pay unto **Josias Curtis** —or to his Order, the SUM of **Two hundred fifty five pounds**

on or before the **FIRST DAY OF MARCH**, in the Year of our Lord One Thousand Seven Hundred and **eighty three** with Interest at **Six per Cent. psr Annum**; Both Principal and Interest to be paid in the then current Money of said STATE, in a greater or less SUM, According as Five Bushels of **CORN**, Sixty-eight Pounds and four-sevenths Parts of a Pound of **BEEF**, Ten Pounds of **SHEEPS WOOL**, and Sixteen Pounds of **SOLE LEATHER**, shall then cost, more or less than One Hundred and Thirty Pounds current Money, at the then current Prices of said Articles.—This Sum being Thirty-Times and an Half what the fame Quantities of the fame Articles would coft at the Prices affixed to them in a L[a]w of this STATE made in the Year of our Lord One Thousand Seven Hundred and Seventy-seven, intitled, "*An Act to prevent Monopoly and Oppression.*" The current Prices of said Articles, and the confequent Value of every Pound of the Sum herein promised, to be determined agreeable to a L[a]W of the STATE intitled, " **An ACT to provide for the Security and Payment of the Balances that may appear to be due by Virtue of a Resolution of the GENERAL ASSEMBLY of the Sixth of** *February* **One Thousand Seven Hundred and Seventy-nine, to this STATE's Quota of the CONTINENTAL ARMY**, agreeable to the Recommendation of **CONGRESS**, and for Supplying the TREASURERY with a Sum of Money for that Purpose."

Witness my Hand.

[Signature] *Treasurer*

**Middling Committee's**
[illegible signature]

title: Share Subscription Contract for the Generale Keijserlijke Indische Compagnie (Antwerp, April 30, 1608)
description: A handwritten share subscription and forward contract for the Generale Keijserlijke Indische Compagnie (General Imperial Indian Company), signed in Antwerp on April 30, 1608. Registered as No. 36. The subscriber promises, in exchange for a received cash premium (Contante premie), to deliver and transfer a specified number of shares (Actien) in the Generale Keijserlijke Indische Compagnie to the counterparty or their order by the first available transfer day at the Company, with conditions relating to transport costs, reimbursement of proceeds, and the value of the shares. The document bears an elaborate embossed seal at top center. Contracted under Antwerp law. A rare and historically significant early modern financial instrument documenting forward share trading in what appears to be a Habsburg-sponsored East India trading company based in the Spanish Netherlands, operating contemporaneously with the Dutch VOC (founded 1602). This contract captures some of the earliest documented equity derivatives activity in European financial history.
creator: Generale Keijserlijke Indische Compagnie
notes: Share subscription/forward contract, Generale Keijserlijke Indische Compagnie. Antwerp, April 30, 1608. Registered No. 36. Subscriber promises to deliver shares against received cash premium. Habsburg Imperial East India Company, Spanish Netherlands. Among the earliest known equity derivatives documents. Contemporary with Dutch VOC (founded 1602).
