# Document Transcription

## Original (English):

**LEFT PAGE:**

MEXICAN
FIVE PER CENT. DEFERRED STOCK.

Letter D.

Bond for
£500 Sterling,

Bond Warrant
For 2000 Acres,

BEARING INTEREST FROM
OCTOBER, 1847.

Whereas The President of the Republic of Mexico did, under date of the 15th of April, 1837, with the concurrence of the Council of Government, issue a Decree for the creation of a National Consolidated Fund, with interest at the rate of Five Per Centum per annum, to be paid and distributed immediately through London Banking houses, to be paid half-yearly.

Whereas, at a General Meeting of the Mexican Bondholders held in London, pursued to public advertisement, on the 9th day of November, 1837, it was resolved, by the Bondholders then present, and the great bulk of the absent Bondholders, that a Committee should be appointed to arrange, adjust and settle the affairs of the Mexican Government at the Court of London; having in the same aid on behalf of and Government, by virtue of their said deed and authority vested in him, entered in full cancellation of said Deferred Stock, into the following Agreement for the purpose of conversion, Bond Deferred a copy in Spanish, and a translation in English, together with a copy of said Agreement, are hereto annexed:

And Whereas all other, they, it is the above named America De Verragas [illegible], all British and Mexicans, at the Court of London, acting under the authority aforesaid, do hereby annul this Obligation, superseding and having had been and other issue of a Bond for Five Hundred Pounds sterling, or a Warrant for Two Thousand Acres in the Republic of Mexico, issued under authority of the said Decree, and whereas the Government in London, are duly authorized to redeem convert for conversion of the proper amount, according to the terms of said Decree and Agreement as herein set forth of the Mexican Government, providing for the application of all monies to be received from the respective Governments of Mexico and England towards the payment of the principal or interest of the whole overdue thereof, as they shall deem fit, and whereas the Government of Mexico, at the Court of London, do hereby annul this Obligation, superseding and having had been and other issue of a Bond for Five Hundred Pounds sterling, or a Warrant for Two Thousand Acres in the Republic of Mexico, issued under authority of the said Decree:

And it is also further Bristler, That the holder of this Bond or Land Warrant shall be entitled in any time, until the first day of October, 1847, to receive in exchange for the same Two Thousand Acres of Land, comprised in the Republic of Mexico; and that, in case of the non-acceptance of such offer, the whole of the above Acres per Bond issued will be appointed in the amount of interest thereon, calculating from the first day of October, 1847, until the date of giving possession of said land, subject accordingly to the conditions and regulations herein contained.

And it is also further Bristler, That the holder of this Bond or Land Warrant shall be entitled in any time, until the first day of October, 1847, to receive in exchange for the same Two Thousand Acres of Land, comprised in the Republic of Mexico; and that, in case of the non-acceptance of such offer, the whole of the above Acres per Bond issued will be appointed in the amount of interest thereon, calculating from the first day of October, 1847, until the date of giving possession of said land, subject accordingly to the conditions and regulations herein contained.

And it, said America De Verragas, acting as aforesaid, do hereby guarantee and confirm to the holder of this Bond or Land Warrant, all and singular the Hypotecations, Securities, and Stipulations, originally granted to the Holders of the Deferred Stock issued under the authority of the said Decree, with all and every other and further advantages, powers, and privileges granted by the same, to the full benefit and effectual discharge of the obligations created by the Bond or Land Warrant, to grant and to pay at all times, and under all circumstances, as follows:

In Witness whereof, I, the said America De Verragas, have hereunto set my hand in London, this 20th day of September, 1837.

[Signature]

Safe Thereof Certifie, that the foregoing is the Signature of Don America De Verragas, the person named in the above Bond as the special Agent of Mexico appointed, by the Government of Mexico, for the purpose of conversion, Bonds of the Mexican Government proceeding from the Loans effected in London, or for internal Revenue, represented by Deferred Stock and Warrants, or Deferred Warrants otherwise, having the same, at the time of his Signature to this Instrument, full power and authority so to do as is by the Government of Mexico.

Consolidated Fund and Court, Paid of this date,

London, this 20th day of September, 1837.

[Signature]

[AGENTS FOR THE UNDERWRITERS OF THE MEXICAN GOVERNMENT]

---

**RIGHT PAGE:**

[Contains approximately 50+ small printed certificates/bonds labeled "MEXICAN FIVE PER CENT. DEFERRED STOCK, 1847" arranged in a grid pattern. Each certificate contains similar text regarding "Bond for £500 Serling" and "Bond Warrant For 2000 Acres" with handwritten signatures and dates. The certificates appear to be a sheet from which individual bonds could be separated.]

title: State of New York: Per Cent Stock Certificate, $300, Walter Terry of Connecticut (Albany, October 10, 1815)
description: A printed and handwritten New York State stock certificate issued by the Comptroller's Office, Albany, New York, dated October 10, 1815. The certificate acknowledges that the People of the State of New York owe Walter Terry of the State of Connecticut the sum of Three Hundred Dollars, bearing interest per cent per annum, payable on the first of January and July each year, created pursuant to an Act of the Legislature. seren ofte geven, al behalpo sicleos meer als zijn bowerschreyiene
te doone naer betalinge, maer [illegible text] moet
[illegible] voorseyt, dese Athien aen mij niet op-gerrught
sijnde, op pden ersten transportdagh Daer de obligatie niet
getransporlaert ende belacdt sijnde, waer toe den oppractcr
geholden is dan blijft dese obligatie ten mijnen halfe Nihil
en vaan gemder waerde imer sijnde dan die Attien gelick oock
den contanten premie die mij voor nu door altoos onbrekelijck
en onweder-roepelych toe-behoort, met alles swat van de Gorn-
pagnie sal ontfangen worden aen mij geatjrdonnert. Aldus
gecontractert ter Hatterstre aam [illegible] — — —
geworven maet telier —

onder verbintermisse van mijn Persoon en goederen in Antwerp-
pen den [date unclear] seventhien hordelert [illegible]
[illegible text] in twentych

**Lower notation:**
door t Athien a/P.C in optie tot prima augste 1730 inden tuschen tijdt [unclear] daght — voor't transport
op te vraegen

---

## Original Language Note:

This document is written in **Dutch** (17th-century Flemish/Antwerp dialect). It appears to be a contract regarding the purchase and transfer of stock in the Dutch East India Company (VOC - Verenigde Oost-Indische Compagnie), dated from the early 18th century, despite the header indicating it was sold in Antwerp.

- Signature line at bottom (largely illegible in reproduction)

title: Lollenpolder en Hassendael Negotiatie: Legal Agreement, 292 Shares (Amsterdam, March 4, 1817)
description: A printed and handwritten legal notarial document passed before Notary Everard Cornelis Bordt, Amsterdam, dated March 4, 1817, concerning the financial and administrative affairs of the Lollenpolder en Hassendael polder investment negotiatie. Principal parties include Cornelis Freymerius (merchant, of C. Freymerius en Zijn), Thomas Cuming (acting as vice-agent for the polder company), and Werbard van Vloter Aanraede (broker/Makelaar) and Siek Jan Voombergh (merchant), both acting as Commissioners of the Negotiatie. The document addresses the qualifications and obligations of the holders of 292 shares (twee honderd vice-en-negentig Aandelen) in the Negotiatie, the management responsibilities of Commissioners, and conditions for administration of the polder's capital. References the Associatie Knip of Vader of Zijn. Registered in Amsterdam on February 21, 1817 at D.S. f.159.
creator: C. Freymerius en Zijn; Everard Cornelis Bordt (Notary)
notes: Lollenpolder en Hassendael Negotiatie legal agreement. Amsterdam, March 4, 1817. Notary Everard Cornelis Bordt. Parties: Cornelis Freymerius, Thomas Cuming (vice-agent), Werbard van Vloter Aanraede and Siek Jan Voombergh (Commissioners). 292 shares. Registered Amsterdam February 21, 1817.

title: Unilever N.V.: Optiebewijs (Option Certificate) No. 86786 (Rotterdam, July 1937)
description: Option certificate (Optiebewijs) No. 86786 of Unilever N.V., established in Rotterdam. Printed in blue and white. The holder of eight such option certificates has the right, against surrender of these certificates, to acquire one ordinary share certificate (certificaat van gewoon aandeel) of Unilever N.V. with a nominal value of f. 100, at the closing price of f. 130½, valid until June 30, 1942 (van onwaarde na 30 Juni 1942). The terms include conditions linked to the Van den Bergh's Fabrieken N.V. 3½% Obligatielening. Dated Rotterdam, July 1937. Signed by the Secretary and a member of the Board of Directors (Lid van de Raad van Bestuur). This is a second example of the same series as No. 97790 (goetzmann0469), differing only in serial number, demonstrating the wide distribution of Unilever option certificates in this period.
creator: Unilever N.V.
notes: Optiebewijs No. 86786. Unilever N.V., Rotterdam. Eight certificates entitle holder to acquire 1 ordinary share (f.100 nominal) at f.130½; valid until June 30, 1942. Related to Van den Bergh's Fabrieken N.V. 3½% Obligatielening. Rotterdam, July 1937. Blue-printed. Same series as No. 97790 (goetzmann0469).
transcription:
# Document Transcription

**Original (Dutch):**

Op den vierden Maart des jaars achtien honderd zeventien; compareerden voor Mr. EVERARD CORNELIS BONDT, Openbaar Notaris te Amsterdam resideerednde, en voor de ondergenoemde getuigen.

De Heer CORNELIS FRYMERSUM, Koopman, wonende binnen deze Stad op de Keizersgracht tusschen de Leliegracht en Heerengracht, negotieernde onder de firma van C. FRYMERSUM en ZOON, en alzoo voor en in den naam van dezelvfe zijne firma van C. FRYMERSUM en ZOON, als Directeuren van de negotiatie groot dicemaal honderd duizend guldens, ten laste van den Heer THOMAS CUMING, onder verband van deszelfs Plantaadjen THE GARDEN OF EDEN EN THE KITTY; in de Kolonie Demerory geleegen; in den jaare seventien honderd twee-en-negentig, onder directie van den Heer SEBASTIAAN VAN NOOTEN JANES, allier opgerijgt.

En de Heeren DIRK WERNARD VAN VLOTEN ABRAHAMES; Makelaar, allier woonachtig op de Heerengracht bij de Vijzèlstraat, en DIRK JAN VOOMBERGH, Koopman, allier woonachtig in de Nieuwe Doelenstrat; beide in qualiteit als Commissarissen der voorschrevene Negotiatie.

Te kunnen gevende: Dat overeengkomdig de gemmaakte fehlkkingen niet den Heer THOMAS CUMING voornemoed, en de qualificatie der houders van de groote meerderheid der twee honderd vier-en-negentig overgeblevene Aandaclen, ieder van daizend guldens, in de voorschrevene Negotiatie, de hier voregenomelde Plantaadje genaamd THE GARDEN OF EDEN, geleegen op de Oost-kust van de rivier Demerory, met alle derzelver gebouwen, haven en verdere toebehooeren, op den achtlen Augustus des jaars achtien honderd vijftien, ten overlslans van Commissarissen van het Hof van crimineeele en civiele Justitie van de Kolonie Demerory en Essequebo, is goedeord, getransporteerd en in vollen vrijen eigendom overgegeven aan en te behoeve van de Heeren CORNELIS FRYMERSUM en CORNELIS FRYMERSUM Jr Jozor, negotieernde onder de firma van CORNELIS FRYMERSUM en ZOON, van Amsterdam, in derzelver qualiteit als Directeuren van zekere leening, gevestigd op de Plantaadjen KITTY EN GARDEN OF EDEN; zoo als blijkt uit het bij hun Heeren Comparanten ontvangene authentieke affidavit van het voormelde transport, waarop gefchreven staat:

"Geregistreerd te Amsterdam den zee en twintigten Febr. 1817, D. S. f. 179 verfo, vak 3 en 4. ontvangen met de Verhoog. negeri "en vijftig en 1 Cents (getteekend BONDAM."

En dat de Heeren Comparanten als nu, ter verdere voldoening aan den wil en het verlangen der voormelde houders van Aandaclen, met overleg van zooladige drie Heeren geinteressecrden in de voorschrevene negotiatie, welke voor het groothste getal Aandaclen tot de gezegde qualificatie hebben geoconcurreerd, hebben bepaald en vastgesteld, zoo als zij verklaren te bepalen en vaststelllen bij deren:

Dat de hier voregenomelde, als nu aan de houders der voorschrevene twee honderd vier-en-negentig Aandaclen in gemeenschaftspelijken eigendom toebehoorde Plantaadije THE GARDEN OF EDEN, met alle derzelver gebouwen, haven en verdere toebehooeren; en al wat werder tot het fonds der Associatio behoerende zijn mag, zal worden gereegerd en geadministreerd door de Heeren C. FRYMERSUM en ZOON, Kooplieden te Amsterdam, als Directeuren van het fonds der Plantaadije THE GARDEN OF EDEN, golegen

---

**English Translation:**

On the fourth day of March in the year eighteen hundred seventeen; appeared before Mr. EVERARD CORNELIS BONDT, Public Notary residing in Amsterdam, and before the hereinafter named witnesses.

Mr. CORNELIS FRYMERSUM, Merchant, residing within this City on the Keizersgracht between the Leliegracht and Heerengracht, negotiating under the firm name of C. FRYMERSUM and ZOON, and thus on behalf of and in the name of his said firm C. FRYMERSUM and ZOON, as Directors of the negotiation of a principal amount of one hundred thousand guilders, charged to Mr. THOMAS CUMING, under pledge of his Plantations THE GARDEN OF EDEN AND THE KITTY; in the Colony Demerory situated; in the year seventeen hundred ninety-two, established under the direction of Mr. SEBASTIAAN VAN NOOTEN JANES, broker appointed.

And the Gentlemen DIRK WERNARD VAN VLOTEN ABRAHAMES; Broker, residing on the Heerengracht near the Vijzèlstraat, and DIRK JAN VOOMBERGH, Merchant, residing in the Nieuwe Doelenstrat; both in the capacity of Commissioners of the aforementioned Negotiation.

To make known: That in accordance with the agreements made with Mr. THOMAS CUMING mentioned beforehand, and the qualification of the holders of the greater majority of the two hundred ninety-four remaining Shares, each of one thousand guilders, in the aforementioned Negotiation, the herein named Plantation called THE GARDEN OF EDEN, situated on the East coast of the river Demerory, with all its buildings, harbor and other belongings, on the eighth day of August in the year eighteen hundred fifteen, by transfer of Commissioners of the Court of criminal and civil Justice of the Colony Demerory and Essequebo, has been approved, transported and in full free ownership transferred to and for the benefit of the Gentlemen CORNELIS FRYMERSUM and CORNELIS FRYMERSUM Jr Jozor, negotiating under the firm name of CORNELIS FRYMERSUM and ZOON, of Amsterdam, in their capacity as Directors of a certain loan, established on the Plantations KITTY AND GARDEN OF EDEN; as appears from the authentic affidavit of the aforesaid transport received by the said Gentlemen Comparants, on which written appears:

"Registered in Amsterdam on the twenty-third of February 1817, D. S. f. 179 verso, section 3 and 4, received with the Higher Court, forty and fifty and 1 Cents (signed BONDAM."

And that the said Gentlemen Comparants now, for further satisfaction of the will and the desire of the aforementioned holders of Shares, with the advice of such three Gentlemen interested in the aforementioned negotiation, which for the greatest number of Shares have concurred in the said qualification, have determined and established, as they declare to determine and establish herein:

That the herein named, as now belonging to the holders of the aforementioned two hundred ninety-four Shares in common joint ownership Plantation THE GARDEN OF EDEN, with all its buildings, harbor and other belongings; and all that further belongs to the fund of the Association, shall be governed and administered by the Gentlemen C. FRYMERSUM and ZOON, Merchants in Amsterdam, as Directors of the fund of the Plantation THE GARDEN OF EDEN, situated...

---

**Notable markings:**

- Reference number "492" visible in upper left corner
- Handwritten notation/marking in upper left
- Document appears to be the first page of a longer legal document
- Text continues beyond visible portion (ends mid-sentence)
- Formal Dutch legal document format typical of 19th-century notarial acts

title: Negotiatie "Concordia Res Parvae Crescunt": Reçu No. 54, f. 500 Aandelen (Amsterdam, January 1902)
description: A receipt (Reçu) No. 54 from the Dutch investment fund/negotiatie "Concordia Res Parvae Crescunt" (Latin motto: "Through harmony small things grow"), acknowledging the transfer (overgenomen) from the Heer [Martonokle?] of shares valued at f. 500 (Aandelen) in the above-named Negotiatie. The transfer is made in accordance with the resolution adopted at the Shareholders' Meeting (Vergadering van Aandeelhouders) held on December 4, 1893. Amsterdam, January 1902. Signed for the Associatiekas (Association Treasury). Amount: f. 430.553. "Concordia Res Parvae Crescunt" was a widely used name for Dutch investment pools (negotiaties) in the 19th and early 20th centuries, typically investing in mortgages, bonds, and other fixed-income instruments on behalf of their shareholders.
creator: Negotiatie Concordia Res Parvae Crescunt
notes: Negotiatie "Concordia Res Parvae Crescunt." Reçu No. 54. Transfer of f. 500 aandelen from [Martonokle?]. Per resolution Shareholders' Meeting, December 4, 1893. Amsterdam, January 1902. Amount: f. 430.553. Signed for Associatiekas.
transcription:
# Document Transcription

**Original (Dutch):**

Bij notariele acte dd° 23 November 1937 is de naam der Unilever N.V. veranderd in: „Lever Brothers & Unilever N.V.". De naam der Van den Bergh's Fabrieken N.V. werd bij notariele acte dd° 2 September 1938 gewijzigd in: „Van den Bergh's en Jurgens' Fabrieken N.V.".

UNILEVER N.V.
GEVESTIGD TE ROTTERDAM.

OPTIEBEWIJS N°.86786
VAN ONWAARDE NA 30 JUNI 1942.

De houder van acht dezer optiebewwijzen heeft het recht, tegen inlevering dezer bewijzen van Unilever N.V. een certificaat van een gewoon aandeel in Unilever N.V., groot ƒ 1000.— nominaal, te verwerven tot de prijs van ƒ 1250.— (195 %) tot en met 31 December 1938 en ƒ 2150.— (215 %) vanaf 1 Januari 1939 tot en met 30 Juni 1942, op de voorwaarden vervat in de aan de taerzijde van dit bewijs vermelde bepalingen. Overeenkomstig art. 5 dezer bepalingen worden bij de storting 3½ %-obligatien, behorende tot de in 1937 ten laste van Van den Bergh's Fabrieken N.V.-obligatielening, ten hoogste groot ƒ 30.000.000.—, in betaling aangenomen tot haar volle nominale bedrag, vermeerderd met opgeslopen rente.

ROTTERDAM, Juli 1937.

[Signatures]
SECRETARIS.                    LID VAN DE RAAD VAN BESTUUR.

---

**English translation:**

By notarial deed dated 23 November 1937, the name of Unilever N.V. was changed to: "Lever Brothers & Unilever N.V.". The name of Van den Bergh's Fabrieken N.V. was changed by notarial deed dated 2 September 1938 to: "Van den Bergh's and Jurgens' Fabrieken N.V.".

UNILEVER N.V.
ESTABLISHED IN ROTTERDAM.

OPTION CERTIFICATE N°.86786
OF NO VALUE AFTER 30 JUNE 1942.

The holder of eight of these option certificates has the right, upon surrender of these certificates of Unilever N.V., to acquire a certificate of an ordinary share in Unilever N.V., nominal value ƒ 1000.—, at the price of ƒ 1250.— (195%) until and including 31 December 1938 and ƒ 2150.— (215%) from 1 January 1939 until and including 30 June 1942, on the conditions set forth in the provisions stated on the reverse of this certificate. In accordance with art. 5 of these provisions, upon payment, 3½% bonds, belonging to the Van den Bergh's Fabrieken N.V. bond loan of 1937, up to a maximum of ƒ 30,000,000.—, shall be accepted in payment at their full nominal value, increased by accrued interest.

ROTTERDAM, July 1937.

---

- Orange circular stamp/seal in upper right corner (50 denomination mark with Dutch coat of arms, dated 1938)
- Serial number: N°.86786
- Two signatures at bottom (Secretary and Board Member)
- Decorative blue lace-pattern border on all sides
- Printer mark at bottom: "JOH. ENSCHEDÉ EN ZONEN - HAARLEM"

title: United States of America: Bill of Exchange No. 43, $120 (600 Livres Tournois) for Interest on Loan Office Certificates (October 1, 1780)
description: A printed and handwritten bill of exchange No. 43, issued by the United States of America on October 1, 1780. At Thirty Days Sight, payable to Jonathan D. Sergeant (or Order) in Six Hundred Livres Tournois, equivalent to One Hundred and Twenty Dollars at five Livres Tournois per Dollar, for Interest due on Money borrowed by the United States. Countersigned by the Commissioner of the Continental Loan Office in the State of Pennsylvania. Signed by H. Atkinson, Treasurer of Loans. This is one of a series of bills of exchange issued by the Continental Congress to pay interest on Loan Office Certificates by drawing on French credit, reflecting the United States' dependence on French currency networks during the Revolutionary War.
creator: United States of America, Loan Office, Pennsylvania
notes: US Bill of Exchange No. 43, $120 / 600 Livres Tournois. October 1, 1780. Payable to Jonathan D. Sergeant. Interest on Loan Office Certificates. Pennsylvania Loan Office; signed by H. Atkinson, Treasurer of Loans. Continental Revolutionary War finance.

title: Potosian Land Grant: Class C, 400 Acres / Concesión de 400 Acres (London, April 1825)
description: A large bilingual English/Spanish land grant certificate (Class C) of 400 Acres in the Potosí region, dated London, April 1825. The document is headed "POTOSIAN LAND GRANT" and includes the full grant text in English as well as a Spanish translation (Concesión de 400 Acres), with signatures from London confirming the Exchange and Redemption of the outstanding claims. The Potosian Land Grant scheme was associated with early 19th-century British speculative investment in Bolivian mining and land ventures following South American independence, when British investors rushed to capitalize on newly opened Latin American markets. Potosí, historically famous for its vast silver mines since the 16th century, attracted significant British capital in the 1820s as part of the broader Latin American investment mania.
creator: Potosian Land Grant Company (London)
notes: Potosian Land Grant, Class C, 400 Acres / Concesión de 400 Acres. London, April 1825. Bilingual English/Spanish. British speculative investment in Bolivian (Potosí) mining/land post-independence. Large format with ornate header and two-column bilingual text.
transcription:
# Transcription of Potosian Land Grant Certificate

## LEFT PAGE (English Version)

**Class C. No. 193 / Class C. No. 193**

**Grant of 400 Acres.**

[Header with coat of arms: "POTOSIAN LAND GRANT" / "STOCK UNIT" / "400 Acres"]

**Copy of the Original Grant from the King of the Mosquito Shore and Nation, to GEORGE FREDERICK II, D.G., King of the Mosquito Shore and Nation.**

By virtue of these Presents, We hereby grant and devise unto His Excellency Colonel the Right Honorable Lord Cochrane, and to his Heirs, Executors, Administrators and Assigns, all that certain parcel or tract of Land, situate, lying and being in that part of the Potosian Republic called Popayan Province, containing Four Hundred Acres of Land in the County of Popayan in a weight line, down the South, until it touches the 1st parallel of South Latitude.

To have and to hold the said parcel of Land, with all and singular the Houses, Buildings, Improvements, Hereditaments and Appurtenances thereunto belonging or in any wise appertaining, to him the said Lord Cochrane, and to his Heirs and Assigns, to the only proper use, benefit and behoof of him the said Lord Cochrane, and his Heirs and Assigns forever.

In Witness Whereof, We have caused our Great Seal to be hereunto affixed. Given under Our Hand, Seal and Signature, in the City of the Mosquito Shore, this [date] Day of [month], in the year of our Reign.

**By His Majesty's Command:—WILLIAM BOWES.**

[Additional official documentation regarding transfer and validation]

**Certified by:**
- WILLIAM SMITH & Co., Chancing Street, Whitechapel
- Deputy Clerk of Lieutenancy of the High Court of Chancery

[Extended legal text regarding the grant conditions and authority]

**Grant of 400 Acres.**

*Viz.* George Mac Gregor, Esquire, do hereby grant unto the Bearer hereof is entitled to Four Hundred Acres of Land in that part of the Popayan Republic called Popayan Province, in consideration of his having received the sum of Four Hundred Pounds Sterling as a proportioned part of the original subscription for the Copy of the Original Grants as in any other original Grants to the Potosian Republic, in manner as will not aid relief of the Potosian Republic, now bearing date, but to immemorial and exclusive possession of the said land in virtue hereof. And we hereby promise that the Bearer shall and will be entitled to the said Lands and Tenements, and deliver to the Bearer the Grant sealed with the seal aforesaid.

*Edinburgh, April 3rd., 1824.*

[Signatures]

---

## RIGHT PAGE (Spanish Version)

**Classe C. No. 193**

**Concession de 400 Acres.**

[Header: "POTOSIAN LAND GRANT" / "STOCK UNIT" / "400 Acres"]

**[TRADUCTION]**

Copie d'Acte de Concession ou ORIGINAL du Roi de la Cité de la Nation Métropolitaine, à GEORGE FREDERIC Deux, p.h. Cham de Dame, Roi de la Cité et de la Nation Métropolitaine.

[Extended French translation of the English grant document, maintaining parallel structure]

**Par ordre de Sa Majesté.—WILLIAM BOWES.**

[French legal documentation and certifications]

**Concession de 400 Acres.**

*Nous*, George Mac Gregor, Esquire du Popoli, certifions par les présentes que le Porteur de cette est autorisé à recevoir d'ordre Quatre Cent Acres de Terre en cette Partie du Popayan que l'on appelle Popayan Province... [continued French text]

*Edimbourg, le 3e Avril, 1824.*

[Signatures]

title: Prys-Courant der Effecten: Amsterdam Securities and Exchange Rate Price List, No. 39 (Amsterdam, 1822)
description: A printed weekly securities and exchange rate price list (Prys-Courant der Effecten) from Amsterdam, Thursday, 1822, No. 39. The broadsheet lists market prices for a wide range of securities: Dutch government bonds (Vereenigde Nederlanden), various Dutch negotiaties (investment pools), and a broad array of foreign government bonds and securities including Spanish, French, Austrian, Russian, and other European and American obligations. The lower portion gives Wissels en Specie Cours (exchange rates for bills of exchange and coin prices) for London, Paris, Hamburg, Frankfurt, Berlin, Vienna, and other centers. The Prys-Courant der Effecten was Amsterdam's authoritative weekly securities price publication, a primary record of early 19th-century European financial market activity and international capital flows.
creator: Amsterdam Stock Exchange
notes: Amsterdam Prys-Courant der Effecten, No. 39, 1822. Lists prices for Dutch government bonds, negotiaties, Spanish, French, Austrian, Russian, and other foreign securities. Also: Wissels en Specie Cours (exchange rates and coin prices) for major European financial centers.

title: Regeering van de Chineesche Republiek: 8% Schatkistbiljet, Lung-Tsing-U-Hai Spoorweg (1923), f. 1000 (No. 11639)
description: An 8% treasury bill (Schatkistbiljet aan Toonder) No. 11639, denomination f. 1,000 Dutch guilders, issued by the Government of the Chinese Republic (Regeering van de Chineesche Republiek) for the Lung-Tsing-U-Hai Railway (Lung-Tsing-U-Hai-Spoorweg), total issue f. 16,667,000 divided into 16,667 notes of f. 1,000 each. The bill features an ornate multicolor printed design with a Chinese bridge or architectural vignette and decorative border. Signed in Chinese characters and Dutch by Chinese authorities. The Lung-Tsing-U-Hai (Longhai) Railway was a major east-west Chinese railway linking the coast to the interior. Dutch guilder-denominated Chinese railway bonds were a significant form of European financing for Chinese infrastructure development during the early Republic period.
creator: Government of the Chinese Republic
notes: 8% Schatkistbiljet, Lung-Tsing-U-Hai Spoorweg (Longhai Railway), 1923. No. 11639, f. 1,000 Dutch guilders. Total issue f. 16,667,000 / 16,667 notes. Regeering van de Chineesche Republiek. Ornate multicolor certificate. Dutch-guilder denominated Chinese railway finance.

title: Kornelis van den Helm Boddaert: Middelburg Plantation Bond No. 99, Essequibo and Demerara (January 1760)
description: A handwritten mortgage bond/plantation loan document No. 99 from the Register of Middelburg (Zeeland), signed by Kornelis van den Helm Boddaert as Directeur and qualified secretary for the money-lenders (geld-Opschieters) of various Plantations in the Colony of Essequibo and Demerara (present-day Guyana), acting according to the General Plan of Hypotheken (mortgages) secured by the colonial plantations. Dated January 1760. Capital of Five Hundred Livres at 5% per cent per annum, interest from January 1, 1760. Authenticated with the Compagnie seal. Multiple endorsements and subsequent annotations in later hands (1772, 1793, 1817). This document exemplifies the Dutch colonial plantation mortgage system (plantageleningen) of Essequibo and Demerara, which bundled West Indian plantation mortgages for sale to Dutch investors — among the earliest securitization schemes in financial history.
creator: Kornelis van den Helm Boddaert; Middelburg Administration
notes: Middelburg plantation bond No. 99. Kornelis van den Helm Boddaert, Director/Secretary. Plantations in Essequibo and Demerara (Guyana). Capital ~500 Livres at 5% p.a. January 1760. Dutch plantageleningen system. Endorsements dated 1772, 1793, 1817.
transcription:
# Transcription

**Original (Dutch):**

[Top of page - handwritten annotations:]
Mortgage Correspondence
1805 à 1815 Elisabeth Bidde v. Nepad 1820
J.J. Cawthey

(i)
No 99

**Register MIDDELBURG.**

Ik Ondergeschreeve KORNELIS VAN DEN HELM BODDAERT,
als Directeur en gequalificeede van de geld Opschieters, op verschillende Plantlagien in de Colonie van Estequebo en Demerary, volgens de Hypotheeken ter Secretaarie aldaar, in behoorlyke Ordre alle ten behoeve van dit fonds gepasseerd, waar van de Copen Authentiek met Kompagnie's Zegel bezegeld, en door den Secretaris der Colonie geteekend onder my zyn beroofduende, Bekenne in die qualiteit aggervolgeus het gedruckte Project en Condition (waar van den inhoud hier agter is geinicrseerd) Ontfangen te hebben
van [handwritten signature: appears to be "Nidal S. Cellas" or similar]

Eene Somma van [handwritten: "Vijf Honderd Ponden à slantz" or similar]

Welke somma ik Ondergeschreeve, beleove aan dezelve of deszelfs Recta en Actie verkrigende, te zullen doen Resfueeren na Expiratie van den tyd in het Plan hier agter vermeld, en immidels daar van te zullen uitkercen, den Interest tot Vijf perCento in 't jaar ingangende primo January 1700 [corrected to 1760], reducerende alzoo van jaar tot jaar, tot de volle en Effectueele betaaling der Hypotheeken, waar van de eerste vervaldng zal zyn, op primo January 1700 [corrected to 1760], en zoo vervolgens alle jaar, en dat van alles vry-geld zonder eenige kortinge aan Capital of Interesten te mogeu doen, zullende de Interesten werdd betaald ten Comptoire van den Ondergeschreeve, tot nakominge van al her welige der Geldleeners boven hunne speciale Hypotheeken hebben verbonden, hunne Persoonen en goedereu, ten bedwang als na Regten.

Ter Oirkonde dezes by my onderteekend
Middelburg den [handwritten: "Octobr" or similar] 1760
[handwritten signatures and annotations]
van [handwritten] 1769 1815 ingelafen
Maankupo [?] July 1793
[Various handwritten endorsements]
C. Anningsman.

Conform Art: 5 betaald voor 3 Jaar versfchenen January 1770:9___
[Additional handwritten notations]

[Signatures at bottom:]
Johann Phillip Ste.                    [?] Schoraitz

---

**English Translation:**

I, the undersigned KORNELIS VAN DEN HELM BODDAERT,
as Director and qualified [representative] of the money-lenders (geld-Opschieters) for various Plantations in the Colony of Essequibo and Demerara, according to the Mortgages filed at the Secretariat there, in proper Order for the benefit of this fund, having been executed by the Company's Seal and signed by the Secretary of the Colony under my supervision, I acknowledge in this capacity, according to the printed Project and Condition (the contents of which are written here below) to have received

from [name]

A Sum of [Five Hundred Pounds at slant or similar amount]

Which sum I, the undersigned, promise to the same [person] or his heirs and rightful claimants to refund after the expiration of the term in the Plan mentioned here below, and meanwhile shall pay out the Interest at Five percent per annum commencing primo January 1760, reducing accordingly year by year, until the full and Effectual payment of the Mortgages, of which the first expiration shall be on primo January 1760, and so continuing every year, and that of all free-money without any deduction from Capital or Interest to be able to pay, the Interests being paid at the Office of the undersigned, to the fulfillment of all which the Moneylenders above their special Mortgages have bound, their Persons and goods, under constraint according to Law.

In Witness thereof signed by me
Middelburg [month] 1760
[Various later endorsements and annotations]

Conform Art: 5 paid for 3 Years [due] January 1770

[Signatures:]
Johann Phillip Ste.                    [?] Schoraitz

- Appears to reference Company seal ("Kompagnie's Zegel")

- Principal amount: **Five Hundred Pounds [currency]** (specific denomination unclear from handwriting)
- Original loan date: **January 1760**
- Signatures of multiple officials and possibly lenders
- Handwritten corrections changing dates from 1700 to 1760

title: Rentes Viagères au Denier Vingt-cinq sur les Quatre Millions de Livres (Édit de Juillet 1723): Life Annuity No. 21839 for Simonne Jenaille (Paris, 1723)
description: A printed and handwritten royal life annuity contract (Rente Viagère au Denier Vingt-cinq) No. 21839, issued by Jean Antoine Paris, Conseiller du Roy en Exercice en cette Ville de Paris, for Simonne Jenaille (and family). The annuity is drawn on the Four Millions of Livres raised under the Édit du Mois de Juillet 1723 on the Tailles and other Impositions. The annual payment is twenty-three livres and approximately seventy-four sous. The rente viagère was tied to the life of the nominee and ceased upon their death. Issued by the Quinzième du Garde du Trésor Royal, Paris, 1723. Signed by M. Lacromont [?]. An important instrument of French royal finance during the Regency and early Louis XV period, when life annuities were a principal mechanism for raising state revenue.
creator: Jean Antoine Paris, Conseiller du Roy
notes: Rente Viagère au Denier Vingt-cinq, No. 21839. Quatre Millions de Livres, Édit de Juillet 1723, Tailles & autres Impositions. Nominee: Simonne Jenaille. Jean Antoine Paris, Conseiller du Roy. Paris, 1723. Annual payment ~23 livres 74 sous. French royal life annuity.
transcription:
# Document Transcription

**Original (French):**

Rentes Viageres au Denier Vingt-cinq, Sur les Quatre Millions de livres de l'Edit du mois de Juillet 1723. sur les Tailles & autres Impositions.
N.°21539.

JE ANTOINE PARIS, Conseiller du Roy en ses Conseils, Garde de son Tresor Royal, Confesse avoir reçû en cette Ville de Paris, de Simonne Jenaille, [handwritten:] d'une de Claude Rabazourt pour jouir par elle de la Rente cy apres. Sur la teste de Simonne agathe Rabazourt femme de Pierre Charles Luciles avocat en Parlement demeurant à Dreux

la Somme de [handwritten:] Onze cents soulante quinze livres
quarante sept livres

pour le Principal de Rentes Viageres au Denier Vingt-cinq, faisant partie des Quatre Millions de livres de Rentes Viageres créées par Edit du mois de Juillet 1723. Registré au besoin a été, à prendre sur les Tailles & autres Impositions, tant des Pays d'Election que des Pays d'Etats, pour jouir par [handwritten:] Jenaille
pour chacun an, de ladite Rente de [handwritten:] ... sur le pied du Denier Vingt-cinq, ainsi qu'il sera plus au long expliqué par le Contrat de Constitution qui sera expedié de ladite Rente par Messieurs les Commissaires du Conseil nommez à cet effet par Lettres Patentes du Roy du 9. Aout de la présente année; de laquelle somme de à moy ordonnée pour employer au fait de ma Charge, je quitte le [handwritten signature]. & tous autres. Fait à Paris le [handwritten:] vingtième jour de septembre mil sept cens vingt-trois.

Quittance du Garde du Trésor Royal, Année mil sept cens vingt-trois.
[Handwritten signature]

M. [handwritten:] Lacromont     N.re

---

## English Translation:

Life Annuities at Twenty-five Deniers, On the Four Millions of Livres from the Edict of the Month of July 1723, on the Tailles & other Impositions.
No. 21539.

I ANTOINE PARIS, Counselor of the King in his Councils, Keeper of his Royal Treasury, confess to have received in this City of Paris, from Simonne Jenaille, [handwritten: one of Claude Rabazourt to enjoy by her of the Annuity hereafter. On the life of Simonne agathe Rabazourt wife of Pierre Charles Luciles advocate in Parliament residing at Dreux

the Sum of [handwritten:] Eleven hundred seventy-five livres
forty-seven livres

for the Principal of Life Annuities at Twenty-five Deniers, forming part of the Four Millions of livres of Life Annuities created by Edict of the month of July 1723. Registered as needed has been, to be drawn from the Tailles & other Impositions, both of the Election Countries and of the States Countries, to enjoy by [handwritten:] Jenaille
for each year, of the said Annuity of [handwritten:] ... at the rate of Twenty-five Deniers, as will be more fully explained by the Constitution Contract which will be issued for said Annuity by Messieurs the Commissioners of Council appointed for this purpose by Letters Patent of the King of the 9th of August of the present year; of which sum ordered to me to be employed in the execution of my Office, I hereby release the [handwritten signature]. & all others. Done in Paris on the [handwritten:] twentieth day of September one thousand seven hundred twenty-three.

Receipt of the Keeper of the Royal Treasury, Year one thousand seven hundred twenty-three.
[Handwritten signature]

M. [handwritten:] Lacromont     N.re [Notary]

---

## Notable Markings:

- **Handwritten signature** at bottom (appears to be "Brun" or similar)
- **Notary marking** ("N.re") at bottom right
- Reference to "Quittance du Garde du Trésor Royal" (Receipt of the Keeper of the Royal Treasury)
- **M. Lacromont** named as signatory official

title: Rentes Viagères Sur une Tête (Édit de Novembre 1738): Life Annuity, Première Classe, for the Comtesse de Saint-Hermaurice (Paris, July 26, 1739)
description: A printed and handwritten royal life annuity contract (Rente Viagère Sur une Tête) for the Comtesse de Saint-Hermaurice (Madame La Comtesse de Saint-Hermaurice), drawn under the Édit de Novembre 1738, Première Classe. Issued July 26, 1739, Paris, before Jean-Baptiste-Élie Canus de Pontcaillé, seigneur de Vuarnet, Sirogu, Briey, and others, Notaires, Gardes-note et Garde du Seau de Sa Majesté au Châtelet de Paris, and other Parisian notaries. The annuity is for acquéreurs of annuities from the Marchands et Echevins (merchant aldermen) of Paris, with four thousand livres of capital each, at a fixed annual rente. The life annuity runs for the lifetime of the nominated individual and provides for specific conditions on re-registration and transfer. An important document from French royal life annuity finance during the reign of Louis XV.
creator: Notaires du Châtelet de Paris; Jean-Baptiste-Élie Canus de Pontcaillé
notes: Rente Viagère Sur une Tête, Édit de Novembre 1738, Première Classe. Nominee: Comtesse de Saint-Hermaurice. Issued July 26, 1739, Paris. Notaires du Châtelet. Marchands et Echevins financing (4,000 livres capital each). French royal life annuity under Louis XV.
transcription:
# TRANSCRIPTION

## Original (French):

M^de La Comtesse de Pont-Marcelle
26 Juillet 1739

RENTES VIAGÈRES SUR UNE TÊTE.
Édit de Novembre 1738.

Première Classe.

PAR-DEVANT les Conseillers du Roi, Notaires, Gardes-notes & Gardes-scel de Sa Majesté au Châtelet de Paris, soussignés, furent prêtés, M^de JEAN-BAPTISTE-ÉLIE CAMUS DE PONTCAILLÉ, seigneur de Vuarmet, Sirogu, Briey & autres lieux, Conseillers d'État, Prévôt [des Marchands & Echevins]

[Handwritten insertions - partially legible]:
Jeanne Antoinette Vallois, Veuves d'un Abbé de lille
Jean Baptiste Normant de St [?]
[unclear handwriting]
et [unclear] a l'enseignement au Colonnet et a l'enseignement de l'[unclear]

Tous Échevins de cette ville de Paris.

ESQUELS dit que Sa Majesté, par son édit du mois de novembre 1738, enregistrié au Parlement le 12 décembre luidit, auroit ordonné que par les Commissaires nommez à cet effet, il feroit vendu & alienié audités Sieurs Prévôt des Marchands & Échevins, trois millions fix sens mille livres actuelles & effectives de rentes viagères, à prendre fur tous les anciens provenant des droits d'aides, Chapelles & cinq grofses fermes, que Sa Majesté a affectées, obligées & hypothéquées par préférence à la partie du tréfor royal, au payement desquelles rentes dédites rentes qui pourroient être acquises, Soit fur une seule tête, à divers taux, suivant l'âge des acquéreurs, ou fut deus indigènes; à raison de huit pour cent par an.

Que les constitutions particulieres desdites rentes, qui ne pourroient être moindres fur une seule tête de cinquante livres, & sur deux têtes de quarante livres de jouissance annuelle, feroient faites par lesdits trois millions fix sens mille livres actuelles & effectives de rentes viagères, à prendre fur tous les anciens provenant des droits d'aides, Chapelles & cinq grofses fermes, que Sa Majesté a affectées, obligées & hypothéquées par préférence à la partie du tréfor royal, au payement desquelles rentes dédites rentes qui pourroient être acquises, Soit fur une seule tête, à divers taux, suivant l'âge des acquéreurs, ou fut deus indigènes; à raison de huit pour cent par an.

Que les rentes seront acquises fur une tête, feroient acquéreurs; la première, depuis la naissance jusqu'à cinquante ans accomplys; la deuxième, depuis cinquante-cinq ans jusqua' soixante; la troisième, depuis soixante jusqua' soixante-cinq; la quatrième, depuis soixante-cinq ans jusqua' soixante-dix; & la sixième, depuis soixante-dix ans jusqua' soixante-quinze ans & au deffus.

Que les rentes fur deux têtes feroient acquises fur une distinction d'âge ni de classe; & dans l'une desdites rentes, il feroit justifiée de l'âge de ceux fur la tête desquels ce qui a été ci-devant prescrit en pareil cas, & qu'annuellement il seroit payé les rentes, pendant leur vie ou de ceux fur la site desquels elles feroient confluées, favoir, pour chaque constitution de mille livres en capital dans la première classe, cent livres; dans la deuxième, cent livres; dans la troisième, cent dix livres; dans la quatrième, cent vingt livres; dans la cinquième, cent trente livres; & pour les rentes fur deux têtes quatre-vingt livres, auffi pour chaque confluence de mille livres en capital.

Que toutes fortes de personnes indiftinctement, de quelque âge, Sexe, qualité & condition qu'elles puiffent être, même les religieufes qui, fuivant leur Ordre, peuvoient avoir capacité, pourroient acquérir lesdites rentes; le leur vivant des pères & mères qui auroient acquis lesdites rentes, en quelque cas qui de leur compte, les enfans & autres qui enteroient en religion & feroient profeffion dans quelque Ordre que ce puiffe être, confidereroient par forme de dotation alimentaire, celles qui auroient été par eux acquises, ou qu'ayant été Ermites nous remarquant, lefquels n'ayant aucuns cas de leur comptes, les enfans & autres qui enteroient en religion & feroient profeffion dans quelque Ordre que ce puiffe être, confidereroient par forme de dotation alimentaire, celles qui auroient été par eux acquises, ou qu'ayant eu [unclear] lefquels feront demeures hors du Royaume, pays, terres & rentes, encores qu'il fuffent fujets de Princes & États avec lesquels Elle est ou pourroit estre en guerre; en confequence Sa Majesté à voulu que lesdites rentes & les arrérages qui en froient dûs au jour du deces de ces derniers, feroient exemptes de toutes lettres de marque & de represailles, droits d'aubaine, bâtardie, confifcation, ou autres qui pourroient lui appartenir, & auxquels pourroient estre renoncé, conformément à l'édit du mois de décembre 1674 & autres fubfequens.

Que lesdites rentes feroient exemptes de la revenne des deux Vingtièmes, des Deux fols pour livre

[Signature at bottom]: U. Gersault M^e

## Notable markings:

- **Seal/emblem**: Decorative royal coat of arms at top center
- **Classification marking**: "Première Classe" (First Class) noted on right side
- **Handwritten annotation**: At top left corner indicating recipient and date: "M^de La Comtesse de Pont-Marcelle / 26 Juillet 1739"
- **Signature**: "U. Gersault M^e" at bottom
- **Notarial marks**: Multiple handwritten insertions and annotations throughout, some partially illegible

title: Republic of New Granada: Deferred Bond, Letter A, No. 7376, £100 Sterling (London, January 1, 1845)
description: A bilingual English/Spanish deferred bearer bond, Letter A, No. 7376, for £100 Sterling, issued by the Republic of New Granada (predecessor to Colombia), dated London, January 1, 1845. Signed by Juan Climaco Ordóñez as Chargé d'Affaires of the Republic of New Granada at the Court of London, and by Powles, Illingworth, Willson & Co. as agents and commissioners. The bond acknowledges £100 of the Foreign Debt of New Granada. Interest is deferred at Four Per Cent per annum; dividends to be paid half-yearly from December 1 each year at the counting house of Messrs. Runneys, Barings & Co. in London, upon delivery of Warrants or Coupons. The Government of New Granada engages to deliver additional bonds as payment for accumulated arrears of interest. Signed in Bogotá, August 15, 1843. The New Granadan external debt, originating in the independence period, underwent multiple restructurings throughout the 19th century.
creator: Republic of New Granada; Powles, Illingworth, Willson & Co.
notes: Republic of New Granada Deferred Bond, Letter A, No. 7376, £100 Sterling. London, January 1, 1845. Chargé d'Affaires: Juan Climaco Ordóñez. Agents: Powles, Illingworth, Willson & Co. 4% interest deferred; dividends at Runneys, Barings & Co. Signed Bogotá August 15, 1843. Bilingual English/Spanish.
transcription:
# REPUBLIC OF NEW GRANADA: DEFERRED BOND

**Letter A N° 7376**
**£100 Sterling**
**DEFERRED**

---

## ENGLISH TEXT (Left Column):

I, the undersigned Chargé d'Affaires of the Republic of New Granada at the Court of London, in pursuance of the Agreement between the Secretary of Finance of the said Republic and the Agents of the Bondholders of the Foreign Debt of Colombia, signed at Bogotá on the 15th January, 1845, of which a copy is subjoined, and in conformity with the Instructions received from my Government, as fiscal Commissioner in London, do hereby declare that the said Republic is indebted to the Bearer hereof in the Sum of One Hundred Pounds Sterling, and hereby, in the most solemn manner, bind the said Republic to pay the same, and the interest thereon, according to the tenor of the said Agreement. The interest of the said Sum of One Hundred Pounds is to be paid Half-yearly, on the first day of June and the first day of December in each year, the first Half-yearly Dividend falling due on 1st December, 1861, according to the rates specified in the Fourth Article of the said Agreement, at the Counting-house of Messrs. Barings, Bevan, Binneys and Co., in London, on delivery of the Warrants or Coupons attached hereto. The Government of New Granada engages to deliver additional Deferred Warrants to the Holder hereof, when those attached shall have been paid; and so from time to time until the Principal of the Bond shall have been redeemed, or payment thereof tendered by the said Government.

### TRANSLATION.

We the undersigned JUAN CLIMACO ORDÓÑEZ, Secretary of the Finance Department of the Government of NEW GRANADA, in the name of the said Government; and POWLES, ILLINGWORTH, WILLSON and CO., as Agents of the Bondholders of the Debt of Colombia, have agreed to the following proposals:—

1. New Granada acknowledges as her Debt Fifty per Cent. of the Loans contracted by the former Republic of Colombia in 1822 and 1824, which Debt had been repudiated by the Government of Venezuela as being consequently cancelled as respects on the half of the value, and New Granada assumes the other half upon condition which are set forth hereinafter.

2. The Bonds which New Granada may issue, in conformity with the following articles, will bear Interest at One per Cent per annum for the first four years, which Interest shall be loaned upon itself; and in each subsequent year, will it transfer at One per Cent designated for four years.

3. In payment of the Interest due and not paid, and of that which shall become payable until the Bond shall have been paid off, New Granada agrees to issue the rate of Four per Cent, of the Principal, as that the Bonds now issued in its name shall be developed for.

4. These last-mentioned Bonds shall not bear Interest during the first six years after their issue; but, in the seventh year, they shall bear One per Cent, which shall be increased by one eighth part in each of the following years, until it reaches Three per Cent, which is the maximum rate of Interest.

5. The Interest on the New Granada Bonds that may be issued in conformity with the stipulation of Article 3, shall be paid in London on the first and fifteenth of June, concurrently eight hundred and forty-five, and to this account may be paid the Government Deferred Bonds of former loans of Powles, Heritors, of London.

6. The Bonds of both Debts shall be redeemed, on the payment of one percent. of the amount of the nominal value of the said property at the proper period of the foreign debt.

7. The net produce of the Tobacco Revenue and the half of the produce of the Customs Revenue, are chargeable for the execution of this contract.

8. These Proposals shall bind the Government of New Granada after the ratification thereof by the Government of New Granada shall have been issued in the condition which have except that the First shall draw interest from the date hereof, and none of the Credits shall not affect thereof, this Agreement shall be null and void of express lich portion.

In faith whereof we sign these presents at Bogotá, on the fifteenth of January, one thousand eight hundred and forty-five.

JUAN CLIMACO ORDÓÑEZ,
POWLES, ILLINGWORTH, WILLSON, and CO.

[Seal A Copy]
The Secretary of Finance, J. C. ORDÓÑEZ.

**Dated in London, this First day of June, 1845**

---

## SPANISH TEXT (Right Column):

### ORIGINAL:

Los infrascriptos, á saber, JUAN CLIMACO ORDÓÑEZ, Secretario de Hacienda del Gobierno de NUEVA GRANADA, á nombre de dicho Gobierno; y POWLES, ILLINGWORTH, WILLSON i Cª, como Agentes de los Tenedores de valores de la Deuda de Colombia, hemos convenido en las siguientes proposiciones:

1º. La Nueva Granada reconoce como deuda suya cincuenta por ciento de los empréstitos que contrató la República de Colombia en 1822 y 1824; cuya Deuda habia sido repudiada por el Gobierno de Venezuela como consiguientemente cancelada respecto de la mitad de su valor, y toma la Nueva Granada la otra mitad bajo las condiciones que se expresan enseguida.

2º. Los obligaciones que emita la Nueva Granada en conformidad con el artículo siguiente, devengará interes al uno por ciento anual durante los primeros años; el cual interés se aumentará en un cuarto por ciento en cada uno de los años siguientes, hasta que llegue al tres por ciento, que es el máximum de interés.

3º. En pago de los intereses vencidos i no pagados, y de los que vayan devengándose subsecuentemente hasta que se redima esta Deuda, se obligation New Granada á emitir obligaciones al cuatro por ciento del Principal, en la forma que se expresará enseguida.

4º. Estas billetes no devengará interes alguno en los diez i seis primeros años de su emisión. Pero en el diez i seis año devengará el uno por ciento, que se aumentará en un octavo por ciento en cada uno de los años siguientes, hasta que llegue al tres por ciento, que es el máximum de interés en la Nueva Granada.

5º. El pago de los intereses de las obligaciones Granadinas que se emitan según lo estipulado en el artículo 3º se hará en Londres por semestres vencidos; verificándose el pago del primer semestre desde primero de Junio de mil ochocientos cuarenta i cinco, i en las oficinas que se designe en Londres para esto, tomando por base la inversión de los fondos que se destinen al pago de esta obligación.

6º. Los valores de ambas Deudas serán admitidos á la par en pago de los derechos de Aduana que se causen después de remitida esta Deuda, destinada por los tesoreros á Amortización de la misma.

7º. El producto neto de la Renta del Tabaco i la mitad del Producto de los Aduanas, quedan afectos al cumplimiento de este contrato.

8º. Estas proposiciones obligarán al Gobierno de la Nueva Granada desde que este fuere santificada por los interesados, al afecto d Gobierno Granadino dado inmediatamente sus ordenes para que se observe las disposiciones que las suscriben se cumpla en todas sus partes; i en caso de que alguno de los suscriptores falta al cumplimiento de lo que en este contrato se conviene, Sí los suscriptores se las suscriben, todo vida convenio será salo i ninguno sobre respecto de ambas partes.

En fé de lo cual firmamos el presente en Bogotá á quince de Enero de mil ochocientos cuarenta i cinco.

JUAN CLIMACO ORDÓÑEZ
POWLES, ILLINGWORTH, WILLSON i Cª

En Copia,
El Secretario de Hacienda, J. C. ORDÓÑEZ.

---

### ENGLISH TRANSLATION:

The undersigned, namely JUAN CLIMACO ORDÓÑEZ, Secretary of the Treasury of the Government of NEW GRANADA, in the name of said Government; and POWLES, ILLINGWORTH, WILLSON & Co., as Agents

title: Rjasan Uralsk Spoorweg Maatschappij: 4½% Certificaat A No. 1977, f. 1,200 (Amsterdam, July 14, 1903)
description: A 4½% Certificaat A No. 1977 of the Rjasan Uralsk Spoorweg Maatschappij (Ryazan-Ural Railway Company), denomination f. 1,200 Dutch guilders (Twaalf Honderd Gulden). The Ryazan-Koslov (Rjasan Koslov) Railway Company was established in Moscow under the Imperial Ukase of January 11, 1897. Issued by the Algemeene Trust Maatschappij (General Trust Company) in Amsterdam, July 14, 1903. This consolidated certificate replaces twelve prior Certificaten A No. 2722, each of f. 100. All obligations, rights, and conditions of the Ryazan-Ural Railway Maatschappij company bond remain attached, and the certificate is subject to Article 6 of the relevant terms. The Ryazan-Ural Railway linked the Ryazan region to the Ural industrial and agricultural zone, playing a key role in Russian grain export logistics. Dutch certificates for Russian railway bonds were a staple of Amsterdam investment portfolios in the early 20th century.
creator: Rjasan Uralsk Spoorweg Maatschappij; Algemeene Trust Maatschappij
notes: 4½% Certificaat A No. 1977, f. 1,200. Rjasan Uralsk Spoorweg Maatschappij (Ryazan-Ural Railway). Algemeene Trust Maatschappij, Amsterdam, July 14, 1903. Replaces 12× Certificaat A No. 2722 at f. 100 each. Imperial Ukase January 11, 1897.

title: The Rock Island Company: Dutch Certificaat, $1,000 Common Stock (No. 52108, Amsterdam)
description: A Dutch Certificaat No. 52108 of $1,000 Common Stock of The Rock Island Company (Chicago, Rock Island and Pacific Railway), administered by the Amsterdam Administratie-Kantoor of Broes en Goosman, Ten Have en van Essen, Jarman en Zoonen, and Plantenga. The holder is entitled to the benefits of one share of $1,000 Common Stock in The Rock Island Company under the conditions of the Dutch administration agreement. Printed in pink/red on cream paper. Last dividend date noted. The Rock Island Company was one of the major American Midwestern railroads, operating routes from Chicago westward. Amsterdam-listed certificates for American railroad shares were widely held by Dutch retail investors seeking exposure to the booming American railway sector in the late 19th and early 20th centuries.

title: Vereeniging tot Bevordering van 's Lands Weerbaarheid: Loterij-Geldleening f. 1,000,000, Series 7734 No. 38 (Rotterdam, May 1877)
description: A lottery bond (Loterij-Geldleening) of the Dutch Association for the Promotion of National Defense (Vereeniging tot Bevordering van 's Lands Weerbaarheid), Series 7734, No. Acht en Dertig (38). Total loan: f. 1,000,000, divided into 400,000 shares at f. 2.50 each, approved by Ministerial Resolution of July 8, 1878, No. 28. Issued in Rotterdam, May 1877. On July 31 and January 15 annually, 12,000 shares are drawn for prizes, with top prizes of f. 10,000, f. 5,000, f. 2,000, f. 1,000, f. 500, f. 200, f. 100, f. 75, f. 50, f. 20, and smaller amounts. The certificate lists the full prize table and redemption conditions. The Vereeniging tot Bevordering van 's Lands Weerbaarheid (Association for National Defense Readiness) was a Dutch patriotic association that used lottery bonds to raise funds for Dutch military preparedness in the post-1870 period.

title: Imperial Russian State Debt Commission: 5% Perpetual Income Certificate (Непрерывный Доход), 960 Roubles (1822)
description: A printed 5% perpetual income certificate (Свидетельство о непрерывном доходе по 5 на сто / "Nepreryvny Dokhod") issued by the Imperial Russian State Commission for the Repayment of Debts (Государственная Комиссия погашения долгов). Denomination: 960 Roubles, registered in the State Debt Book (Государственная долговая книга) on March 1, 1822. The Imperial Russian double-headed eagle is displayed prominently at top. The certificate entitles the holder to perpetual income at 5% per annum, payable semi-annually from the State Treasury. Conditions regarding transfer, inheritance, and interest payment are printed below. The "Nepreryvny Dokhod" (perpetual income) certificates were the Russian equivalent of Western government consols, representing a permanent claim on state revenues, and were among the principal instruments of 19th-century Russian public finance.
creator: Imperial Russian State Commission for the Repayment of Debts
notes: Russian 5% Perpetual Income Certificate (Непрерывный Доход по 5 на сто), 960 Roubles. Registered State Debt Book, March 1, 1822. Imperial double-headed eagle. Государственная Комиссия погашения долгов. Perpetual 5% income, semi-annual payments. Russian equivalent of consols.

title: Inscription au Grand Livre de la Dette Publique de Russie: Commission Impériale d'Amortissement, Première Série (1876)
description: A French-language certificate of inscription in the Grand Livre de la dette publique de Russie (Grand Ledger of Russian Public Debt), administered by the Commission Impériale d'amortissement (Imperial Amortization Commission), 1876, Première Série (First Series). Inscribed for a capital of [?] Roubles in silver, in the names of Ketwich & Voombergh and Mme. W. Borski in Amsterdam, with an annual income (revenu annuel) of 500 Roubles. Payable on the 1st and 15th of March and the 1st and 15th of September (four times yearly). Signed by the Director of the Commission. Includes an Extrait du Règlement de la Commission, Chap. II, describing the conditions of the inscription. This certificate documents Dutch investment in Russian sovereign debt via the Commission Impériale d'amortissement's registration system, a key channel for foreign capital into Russia.

**EXTRACT FROM THE COMMISSION REGULATIONS CHAP. II.**

The Director of the Commission
[Signature]

title: Билет Государственной Комиссии Погашения Долгов: Perpetual Capital Certificate (Капитал Безсрочный), 300 Roubles (1839)
description: A printed certificate (Билет) of the Russian State Commission for the Repayment of Debts (Государственная Комиссия погашения долгов), issued 1839, for a Perpetual Capital (Капитал Безсрочный) of 300 Roubles. The Imperial Russian double-headed eagle is prominently displayed. The certificate confirms the holder's perpetual deposit in the State Debt Book (Государственная Долговая Книга), with income at 6% per annum, paid semi-annually on January 15 and July 15 (по Шести на Сто). Signed by the Administrator and Bookkeeper of the Commission. An important early 19th-century Russian state finance instrument representing a perpetual income claim on the Russian Treasury, functionally equivalent to Western consols or rentes perpétuelles.
creator: Russian State Commission for the Repayment of Debts
notes: Билет Государственной Комиссии погашения долгов. Perpetual Capital (Капитал Безсрочный), 300 Roubles. 1839. Income 6% p.a., paid January 15 and July 15. Imperial double-headed eagle. Signed by Administrator and Bookkeeper.

title: Билет Государственной Комиссии Погашения Долгов: Perpetual Capital Certificate (Капитал Безсрочный), 50 Roubles in Silver (1854)
description: A printed certificate (Билет) of the Russian State Commission for the Repayment of Debts, issued 1854, for a Perpetual Capital (Капитал Безсрочный) of 50 Roubles in Russian silver currency (Российским серебряным монетою). The Imperial Russian double-headed eagle is displayed at top. Income at 6% per annum (по Шести на Сто), payable semi-annually on January 15 and July 15 (по 15 Генваря и по 15-го Июля). Signed by the Administrator and Bookkeeper of the Commission. The silver denomination reflects Russia's monetary reform of 1839–1843 (the Kankrin Reform), which stabilized the ruble on a silver standard. This certificate is from the same series as the 1839 certificate (goetzmann0518) but represents a later issuance under Tsar Nicholas I, with the specific designation of silver currency.
creator: Russian State Commission for the Repayment of Debts
notes: Билет Государственной Комиссии погашения долгов. Perpetual Capital (Капитал Безсрочный), 50 Roubles in Russian silver (серебряным монетою). 1854. Income 6% p.a., paid January 15 and July 15. Imperial double-headed eagle. Post-Kankrin monetary reform denomination.

title: Hope en Comp., Ketwich en Voombergh, en Wed. W. Borski: 6% Certificaat / Certificat d'Inscription Russe, Russische Fondsen in Zilver, 500 Roubles (No. 670, Amsterdam, November 19, 1857)
description: A bilingual Dutch/French certificate No. 670 (R° 500 Roubles) for 6% Russian Funds in Silver (Russische Fondsen in Zilver / Inscription Russe en Argent), issued in Amsterdam on November 19, 1857, by Hope en Comp., Ketwich en Voombergh, and Weduwe W. Borski. The certificate represents a 500-ruble inscription in the Russian State Debt Book (Grand Livre de St. Pétersbourg / Groot Boek te St. Petersburg), held by the Bureau d'Administration established in Amsterdam under direction of the issuing firms. Interest at 6% per annum, payable for the account and risk of the holder on Coupons (Art. 6 of the published notice). The holder may at any time reclaim the original inscription against return of the certificate and unused coupons. Includes ten half-yearly coupon tickets. A standard Amsterdam intermediary instrument for Dutch retail investment in Russian government silver bonds.
creator: Hope en Comp.; Ketwich en Voombergh; Weduwe W. Borski
notes: 6% Certificaat/Certificat d'Inscription Russe, Russische Fondsen in Zilver/en Argent. No. 670, R° 500 Roubles. Amsterdam, November 19, 1857. Hope en Comp.; Ketwich en Voombergh; Wed. W. Borski. Interest 6% p.a. Ten half-yearly coupon tickets.

title: Hope en Comp., Ketwich en Voombergh, Wed. W. Borski: 6% Certificaat / Certificat d'Inscription Russe, Russische Fondsen in Bank-Assignatien, 1000 Roubles (No. 4031, Amsterdam, 1822)
description: A bilingual Dutch/French certificate No. 4031 (R° 1,000 Roubles) for 6% Russian Funds in Bank Assignations (Russische Fondsen in Bank-Assignatien / Inscription Russe en Assignations de Banque), issued in Amsterdam on August 16, 1822, by Hope en Comp., Ketwich en Voombergh, and Weduwe W. Borski. The certificate represents a 1,000-ruble inscription in the Russian State Debt Book in bank assignations (Russian paper currency of the era). Interest at 6% per annum. Includes ten half-yearly coupon tickets (deliverable from July 1, 1829). Signed by Ketwich Voombergh and partner; witnessed by a notary. The denomination in Bank Assignations (paper rubles) predates Russia's 1839 Kankrin Reform, which established a silver standard. An earlier example in the same series as No. 670 (goetzmann0520), illustrating the evolution of Dutch-intermediated Russian debt instruments.

Borski. Ten half-yearly coupons (from July 1, 1829). Denominated in Bank Assignations (pre-Kankrin Reform Russian paper currency).

title: Sillem, Benecke & Comp. and H.J. Stresow: Certificaat No. 13549, 500 Roubles 5% Russische Fondsen betaalbaar in Metalick (Hamburg, March 13, 1821)
description: A Dutch certificaat No. 13549 issued in Hamburg on March 13, 1821, for 500 Roubles of 5% Russian Funds payable in metal (Metalick / specie). Administered by the Heeren Sillem, Benecke & Comp. and Heer H.J. Stresow, who established an administrative bureau in Hamburg for Russian State Inscriptions (Russische Inschrijvingen) registered in the Grand Ledger in the name of the Hamburg administration. Interest at 5% per annum, received for the account and risk of the holder, paid on Coupons per Art. 5 of the published notice. The holder may at any time reclaim the original inscription of 500 Roubles against return of the certificate and unused coupons. Signed by Sillem Benecke & Co. and H.J. Stren [?]. Notarized by N. Dongkants. Side stamp: Kantoor van Administratie. Illustrates the Hamburg market's significant role in distributing Russian government bonds to northern European investors in the early 19th century.
creator: Sillem, Benecke & Comp.; H.J. Stresow
notes: Certificaat No. 13549, 500 Roubles 5% Russian Funds payable in Metalick (specie). Sillem, Benecke & Comp. and H.J. Stresow, Hamburg. Grand Ledger inscription. Hamburg, March 13, 1821. Notary: N. Dongkants. Hamburg-issued Dutch certificate for Russian government debt.

title: Volksstaat Hessen: Schuldverschreibung, Eine Einheit der 6% Braunkohle-Roggen-Anleihe von 1923 (Reihe 37, No. 20002, Darmstadt, April 5, 1923)
description: A Schuldverschreibung (bond certificate) of the Volksstaat Hessen (People's State of Hesse), one unit (Eine Einheit) of the 6% Braunkohle-Roggen-Anleihe (Brown Coal-Rye Loan) of 1923. Reihe (Series) 37, No. 20002. Issued in Darmstadt, April 5, 1923, by the Hessische Staatsschuldenverwaltung (Hessian State Debt Administration). The bond carries 6% annual interest, payable on May 1 and November 1. The unit's monetary value (Geldwert) is indexed to the market price of brown coal and rye — a commodity-linked bond designed to preserve real value against the hyperinflationary collapse of the Reichsmark then underway. The loan runs from May 1, 1923 to April 30, 1924, with early redemption possible from May 1, 1928 at 2% annually. Attached are 30 Zinscheine (interest coupons) and 1 Erneuerungsschein (renewal coupon). The commodity-indexed "Sachwertanleihe" (real-value bond) was a widespread Weimar-era innovation.
creator: Hessische Staatsschuldenverwaltung (Volksstaat Hessen)
notes: Schuldverschreibung, Volksstaat Hessen. Eine Einheit, 6% Braunkohle-Roggen-Anleihe 1923. Reihe 37, No. 20002. Darmstadt, April 5, 1923. Value indexed to brown coal and rye prices. 30 interest + 1 renewal coupon. Weimar hyperinflation commodity-indexed bond (Sachwertanleihe).
transcription:
# Transcription

**Original (German):**

Reihe 37                           Eine                              № 20002
                                   Einheit

Schuldverschreibung

des

Volksstaats Hessen

über den Geldwert von 1 Einheit der 6% Braunkohle-Roggen-
Anleihe von 1923.

Gegenwärtige Schuldverschreibung ist für jeden Inhaber gültig. 30 Zinscheine
und 1 Erneuerungsschein sind beigefügt.

Das Zinsjahr läuft vom 1. Mai bis 30. April. Die Zinsen werden jährlich
gezahlt am 1. Mai und am 1. November fällig, erstmals am 1. Februar 1924 für die Zeit
vom 1. Mai 1923 bis 30. April 1924.

Die Rückzahlung der Anleihe erfolgt durch Auslosung über freihandigen
Rückkauf vom 1. Mai 1926 an mit jährlich 2 2/10 des ursprünglichen Gesamtnennn-
betrages. Vom 1. Mai 1928 an können die noch umlaufenden Schuldbor-
schreibungen mit halbjährlicher Frist ganz oder teilweise gekündigt werden.
Für die Anleihe haftet der hessische Staat mit seiner Steuerkraft und seinem
gesamten Vermögen.

Im übrigen gelten die auf der Rückseite abgedruckten Bedingungen.

Darmstadt, den 5. April 1923.

Hessische Staatsschuldenverwaltung.

Ausgefertigt: [signature]

---

**English translation:**

Series 37                          One                               No. 20002
                                   Unit

Debt Certificate

of the

People's State of Hesse

for the monetary value of 1 unit of the 6% Brown Coal-Rye
Loan of 1923.

This debt certificate is valid for any bearer. 30 interest coupons
and 1 renewal coupon are attached.

The interest year runs from May 1 to April 30. Interest is paid annually,
due on May 1 and November 1, first due on February 1, 1924 for the period
from May 1, 1923 to April 30, 1924.

Repayment of the loan occurs by drawing lots via free negotiated
repurchase from May 1, 1926 onward at 2 2/10 percent annually of the original total
principal amount. From May 1, 1928 onward, the still-circulating debt
certificates may be terminated in whole or in part with six-month notice.
The Hessian state guarantees the loan with its tax revenue and its
entire assets.

Moreover, the conditions printed on the reverse side apply.

Darmstadt, April 5, 1923.

Hessian State Debt Administration.

Executed: [signature]

- **State seal**: Official circular seal of the Hessian State Debt Administration (Hessische Staatsschuldenverwaltung) at lower left
- **Signature**: Handwritten signature of an authorized official at lower right, marked "Ausgefertigt" (executed)
- **Ornamental border**: Elaborate decorative frame with geometric and floral patterns typical of Weimar-era bond certificates
- **Series and number**: Reihe 37, № 20002 printed at top right
- **Printer mark**: "H. HERRMANN, DARMSTADT" at bottom left

title: Conversion of Spanish Debt: Passive Stock Certificate No. 457, £14 3s 4d (London, May 4, 1835)
description: A certificate No. 457 for Fourteen Pounds Three Shillings and Four Pence (£14.3.4) of Spanish Five Per Cent Passive Stock, issued as part of the Conversion of Spanish Debt in London, May 4, 1835. The bearer is entitled to this sum of Passive Stock, and upon presentation of sufficient certificates forming together any of the listed standard amounts (£42.10, £85, £170, £255, £510, £1,020), the undersigned engages to deliver in exchange a Bond of the Spanish Government. Signed by [?] Ricardo. The Spanish Passive Stock represented capitalized arrears of interest on Spanish government bonds, converted into a new class of deferred-interest securities as part of the Martínez de la Rosa government's 1834–35 restructuring of Spain's troubled external debt. These fractional certificates circulated on the London market as interim claims pending conversion into full bonds.
creator: Spanish Government (London agent: Ricardo)
notes: Conversion of Spanish Debt, Passive Stock Certificate No. 457, £14 3s 4d. Spanish 5% Passive Stock. Bearer exchanges certificate at standard amounts: £42.10, £85, £170, £255, £510, £1,020 for Spanish government bond. Signed by [Ricardo]. London, May 4, 1835. 1834–35 Spanish debt restructuring.

title: London Stock Exchange: WWI Regulation 10 Good Delivery Certificate, City of Baker 5% 1910 Bond No. 58707 (April 29, 1916)
description: A printed certificate issued under the Temporary Regulations for the Re-Opening of the Stock Exchange, Regulation 10, certifying that the security "City of Baker 5% 1910, Bond for £20, Numbered 58707" has been expressly passed by the Committee as a good delivery, special cause having been shown. Signed by the Secretary, Share and Loan Department, Stock Exchange, dated April 29, 1916. Regulation 10 required that no securities to bearer or endorsed in blank could be delivered as good delivery unless stamped prior to October 1, 1914 and accompanied by a broker/dealer declaration, or specially passed by the Committee. The regulations were introduced at the outbreak of World War I to prevent trading in securities possibly held by enemy nationals.
creator: London Stock Exchange (Share and Loan Department)
notes: London Stock Exchange WWI Regulation 10 good delivery certificate. City of Baker 5% 1910 Bond No. 58707, £20. Signed Secretary, Share and Loan Department. April 29, 1916. WWI wartime enemy-ownership regulations.
transcription:
# Transcription

**TEMPORARY REGULATIONS FOR THE RE-OPENING OF THE STOCK EXCHANGE.**

---

**REGULATION 10.**

No Securities to bearer or endorsed in blank will be a good delivery unless impressed with the Government stamp dated previous to the 1st October and accompanied by a declaration by a Banker, Broker or other responsible party that they have not, since the outbreak of war, been in enemy ownership, except in cases permitted under Regulation 4 (3) or Securities sent for registration abroad under Clause 9 and such other Securities as may be expressly passed by the Committee as a good delivery on special cause being shown.

Title of Security .... *City of Baker 5% 1910* ....

Bond for £20 Numbered .... *58707* ....

I hereby certify that the above Security has been expressly passed by the Committee as a good delivery, special cause having been shown.

Signature .... *[Handwritten signature - illegible]* ....

Secretary .... *SECRETARY, SHARE AND LOAN DEPARTMENT, STOCK EXCHANGE.* ....

Date .... *29 4 1916* ....

---

**REGULATION 4 (3).** — No dealings will be allowed in any new issue, other than an issue of British Government Securities made after the 4th January, 1915, unless specially allowed by the Committee and approved by the Treasury.

**REGULATION 9 (Sentence 3).** — Securities which have been transmitted abroad merely for registration shall for the purpose of this regulation be deemed to have remained in physical possession in the United Kingdom.

**REGULATION 11.** — *The expression "Enemy" in these regulations means any person or body of persons of whatever nationality, resident, or carrying on business in an enemy country, but does not include persons of enemy nationality who are neither resident nor carrying on business in an enemy country. In the case of incorporated bodies, enemy character attaches only to those incorporated in an enemy country.*

COPYRIGHT. — Printed and sold by E. COUCHMAN & CO., LTD., 23, Throgmorton Street, E.C.

---

## Notable markings:

- Handwritten signature (Secretary's signature - illegible)
- Date stamp: "29 4 1916" (29 April 1916)
- Bond number: 58707
- Bond denomination: £20
- Security title: City of Baker 5% 1910
- Printed stamp: "SECRETARY, SHARE AND LOAN DEPARTMENT, STOCK EXCHANGE."
- Copyright attribution to E. Couchman & Co., Ltd.

title: Treasury of the United States: 6% Funded Debt Certificate, $10,000, Daniel Ternwiles & Wife (Register Office, June 16, 1792)
description: A printed and handwritten certificate from the Treasury of the United States, Register Office, dated June 16, 1792, No. 42.94. "Be it known that there is due from the United States of America unto Daniel Ternwiles & Wife, or their Assigns, the Sum of Ten Thousand Dollars, bearing Interest at Six per Cent per Annum from the first Day of January A.D. One Thousand Eight Hundred and One, inclusively; payable quarter-yearly, and subject to Redemption by Payments not exceeding, in one Year, the Proportion of Eight Dollars upon a Hundred of the Stock bearing Interest at Six per Cent; created by Virtue of an Act making Provision for the Debt of the United States, passed on the fourth Day of August, 1790." Signed by Joseph Nourse, Register of the Treasury. Notarized by Clement Biddle, Notary Public for the Commonwealth of Pennsylvania. A key instrument of Alexander Hamilton's federal debt consolidation under the Funding Act of 1790.

title: Bewijs van Voldoening aan de Wet: Uitgestelde Schuld Certificate No. 6231, f. 500 (Netherlands, ca. 1814–1815)
description: A Dutch certificate of compliance with the law (Bewijs van Voldoening aan de Wet) No. 6231, for a Uitgestelde Schuld (Deferred/Delayed Debt) of f. 500 capital, issued pursuant to the Commission's decree (Besluit van Z.K.H.) of June 7, 1814, No. 23, concerning the Wisselbank exchange advances and conditions of the Wet (Law) of May 14, 1814. Holder W.F. Holling and the Burgen[?] are recorded. The certificate confirms compliance with Articles 5, 12, and 18 of the aforementioned Law, with the Kapitalen recorded in the Oude Grootboek der (Hollandsche Schuld), re-registered in the name of C. Niemanstedt [?]. References Afschrijvings-Billet No. [?] van het Grootboek, and a new Grootboek amount of Veertien Honderd Guldens. The Uitgestelde Schuld was the class of Dutch national debt whose interest payments were deferred during the Napoleonic period, restructured after the restoration of the Dutch monarchy in 1813-1814.
creator: Dutch Government Commission (Commissie voor de Uitgestelde Schuld)
notes: Bewijs van Voldoening aan de Wet, Uitgestelde Schuld No. 6231, f. 500. Pursuant to Besluit Z.K.H. June 7, 1814, No. 23, and Wet of May 14, 1814. Holder: W.F. Holling. Post-Napoleonic Dutch deferred debt restructuring. Recorded in Hollandsche Schuld Grootboek.

blijkens AFSCHRIJVINGS-BILLET No. [?] van het GROOTBOEK zal deze Rekening overeenkomstig Art. 16 der Wet, op het Nieuwe Grootboek der UITGESTELDE SCHULD, ten voorlichreven hoofde worden overgebracht ter Somma van [Veertien Honderd Guldens] welke misdien reeds zal deelen in de kans der Eerste Uitlooting van dezelven.
