title: Volksstaat Hessen: Roggen-Anleihe, 5% Schuldverschreibung über den Geldwert von 10 Zentner Roggen (Abteilung 1 No. 002152, Darmstadt, December 12, 1923)
description: A 5% Schuldverschreibung (bond certificate) of the Volksstaat Hessen (People's State of Hesse), Roggen-Anleihe (Rye Loan), Abteilung 1, Buchstabe D, No. 002152. The bond's face value equals the market price of 10 Zentner (hundredweights) of rye (Roggen), denominated to preserve real purchasing power during the German hyperinflation. Issued in Darmstadt, December 12, 1923, by the Hessische Staatsschuldenverwaltung (Hessian State Debt Administration). The State of Hesse pledges its entire property and revenues as security. The bond carries 5% annual interest and is part of the broader Weimar-era practice of issuing commodity-indexed Sachwertanleihen (real-value bonds) tied to agricultural and industrial commodities to circumvent the destruction of nominal monetary values by hyperinflation.
creator: Hessische Staatsschuldenverwaltung (Volksstaat Hessen)
notes: Roggen-Anleihe, Volksstaat Hessen. 5% Schuldverschreibung, 10 Zentner Roggen. Abteilung 1, Buchst. D, No. 002152. Darmstadt, December 12, 1923. Hessische Staatsschuldenverwaltung. Commodity-indexed rye bond (Sachwertanleihe). Weimar hyperinflation.

title: Conditien Eener Negotiatie: Suriname Plantation Loan, f. 1,000,000, Lever en de Bruine, Directors; Samuel & Jacobus Walland, Commissioners (Amsterdam)
description: A printed prospectus of conditions (Conditien eener Negotiatie) for a plantation mortgage negotiatie of one million (Tienmaal honderd Duyzend) Dutch guilders, under the direction of Lever en de Bruine, Kooplieden t'Amsterdam, and with Samuel en Jacobus Walland as Commissioners. The negotiatie is open to Planters in the COLONIE ZURINAMEN (Suriname) who wish to participate. The conditions set out the mortgage obligations (Hypotheek) on the colonial plantations, the terms for loans to planters, the administration of the fund by the Amsterdam directors, the role of the Local Director of the Colony of Suriname, and the conditions under which the planters' production and property is pledged as security. This is an example of the Dutch colonial plantation mortgage fund system (plantageleningen), through which Amsterdam investors financed West Indian sugar, coffee, and cotton plantations.
creator: Lever en de Bruine; Samuel en Jacobus Walland (Commissioners)
notes: Conditien eener Negotiatie, f. 1,000,000. Suriname plantation mortgage negotiatie. Directors: Lever en de Bruine, Amsterdam. Commissioners: Samuel & Jacobus Walland. Dutch plantageleningen system for West Indian colonial plantation finance.
transcription:
# Original:

CONDITIEN EENER NEGOTIATIE
Van een Millioen of Tienmaal honderd Duyfend Guldens, onder Directie van de Heeren LEVER EN DE BRUINE, Kooplieden te Amsterdam, en ten overflaan van de Heeren SAMUEL en JACOBUS WALLAND, als Commisariffen deczer Negotiatie en in alle gevallen Geauthorifeerde Representanten van de Gevolmachtigde Geinterseffeerders in dezelve, ten behoeve van Planlyke Geintereffeerders in de COLONIE ZURINAMEN, welke geneecgen zyn en aaneemlyк bevonden worden daar in Deel te neemen, opgerigt.

Art. I.

De Planters dewelke geadmitteerd zullen worden, uit deeze Negotiatie Penningen op te neemen, zullen hunne Plantagiën (met uytfluitiing van Meubilen en andere Zaaken, onnodig tot het voortzetten derfelven) behoorlyк doen Inventariseeren en door besiglde oin daar toe aangeftelde Prijzeurs doen prijzeeren.

En wyders die Plantagiën met alle derzelver aan en toebehooren Legaal en Speciaal na style Locaal der COLONIE ZURINAMEN, verbinden en verhypothequeeeren aan de Heeren Directeurs dezer Negotiatie, en zulks voor en ten behoeven der Negotiatie, welck Hypotheeck geftarkt zal moeten worden met willinge Condemnatie van den Ed. Hove van Civiel Justitie der gedagte Colonie.

Art. II.

De Planters zullen zig by dat Hypotheecq verpligten, alle de producten profluerende van hunne Plantagien, van tyd tot tyd te Configneren aan de Directeuren dezer Negotiatie en van dezelven hunnen benodigheden voor de Plantagien te doen toekomeli, en wyders overgefte dat ingavallen zy daar pan niet mogten voldoen, of hunne Producten in ZURINAMEN kwamen te verkoopen of op andere wyzen elders te diverteeren, het by hun verschuldigde Capitaal met de Intresten en kosten diretkelijk kan teeren gebruikt en by Executie ingevorderd by Executie ingevorderd zy by dezelven hun verhalt aan hunne Persoonen, Plantagiën, roerenде en onroerenде Goederеn.

Art. III.

De Heeren Directeuren zullen moeten werden geequalificeerd eens voor al tot het laten doen van Affuitantie, zo op de benodigtheeden, als op de af te zenden producten, van welke afzending de Planters ter behooryker tyd advertentie zullen moeten geeven.

Art. IV.

De Planters verbinden zig, om van de verfchuldigde Somma Jaarlycks te betaalen de Intreffеn van zes percent tot de darelycke voldoening en aflossing toe, welke Intreffеn uit de Producten der Plantagiën voor alle anderen schulden zullen moeten werden voldaan.

Art. V.

De Restitutie of aflossing der Capitaalen zal gefchieden in Termynen, duydelyk in het Hypotheeck te expreffeeren, zodanig die door de Planters zullen werden verkoeен, edog welke verfchuldige zodanig zal moeten zyn gereculeerd, dat binnen de tyd van 20 Jaaren de Termynen wel micerder, maar niet minder dan 10 percento zal mogen bedragen.

Art. VI.

De Planters zullen gehoude zyn binnen drie Maanden, na dat zy de Jaarlycke Reckening Courant van de Heeren Directeuren bekomen hebben, dezelve te approbeeren of hunne belanпgen daar tegen op te geeven en aan Directeuren toe te zenden, en zulks niet gedaan hebbende, zal de Reckening als door hun geapprobeert zynde, gehoude worden.

[Followed by signature mark and "A" at bottom]

---

## English translation:

CONDITIONS OF A NEGOTIATION
For one Million or Ten times one hundred thousand Guilders, under the Direction of the Messrs. LEVER AND DE BRUINE, Merchants in Amsterdam, and with the representation of the Messrs. SAMUEL and JACOBUS WALLAND, as Commissioners of this Negotiation and in all cases duly Authorized Representatives of the Authorized Shareholders in the same, for the benefit of Planters and interested Shareholders in the COLONY OF SURINAME, who are willing and found suitable to participate therein, established.

Art. I.

The Planters who shall be admitted to take up monies from this Negotiation shall cause their Plantations (with the exclusion of Movables and other goods unnecessary for the continuation thereof) to be properly inventoried and valued by appointed Appraisers.

And further they shall bind their Plantations with all that belongs thereto legally and specially in the manner customary in the COLONY OF SURINAME, and mortgage them to the Messrs. Directors of this Negotiation, and this for and on behalf of the Negotiation, which Mortgage shall be strengthened with willing Condemnation by the Honored Court of Civil Justice of the said Colony.

Art. II.

The Planters shall obligate themselves by that Mortgage to assign all the products flowing from their Plantations, from time to time, to the Directors of this Negotiation and to receive from the same their necessities for the Plantations; and further that if it should happen that they cannot satisfy this, or that their Products are sold in SURINAME or diverted elsewhere in any other way, the Capital owed by them with the Interest and costs can be directly pursued and recovered by Execution, with recourse against all their Persons, Plantations, movable and immovable Goods.

Art. III.

The Messrs. Directors shall be authorized once and for all to make settlements regarding both the necessary supplies and the products to be sent, of which shipment the Planters shall give proper notice in due time.

Art. IV.

The Planters obligate themselves to pay yearly on the owed Sum the interest of six percent toward the yearly payment and repayment thereof, which Interest from the Products of the Plantations shall be satisfied before all other debts.

Art. V.

The Repayment or redemption of the Capitals shall occur in Terms, clearly expressed in the Mortgage, to be determined by the Planters themselves, provided that such payment obligation shall be so calculated that within the period of 20 years the Terms may become less, but not less than 10 percent per annum.

Art. VI.

The Planters shall be obligated within three Months after they have received the yearly Account Current from the Messrs. Directors to approve the same or give notice of their objections thereto and send them to the Directors, and if they do not do so, the Account shall be held as approved by them and shall be maintained accordingly.

title: Negotiatie, f. 75,000: Loterij ten behoeve van het Weeshuys der Stad Vlaardingen, Bataafschen Volks (May 9, 1800)
description: A printed lottery/negotiatie prospectus of f. 75,000 Dutch guilders (Vyf en Zeventig Duyzend Gulden), constituted by order of the Vertegenwoordigend Lichaam ver Bataafschen Volks (Representative Body of the Batavian People), dated May 9, 1800. Established for the maintenance of the Orphanage (Weeshuys) of the city of Vlaardingen. The negotiatie consists of 1,500 shares at f. 50 each, with a lottery prize structure: the first prize is f. 10,000, then f. 5,000, f. 2,500, f. 1,000, f. 500, f. 250, f. 125, and smaller prizes, totaling f. 48,000. Prize drawings are held on July 31 and January 15 annually. Holders of the remaining (non-prize) classes receive annual interest (Instrerende Lijfrentebrieven) divided into five classes by age. An innovative Batavian Republic instrument combining charitable endowment, lottery, and life annuity.
creator: Vertegenwoordigend Lichaam van het Bataafschen Volks; Weeshuys Vlaardingen
notes: Negotiatie f. 75,000 / 1,500 shares at f. 50. For Weeshuys (Orphanage) of Vlaardingen. Batavian Republic, May 9, 1800. Lottery prizes: f.10,000 down to f.125. Annual life annuity interest by age class for non-prize holders.

title: Voor den Armen (For the Poor), Haarlem: Charitable Bond No. 110, f. 500 at 2½% (January 1, 1805)
description: A Dutch charitable bond No. 110 (Voor den Armen — For the Poor), Haarlem, issued by the Ontvanger der Stadslijke Belasting (Receiver of the City Tax), Haarlem, by authority of the special qualification of the Raad (Council) of the city, by resolution of June 5, 1804. The bond acknowledges receipt from M. Bodisco of the sum of Vyf-Honderd Guldens (f. 500) placed at interest at the rate of Penning 40 (2½%) per year from January 1, 1805. The capital (f. 500) and interest (f. 12.50 per year) are secured upon all city revenues and goods, especially the revenues of the former city Guilds. Multiple later annotations record coupon payments from 1822 through the 1860s. An example of municipal charitable bond finance in the Napoleonic-era Netherlands.
creator: City of Haarlem (Ontvanger der Stadslijke Belasting)
notes: Voor den Armen, Haarlem. Charitable bond No. 110, f. 500 at Penning 40 (2½%). Issued by Ontvanger der Stadslijke Belasting, Haarlem. Council resolution June 5, 1804. Signed M. Bodisco. Coupon annotations from 1822–1860s. Municipal charitable finance.

# VOOR DEN ARMEN

**Original:**

Num. 110. — Ik ondergeschrevene, Ontvanger van de STEDELKE BELASTING HAARLEM, geurresteerd den 3de April 1804, bekenne in die qualiteit, uit krachte der speciale qualificatie van den RAAD dezer Stad, bij Resolutie van den 5de June 1804 daartoe verleend, ontvangen te hebben van

de Somma van VYF-HONDERD GULDENS, geliggt op Infveft tegen den Penning 40. in 't jaar, te betalen over één Jaar na dato dezes, tot de effectueele Aflosing der Hoofdsomme toe, en tot meerdere verzekering is deeze bij STEDTS Wedouwdert dezer Stad gesgrocëerd en geteekend.
In oircode deze geteekend den eersten January 1805.

Capitaal f. 500 - - -
In Intresse = f.2-50-

Hierla afgeaam TEENEN
Striks COUPONS cum No. 1.
tot 16s, ledet groef f. 12-10-:
beginnende primo January 1806: Als izo de voorichteere Somma van VYF-HONDERD GULDENS, op Intrest geliggt op Penning 40. in 't jaar, aan dienste der Armen, aen de Stad verkrekt is: Z00 is 'T, dat wy 300 af goudert der Sad Haarlem / als daartoe special gequalificeerd, bij Resolutie van den RAAD, in dato den 14de December 1804, tot meerdere verzekering van de betaling van het vooricheve Capitaal en Intresfen, tot de effectueele Aflosling toe, verbonden hebben en verbinden bij dezen, ten behoeve van den voornoemden

alle de Stads-Inkomsten en Goederen, en speciaal de Inkomsten der voormalige GILDENS ingevolghe het 56e Articul der bovengemelde Publicate.

[Signature] Uting van Berthuall & S: Druijsteeÿn

In kennisse van my,

[Signature] Willm Spallang?

[Handwritten annotations below:]
1822 in August No Coupons
[unclear dates and amounts - 1822-1833 in Notaken?]
[unclear] in Januari van 1839-1853 Ingesloten
[unclear] 1854-1860[?]

No. 110. — I, the undersigned, Receiver of the CITY TAX of HAARLEM, appointed on the 3rd of April 1804, acknowledge in that capacity, by authority of the special qualification of the COUNCIL of this City, by Resolution of the 5th of June 1804 granted therefor, to have received

the Sum of FIVE HUNDRED GUILDERS, placed at interest at the rate of 40 Pennies per year, to be paid in full after one year from the date hereof, until the effectual Redemption of the Principal Sum, and for further security this is registered and signed by the CITY [authority] of this City.
Executed in duplicate this first day of January 1805.

Capital f. 500
Annual Interest = f. 12.50

Hereupon affixed COUPONS
Strict COUPONS cum No. 1.
to 16s, each coupon f. 12.10:
beginning primo January 1806: Now, as the aforesaid Sum of FIVE HUNDRED GUILDERS, placed at interest at 40 Pennies per year, in the service of the Poor, has been received by the City: SO it is, that we, [the official authority] of the City of Haarlem / being thereto specially qualified, by Resolution of the COUNCIL, dated the 14th of December 1804, for further security of the payment of the aforementioned Principal and Interest, until the effectual Redemption, bound ourselves and bind ourselves by these presents, for the benefit of the aforesaid

all the City-Revenues and Goods, and especially the Revenues of the former GUILDS in accordance with the 56th Article of the aforementioned Publication.

[Signature] [unclear]

In witness whereof,

[Signature] [unclear]

title: Wy Karel, de Tweede: Habsburg Royal Bond / Obligation Document (Amsterdam, early 18th Century)
description: A printed Dutch-language bond or obligation document beginning "Wy KAREL, de Tweede, door Gods/Godes Roome Keyser..." (We Charles, the Second, by the Grace of God, Holy Roman Emperor...). The document references Willem Gideon Donz. tot Amsterdam as a director or agent, and discusses shares (Aandelen) and obligations (Obligaties) in the context of a loan or financial instrument. The text mentions various parties, interest payments in Hollandsch Courant Geld, and conditions related to a sovereign bond. "Karel de Tweede" here likely refers to Emperor Charles VI (1685–1740), also known as Charles III as a claimant to the Spanish throne, who was the Habsburg Holy Roman Emperor from 1711 to 1740 and sponsored various financial ventures including the Ostend Company. The document appears to be a Dutch-intermediated Habsburg imperial bond from the early 18th century.
creator: Habsburg Imperial Government (Charles VI / Karel de Tweede)
notes: Dutch bond/obligation document. "Wy KAREL, de Tweede, door Gods Roome Keyser..." Habsburg imperial bond. Agent: Willem Gideon Donz., Amsterdam. Aandelen and Obligaties in Hollandsch Courant Geld. Likely refers to Emperor Charles VI (1711–1740). Dutch-intermediated Habsburg imperial finance.
transcription:
# Transcription

**Original (Dutch):**

(1)

WY KAREL de Zesde, door Gods gnaden verkooren Rooms Keyzer, ten allerijen en Boheemien, Lands-Hertzog van Oost-enryck, Hertzog van Burgondien, Styrien, Carinthien, Crain, en Wirtemberg, in Opper- en Neder-Silezien, Markgraaf tot Mahren, in Opper en Neder-Lausitz, Graaf tot Habsburg, Tyrol en Gortz &c. beken-nen, voor Ons., onze Vryen en Nakomlingen, in kracht dezes schuldbrief openlijk., en doen door middel en allardeste van Willem Gideon Deutz, tot Amsterdam van verscheydene Persoonen en Particulieren, ten deele onderhet gebied van de Heeren Staaten der Vereenigde Nederlanden, ten deele ook op andere Plaatsen, woonende, ten dienst van onsen van het gemeente weezen genegetieerd, en by forme van beleeninge op-gebragt, zedert in handen van onze Keizerslyke schatkamer in contant geld, tegens de daar voor ontfangende behoorlyke equipanten, voldaan is zekere Capitaal, ende Courant geld, bedragende zevenaaml hondert duyzend gyldens Hollands Courant, en wie dierulyk in de gemeene Somme was vereymaid hondert duyzend Guldens Hollands Courant aen Willem Gideon Deutz., en verdere mede Geinterestlder dens., de welcke Bovenitaande leeningen te zamen hebben gekooten, verichulydt syn gewornen, en van dien aan tegen voor Ons., onze Vryen en Nakomlingen, voorwetens op voldaagdlyck, in de Interessen, voorwetens na voorgaande onderhandelinge daar over met syne daar toe benoemde Gevollmachtigdens gehoueden, tegeevoguiven, en vyt tieden beleeven, ioezeggen, en verrigsdelen, dat wy voor het geneelde for leen opgenoomene Capitaal 's jaarlyks aan Intereilen wy rato tot betalen sullen pro sexe. de vorige nominale opgenomene, en verrigde somme van zevenaal hondert duyzend gyldens Hollands Courant vyftien Jaaren binnen Amsterdam, in gelyke Hollandsche Courante Munt weder sullen betaelen en doen Edgesldo en in derzelvige jaren, synde Anno een duyzend zevenhonderd zeven en dertig, tot Anno een duyzend zevenhonderd zeven en veertig, en twee duyzend zevenhonderd aght en veertig, en twee duyzend zevenhonderd negen en veertig, een duyzend zevenhonderd vyftigh, en een duyzend zevenhonderd-rt een en vyftig, het Capitaal zelis telsens voor een Jaer geplaert wytti pro ratore, gehevens de Interesten pro rata op halve verloopen, algesoi werden val; zullende de vooriz. afslofinge te opzigtige van het Kapitaal van half Jaar tot half Jaar, doch de betaalnge der Interetlen alle vierdeel Jaars geschiedde, en wel in-pleids uyt de middelen door onze Hertzogdommen van Opper en Silzien bewilligd., en vantyd tot tyd iaadande vervallen, gelyk ook ten dien ten byhe trouwe en geneotse houden zullen aldaar, volgens onze genadedie Obligatie, zich in de beste forme van rechten verunbonden zenden, om de gemelde be'lligige, en door baar te voldoende's Lands Inkomsten ten erbelteemder ryd, op de hler vorengeltalte termynen, zo wel de gestipuleerde Inte-

resten, als het Kapitaal zelfs voor z. zevenaal hondert duyzend gyldens Hollands Courant, tot bilyk en welkomen geneogen van hem Willem Gideon Deutz, en der meede mede Geintereslders, zonder weinig manquement afgesiot, en over gelyke mede afslotinge door onze Vorten en Stauden in Silzien deswegens opgemaakt, dat namemtyk de allos-tinge zo wel van het ter leen gelichrickte Kapitaal, als der Interesten pro rata op de bo-ventlande wyze, en in de vattgestelde termynen, zonder cerize exception of uyttel geslcheiden zal, allesins voor aangenaam gehouden, en daar over onze genadigte toe-

WY

(1)

WE CHARLES the Second, by the Grace of God Elected Roman Emperor, [etc.]. To all and every one, whom this present deed may concern, be it known, that We, together with Our Heirs and Successors, do hereby openly and publicly declare and make known by this letter of obligation and bond, that through the mediation and directed agency of Willem Gideon Deutz, of Amsterdam, from diverse Persons and Individuals, partly within the jurisdiction of the States of the United Netherlands and partly residing in other Places, in service to our commonly administered treasury, and in the form of a loan, has been brought into the hands of Our Imperial Treasury in current money, against appropriate securities, a certain Capital Sum, and Holy Roman money, amounting to seven hundred thousand guilders in Holland Currency, and this common sum having been computed at one hundred thousand Guilders Holland Currency, [which] to Willem Gideon Deutz., and the further and mutual Interested Parties, the which aforementioned loans taken together have been subscribed, obligated and concluded between Us, Our Heirs and Successors, and from that time forth against Us, Our Heirs and Successors, with knowledge and agreement, on the condition and understanding that, having received the aforementioned loan amount of Capital, in yearly Interests We shall pay at a rate to the existing and stipulated Interest pro rata, the previous nominal borrowed sum of seven hundred thousand guilders Holland Currency for fifteen Years within Amsterdam, in like Holland Currency Coinage, shall be repaid and discharged from Anno one thousand seven hundred seven and thirty, until Anno one thousand seven hundred seven and forty, and two thousand seven hundred eight and forty, and two thousand seven hundred nine and forty, one thousand seven hundred fifty, and one thousand seven hundred fifty-one, the Capital itself in each and every Year pro rata to be placed; such being the aforesaid discharge regarding the Capital from half Year to half Year, but the payment of the Interests every quarter Year shall be made, and well through the resources from Our Duchies of Upper and Lower Silesia granted, and from time to time as the aforementioned obligations fall due, as also at that time by his loyalty and promise shall hold themselves, according to our gracious Obligation, bound in the best form of law and of right, to fulfill and to execute's Lands Income for the aforementioned matter and time at the aforementioned terms, so well the stipulated Inte-

rests, as the Capital itself for said seven hundred thousand guilders Holland Currency, to the satisfaction and acceptance of him Willem Gideon Deutz, and the likewise also Interested Parties, without any hindrance whatsoever satisfied, and further over like discharge by Our Princes and States in Silesia for these purposes drawn up, that namely the payment so well of the amount loaned out of Capital, as of the Interests pro rata in the above manner, and in the time fixed, without any exception or evasion whatsoever shall be executed, all in every way for approved held, and thereupon our gracious...

title: German Government International 5½% Loan, 1930: Belgian Issue Bond to Bearer, 100 Belgas / 500 Belgian Francs (No. A.22399)
description: A trilingual (German/English/French) bearer bond No. A.22399 of the Internationale 5½%ige Anleihe des Deutschen Reichs 1930 / German Government International 5½ Per Cent. Loan, 1930 / Emprunt International 5½ P.C. 1930 du Gouvernement Allemand. Belgian issue (Belgische Ausgabe / Belgian Issue / Tranche Belge). Denomination: 100 (Hundred) Belgas = 500 (Five Hundred) Belgian Francs. Printed with an ornate design including the Weimar German eagle seal. Signed by the Reichsschuldenverwaltung (Reich Debt Administration). The Young Plan International Loan of 1930 was a major international bond issue by the Weimar Republic, placed simultaneously in multiple European currencies (British pounds, French francs, Belgian francs, Swedish kronor, Swiss francs, and US dollars) as part of the reparations settlement under the Young Plan.
creator: German Government (Reichsschuldenverwaltung)
notes: German Government International 5½% Loan 1930. Belgian Issue. No. A.22399, 100 Belgas = 500 Belgian Francs. Trilingual German/English/French. Reichsschuldenverwaltung. Young Plan international bond issue, simultaneous multi-currency placement.

title: Dutch Essequibo Plantation Negotiatie: De Vyver Plantation, Remy & Comp., Directors (Amsterdam, July 1789)
description: A Dutch legal document drawn up before notaries Albertus Bakker and Paules Cordes, Raaden in den Edelen Aghtbaren Heeren van Public, Criminele en Civiele Justicie der Rivier en onderhorige districten van Essequebo (Essequibo Colony). The parties include Johan Gottlieb Detericks and Vrouwe Johanna Maria von de Vyver, owners of plantations in Essequibo. The document concerns the establishment of a negotiatie (Nieuwe Creditors) for the plantation "De Vyver" and other Midland Plantations, with capital of f. 13,000 (Dertienduizend Guldens), under the management of Remy & Comp., Kooplieden t'Amsterdam as Directors, and with the Amsterdam firm directing operations under the terms of a July 1789 plan. This is an example of the Essequibo plantation mortgage lending system administered from Amsterdam in the late 18th century.
creator: Remy & Comp. (Amsterdam); Albertus Bakker; Paules Cordes (Notaries)
notes: Dutch Essequibo plantation negotiatie. "De Vyver" plantation and Midland Plantations. Capital f. 13,000. Directors: Remy & Comp., Amsterdam. Notaries: Albertus Bakker, Paules Cordes, Essequibo Colony. July 1789. Dutch colonial plantation mortgage system.
transcription:
# Transcription

**Original:**

Copie

Compareeren voor ons ALBERTUS BAKKER en PAULUS CORDES, Raaden in den Edelen Aghtbaren Heeren van Politie, Criminele en Civiele Justitie der Rivier en onderhorige districten van Essequebo,
De Heer JOHAN GOTTLIEB DETERICKS, en Vrouwe JOHANNA MARIA von de VYVER Echtelieden, zynde de Vrouwe Comparante ten deezen met dezelve haare Man geadmitteerd, en door hem tot redeneering en posteering dezes (speciaal gerechtigd[?]eerd;

Te kennen geevendes: dat zy Comparanten waarneemens zynde te Negotatoeren en van diverse Persoonen op te neemen, een Capitale Somma van TAG TIG DUYZEND GULDENS, HOLLANDS COURANT; ter meede voldooge van huune Crediteuers in den Vaderlanden, op Conditien en onder zodanige speciale Hypothecair Verband van de Comparanten nametmelde Plantage De VYVER als in 't vervolg zal werden gemeld; daar over by blisfive van dato 25 Augustus 1788. hadden gefchreeven, aan de Heeren REMY & COMP. Kooplieden t'Amsterdam, en dezelve met de Commisie gechargeerd, om het zelve aan hun Comparanten Crediteuers voortestellen, en unsere het moggelyck deeldens door hunne Interesses te perfeitaisueren, op het deelmeeing door deezen en dezelve Negotiatie byna volleekend was; Edog niet ziin onder de Rihere tyo die Agte van deelneming gemaeld, als:

**Articul 1.**

Dat de voorz. Negotiatie zal gefchieden onder directie van de Heeren REMY & COMP. Kooplieden t'Amsterdam, als Directeuren, en onder 't opgzegvang van de natetoemene Heeren Commissarischen'.

**2.**

Dat de voorz. Negotiatie groot en niet groozer zal mogez zyn, als TAGTIG DUYZEND GULDENS's Capitaal HOLLANDS COURANT;

**3.**

Dat de voorz. Negotiatie gfchied, voor den tyd van TIEN JAAREN, ingaande primo July 1789, op die van de na opgezegene Somma, 's Jaarlyka zal moeten werden afgeslost tien per Cento van 't Capital of AGT DUYZEND GULDENS, waar meede 's jaarlyka Sestien fluks der natetoemene te formeere Obligatien, en overplan van Noaris en genuygen uyrrelooten, zullen moeten worden afgeslost, op de respective verftchyiden, zynde primo July van vder Jaar, en motecende over zulks de certe Allosding eerste inschuiving primo Jaren af 1790, bencyens de Interessen, gereekend tegens zas per Cento in 't Jaar, eerot van 't geheele en vervolgeans van 't retgecende Capitaal;

**4.**

Dat door him Comparanten, direct na dat by hun ryding van de volleeckelung deezes Negotiatie zoude zyn bekoomea, ten behoeven van de respective Deelneemers in behoorfyke forma en orden renuntatiatie van afte hypotheken, als plaatse zoude kunnen en mogen hebben; Dezelve alle specificiid zullen moeten werden gepaasterd, behoorfyke en duuderkeike Acte van Obligatie en Hypothecq Inschrift en voorz. Plantage gemaeld. De VYVER cum annexis, gewergigd, met Intresse van de Conditien waa op de deelneming is gefchicd, en bafotte van Hipte markooning van alle deelche, of the by forme van den Hypotheeck Acte als allen ryden zal mogea worden opgezegd, en de misgaders dat by de voorz. Plantage cum annexis, als eenige geretefcyck Declaratorit, dat dezelve Acle van Hypotheecq, zoude mogea worden gevorgd, een behoorfyke, deugsdelyke en gerechts[?]ck Declaratorit, dat dezelve van Hypotheecq, is, burrijen de natetoemele, deeren Ed. Aghtbare Raaden ten behoeven van voorn. Heeren REMY & COMP; ingecertificeerd' waas van 't' y werelde noch zal worden gemeld, en datzelve met geen andere en meedere Hypothecquaire of Laslen bewaard: —

**5.**

Dat van de voorz. Adte van Obligatie en Hypothoecquarie, zullen worden uysgegeven 160 Copies authentici door een Notaris t'Amsterdam onderteekend, en met een daar onder ftaande Acte van deelgewing, welke groote VIF HONDERD GULDENS Capitaal, behoorly genaamoot van No. 1 tot No. 160, explicatie, en welke blanco in op naam, door de deelneemers refpectivvely te verkezzen, onderteekend door de Heeren REMY & COMP. als Directeuren, en ten einde daarvan geen meedere an de voorz. Houeeren Commissarischen reconnostreeren en door een Notaris t'Amsterdam geprocureerd ingeschi werden, als Directeuren onderteekend door de Heeren REMY & COMP.

**6.**

Dat ten einde de voorz. Allosding van 't Capitaal en betaaling der Interessen, door de voornoemde Heeren REMY & COMP. als Directeuren behoorlyк zal kunnen gefchieden, door hin Comparanten als Achthuseigenares, alle Producten hoe gaanaaml, die van de voorz. Plantage voorkomen, altos een inpoedigllen zullen moeten worder afgezonden, aan de voorz. Heeren REMY & COMP., om dezelve t'Amsterdam te verzoggen, en dan wytz, zoo evenegenoemde Allosding en betaaling als de onkosten van de Premien van Artillante ze zenden van de nodige provisien en benoodigheeden zoo Voor- als Najars, voor de voorz. Plantage te hanenen doen; Edog desze laastte niet o doen, dan met godesvindea en overwieg van de natetoemehe Heeren Commissarischen.

**7.**

Dat de Heeren REMY & COMP., voor hunne moeite voor provisie zullen genieten, zoo veel als in diergelyce Negotiation gebruskely en t'Amsterdam bereekend werd; —

**8.**

Dat de Heeren REMY & COMP. dxog on dat deze Negotiatie tot volkcomen land zal syn gebracht, en de Pentingen (die met de voorn. primo July 1789. in gereechheid zullen moeten zyn,) het zy in contani

Appear before us ALBERTUS BAKKER and PAULUS CORDES, Councillors in the Noble and Honorable Gentlemen of Police, Criminal and Civil Justice of the River and subordinate districts of Essequebo,
The Gentleman JOHAN GOTTLIEB DETERICKS, and the Lady JOHANNA MARIA von de VYVER, Spouses, the Lady Comparant being herein authorized with her Husband, and by him admitted to reason and posting of this (specially authorized);

To make known: that they the Comparants, intending to arrange to receive from diverse Persons, a Capital Sum of THIRTEEN THOUSAND GUILDERS, DUTCH CURRENCY; for the satisfaction of their Creditors in the Fatherland, on Conditions and under such special Mortgage Bond of the Comparants named Plantation De VYVER as in the following shall be stated; whereupon by letter of date

title: Conditien van Negotiatie: Daniel Changuion, Director; Plantation Loans for Essequibo & Demerara Planters, f. 400,000 at 6% for 10 Years (Amsterdam, ca. 1816)
description: A printed set of conditions (Conditien van Negotiatie) for a Dutch plantation mortgage fund, under the direction of Daniel Changuion, to provide a sum of f. 400,000 Dutch guilders to planters in the Rio Essequebo and Rio Demerary (Essequibo and Demerara, present-day Guyana) for the improvement and continuation of their plantations, at 6% interest per annum for 10 years. [1816, 10 Claviers, 10 Coupons]. The six articles set out: the obligation of planters to mortgage their plantations and inventories (enslaved persons, livestock, equipment), the conditions for valuation of the mortgaged property at one-fifth of assessed worth, the requirement for planters to deliver produce to the Directors, the annual valuation of the plantation, and provisions for default and foreclosure. A significant example of Dutch colonial plantation mortgage finance in the post-Napoleonic period.
creator: Daniel Changuion (Director)
notes: Conditien van Negotiatie. Director: Daniel Changuion. Plantation loans for Essequibo & Demerara planters. f. 400,000 at 6% p.a. for 10 years. 10 Claviers, 10 Coupons. Ca. 1816. Mortgages on plantations and inventories. Dutch colonial plantation finance, post-Napoleonic period.
transcription:
# ORIGINAL TEXT (Dutch):

**Conditien van Negotiatie, tot een Fonds waar uit, onder Directie van DANIEL CHANGUION, aan eenige Planters in Rio Essequebo en Rio Dém-merary, tot voortzetting en verbeteering hunner Plantagien een Somma van ƒ 40000:- voor 10 Jaaren zal werden gefoumcert, tecgens den Intreft van 6 pC⁰. 's Jaars.**

**Art. 1.**

De Planters in ongemelde Colonien, welke geneegen zyn eenige Gelden ter verbetering en voortzetting hunner Plantagien op te neemen; en daar toe door den Directeur zyn geaccepteerd, zullen gehouden zyn ten hunnen kosten, gemelde Plantagien door de Beedigde Prijseurs behoorlyks te laten Inventarisseren, zoo de Gronden, Gebouwen, Werken, Slaven en Slavinnen, en generaalsyk wat verder daar toe behoord, met uitsluiting van Meubelen en andere Zaken, onnodig tot her he-werken van den Plantagien, en den vollen en onbelisten Eigendom van dien Inventa-ris door hun betotsigd, door gemelde Taxateurs doene waarde brengen.

**Art. 2.**

Den Inventaris zal hier voren bepaald, opgemacke en geprisserd wesfende, zal aan Daniel Changuion, moeten warden gezonden, beneevens de behoeifvens der Verbandbrieff, waar uyt zal moeten blyken dat deifele Hypotheeck, ter Secretarij van de Colonie behooriyck geregistreerd is, en dat geen andere Speciale of Generale verbanden op deifele Effecten zyn leggenge, als meede dat geen verban-den zullen warden geaduanceerd of geregistreert, buyten confent van de Geldschuiders in deifeen.

**Art. 3.**

Aan de Planters welke den Inventaris en Verbandbriefs voors zullen overmaken, zal worden geflooten de helft of uytterlyck vyff agtle der waarde waar op derzelpatagien, met hun toebehooren, zyn gercexeerd, tecgens den Intreft van 5es pCento in 't Jaar, dz zy zich verbinden jaarlylka boven alle anders Laffen te voldogh op Penne in de Verbandbrief vermeld.

**Art. 4.**

De Geld - opneemers, welke Jaarlylks met Valuable bewygen kunnen aantoonen, dat hunne bezitttingen daar door zyn verbetert, zullen vryheid hebben, op een niewe Prifatie als boven gemeld te doen, van het meerder bedragen der waardee, nog op te neemen de helft van die verbeetering, mits dezelve aan voorn: Daniel Chan-guion mede Vebbogueerende.

**Art. 5.**

De Respectieve Planters Geld-opneemers, verbinden zig alle hunne te zendene Producten te addresseeren aan, nodige Provisiën te ontbieden van, en de Wiffelt-brieven te teekenen op Daniel Changuion, als mede by Affentding hunner Producten, aan hem in tyds behoorlylke opgave ter bezorging der nodie affiraatie te geeven, mits overeengst dat zy daar aan niet moeten voldoen of ook hunne Producten in de respective Colonien kunnamen te Verkioopen, of op andere wyzen te divertecren, hij welke geval hunne Intreft te voldoen, het hy hun genoeme Capitaal met de Intretten directedyck zal kunnen warden opgeeysfyck ten kaffen van zodanige Planters, by Executie verhaald, aan hunne Perloonen, Plantagien doen al dierzelver toebehooren, en verdere zoo roerende als onroerende Goederen, zig hier toe voor den Hove van Justitie in de Colonie, waar der ander nog worden waarigt worden, by vryvillige Condemmatie verbindende.

**Art. 6.**

De Geldgeevers behouden aen zig de vryheid, zodanige en zoo veele Persoonen in de Rivieren van Essequebo en Demerary aan te [leilen en te qualifisecren, als zy zul-

---

# ENGLISH TRANSLATION:

**Conditions of Negotiation, for a Fund from which, under the Direction of DANIEL CHANGUION, to certain Planters in Rio Essequibo and Rio Demerary, for the continuation and improvement of their Plantations, a Sum of ƒ 40,000 shall be furnished for 10 Years at an Interest Rate of 6 percent per annum.**

**Article 1.**

The Planters in the aforementioned Colonies, who are inclined to take up some Monies for the improvement and continuation of their Plantations, and who have been accepted for this purpose by the Director, shall be obliged at their own cost to have said Plantations properly inventoried by Sworn Appraisers, including the Lands, Buildings, Works, Male and Female Slaves, and generally whatever else pertains thereto, with the exclusion of Furniture and other Items unnecessary for the operation of the Plantations, and the full and undisputed Ownership of such Inventory thereby proved by said Appraisers to be valued.

**Article 2.**

The Inventory thus determined, prepared, and valued as above shall be sent to Daniel Changuion, together with the necessary Mortgage Deeds, wherein it must be evident that said Mortgage has been duly registered at the Secretary's Office of the Colony, and that no other Special or General liens on said Effects exist, likewise that no liens shall be registered or advanced without the consent of the Money-lenders herein.

**Article 3.**

To those Planters who shall deliver the Inventory and Mortgage Deeds as aforesaid, shall be loaned one half or at most five eighths of the value at which their Plantations, with their appurtenances, have been assessed, at an Interest Rate of 5 percent per annum, which they undertake to pay annually above all other Charges on the Penne as mentioned in the Mortgage Deed.

**Article 4.**

The Money-borrowers, who can annually produce Valuable proof that their holdings have thereby been improved, shall have the liberty to make a new Valuation as mentioned above, of the increased value of the property, and to take out an additional one half of said improvement, provided such be communicated to aforesaid Daniel Changuion.

**Article 5.**

The respective Planter Money-borrowers bind themselves to address all their Products to be sent to [Daniel Changuion], to provide necessary Provisions, and to sign Bills of Exchange on Daniel Changuion, and also in a timely manner when sending their Products, to provide him with proper notice for the arrangement of the necessary affaires; provided that they are not permitted to fail in this or to sell their Products in the respective Colonies, or to divert them in other ways, in which case their Interest shall be due, and also their principal Capital with the Interest shall be immediately demandable and collectible from the Persons, Plantations with all that appertains thereto, and all other moveable and immoveable Goods, binding themselves hereto before the Court of Justice in the Colony, where the other [conditions] shall likewise be upheld, by voluntary Condemnation.

**Article 6.**

The Money-lenders retain for themselves the freedom to appoint such and so many Persons in the Rivers of Essequibo and Demerary as they shall [deem necessary and to] qualify them as...

[Text continues on next page - final article incomplete]

title: Pekin Syndicate Limited, Bearer Certificate No. 835003, Five Ordinary Shares, 1900
description: Bilingual English/French bearer share certificate for the Pekin Syndicate Limited (London), No. 835003, representing five ordinary shares of £1 each in the company. The Pekin Syndicate was a British company formed to exploit mining and railway concessions in northern China (Shanxi province). Printed in green on cream with a fine decorative border. Coupon sheet (coupons 1–33) is attached at the bottom. Capital structure: £1,200,000 ordinary stock and £800,000 in 6% First Mortgage Debentures.

## Main Certificate Text

**Header:**
PEKIN SYNDICATE LIMITED
(INCORPORATED UNDER THE)
[Chinese characters: 福]

**Capital Section:**
CAPITAL £500,000
500,000 KANCHE SHARES OF £1 EACH.
399,000 ORDINARY SHARES OF £1 EACH ARE 2,000 DEFERRED SHARES OF £1 EACH.

SHARES WARRANT TO BEARER
No [number not clearly visible]
TITLE AND FOREIGN ACTIONS - 5 SHARES

"This is to Certify that the Bearer of this Warrant is entitled to
FIVE
Fully Paid Shares of One Pound each (numbered under the PEKIN SYNDICATE LIMITED, registered as Ordinary Shares) in conformance with the Memorandum and Articles of Association and subject to their provisions.

GIVEN under the Common Seal of the Syndicate this
20th day of NOVEMBER 1900"

a droit a

title: Negotiatie Prospectus/Conditions, Vlaardingen Weeshuis (Orphanage), f.75,000, Batavian Republic, 1800
description: Printed lottery-style negotiatie prospectus and conditions document for a fund of f.75,000 established under the Batavian Republic's representative assembly on May 9, 1800, for the maintenance of the Weeshuis (Orphanage) of the city of Vlaardingen. Details the prize structure: 514 shares of f.150 each, with 14 premium prizes ranging from f.50,000 down to f.125, and 500 premia of f.60 each. Also outlines five age-class life annuity rates (under 15, 15–24, 25–36, 36–48, over 48 years). Signed at the Vlaardingsche Weeshuys.
creator: Vertegenwoordigend Lichaam des Bataafsen Volks
notes: Lottery-style charitable negotiatie for Vlaardingen orphanage; f.75,000 total; five age-class life annuity structure; prize structure listed; see also related obligation certificate
transcription:
# Transcription

**Original (Dutch):**

DANKBAARHEID EN ERKENTENIS
NEGOTIATIE.
Groot
VYF EN ZEVENTIG DUYZEND GULDENS.
Geconfineerd by befreur van het
VERTEGENWOORDIGEND LICHAM
der BATAVISCHE VOLKS,
In dato 9 Mey 1800.
Opgerigt ter Inflandhoudding van het WEESHUYS der Stad
VLAARDINGEN.

Deze NEGOTIATIE beftaat uit Vyfien Honderd Aandeclen à vyftig Guldens ieder, op welke by Looting zyn getrokken.

1 Prys à ƒ10000. . . . . . ƒ 10000 . : -
1 — à. 5000. . . . . . 5000 . : -
3 — à. 1000. . . . . . 3000 . : -
1 — à. 500. . . . . . 2500 . : -
5 — à. 250. . . . . . 1250 . : -
10 — à. 125. . . . . . 1250 . : -
25 — à. 100. . . . . . 2500 . : -
450 — à. 50. . . . . . 22500 . : -
500 Pryzén bedmagen . . . . . ƒ 48000 . : -
1 Premie voor de ƒ 10000. . . . . . 250 . : -
1 — à de 10000. . . . . . 250 . : -
1 — voor de 5000. . . . . . 125 . : -
1 — à de 5000. . . . . . 125 . : -
1 — voor het eerste en laatste uitkomende Lot, op ieder
der 5 trekdangen à ƒ 125. . : - ieder . . . 1250 . : -

Bedragende deeze 514 Pryzén en Premien. . . . . ƒ 50000 . : -
En nog 1000 Pryzen à ƒ 50. . . . . . 50000 . : -
Maakende te faamen, . . . ƒ 100000 . : -

**Alles onder de volgende Conditien.**

Dat voor de eerstgemeelde 300 PRYZEN en 14 PREMIEN, bedragende VYFTIG DUYZEND GULDENS, zullen worden uitgegeven OBLIGATIEN, Rentende tegen DRIE Percent.

Dat voor de laastgemeinde 1000 PRYZEN, mede bedragende VYFTIG DUYZEND GULDENS, zullen worden uitgegeven INSCRIPTIE LYFRENTENTERIE vendoo in Dfl Clasfen ieder van
Twee honderd Aandeclen.

Dat in voorzis, Clasfen zullen worden gesteld, en aan de Houders van dien, tot zoo lange de Langstleevende in iedere Clasfe zal overblvven, zal worden betaald Jaarlyksche INTEREST als volgt, te weeten in de:

**Eerste CLASSE.**
PERSONEN beneeden de 12 Jaaren. . . . De somma van ƒ 300 . : -

**Tweede CLASSE.**
PERSONEN van 12 tot 24 Jaaren. . . . De somma van ƒ 350 . : -

**Derde CLASSE.**
PERSONEN van 24 tot 36 Jaaren. . . . De somma van ƒ 400 . : -

**Vierde CLASSE.**
PERSONEN van 36 tot 48 Jaaren. . . . De somma van ƒ 450 . : -

**Vyfde CLASSE.**
PERSONEN booven de 48 Jaaren. . . . De somma van ƒ 300 . : -

Dat

[Decorative border frames the entire document]

---

**English translation:**

GRATITUDE AND RECOGNITION
NEGOTIATION.
Grand
FIFTY-FIVE THOUSAND GUILDERS.
Confirmed by resolution of the
REPRESENTATIVE BODY
of the BATAVIAN PEOPLE,
Dated May 9, 1800.
Established for the Maintenance of the ORPHANAGE of the City of
VLAARDINGEN.

This NEGOTIATION consists of Fifteen Hundred Shares of fifty Guilders each, upon which prizes shall be drawn by lottery.

1 Prize of ƒ 10,000 . . . . . ƒ 10,000 . : -
1 — of 5,000 . . . . . 5,000 . : -
3 — of 1,000 . . . . . 3,000 . : -
1 — of 500 . . . . . 2,500 . : -
5 — of 250 . . . . . 1,250 . : -
10 — of 125 . . . . . 1,250 . : -
25 — of 100 . . . . . 2,500 . : -
450 — of 50 . . . . . 22,500 . : -
500 Prizes amount to . . . . . ƒ 48,000 . : -
1 Premium for the ƒ 10,000 . . . . . 250 . : -
1 — for the 10,000 . . . . . 250 . : -
1 — for the 5,000 . . . . . 125 . : -
1 — for the 5,000 . . . . . 125 . : -
1 — for the first and last winning Lot, on each
of the 5 draws at ƒ 125 each . . . 1,250 . : -

These 514 Prizes and Premiums amount to . . . . . ƒ 50,000 . : -
And furthermore 1,000 Prizes of ƒ 50 . . . . . 50,000 . : -
Total amount . . . ƒ 100,000 . : -

**All subject to the following Conditions.**

That for the aforementioned 300 PRIZES and 14 PREMIUMS, amounting to FIFTY THOUSAND GUILDERS, shall be issued OBLIGATIONS, bearing interest at THREE Percent.

That for the lastmentioned 1,000 PRIZES, likewise amounting to FIFTY THOUSAND GUILDERS, shall be issued LIFE ANNUITY INSCRIPTIONS sold in Classes of
Two hundred Shares each.

That in the aforementioned Classes shall be established, and to the Holders thereof, for so long as the longest-lived in each Class remains alive, shall be paid Annual INTEREST as follows, namely in the:

**First CLASS.**
PERSONS under 12 Years of age . . . The sum of ƒ 300 . : -

**Second CLASS.**
PERSONS from 12 to 24 Years of age . . . The sum of ƒ 350 . : -

**Third CLASS.**
PERSONS from 24 to 36 Years of age . . . The sum of ƒ 400 . : -

**Fourth CLASS.**
PERSONS from 36 to 48 Years of age . . . The sum of ƒ 450 . : -

**Fifth CLASS.**
PERSONS over 48 Years of age . . . The sum of ƒ 300 . : -

That

title: 3% Buitenlandsche Schuld van Portugal, Certificaat No. 1402, Amsterdam, 1892
description: Certificate No. 1402 issued by the Vereeniging voor den Effectenhandel (Amsterdam Securities Exchange Association) representing an arrears claim against the Portuguese State for unpaid interest of two-thirds of the 3% Portuguese Foreign Debt (1853–1884), due July 1, 1892. Issued in connection with the Nederlandsche Brekeringe-Comité voor Portugeesche Fondsen te Amsterdam (Dutch Arbitration Committee for Portuguese Funds in Amsterdam). Dated Amsterdam, September 30, 1892.
creator: Vereeniging voor den Effectenhandel, Amsterdam
notes: Portuguese 3% foreign debt 1853–1884; arrears interest certificate; issued by Amsterdam Securities Exchange; Dutch-Portuguese debt arbitration
transcription:
# Document Transcription

**Original:**

3 pCts. BUITENLANDSCHE SCHULD VAN PORTUGAL,
1853—1884.

Certificaat No. 1402

VORDERING op den Portugeeschen Staat,
wegens onbetaald gebleven twee derde deel der renten van 3 pCts.
Portugeessche Schuld, vervallen 1 Juli 1892.

Het Bestuur der Vereeniging voor den Effectenhandel te Amsterdam certificeert, dat de inleveraar van de op Lijst No. [.....], ingevolge aankondinging van het Bestuur dd. 13 September 1892, ingeleverde Coupons van Obligatiën der 3 pCts. Portugeessche Staatsschuld zijne rechten handhaft op de voldoening van het onbetaald gebleven twee derde deel dier Coupons, ten bedrage van £ .....
en de handhaving dezer rechten opdraagt aan het Nederlandsch Beschermings-Comité voor Portugeessche Fondsen te Amsterdam, of aan zoodanig Comité of zoodanige instelling als, bij ontstentenis van eerstgenoemd Comité, door de Vereeniging voor den Effectenhandel met de handhaving dier rechten mocht worden belast.

De uitkeering van een evenredig deel van hetgeen eventueel wegens deze rechten aan de inleveraars mocht te goed komen, zal geschieden aan den houder van dit Certificaat.

HET BESTUUR DER
Vereeniging voor den Effectenhandel te Amsterdam,

AMSTERDAM, 30 September, 1892.

[Signatures]
P. Straderty, Voorzitter.
[illegible], Secretaris.

---

## English Translation:

3% FOREIGN DEBT OF PORTUGAL,
1853—1884.

Certificate No. 1402

CLAIM against the Portuguese State,
concerning unpaid two-thirds of the interest on 3% Portuguese Debt, due July 1, 1892.

The Board of the Association for Securities Trading in Amsterdam certifies that the depositor of the Coupons from Bonds of the 3% Portuguese State Debt, delivered on the list No. [.....], in accordance with the announcement of the Board dated September 13, 1892, maintains his rights to payment of the unpaid two-thirds portion of those Coupons, amounting to £ .....
and entrusts the enforcement of these rights to the Dutch Protection Committee for Portuguese Funds in Amsterdam, or to such Committee or such institution as, in the absence of the aforementioned Committee, may be entrusted by the Association for Securities Trading with the enforcement of these rights.

The payment of a proportional share of what may eventually be due regarding these rights to the depositors shall be made to the holder of this Certificate.

THE BOARD OF
the Association for Securities Trading in Amsterdam,

AMSTERDAM, September 30, 1892.

[Signatures]
P. Straderty, Chairman.
[illegible], Secretary.

---

## Notable Markings:

- **Seal:** Blue circular stamp in upper right corner (Vereeniging voor den Effectenhandel te Amsterdam)
- **Serial Number:** 1402
- **Signatures:** Two handwritten signatures at bottom (Chairman and Secretary)
- **Date stamp:** "30 September, 1892"
- **Decorative border:** Ornamental frame on left side with text "VEREENIGING VOOR DEN EFFECTENHANDEL TE AMSTERDAM"
- **Handwritten annotations:** Blank amount field (£) and partially illegible list number

title: New Russia Company Limited, 6% First Mortgage Debenture No. 277, £100, London, 1901
description: 6% First Mortgage Debenture No. 277 of New Russia Company Limited, London, £100 denomination, from a total issue of £800,000. Secured on property of the Company consisting of land in the Government of Ekaterinoslav, southern Russia (now Ukraine). Repayable on October 1, 1933, with semi-annual 6% interest payments on April 1 and October 1. Issued under the Company's Deed of Trust signed by directors including Alfred Lyttelton KC and Charles Eden. Printed in blue/black on cream with decorative border.

pages.description: Page 1 of 3 - New Russia Company limited 6 percent first mortgage debenture._x000B_ Russia is often viewed as a land of rich resources and a scarcity of capital and modern technology to develop them. So is was in the late 19th century when the Imperial Russian Government sought ways of attracting international capital and technical expertise. One particularly fascinating example of this was The New Russia Company. In April of 1868 Hughes signed an agreement with the Russian Prince Serge Kochubei to form the New Russia Company for the "exploitation of mineral deposits" in the Donbass region of the Ukraine. A skillful manager, Hughes turned his knowledge of mining and manufacturing into a virtual monopoly within Russia. With a combination of modern manufacturing techniques and imperial favors, Hughes became the primary supplier to the rapidly expanding Russian Railway system. Firms like The Grand Russian Railway Company for which we have a rich green 1881 share certificate on display) become his main customers. A large town grew up among the shadows of the factory, known first as Hughesovka. With Hughes death in 1889, the company was left to the direction of his four surviving sons. By 1895 the Company had doubled its original registered capital and by the end of the 19th century, the New Russia Company was the largest iron producer in the Russian Empire. Following the Bolshevik Revolution in 1917, almost all of the Company's foreign employees left the country and the factories were taken over in 1919. Hughesovka was renamed Stalino in 1924 and Donetsk in 1961, but remained the largest city in the Donbass and one of the largest metallurgical centers in the country. Investor hopes spring eternal. British investors finally dissolved The New Russia Company almost one hundred years after its revolutionary conception in1970. The success of Hughes enterprise is a testament to his contribution to the spread of industrialization throughout the world. As Russia seeks foreign development capital and joint-ventures, it is well to remember the pioneering success of John Hughes and the legacy of Hughesovka. creator: New Russia Company Limited

transcription:
# New Russia Company Limited - 6% First Mortgage Debenture No. 277

## Transcribed Text:

**NEW RUSSIA COMPANY LIMITED**

INCORPORATED UNDER THE COMPANIES ACT, 1862.

CAPITAL ORDINARY STOCK, $1,200,000.

ISSUE OF
£600,000

SIX PERCENT FIRST MORTGAGE DEBENTURES
OF £100 EACH.

NO 277                                                                                                                £100.

NUMBERED FROM 1 TO 6,000 (BOTH INCLUSIVE).

[Extended legal text regarding terms and conditions of the debenture, including details about interest payments, principal repayment, and security provisions - text is dense and partially difficult to read in places]

**SIX PERCENT FIRST MORTGAGE DEBENTURE.**

NEW RUSSIA COMPANY, LIMITED [Hereafter called the Company]
will on the 1st day of October aforesaid and on similar date in the principal amount therein mentioned (if punctually paid) in accordance with the conditions endorsed hereon, pay to the bearer of this Debenture unless then registered and in such case to the registered holder hereof the sum of £100 sterling in London and the Company will pay interest thereon at the rate of 6 per cent per annum, by equal half yearly payments on the 2nd April and the 1st day of October in each year in accordance with the coupons annexed hereto unless forfeited by non-presentation and surrender of such Debenture.

By virtue of the Trust Deed mentioned below these Debentures are specially secured upon a portion of the company's property consisting of estates in the Government of Catherinaslav, Russia; on part of which property are situated the principal Collieries and works of the Company. The said estates comprise an area of about 1,300 acres and are more particularly described in the Deed of Mortgage and are issued upon and subject to the conditions endorsed hereon and to those contained in the Deed of Trust dated the 22nd day of March, 1901, and made between the Company of the one part and Mr. Right Honourable Alfred Lyttleton, M.A.; M. P., and Charles Cohn, Marine Eng'r, by the other part, and on the condition that the bearer or registered holder hereof has the same may do, stand thereby.

Given under the Common Seal of the Company
this sixth day of April 1901.

[Signatures for Director and Scrip Secretary appear to be present but are not clearly legible]

---

title: Société Selim et Samaan Sednaoui S.A., 25 Registered Shares of LE.10 Each, Cairo, United Arab Republic
description: Share certificate in Arabic and French for the Société Selim et Samaan Sednaoui, Société Anonyme R.A.U. (République Arabe Unie/United Arab Republic), headquartered in Cairo. Certificate represents 25 registered (nominative) shares of Egyptian Pound (LE) 10 each, total LE.250. The Sednaoui family founded one of Cairo's premier department stores in 1886; the company was later nationalized. The certificate is printed in red/terracotta with Arabic title text and has a detachable dividend coupon sheet at the bottom. Dated ca. 1958–1960.

transcription:
# Transcription: Costa Rica Railway Company Limited, First Mortgage Debenture £100

## CONDITIONS

1. This Debenture is one of a series of 5,250 Debentures for securing the aggregate principal sum of £525,000 issued or about to be issued by the Company. The debentures of the said series will in each year be puan as a first charge on the property hereby charged, without any preference or priority one over another, and such charge is by the a floating security, but as that, except as hereafter expressly provided, the Company is not to be at liberty to create any subsequent mortgages on the property making prior claim with or in priority to this said Debentures.

2. A register of the Debentures will be kept at the Company's registered office, wherein there will be entered the names, addresses, and descriptions of the holders, and particulars of the Debentures held by them respectively.

3. The registered holder will be regarded as exclusively entitled to the benefit of this Debenture, and all persons may at all times look exclusively to the Company shall not be bound to enter in the register notice of any trust or to recognize any right in any other person, save as herein provided.

4. Every transfer of the Debenture must be in writing under the hand of the registered holder, his executors or administrators. The transfer must be delivered at the registered office of the Company, with a fee of 2s. 6d. and such evidence of identity or title as the Company may reasonably require, and thereupon his transfer will be registered.

5.—In the case of joint registered holders, the principal moneys and interest hereby secured shall be paid to them upon a joint account.

6. The principal moneys and interest hereby secured will be paid without regard to any equities between the Company and the original or any intermediate holder thereof and the receipt of the registered holder for each principal, moneys and interest respectively, shall be a good discharge to the Company.

7.—The principal moneys hereby secured shall immediately become payable at the office of the Company unless default be made in the payment of any interest hereby secured, and the registered holder hereof

before such interest is paid, by notice in writing to the Company, calls in such principal moneys ; (b) If an order is made or an effective resolution is passed for the winding up of the Company.

8. The registered holder of this Debenture will be entitled to receive notices of and to vote at meetings of the Company, as provided by the Articles of Association.

9. The Company may, with the sanction of a majority of not less than two-thirds of such Debenture holders of this issue as are present in person or by proxy at any meeting of Debenture holders of which notice specifying the intention to propose a resolution to such effect shall have been duly given as aforesaid, and at any subsequent meeting of the Company, make a further issue or issues of Debentures either to redeem debentures of the Company as subsisting liens or otherwise ranking pars pass with or in priority to the Debentures of this series, or may otherwise alter or vary any of the terms or conditions upon which this series of Debentures have been issued.

10. In the event of the becoming necessary, either for reason of any requirement of the Government of Costa Rica in that behalf or otherwise, to establish a sinking fund for the purpose of redeeming the Debentures of this series, any question whether in regard to the fixing of the rate of the per centage or otherwise arising in relation to the establishment of such sinking fund, and which may require the sanction or determination of the Debenture holders, shall be referred to a meeting of Debenture holders, of which notice shall be duly given as aforesaid, and the resolution of a majority of Debenture holders present in person or by proxy at such meeting, shall be referred to the decision of, and shall be binding upon all the Debenture holders of this series.

11. A notice may be served by the Company upon the holder of this Debenture either personally or by sending it through the post in an envelope addressed to such holder at his registered address.

12. Any notice served by post shall be deemed to have been served at the time when it was posted, and the postage must serve it shall be sufficient to prove the address on the envelope enclosing the notice and the posting of it.

---

## DOCUMENT HEADER

**THE COSTA RICA RAILWAY COMPANY, LIMITED.**

**FIRST MORTGAGE DEBENTURE.**

**£100** | **No. 5426**

# Transcription of Costa Rica Railway Company Limited Second Debenture

## Main Text Transcription:

**Header:**
No 6886                                £100

**Company Name:**
THE COSTA RICA RAILWAY COMPANY LIMITED

**Bond Type:**
Second Debenture

**Issue Details:**
Issue of £600000 in 6000 Second Debentures of £100 each
bearing interest at the rate of 5 per cent per annum
numbered from 6,551 to 12550 inclusive.

**Body Text:**
The Costa Rica Railway Company, Limited

[Official seal/stamp visible in orange/red]

...called the Company will on the 1st September 1916, or on such earlier day as the Company represented hereby shall become legally entitled to redeem this Debenture at the Company's registered office, London Street Sydney [unclear], E.C.

[The text continues with terms and conditions regarding the debenture, mentioning payment of interest and conditions for redemption]

This Debenture is issued subject to the conditions endorsed hereon and under the common seal of the Company the 23rd day of January 1880

The Common Seal of the Company
Witnessed affixed in the presence of

**Signatures:**
[Signature] — DIRECTOR
[Signature] — SECRETARY

**Notable markings:**
- Serial number: No 6886
- Denomination: £100
- Date: 23rd January 1880 (issued)
- Orange/red company seal visible on left side
- Ornate decorative border with Celtic-style patterns throughout
- Intricate engraving work typical of 19th-century bond certificates
- Page marked as "Page 1 of 2" at bottom
- Printer's mark: "GOERTZ & CO SC. ENGRAVERS 57 W.C."

---

# TRANSCRIPTION

## CONDITIONS.

1. This Debenture is one of a series of 5,000 Debentures for securing the aggregate principal sum of £500,000, issued, or about to be issued, by the Company.

2. The Company shall appropriate in each year a sufficient proportion of the net revenue of the Company for that year remaining, after providing for the seven-fold subscription (if necessary) of the First Mortgage Debentures for the time being of the Company, to pay interest at the rate of 5 per cent. for that year upon all the Debentures of this series, and shall apply the same accordingly, or in case the revenue shall not be sufficient for that purpose then shall it split the whole of the revenue equally amongst all the Debentures issued to the holders of the Debentures of this series of interest for that year upon their Debentures.

3. The interest upon the said Debentures shall not be cumulative, and the net revenue of any year shall only be subject to payment of the interest for that year.

4. A register of the Debentures will be kept at the Company's registered office, wherein there will be entered the names, addresses, and descriptions of the holders, and particulars of the Debentures held by them respectively.

5. The registered holder will be regarded as exclusively entitled in benefit of the Debenture, and all persons that act accordingly, and the Company shall not be bound to enter in the register notices of any trust or to recognize any other person as in any other person as in any other person.

6. Every transfer of this Debenture must be in writing under the hand of the registered holder, his executors, or administrators. The transfer must be delivered at the registered office of the Company with a fee of 2s. 6d. and such evidence of identity or title as the Company may reasonably require, and thereupon the transfer will be registered.

7. In the case of joint registered holders the principal moneys and interest payable hereunder shall be deemed to be owing to them upon a joint account.

8. The principal moneys and interest payable hereunder will be paid without regard to any equities existing between the Company and any intermediate holder hereof, and the receipt of the registered holder for such principal moneys and interest shall be a good discharge to the Company.

9. The principal moneys hereby secured shall immediately become payable if an order is made or an effective resolution is passed for the winding up of the Company.

10. The registered holder of this Debenture will be entitled to receive notices of and to vote at Meetings of the Company as provided by its Articles of Association.

11. In the event of its becoming necessary either by reason of any requirement of the Government of Costa Rica in that behalf, or otherwise, to establish a sinking fund for the purpose of redeeming the Debentures of this series, the same shall be established and managed in accordance with, or otherwise, arising in relation to the establishment of such sinking fund and which may require, the direction of a meeting of the Debenture holders, of which notice shall be duly given as aforesaid, and the decision of a majority of Debenture holders present in person or by proxy at such meeting shall be deemed to be the decision and shall be binding upon all the Debenture holders of this series.

12. A notice may be served by the Company upon the holder of this Debenture either personally or by sending it through the post in an envelope addressed to such holder at his registered address.

13. Any notice served by post shall be deemed to have been served at the time when it was posted, and in proving such service it shall be sufficient to prove the address on the envelope enclosing the notice, and the posting of it.

transcription:
# Document Transcription

## Original (Spanish):

**Header and Title:**
Bética

SOCIEDAD ANÓNIMA COOPERATIVA AGRÍCOLA INDUSTRIAL

Constituido en Sevilla el 15 de Diciembre de 1924 por escritura otorgada ante el Notario D'Ilíc. Sánchez Blanco y Sánchez con un

CAPITAL SOCIAL DE 17.000.000 DE PESETAS

EMISIÓN DE 6.000.000 DE PESETAS EN OBLIGACIONES HIPOTECARIAS DIVIDIDAS EN 12.000 TÍTULOS AL PORTADOR, DE 500 PESETAS NOMINALES CADA UNO, ACORDADA EN JUNTA GENERAL EXTRAORDINARIA DE 30 DE JUNIO DE 1931, Y FORMALIZADA POR ESCRITURA PÚBLICA ANTE EL NOTARIO DON FÉLIX SÁNCHEZ BLANCO Y SÁNCHEZ EN SEVILLA EL 21 DE SEPTIEMBRE DE 1931.

**Bond Details:**
OBLIGACIÓN Nº [number] 500 PESETAS

El portador de esta Obligación tiene derecho al interés del siete por ciento anual, libre de impuestos presentes y futuros. Pagadero en pesetas oro semestres vencidos el 31 de Marzo y 30 de Septiembre de cada año. A contar desde el 31 de Marzo de 1932, hasta el día de su amortización que se hará en el plazo máximo de treinta años. Por sorteos, en la cuantía mínima de doscientas mil pesetas anuales, a partir del 30 de Septiembre de 1934, rendiendo aumentar la cuantía indicada por la amortización y resarcimiento de la Sociedad el derecho de retiramiento todas de la circulación previo pago de las mismas, y sin necesidad de sorteo, coincidiendo con la fecha destinada para el mismo.

EMENDADO A VALLE                                   SEVILLA 1ª DE OCTUBRE DE 1931

EL PRESIDENTE.                    [signature]                    EL SECRETARIO.

La S.A. "Azucarera de Sevilla"

[Additional text stamps and markings below main bond]

**Coupon Sheet:**
Multiple coupon boxes labeled "BETICA S.A. COOPERATIVA AGRÍCOLA INDUSTRIAL" with numbers (50, 51, 52, etc.), serial numbers, and payment dates from September 1931 and March 1932 onwards.

---

## English Translation:

**Bética**

ANONYMOUS COOPERATIVE AGRICULTURAL AND INDUSTRIAL COMPANY

Established in Seville on December 15, 1924 by deed executed before Notary D. Ilíc. Sánchez Blanco y Sánchez with a

SHARE CAPITAL OF 17,000,000 PESETAS

ISSUE OF 6,000,000 PESETAS IN MORTGAGE BONDS DIVIDED INTO 12,000 BEARER TITLES OF 500 PESETAS NOMINAL VALUE EACH, AUTHORIZED AT THE EXTRAORDINARY GENERAL SHAREHOLDERS' MEETING OF JUNE 30, 1931, AND FORMALIZED BY PUBLIC DEED BEFORE NOTARY DON FÉLIX SÁNCHEZ BLANCO Y SÁNCHEZ IN SEVILLE ON SEPTEMBER 21, 1931.

**Bond Details:**
BOND No. [number] 500 PESETAS

The bearer of this Bond is entitled to seven percent annual interest, free of present and future taxes. Payable in gold pesetas in semi-annual installments due on March 31 and September 30 of each year. Starting from March 31, 1932, until the date of amortization, which shall occur within a maximum period of thirty years. By lottery drawing, in a minimum amount of two hundred thousand pesetas annually, beginning September 30, 1934, the Company may increase said amount for amortization and retains the right to retire all bonds from circulation by prior payment of the same, without need for drawing, coinciding with the date set for the same.

AMENDED AT VALLE                                   SEVILLE OCTOBER 1, 1931

THE PRESIDENT.                    [signature]                    THE SECRETARY.

The S.A. "Azucarera de Sevilla"

---

## Notable Markings:

- **Signatures:** Two signatures present for El Presidente and El Secretario, dated October 1, 1931
- **Serial Numbers:** Various coupon numbers visible (50-59 range noted)
- **Decorative Border:** Ornate geometric frame with "500" in corners
- **Allegorical Figure:** Female figure with agricultural/industrial imagery in top section
- **Coupon Sheet:** Attached at bottom with multiple detachable coupons for interest payments
- **Stamps/Seals:** Multiple official stamps and "JUSTIFICADO" marking visible
- **Date Discrepancy:** Document mentions establishment in 1924 but formalization in 1931

**EMPRUNT INDUSTRIEL DU GOUVERNEMENT DE LA REPUBLIQUE CHINOISE**

DE 150.000.000 DE FRANCS
DIVISÉ EN 300.000 OBLIGATIONS DE 500 FRANCS CHACUNE

transcription:
# Transcription of Republic of Peru 5% Gold Bonds of 1920

## Left Column (English):

**REPUBLIC OF PERU**

**5 PER CENT GOLD BONDS OF 1920 FOR £720,620.**

**IN 72,062 BONDS OF £10 EACH. NUMBERED 1 TO 72,062 INCLUSIVE.**

[Small text regarding bond details and conditions - partially visible]

**BOND TO BEARER FOR £10 STERLING**

No 71547

The Government of the Republic of Peru will on the 1st day of January 1926 or on such earlier day as they may think fit, redeem these Bonds at par with accrued interest in London in the usual American gold coin or in sterling exchange at the rate of exchange ruling on the day of redemption, and will in the meantime pay interest thereon at the rate of five per centum per annum on the 1st January and 1st July of each year upon presentation of the coupons corresponding to such payment.

This Bond is one of a series of Bonds of like tenor and effect issued and to be issued in the aggregate £720,620 and all the Bonds of this series shall make their bonds jointly and severally binding over another and the holder of this Bond shall be entitled to all such of the registered holders of the other bonds of this series outstanding to the benefit of the General Bond.

The Bonds are issued under and pursuant to the authorization given by the Government of the Republic of Peru shall be charged upon the property forming the security for the Mexican and other equally valid Bonds of this series.

In witness whereof the authorized representatives of the Government of the Republic of Peru have affixed this seal this 25th day of May 1924.

Govts of ETHELIBURGA SYNDICATE LIMITED,
Agents for the Issuer

[Signature]

## Right Column (French):

**REPUBLIQUE DU PEROU**

**OBLIGATIONS 5 POUR CENT OR DE 1920 POUR £720,620.**

**EN 72,062 OBLIGATIONS DE £10 CHACUNE. NUMEROTEES DE LA 72,062 INCLUSIVEMENT.**

[Small text regarding bond details and conditions - partially visible]

**OBLIGATION AU PORTEUR DE £10 STERLING**

N° 71547

Le Gouvernement de la République du Pérou, le 1er Janvier 1926 ou à toute époque antérieure à laquelle il croirait devoir procéder, remboursera ces Obligations au pair avec intérêts courus à Londres soit en espèces d'or américain ou couramment reconnu, soit en sterling au taux du change des cours de Londres à la date du remboursement, et il servira en attendant un intérêt de cinq pour cent l'an payable le 1er Janvier et 1er Juillet de chaque année à la présentation des coupons correspondants au dit paiement.

Cette Obligation fait partie d'une série d'Obligations de la Républica du Pérou montant au total à £720,620 et toutes les Obligations de cette série forment un tout inséparable soumis à la même hypothèque garantissant l'ensemble et le porteur de cette Obligation aura les mêmes droits et privilèges que les porteurs des autres Obligations de la même série.

En foi de quoi le représentant autorisé du Gouvernement de la République du Pérou a appesé sa signature et son cachet sur le présent titre de l'Obligation Générale.

En témoins donné est emprunté qu'autorisé autorisé Gouvernement de la République du Pérou a apposé le contre-seing sur le même papier que les Obligations de cette série.

En fait le quoi le représentant autorisé du Gouvernement de la République du Pérou a apposé les présentes le 25 Mai 1924.

[Signature: Leu R Wallen]

Authorized representative of the Government of the Republic of Peru
Représentant autorisé du Gouvernement de la République du Pérou

---

## Notable Markings:

- **Serial Number:** No 71547
- **Denomination:** £10 Sterling
- **Date:** 25 May 1924
- **Signature:** Leu R Wallen (Authorized representative)
- **Official Seal:** Coat of arms of Peru at top center
- **Ornamental Border:** Elaborate green decorative frame with scrollwork
- **Orange stamp/seal:** Upper left corner
- **Agents:** Etheliburga Syndicate Limited, London

---

# Transcription of Republic of Peru 5% Gold Bonds of 1920

## Document Overview
This page shows a sheet of bond coupons arranged in a 4×14 grid format (56 coupons total), each representing interest payment vouchers.

## Original Text (French):

**Header on each coupon:**

REPUBLIQUE DU PÉROU
DE L'EMPRUNT 5 % OR
DE 1920 POUR £720,620

[Each coupon contains the following standard text:]

Obligations Gouvernementales à 5 p.c. garanties par le Gouvernement de la République du Pérou, autorisées par Décret du 13 juillet 1920.

Le Gouvernement de la République du Pérou paiera au porteur, le [DATE] à Londres, à Paris ou à Amsterdam, contre la présentation et remise de ce coupon, la somme de [AMOUNT] représentant l'intérêt semestriel à 5 p.c. l'an sur les Obligations de 10 Livres Sterling de son Emprunt 5 p.c. Or de 1920.

Les coupons devront être présentés à l'une des Banques désignées ci-dessus ou à tout Banquier autorisé.

En cas de perte ou de destruction de ce coupon, il ne sera pas remplacé.

---

## English Translation:

**Header on each coupon:**

REPUBLIC OF PERU
OF THE 5% GOLD LOAN
OF 1920 FOR £720,620

[Each coupon contains:]

Government Obligations at 5% guaranteed by the Government of the Republic of Peru, authorized by Decree of 13 July 1920.

The Government of the Republic of Peru will pay to the bearer, on [DATE] in London, Paris or Amsterdam, upon presentation and surrender of this coupon, the sum of [AMOUNT] representing the semi-annual interest at 5% per annum on the Obligations of 10 Sterling Pounds of its 5% Gold Loan of 1920.

Coupons must be presented at one of the designated Banks above or to any authorized Banker.

In case of loss or destruction of this coupon, it will not be replaced.

---

All denominations shown as "L10" (10 Pounds Sterling) with "L0.5.0" notation

# Bulgarian 5% State Gold Loan 1904 - Document Transcription

## Original Text (Bulgarian Cyrillic and French):

**Header:**
КНЯЖЕСТВО БЪЛГАРИЯ

БЪЛГАРСКІ 5% ДЪРЖАВЕНЬ ЗАЕМЪ ВЪ ЗЛАТО ОТЪ 1904 ГОДИНА

[followed by detailed Bulgarian text describing the loan terms, collateral, and financial details]

**Main Title Section:**
ПРЕДСТАВЛЕНЪ ОТЪ 200.000 ОБЛИГАЦІИ

DE 500 FRANCS CHACUNE = 100.000.000 francs nominal — 100.000.000 francs sterling nominal — 37.500.000 roubles or — 81.000.000 germains marks — 3.900.000 livres sterling — 95.200.000 austro-hongroise couronne — 48.000.000 kouladi florins.

**Four Language Sections (French, Bulgarian/Cyrillic, German, English):**

Left Column - French:
"EMPRUNT DE L'ÉTAT BULGARE"
"5% OR 1904"
"200.000 OBLIGATIONS"
[loan details and terms]

Center-Left - Bulgarian (Cyrillic):
"БОЛГАРСКІ ГОСУДАРСТВЕННЫЙ"
"5% ЗОЛОТОЙ ЗАЕМЪ"
"1904 г."
[corresponding loan details]

Center-Right - German:
"BULGARISCHE STAATS"
"GOLD-ANLEIHE"
"von 1904"
[German language terms]

Right Column - English:
"BULGARIAN GOVERNMENT'S"
"GOLD LOAN OF 1904"
"SECURED"
[English language terms]

**Bottom Section Contains:**

Three sections for different denomination obligations:
- "OBLIGATION AU PORTEUR - Cinq Cents Francs" (500 Francs)
- "ОБЛИГАЦІЯ - Пятьсот Франков" (500 Francs - Bulgarian)
- "OBLIGATION - Fünfhundert Francs" (500 Francs - German)
- "BOND - Five hundred Francs" (500 Francs - English)

**Date and Location:**
SOFIA, le 15/28 Mai 1905.

---

## Notable Markings:

1. **Serial Number:** 2°079,150 (appears in ornamental box in upper center)

2. **Signatures:** Multiple signatures visible at bottom, including:
   - L. Fauvenet (or similar - upper center)
   - [unclear signatures] (lower portions)

3. **Seal/Stamp:** Circular official stamp visible in lower left corner, dated 1905, with text "BANQUE DE PARIS ET DES PAYS-BAS"

4. **Decorative Elements:** Ornate borders with heraldic coat of arms at top, elaborate corner designs with floral/geometric patterns throughout

5. **Printer's Mark:** Bottom footer reads "IMPRIMERIE CHAIX, RUE BERGÈRE, 20, PARIS. — B-13188. — Sans Lithogr."

6. **Languages Present:** Bulgarian (Cyrillic), French, German, and English - indicating international circulation

---

## English Translation of Key Header Text:

"PRINCIPALITY OF BULGARIA

BULGARIAN 5% STATE GOLD LOAN OF 1904

[Details about the loan being authorized by the Prince's ukase, ratified by contract with the Banque de Paris et des Pays-Bas on October 1, 1904, with the loan sanctioned by ukase dated November 15/28, 1904...]

REPRESENTED BY 200,000 BONDS

Of 500 Francs each = 403 grams = 9,160 ounces of fine gold = 240 [Austrian?] florins"

title: Imperio de Mexico, Deuda Publica Exterior, Obligacion de 500 Francos al Portador, No. 1,085,528, 1865
description: Spanish-language 500 Franc bearer bond (Obligacion al Portador) No. 1,085,528 of the Empire of Mexico's External Public Debt (Deuda Publica Exterior), 1865. Issued under Emperor Maximilian I of Mexico. Identical structure to the French-language version (see No. 1,086,528): main obligation at top with coupon sheets on both sides, and a smaller subsidiary billet at the bottom reading "IMPERIO DE MEXICO 1865 DEUDA PUBLICA EXTERIOR." Both French and Spanish versions of these bonds were distributed across European markets during Maximilian's reign.

title: Uzinele de Fier și Domeniile din Resita S.A. / Aciéries et Domaines de Resita S.A., 10 Registered Shares of 500 Lei, Bucharest, 1928
description: Registered share certificate (titru/titre) for 10 ordinary registered shares (Actiuni Nominative) of Uzinele de Fier și Domeniile din Resita S.A. (Romanian Iron Works and Domains of Resita / Aciéries et Domaines de Resita S.A.), nominal value 500 Lei each, total 5,000 Lei. The certificate is bilingual Romanian and French. The Resita ironworks in Banat (now Romania) were one of the oldest and largest steel-producing facilities in Eastern Europe. The certificate includes a transfer register ("CESIUNI") on the right. Dated Bucharest, September 1928. Company seal "UD" at bottom.

title: Empire Ottoman, Emprunt à Primes de 792,000,000 Francs, Obligation 400 Francs, No. 1,898,329, Constantinople, 1870
description: Bearer prize bond (Obligation au Porteur) No. 1,898,329 of the Ottoman Empire Prize Loan (Emprunt à Primes / Prämien-Anleihe) of 792,000,000 Francs. Total emission of 1,860,000 obligations at a nominal capital of 400 Francs each. The Ottoman Imperial Government pledges to pay principal and interest, and to honor prize draws, established in Galata/Constantinople. Multilingual text in French, German, and Ottoman Turkish (Arabic script). Signed by the Imperial Ottoman Government's authorized agent in Constantinople and the General Agent in Paris. Dated Constantinople, January 3, 1870. Two Ottoman revenue stamps affixed at top.
