# Ottoman Empire Bond - 1870

## Original Text (French):

**PRIMES**

**EMPIRE OTTOMAN.**

Emprunt à Primes de 792,000,000 Francs.

ÉMISSION DE 1,980,000 OBLIGATIONS AU CAPITAL NOMINAL DE 400 FRANCS

dont le service des intérêts et de l'amortissement est exclusivement payable par le Gouvernement Impérial Ottoman.

**OBLIGATION AU PORTEUR DE 400 FRANCS**

1,898,329. N° 1,898,329 1,898,329.

Le Gouvernement Impérial Ottoman paie en porteur de la présente obligation au intérêt annuel de douze francs, payable semestriellement, le 1er avril et le 1er octobre de chaque année, jusqu'à jour où le capital sera intégralement remboursé.

Les obligations de présent emprunt seront remboursées par le Gouvernement Impérial Ottoman, conformément au tableau d'amortissement ci-joint. Le premier tirage aura lieu au mois de juin 1871.

Constantinopple, le 5 janvier 1870.

title: California Mexico Land Company Limited, Land Certificate No. 1423, 50 Hectares, £20 / Frs. 500, 1885
description: Bilingual English/French land certificate No. 1423 of The California Mexico Land Company, Limited (London), representing 50 hectares of land in Lower California (Baja California, Mexico), valued at £20 Sterling or Francs 500. The Company issued certificates to a total nominal value of £240,000 or 6,000,000 Francs, representing the purchase of 500,000 hectares of land situated in Lower California. The certificate entitles the bearer to a proportional share of proceeds from the development and realization of the Company's land. Conditions dated under the Deed of Trust of May 23, 1885. Signed by company directors and secretary. Coupon sheet attached at bottom.

pages.description: Page 1 of 2 - This equity certificate would distribute the proceeds from developing land in southern California. Certificates were offered in London and Paris thus giving the opportunity for Europeans to invest in America's land. This share certificate was offered at 20 pounds and represented 50 hectares. _x000B_

# Transcription: California Mexico Land Company Limited Certificate

## English Text:

**THE CALIFORNIA MEXICO LAND COMPANY, LIMITED.**

Issue of Certificates to a total nominal value of £240,000 or 600,000 Francs Representing the Purchase of 600,000 hectares of Land situated in Lower California of that total nominal value such certificates consisting of:

2550 Certificates for 100 Hectares each = £40 of 1000 fcs
1500 - 50 - 20 - 500 -
1500 - 27 - 10 - 250 -
2500 - 15 - 6 - 150 -
2500 - 10 - 4 - 100 -

**CERTIFICATE NO 1423**

**£.20** **50 HECTARES** **Fcs 500.**

This is to Certify that the bearer of this Certificate is seized of the above named Company one Twenty thousand eighth part or Share of the proceeds of the development and realisation of the above described Land as therein distributed, in terms of a Deed of Trust executed by the... for this purpose on the 22nd of April, 1885 and conditions endorsed on this Certificate.

IN WITNESS whereof the Seal of the above named Company was hereto affixed in the presence of

[Signatures present but largely illegible]
COUNTERSIGNED [signature]
DIRECTORS [signature]

---

## French Text (Right Column):

**Original:**

Émission s Certificats d'une valeur nominale totale de 240,000 £ ou 600,000 frs de francs représentant à l'achat de 600,000 hectares de terres de la même valeur nominale sises dans la Basse Californie la dite émission se composant de:

2550 certificats de 100 hectares chacun = £ 40 of 1000 fcs
1500 - 50 - 20 - 500 -
1500 - 27 - 10 - 250 -
2500 - 15 - 6 - 150 -
2500 - 10 - 4 - 100 -

Par Présent Certificat constate que le porteur à droit de recevoir de la susdite Compagnie un Deux millième du produit de l'exploitation et de la vente des terrains et dons mentionnés et dont la répartition se fera conformement aux conditions énoncées au verso de la présente Deed of Trust fait d'après par le... en date en... 1885 Sous les conditions spéciales au des termes de la présent certificat.

On fait de que le sceau de la susdite compagnie a été apposé au présent certificat en présence it.

**English Translation:**

Issue of Certificates of a total nominal value of 240,000 £ or 600,000 frs francs representing the purchase of 600,000 hectares of land of the same nominal value located in Lower California the said issue consisting of:

2550 certificates of 100 hectares each = £40 of 1000 fcs
1500 - 50 - 20 - 500 -
1500 - 27 - 10 - 250 -
2500 - 15 - 6 - 150 -
2500 - 10 - 4 - 100 -

By Present Certificate it is established that the bearer has the right to receive from the aforesaid Company one Two-thousandth of the proceeds from the exploitation and sale of the lands and mentioned and whose distribution shall be made in accordance with the conditions set forth on the back of the present Deed of Trust made according to by the... dated... 1885 Under the special conditions in the terms of the present certificate.

Whereas the seal of the aforesaid company has been affixed to the present certificate in the presence of [it].

## Notable Markings:

- **Certificate Number:** 1423
- **Denomination:** £20 / Francs 500
- **Land Area:** 50 Hectares
- **Ornamental border:** Green decorative frame around entire document
- **Coupons:** Eleven numbered coupons (numbered 1423) displayed at bottom of page
- **Seals/Stamps:** Company seal visible (appears to be affixed)
- **Signatures:** Two signatures present but largely illegible (Countersigned and Directors)
- **Date:** References to April 22, 1885 in text
- **Language:** Bilingual (English and French)

---

# Transcription

## CONDITIONS.

1. Under an Agreement dated the 3rd day of May, 1885, "La Mexicana Compania Europea de Minas y Terrenos," the Vendor has agreed to sell to the Company the land herein set as therein provided in the General Agents of the said Company for the administration and direction (including the realty) of the said land, the subdivision or cancellation of any of the said proceeds of selling, said to 10 per cent. of the net proceeds of such thereof, and the respective holders of these proceeds shall be entitled to Certificates representing their amount in Francs, will belong to, and be distributed pro rata among the holders of the Certificates of the Company respectively.

Such illustration will be effected by means of the certificates issued in the form hereof as required.

2. The Company will furnish to the holder of the Company distributable as aforesaid amount of not less to the sum of £1,000, three times in succession with the approval of the Meetings: the first advertisement in the London and one Paris daily paper, to be repeated in each paper specifying the title and object of the Company, and the three price, the sharecopies payment will be made on presentation and delivery of the relevant coupon in the Bank in hand, and according to the terms and conditions provided by such order.

3. All Certificates will be fully paid without any preference of priority the one over the other. All Certificates and Coupons are transferable by delivery or endorsement. The Company will pay all sums due on the Certificates, issued to the bearer of this Certificate on or when it may become due.

4. Meetings of the Company will be held in London or Paris as determined upon by the Board of Certificates with all unpaid Coupons attached will be entitled to be present and vote at all such meetings, and will have equal voting powers as other holders of the Certificates produced by them respectively.

5. The seal of the Company will be kept at the registered office of the Company.

---

**Original (French section):**

L. En conformité des termes d'un Contrat en date du 3me jour de Mai, 1885, "La Mexicana Compania Europea de Minas y Terrenos en Mexico"—vendeuses à la Californie (Mexico) Land Company, Limited, s'engage ainsi qu'il est spécifié en dit acte, à agir en qualité d'agents général de cette dernière pour l'administration et la direction (y compris la realty) de ces terres ; la subdivision ou l'annulation de quelconques des dites terres et la vente, au moyen des prospectus et prospectus du produit net, l'abolition est une répartition, les porteurs respectifs de ces procédés recevront l'annulation et la direction et Francs, appartenant à, et be distributed pro rata parmi les porteurs des Certificats de la Compagnie respectivement.

Cette répartition sera effectuée au moyen des coupons attachés au présent certificat ou du coupon autrement.

2. title: Japanese Financial Document, Handwritten Manuscript with Official Seals, Meiji Era
description: Handwritten Japanese-language financial document on paper, densely written in vertical columns of cursive Japanese script (kana and kanji). Two red official seals (hanko) are affixed at the bottom. A red paper label appears in the upper right. The document style and script suggest Meiji era (1868-1912) or possibly late Edo period.

# Document Transcription

**Original (Classical Chinese/Japanese):**

The document contains extensive handwritten text in classical Chinese characters arranged in vertical columns reading from right to left. Due to the historical manuscript nature, aged paper quality, and handwritten script style, complete character-by-character transcription is challenging. However, key visible elements include:

**Header (Right margin, red seal box):**
明治十三年新正月合算 
(Meiji 13th Year, New Year's First Month, Combined Calculation)

**Main body:** Multiple columns of financial records with repeated characters including:
- Various numerical entries
- Account notations
- Administrative records

**Notable repeating character patterns visible:**
- 合 (sum/total) appearing frequently in structured rows
- 支 (branch/expenditure) 
- Multiple columns of subsidiary entries

---

**English Translation:**

**Header:** "Meiji 13th Year, New Year's First Month, Combined Calculation"

The document appears to be an annual financial statement or accounting record for an agricultural company, compiled in the first month of the Meiji 13th year (1874 A.D.).

---

**Notable markings:**

- **Red seal stamps** (vermillion seals) visible in upper right corner and scattered throughout lower portion of document
- **Official seals/chops** indicating authorization
- **Handwritten manuscript** in classical administrative script
- **Aged paper** with visible wear and foxing
- **Page 1 of 2** (as noted in description)

---

# Transcription of Japanese Financial Document

## Original (Japanese - Classical/Formal Script):

This is a handwritten manuscript in classical Japanese (likely Kuzushiji/草書き script), which makes precise transcription extremely challenging without specialized expertise in historical Japanese documents from the Meiji era.

The document appears to be structured as a financial statement with:
- Multiple columns of entries (likely representing different categories or months)
- Numerical values and calculations
- Official stamps/seals (visible as darker impressions)
- Repeated characters and formatting patterns typical of accounting documents

**Key observations:**
- The document is dated to Tongzhi 13th year (同治13年) = 1874 A.D.
- It is identified as an "annual income statement of an agriculture company" (農業会社年間収入報告)
- Contains extensive handwritten entries in vertical columns (right to left, traditional format)
- Multiple official seals are visible throughout

## Notable Markings:

- **Official seals/stamps**: Several dark circular and rectangular stamps are visible, indicating official documentation and verification
- **Handwritten annotations**: Scattered marginal notes and corrections
- **Structural divisions**: Clear column separations suggesting a ledger format
- **Page indicator**: "Page 2 of 2" - this is the final page of a two-page document

**Note**: Complete accurate transcription would require expertise in Meiji-era Japanese paleography and historical financial terminology.

title: Chinese Government 5% Reorganisation Gold Loan of 1913, Obligation de Frs. 505 / £20, No. 489,731
description: Quintilingual bearer bond No. 489,731 of the Chinese Government 5% Reorganisation Gold Loan of 1913, for £23,000,000 Sterling (or Marks 236,250,000 or Francs 631,250,000 or Roubles 236,500,000 or Yen 193.86). title: Colombian National Railway Company Limited, First Mortgage Debenture No. 2081, £100 at 6% per annum, 1909
description: First Mortgage Debenture No. 2081, £100 of The Colombian National Railway Company, Limited (incorporated under the Companies' Acts 1862-1886). Share capital £900,000 (900,000 ordinary shares of £1 each). Total first mortgage debentures issued: £200,000, carrying interest at 6% per annum. **THE**

**Colombian National Railway Company,**
**LIMITED.**

Incorporated under the Companies' Acts, 1862 to 1898.

**SHARE CAPITAL - - £900,000.**
**DIVIDED INTO**

**900,000 ORDINARY SHARES OF £1 EACH.**

**First Mortgage Debentures for £200,000.**

Carrying interest at the rate of Six per Cent. per annum. Created by a Resolution of the Board of Directors on the 29th day of October, 1899, pursuant to Clause 106th of the Articles of Association.

**No. 2081** | **DEBENTURE.** | **£ 100–**

[illegible]

**2.** The Company will in the meantime, and until payment of the said principal sum, pay to such Registered Holder interest thereon, or on so much thereof as shall for the time being remain unpaid at the rate of 6 per cent. per annum, by half-yearly payments on the 1st day of January and the 1st day of July in each year, the first of such half-yearly payments to be made on the 1st day of July, 1910, for interest from the 1st day of January, 1910.

## Body Text

To all whom it may Concern:

Be it known, That the Compañia Azucarera Baragua, a Corporation organized and existing under the laws of the Republic of Cuba, for value received, hereby acknowledges itself indebted and obligated to pay to the bearer hereof, or if registered to the registered holder hereof, the sum of **One Thousand Dollars** in gold coin of the United States of America, of the present standard of weight and fineness, on the **15th day of January, 1914**, with interest thereon from the date hereof at the rate of seven and one-half per centum per annum, payable semi-annually on the 15th days of January and July in each year, upon presentation and surrender of the annexed interest coupons at the office of the Trustee hereinafter named in the City of New York or at such other place as the Company may from time to time designate.

[Long legal text continues with detailed terms regarding the mortgage, sinking fund provisions, and conditions - text becomes increasingly difficult to read due to the diagonal striped cancellation mark overlaying the document]

title: Conditiën van een Fonds of Negotiatie, onder Directie van Theodore Passalaigue en Zoon, Suriname, Amsterdam
description: Printed conditions document (No. 306) for a plantation negotiatie fund under the direction of De Heeren Theodore Passalaigue en Zoon (Theodore Passalaigue and Son), merchants in Amsterdam, established for the benefit of planters in the Colony of Suriname. Article I states that the plantations of participating planters must be properly pledged as security, free of encumbrances, and a full inventory submitted. Article II requires planters to pledge a mortgage on all plantation production, pay interest annually at 10% per cent, and assigns a commission of van Justitie de Colonie Surinamen. Consistent with Dutch Suriname plantation negotiatie financing of the late 18th century.
creator: Theodore Passalaigue en Zoon
notes: Suriname plantation negotiatie conditions; No. 306; Theodore Passalaigue & Zoon, Amsterdam; Article I-II of conditions; 10% interest; plantation mortgage; late 18th century
transcription:
# Document Transcription

**Original (Dutch):**

N°.306.

D

CONDITIËN
VAN EEN

FONDS OF NEGOTIATIE,

Onder Directie van

De Heeren THEODORE PASSALAIGUE en ZOON,

Kooplieden te Amsterdam, ten behoeven van daar in deelhebbende Planters in de Colonie Surinaamen, en die vervolgens daar in door gedachten Directeurs zullen werden geadmitteerd en aangenoomen.

A R T. I.

De Planters welke door de Heeren Directeuren geadmitteerd werden in dit Fonds deel te neemen, zullen na dat hunne Plantagiën behoorlyк door beëdigde Prijsgeers geïnventariseerd zijn geworden, dezelve Plantagiën in behoorlyke Forma verhypothecheeren en verbinden aan en ten behoeven van dit Fonds, by Acte ten overstaan van de Heeren Raaden in den Hove van Civile Justitie der Colonie Surinaamen te passeeren, welke met willige Condemnatie van welgemelde Raaden zal moeten werden geffeckt, en zal dezelve Acte beneevens de Inventaris en Prisatie behoorlyк geregistreerd zijnde, en een Declaratoir van Secretarissen waar by Consteert dat dezelve Verhypothecqueerde Effecten zijn vry en onbefraward, aan de Heeren Directeuren moeten toegezonden worden.

A R T. II.

De Planters zullen zich in 't zelve Hypotheecq moeten verplichten, om alle de Producten van hunnen Plantagien provenieerde, te Consigneeren en affezenden aan de Heeren Directeuren deezèr Negotiatie, van wien zy ook de benoodigheeden voor hunnen Plantagien zullen doen ontbieden, dat zy dezelve door de Heeren Directeuren alher zullen laaten verslueeren, en ten dien einde ter behoorlyke tyd kennis van 't afsenden der Producten, en de Jaarlyks van hunne verschulddigde Capitaelen zullen betaalen Interessen van Ses per Centybot de dadelyk voldoening toe, en dat de voldoeininge en aflossinge zal geschieden in tien Termynen, te weeten; na verloop van d'eerste tien Jaaren van de Geldopneeminge, tien per Cento van de verschulddigde Somma, en voorts van Jaar tot Jaar tien per Cento, zoo lang de geheele Schuld zal zijn gekweeten en affedaan, met overgifle, dat ingevalle zy aan het bovengomelde niet morgen voldoen, het by hun verschulddigde Capital met de Interesten en Kolten directelyк kan werden opgeëffychr en gevordert, en by Executie verhaald aan hunne Persoonen, Plantagien, roerande en onroerende Goederen.

A

Art.

---

**English Translation:**

N°.306.

D

CONDITIONS
OF A

FUND OR NEGOTIATION,

Under the Direction of

The Gentlemen THEODORE PASSALAIGUE and SON,

Merchants in Amsterdam, for the benefit of participating Planters in the Colony of Suriname, and those who shall subsequently be admitted and accepted therein by said Directors.

ART. I.

The Planters who are admitted by the Gentlemen Directors to participate in this Fund shall, after their Plantations have been duly inventoried by sworn appraisers, mortgage said Plantations in proper Form and bind them to and for the benefit of this Fund, by deed executed before the Gentlemen Counsel in the Court of Civil Justice of the Colony of Suriname, which shall be effected with the willing consent of said Counsel, and when said deed has been properly registered along with the inventory and appraisal, and a Declaration from the Secretaries certifying that said mortgaged effects are free and unencumbered, shall be sent to the Gentlemen Directors.

ART. II.

The Planters shall obligate themselves by said mortgage to consign and send to the Gentlemen Directors of this negotiation all the products from their plantations, from whom they shall also obtain the necessities for their plantations, which they shall cause to be delivered to them by the Gentlemen Directors, and to that end at the proper time give notice of the shipment of products, and the yearly [amount due], and they shall pay interest on their owed capitals at six per cent per annum, and that the payment and redemption shall occur in ten terms, namely: after the expiration of the first ten years from the borrowing of money, ten per cent of the owed sum, and thereafter from year to year ten per cent, so long as the entire debt shall be paid and discharged, with the proviso that should they fail to meet the aforesaid obligation, their owed capital with interest and costs may be directly enforced and collected, and by execution recovered from their persons, plantations, moveable and immoveable property.

A

Art.

title: Confederate States of America, $1,000 Bond, Loan Authorized by Act of Congress, April 30, 1863
description: $1,000 bearer bond of the Confederate States of America, issued under the Loan Authorized by Act of Congress Approved April 30, 1863. Features the Confederate States of America title in ornate script at top, a central portrait of a Confederate official (possibly Secretary of the Treasury Christopher Memminger or another Confederate leader), and $1,000 / ONE THOUSAND DOLLARS denomination prominently displayed. Printed in black on gray/white paper. Dated June 1, 1863 (approximately). Signed by the Register of the Treasury in pursuance of the Act of Congress. This is one of the major Confederate war finance bond issues of the American Civil War.
creator: Confederate States of America, Treasury Department

# Transcription

£.12. 17. 072                                                          Numb. 11593

STATE of CONNECTICUT.

Treasury-Office, June 1, A. D. 1782.

THE State of Connecticut doth owe unto _________________ who hath served in the [Continental Army], the Sum of Twelve Pounds Seventeen Shillings and [Eight Pence], Being one fourth Part of the Balance found on his Order at this Office, in Gold or Silver, on the first Day of June, A. D. One Thousand Seven Hundred and Eighty-Two, with annual Interest thereof; which Interest shall be paid unto him or his Order annually, at the same Place, on the first Day of June, if demanded, pursuant to an Act of the General Assembly, held at Hartford, the second Thursday of May last.

title: Dutch Life Annuity Tontine Conditions, No. 21 (Een en Twintig), f.500 on 20 Lives, 1688
description: Printed copy (COPIA) of life annuity tontine conditions No. 21 (Nummer Een en Twintig), setting out the agreed terms for investing 500 guilders capital on the lives of twenty persons over 30 years old. The document establishes that the full capital sum of 500 guilders per lyer (life-share) must be paid within 45 days; interest payments are to be made annually each August; and the tontine fund is to run for thirty years. References "Pieter de Lange" as a principal figure and sets the first year's interest day as August 1, 1688. An important early Dutch life annuity/tontine document.
creator: Pieter de Lange (director)
notes: Life annuity tontine No. 21; f.500 capital on 20 lives over 30 years; August 1688 first interest payment; 30-year term; early Dutch tontine document
transcription:
# Document Transcription

**Original (Dutch):**

8
COPIA.                                    No. EEN EN TWINTIG.

Conditien en Voorwaarden, daar op de
ondergetekende Persoonen met den anderen geac-
cordeert, en over-een gekomen zijn, nopende
het fournissement van een somme van tien duy-
sent guldens Capitaal, te constituëren op de
respective Lyven van twintig Persoonen, boven
de dertig Jaaren oudt.

I.

Dat elk der Contractanten, tot de gefeyde Somme fal moeten fourneren en opbrengen, voor
Jaaren, posten, een Somma van vyf honderd Caroli guldens, hunnen deel vyt van acht dagen
naar 't pasferen defes, op Affigatie van de Gecommitteerdens, in handelen van Pieter de Lange,
als fpecialyk by de Geinterefleerdens daar toe gauthorifoert mitsdien, verklaerende yeder
zyn bellofte de Somme te constituëren ten lyve van loodiange Persoonen, als vyder by Lyve on-
dertekeninge fal nomineren, fyndse gebooren voor den eerften Iuly des Jaars lestienhoridert tevyn fyftg.

Dat het voorfz. Capitaal van tien duysfent guldens alloo gefourneerdt fynde, da naar te noemen Gecommitteer-
dens 't'telve met de aiderecriften doenlyek, fullen aanleggen en beftedien in Obligation ten laften van 'rgemeene Landt,
of noch vyt de Stadt, en andere publyque Comptoren binnen de felve, naar fy Gecommit-
teerdens raadtdaam en beft doordcelén fullen.

III.

Dat de voorfz. Obligatie, voor de voornoemde Capitale Somma als boven komende te provenieren, op
Intereffen fullen moeten loopen, foo lange dit Collegïe of Contract fal komen te duuren; en by aldien de felve
Obligatien eerder mochten werden afgesloft, fullen de Gecommitteerdens als dan geholden welen, met den
aldercerijten of wel binnen den tytsk van vier weken, de penningen daar van procedaende, wedeom aan te leg-
gen, als vooren Articul 2 geifrt ?, oock mede beyorens de afloffinge, alle mogeyicke devooire moeten aan
wenden, omme de felve Obligation, vas 't doenlyek, noch loopende te houden.

IV.

Dat de Gecommitteerdens de Intereffen der vooren geroerde Obligation, r'elkens ten vervaldage, of bin-
nen korten tyd daar na fullen latén ontfangen, en alle Jaren laten maken eene rekeninge, gereekent
naar 't getal der Persoonen, die op de vervaldinge van het—vooraande Jaren in het leven geweeft zyn, en ingevol-
ge van dien aan de Participanten, wegcnds felve Persoonen, de voorfz. oultangen Intereft, yder pro rata
fyn ingelegt Capitaal uytgeleyt, en doen genieten, tot welcke uytreckeninghe de voornoemde Pieter de Lange,
en Sara de la Forterie 't'Huysvrouwe, of de langft levende van hen beyde gecommitteerd werden mitsdeden
en werden de Gecommitteerdens geathorisiert om naar 't'overlvden van de langtlivende, een ander in fyn
plaatfe te cligen en aan te ftellen, mits dat de felve woonachtig zy binnen defte Stad.

V.

Dat de vervaltyd van deie Lyfsfenten Jaarlyck fal zyn den cerften Auguiti, aanvang nemende zoo ras de
kloeke des nachts naar den laafste Iuly, 12 iuren zal hebben gejlugen, en zulcks het eerfte Iaar den eerften
Auguiti 1688.

VI.

Dat alle de Intereffen of Renten, die jaarlycks door 't overlvden van eenige der gemelte Lyven of Persoonen,
waar op ymandt van de Contractanten fyne Actie fal hebben conftitveertt fullen komen te vervallen, weder-
om fullen komen te accresieeren by egale repartitie, naar het getal van de noch vordere in 't leven blyvenide
Persoonen, en een iegde rante de felve, of der felver recht hebbende, en niet te conftitveren tot de twee laat-
fie toe, als in 't volgende Articul nader wordt uitgedruckt; des nochtans dat de Erfgenamen, of het recht
hebbende van deriglftorvcne, fullen genieten de repartitie van het Sterf-Jaar, daar mne de felve overleden fal
zyn; 's doelantg als of ihev geleche Sterf-jaer hadt uitgeleeft, mits het contract overleverde. Kommentceren
de de Contractanten in cas van overlvden van nul af aan, voor haar en hare Erigenamen, van alle foudange.
recht en achtie, als fy refpectivelcyk door haar fomfillement, tot de gemeete Capitaal en Renten hebben ge-
hadt, en behooste van alle vordere Intereffen, aan welcke het voorfz. Capitaal, en de volgende Intereffen
fucceffivelyck invoegen als vooren, fullen komen te aceresleeren.

VII.

Dat alle de gemeelte Lyven of Persoonen, tot op twee na overlrden fynde, alle de voorfz. Obligatien als dan
volkomen eigen fullen zyn, aan de genen de welcke op de voorfz. twee laaffte Persoonen hebben getekent, of
hun recht hebbende, en onder de felve in gelyckc Portien verdeelt moeten werden, niet tegenftaande den
eenen van beter of lleelicbter Confituutie al'andere foude mogen welen, s foo dat als dan yeder gerechte hclft
als in geen goedti, naar fich zal vermogen nemen, en daar mede doen en handelen maar ijn blleuen als ihy
met syn ander vry eysen goedi kan en vermacht te doen, en dat daar toe mede gerechtigt fullen zyn d'Erfgena-
men of het recht hebbende van die gene, die uyt de gemelte twee laatfte Persoonen voor de uytdeelinge overle-
den foude mogén welen, werdende mitsdelen de Geinterefleercénts ten lyve van de felve twee laaffte Persoonen
van geleyde Collegïe, oock foo veel noodt zy, door hun overige Contractanten, nu voor als dan, de
voorfz.

---

## English Translation:

**8
COPY.                                    No. TWENTY-ONE.**

**Conditions and Terms which the undersigned Persons have mutually agreed upon, concerning the provision of a sum of ten thousand guilders Capital, to be constituted on the respective Lives of twenty Persons, over thirty years of age.**

**I.**

That each of the Contractors shall furnish and provide, for the aforesaid Sum, for Years, amounts, a Sum of five hundred Caroli guilders, their portion out of eight days after the passing thereof, on Affigation of the Commissioners, in the handling of Pieter de Lange, as specifically authorized by the Interested parties herein, declaring each his promise to constitute the Sum for the life of lodging Persons, as further by Lives under-signed shall name, being born before the first July of the Year one thousand six hundred eighty-eight.

That the aforesaid Capital of ten thousand guilders, having been thus furnished

title: Nicolaas Brant Suriname Plantation Negotiatie Conditions, f.150,000 at 6%, Amsterdam, May 1, 1765
description: Printed conditions and pledge document for the Suriname plantation negotiatie under the direction of Nicolaas Brant, merchant in Amsterdam, dated May 1, 1765. The director's pledge statement opens: "Ik Ondergeschreven NICOLAAS BRANT, als dirigerende de Negotiatie breeder in het geannexeerde Plan gemeld, beloove, en verbindende my by dezen, omme my in myne gedagte qualiteit stipulyk te zullen gedragen aan de Conditien..." The negotiatie was to advance 150,000 Dutch guilders to planters in the Colony of Suriname at 6% per annum interest. Articles I–III of the conditions follow, covering mortgage requirements, interest obligations, and planter eligibility.
creator: Nicolaas Brant
notes: Suriname plantation negotiatie; f.150,000 at 6%; Nicolaas Brant director, Amsterdam; Articles I–III of conditions; plantation mortgage requirements; 1765
transcription:
# Transcription

**Original:**

Copia.

Ik Ondergeschreven NICOLAAS BRANT, als dirigerende de Negotiatie breeder in het geannexeerde Plan gemeld, beloove, en verbindende my by deezen, omme my in myne gedagte qualiteit stiptelyk te zullen gedragen aan de Conditien daar inne ter nedergesteld, zullende by vergrooting deazer Negotiatie gelyke Obligation als ten deezen is geslchied, werden uitgegeven.

Actum Amsteldam den Eerften May 1765.

(Was geteekent)

NICOLAAS BRANT.

Conditien en Voorwaardens waar op door verscheiden Particulieren aan de Planters in de Colonie van Surinaamen, onder directie van de Heer NICOLAAS BRANT, Koopman tot Amsteldam, zal werden verstrekt provisioned een somma van honderd à honderd en vyftig duizend Guldens Hollands, tegens den Interesse van zes percent in het Jaar, bestaande in de volgende:

I.

Dat door ieder der Planters in de Colonie Surinaamen voor de penningen aan deselve te verstreken, ten behoewen van NICOLAAS BRANT, als Directeur van dit Fonds, uit dus voor Rekening van de respectieve Geinteresseerdens, en derzelver regt vorkryggende, zal werder verbypotheceert en speciaal verbonden desselfs Plantage of Plantageën, met alle de Gebouwen, Slaaven, Paarden, Beesten, Despot en Beplantingen, en al het gene daar aan verder op en dependant is, en daar van passeeren en uitbrengen een bevoolyke schuldbekentenis en hypothecatie, verflekt met willige condemnatie van de Edelen Hooj van Justitie aldaar.

II.

Tot securiteit van de Geldgeevers zullen de Plantageën, die in deezen zullen worden verbonden op nieuw door de ordinaris daar toe gequalifficeerde de Beldigde Persoonen moeten zijn gewaarborgd.

III.

---

# English Translation

Copy.

I, the undersigned NICOLAAS BRANT, directing the Negotiation as further mentioned in the annexed Plan, do promise and obligate myself by these present, to conduct myself according to the Conditions specified therein, and obligations similar to this one shall be issued upon enlargement of this Negotiation.

Done in Amsterdam on the First of May 1765.

(Was signed)

NICOLAAS BRANT.

Conditions and Terms under which various Individuals to the Planters in the Colony of Suriname, under the direction of Mr. NICOLAAS BRANT, Merchant in Amsterdam, shall be advanced a sum of one hundred to one hundred and fifty thousand Guilders Holland, against Interest of six percent per annum, consisting in the following:

I.

That by each of the Planters in the Colony of Suriname for the monies to be advanced to them, for the benefit of NICOLAAS BRANT, as Director of this Fund, out of the account of the respective Interested parties, and their rightful receipts, shall be mortgaged and specially pledged that Planter's plantation or plantations, with all the Buildings, Slaves, Horses, Cattle, Tools and Improvements, and all things thereto annexed and depending thereon, and thereupon shall pass and issue a satisfactory deed of acknowledgment and mortgage, executed with voluntary subjection to the judgment of the High Court of Justice there.

II.

For the security of the Money-lenders, the Plantations which shall be bound in these present shall anew be warranted by the ordinary persons properly qualified for the Certified Persons.

III.

[Text continues but is cut off in the image]

title: Credit Foncier Cubain / Banco Territorial de Cuba, 3% Series A Bearer Obligation, $96.16 US Gold / 500 Francs

title: Denver & Rio Grande Spoorweg-Maatschappij (Preferred Stock), Dutch Certificaat van $1,000, No. 669, Amsterdam, 1890
description: Dutch bearer certificate (Certificaat) No. 669 for $1,000 face value, representing 10 shares of $100 each in the Preferred Stock of the Denver & Rio Grande Spoorweg-Maatschappij (Denver & Rio Grande Railway Company). The certificate was issued and administered by the Internationale Administratie-Kantoor in Amsterdam, enabling Dutch investors to hold American railroad preferred stock through a Dutch bearer instrument. Interest and dividends payable in Amsterdam. Dated Amsterdam, December 6, 1890. Signed by the administrators.
creator: Internationale Administratie-Kantoor, Amsterdam
notes: Denver & Rio Grande Railway preferred stock; Dutch bearer certificaat; No. 669; $1,000 = 10 shares at $100; Internationale Administratie-Kantoor Amsterdam; December 6, 1890
transcription:
# Document Transcription

## Original Text (Dutch):

Denver & Rio Grande Spoorweg-Maatschappij.
(PREFERRED STOCK.)

CERTIFICAAT van $ 1000,
No. 669

Zonder deze is gerechtigd tot Tien bevoorregte Aandelen à $ 100,— in de Denver Rio Grande Spoorwegen-Maatschappij (Preferred Stock), MET MEER ANDERE INGESCHREVEN OP NAAM VAN DE MAATSCHAPPIJ BEHEERD VAN HET ADMINISTRATIE-KANTOOR, OPGERICHT DOOR HUEBRECHT, VAN HARENCASPEL & VAS VISSER, Jon geduld onderworpen bij den ondergetekenden Notaris. De uitdeeligingen op de geleponeerde Aandelen zullen voor rekening van den houder in Amerika geïnassecerd, overgemaakt en aldier uitbetaald worden.

De invisseling van Certificaten met de onvervallen Dividend-Bewijzen tegen de oorspronkelijke Aandelen geschiedt overeenkomstig het Bericht door bovenvermeld Administratie-Kantoor, uitgegeven in December 1880.

Amsterdam, [date], 188[0]

Getekend door mij,
[Signature] A. Meysenbauer
Notaris.

De Maatschappij tot beheer van het Administratie-Kantoor Voorzitend:
[Signature - illegible]

Hierbij afgegeven Dividend-Bewijzen van No. / tot No. 24, met een Talon.

---

## English Translation:

Denver & Rio Grande Railway Company.
(PREFERRED STOCK.)

CERTIFICATE of $1,000
No. 669

Without this [certificate] is entitled to Ten preferred Shares at $100 each in the Denver Rio Grande Railway Company (Preferred Stock), WITH MORE OTHERS REGISTERED IN THE NAME OF THE COMPANY MANAGED BY THE ADMINISTRATION OFFICE, ESTABLISHED BY HUEBRECHT, VAN HARENCASPEL & VAS VISSER, now subject to the authority of the undersigned Notary. The distributions on the paid-in Shares shall be collected on behalf of the holder in America, transferred and paid out there.

The conversion of Certificates with the outstanding Dividend Vouchers against the original Shares takes place in accordance with the Notice issued by the aforementioned Administration Office in December 1880.

Amsterdam, [date], 188[0]

Signed by me,
[Signature] A. Meysenbauer
Notary.

The Company for the management of the Administration Office, President:
[Signature - illegible]

Hereby issued Dividend Vouchers from No. / to No. 24, with a Coupon.

---

## Notable Markings:

- **Serial Number:** 669
- **Face Value:** $1,000 (representing 10 shares of $100 each)
- **Signatures:** Two handwritten signatures present (notary A. Meysenbauer and company administrator - partially illegible)
- **Stamps/Seals:** Circular seal visible in upper right corner (text partially legible, appears to be institutional stamp)
- **Watermark:** "AMSTERDAM" text visible as security watermark across document
- **Ornamental Border:** Elaborate decorative frame with floral and geometric patterns typical of 19th-century financial documents
- **Printer Mark:** "Gedruk bij Gebroe[de]rs Binger/ Amsterdam" (Printed by Brothers Binger/Amsterdam) at bottom
- **Date:** December 6, 1890 (Amsterdam)

title: Deutsche Äußere Anleihe 1924 (Dawes-Anleihe), Schweizerische Ausgabe, Fundierungsschuldverschreibung No. 10080, 200 CHF, 1953
description: Post-war German funding bond (Fundierungsschuldverschreibung) No. 10080 for 200 Swiss Francs, issued by the Federal Republic of Germany (Bundesschuldenverwaltung, Bad Homburg) in 1953. Issued under the terms of the London Debt Agreement on German Foreign Debts (Londoner Abkommen über Deutsche Auslandsschulden 1953), Annex I, in exchange for interest arrears on the Deutsche Äußere Anleihe 1924 (Dawes Loan), Swiss issue. Represents 8 semi-annual interest payments of 25 CHF each that accrued between October 16, 1944 and October 15, 1952. Redemption deferred until German reunification ("nicht vor der Wiedervereinigung Deutschlands"). Signed by Bundesschuldenverwaltung. Bad Homburg v.d.H., 1953.
creator: Bundesrepublik Deutschland, Bundesschuldenverwaltung
notes: Dawes Loan 1924 Swiss issue; post-WWII arrears bond; No. 10080; 200 CHF; 8 semi-annual arrears 1944-1952; London Debt Agreement 1953; redemption deferred until German reunification
transcription:
# Original:

Deutsche Äußere Anleihe 1924
(Dawes-Anleihe)
Schweizerische Ausgabe

Nr. 10080

Die Bundesrepublik Deutschland verpflichtet sich, dem Inhaber im Umtausch gegen diesen Bezugsschein eine nach den Bestimmungen des Londoner Abkommens über Deutsche Auslandsschulden 1953 und seiner Anlage 1 ausgestellte Fundierungsschuldverschreibung im Nennwert von

200 Schweizer Franken
für Zinsrückstände der Deutschen Äußeren Anleihe 1924
(Schweizerische Ausgabe)

zu übergeben, jedoch nicht vor der Wiedervereinigung Deutschlands.

Der in dieser Fundierungsschuldverschreibung verkörperte Anspruch entspricht dem Fundierungswert von zwischen dem 16. Oktober 1944 und dem 15. Oktober 1952 aufgelaufenen 8 Halbjahrssinsen von je 25 Schweizer Franken.

Bad Homburg v.d.H., im Jahre 1953

Für die Bundesrepublik Deutschland
Bundesschuldenverwaltung

[Signatures: Präsident / Vizepräsident]

---

# English translation:

German Foreign Loan 1924
(Dawes Loan)
Swiss Edition

No. 10080

The Federal Republic of Germany undertakes to deliver to the holder, in exchange for this certificate, a funding debt certificate issued in accordance with the provisions of the London Agreement on German Foreign Debts 1953 and its Annex 1, with a face value of

200 Swiss Francs
for interest arrears of the German Foreign Loan 1924
(Swiss Edition)

to be handed over, however not before the reunification of Germany.

The claim embodied in this funding debt certificate corresponds to the funding value of 8 semi-annual interest accruals of 25 Swiss Francs each accumulated between October 16, 1944 and October 15, 1952.

Bad Homburg v.d.H., in the year 1953

For the Federal Republic of Germany
Federal Debt Administration

[Signatures: President / Vice-President]

---

- **Printer Mark:** "Bundesdruckerei" (Federal Printing Office) at bottom center
- **Document Classification:** Light pink/mauve background paper with watermark impressions visible

title: Certificaat van Drie Percents Fransche Fondsen, No. 309, Frs. 300/year (on Frs. 10,000 capital), Hope & Co., Amsterdam, 1861
description: Dutch certificate No. 309 (Certificaat van Drie Percents Fransche Fondsen) entitling the bearer to Frs. 300 annual income (Trois Cents Franken Rente) inscribed in the French National Debt Register (Grootboek der Publieke Schuld van Frankryk), representing a nominal capital of Frs. 10,000. Managed by the Administratie-Kantoor in Amsterdam under the direction of Hope & Co., Ketwich & Voombergh, and Wed. W. Borski. Interest to be received and paid out by the Company on behalf of the holder, according to the conditions of the certificate. Amsterdam, October 19, 1861. Signed by all three managing houses. The twentieth issue of this certificate type (Twintigste Uitreikingbrief).
creator: Hope & Co.; Ketwich & Voombergh; Wed. W. Borski
notes: 3% French public debt (Rente); No. 309; Frs. 300/year income on Frs. 10,000 nominal capital; Hope & Co., Ketwich & Voombergh, Wed. Borski; Amsterdam October 19, 1861; 20th certificate series
transcription:
# Transcription

**Original (Dutch):**

CERTIFICAAT
VAN
DRIE PERCENTS FRANSCHE FONDSEN.

N°.309 Drie Honderd Franken Rente.
(Voldoende voor F. 10000.— nominaal kapitaal.)

Zonder drzes is geregtigd tot DRIE HONDERD FRANKEN JAARLIJKSCHE RENTE, ingeschreven in het Grootboek der Publieke Schuld van Frankryk, ten name van het Administratie-Kantoor, opgericht te Amsterdam, onder Directie van:

HOPE & CO.,
KETWICH & VOOMBERGH
EN
Wed. W. BORSKI.

Welke Renten voor rekening en risico van den HOUDER zullen worden ontvangen en uitbetaald op Coupons ingevolge Art. 6 van het uitgegeven Bericht.

De Houder dezes is bevoegd dit Certificaat met de bijbehoorende vervangen coupons in te wisselen voor Titres Coupons in te wisselen tegen een Orderbiljet op hare Gemagtdigden te PARIJS, ten einde de Rente, waaroe de Houder bij dit Certificaat is geregtigd, te doen transporteeren op zoodanige Naam of Namen, als door hem zal worden opgegeven, en zulks tegen voldoening aan het Administratie-Kantoor van de onkosten in Art. 10 van het Bericht vermeld.

Amsterdam, 19 Oktober 1861.

Geteekend bij ons
[signature] C. Euwenshalter [?]
[signature]
Notaris.

Hierbij gevoegd TWINTIG Uitreikingbrief COUPONS, del 1 Oktober 1873, met TALON.

---

**English translation:**

CERTIFICATE
OF
THREE PERCENT FRENCH FUNDS.

No. 309 Three Hundred Francs Income.
(Sufficient for F. 10,000.— nominal capital.)

The bearer hereof is entitled to THREE HUNDRED FRANCS ANNUAL INCOME, registered in the Public Debt Register of France, in the name of the Administrative Office, established in Amsterdam, under the Direction of:

HOPE & CO.,
KETWICH & VOOMBERGH
AND
Wed. W. BORSKI.

Which Income shall be received and paid out on behalf of and at the risk of the HOLDER according to Coupons in accordance with Article 6 of the issued Notice.

The Holder hereof is entitled to exchange this Certificate with the attached coupons for Titre Coupons to exchange against an Order Letter to his Authorized Agents in PARIS, in order that the Income to which the Holder is entitled by this Certificate shall be transferred to such Name or Names as shall be designated by him, and such upon payment to the Administrative Office of the costs mentioned in Article 10 of the Notice.

Amsterdam, 19 October 1861.

Signed by us
[signatures]
Notary.

Hereto attached TWENTY Issue Coupons, of 1 October 1873, with TALON.

---

title: German External Loan 1924 (Dawes-Anleihe), Trilingual Rights Certificate No. A 025220, £40, Bad Homburg, January 4, 1960
description: Trilingual rights certificate (Bezugschein / Rights Certificate / Bon) No. A 025220 for £40 Sterling, issued by the Federal Republic of Germany under the London Debt Agreement of 1953. Covers both the British Issue (Britische Ausgabe) and French Tranche of the Deutsche Äußere Anleihe 1924 (German External Loan 1924 / Emprunt Extérieur Allemand 1924), also known as the Dawes Loan. The certificate entitles the holder to receive new 3% funding bonds (Fundierungsschuldverschreibungen) from the German Federal Republic in exchange for arrears interest on the original 1924 bonds. Trilingual in German, English, and French. Bad Homburg, January 4, 1960. Signed by Bundesschuldenverwaltung.
creator: Bundesrepublik Deutschland, Bundesschuldenverwaltung
notes: Dawes Loan 1924; trilingual Bezugschein/Rights Certificate/Bon; No. A 025220; £40; British and French tranches; London Debt Agreement 1953; Bad Homburg January 4, 1960
transcription:
# Transcription: German External Loan 1924 (Dawes Loan) Rights Certificate

## Serial Number
Nr. A 025220 (appears twice, top left and right)

## Denomination
£40 (Forty Pounds Sterling)

---

## GERMAN TEXT (Left Column)

**DEUTSCHE ÄUSSERE ANLEIHE 1924**
(DAWES-ANLEIHE)
**BRITISCHE AUSGABE**
**BEZUGSCHEIN**

Dieser Bezugschein gewährt seinem Inhaber einen Anspruch auf Auslieferung von drei-prozentigen Schuldverschreibungen gemäß den Bestimmungen des Abkommens über deutsche Auslandsschulden vom 27. Februar 1953 und seiner Anlage I zur Fundierung von Zinsrückständen für die Zeit vom 15. Oktober 1944 bis zum 14. Oktober 1952 einschließlich auf eine Schuldverschreibung über £ 100 der ursprünglichen britischen Ausgabe der Deutschen Äußeren Anleihe 1924. Die Zinszuschläge, berechnet zu einem Satz von 5 v. H. [jährlich ohne Zinseszinsen, haben einen Fundierungswert von nominal

**£ 40**

**VIERZIG PFUND STERLING.**

Die Fundierungsschuldverschreibungen werden im Falle der Wiedervereinigung Deutschlands gegen Eintragung dieses Bezugsscheins ausgegeben.

Bad Homburg v.d.H., den 4. Januar 1960

GEGENGEZEICHNET ZUR BEGLAUBIGUNG:
BANK VON ENGLAND UND DIE GESELLSCHAFT DER
BANK VON ENGLAND
COUNTERSIGNED FOR IDENTIFICATION:
FOR THE GOVERNOR & COMPANY OF THE BANK OF ENGLAND
CONTRESIGNÉE POUR CONTRÔLE:
POUR LE GOUVERNEUR ET LA COMPAGNIE DE LA
BANQUE D'ANGLETERRE PAR

[Signature]

BEVOLLMÄCHTIGTER KASSIERER
AN AUTHORISED CASHIER
CAISSIER AUTORISÉ

---

## ENGLISH TEXT (Center Column)

**GERMAN EXTERNAL LOAN 1924**
**(DAWES LOAN)**
**BRITISH ISSUE**
**RIGHTS CERTIFICATE**

This rights certificate confers upon the holder a claim to receive 3% bonds in accordance with the terms of the Agreement on German External Debts of 27 th February 1953 and Annex I thereto in respect of the funding of arrears of interest for the period 15 th October 1944 to 14 th October 1952 inclusive, on a bond of £ 100 of the original British Issue of the German External Loan 1924. The arrears of interest, calculated at a rate of 5% per annum simple interest, have a funding value of

**£ 40 nominal**

**FORTY POUNDS.**

In the event of the reunification of Germany the funding bonds will be issued against surrender of this rights certificate.

Bad Homburg v.d.H., 4 th January 1960

---

## FRENCH TEXT (Right Column)

**EMPRUNT EXTERIEUR ALLEMAND 1924**
**(EMPRUNT DAWES)**
**TRANCHE ANGLAISE**
**BON**

Le porteur du présent bon a le droit de recevoir, conformément aux dispositions de l'Accord de Londres sur les dettes extérieures allemandes du 27 février 1953 et de son Annexe I, des obligations 3% à titre de consolidation des arrérages d'intérêts, dus pendant la période du 15 octobre 1944 au 14 octobre 1952 inclus, sur une obligation de 100 livres sterling de l'émission initiale de l'Emprunt extérieur allemand 1924 (tranche anglaise). Les arrérages d'intérêts, calculés au taux de 5% (intérêts simples), ont, pour la consolidation, une valeur nominale de

**40 Livres sterling**

**QUARANTE LIVRES STERLING.**

Les obligations de consolidation seront délivrées, contre remise du présent bon, en cas de réunification de l'Allemagne.

Bad Homburg v.d.H., le 4 Janvier 1960

**FÜR DIE BUNDESREPUBLIK DEUTSCHLAND**
**FOR THE FEDERAL REPUBLIC OF GERMANY**
**POUR LA REPUBLIQUE FEDERALE D'ALLEMAGNE**

**BUNDESSCHULDENVERWALTUNG**

[Signature]
**PRÄSIDENT** / **VIZEPRÄSIDENT**

title: German Government International 5½% Loan 1930 (Young Plan), Dollar Gold Bond No. 16917, $1,000, Due 1965
description: US Dollar-denominated bearer gold bond No. 16917 of the German Government International 5½ Per Cent Loan 1930 (Young Plan), face value $1,000, due June 1, 1965. Interest at 5½% per annum payable semi-annually on June 1 and December 1. Principal and interest payable at the office of J.P. Morgan & Co. in the Borough of Manhattan, City of New York. The left side of this large-format bond contains the full "GENERAL BOND" conditions text. Printed in red/maroon on cream paper. The Young Plan Loan (1930) was issued to fund Germany's WWI reparations under the Young Committee schedule.

title: United States Treasury: 3% Debt Certificate, No. 4693, $14,000 (Donato A. Burton of London, 1792)
description: This handwritten and engrossed document is a United States Treasury 3% Debt Certificate, No. 4693, issued from the Register's Office on the 14th of June, 1792. It attests that the United States of America owes to Donato A. Burton of London, or their Assigns, the sum of Fourteen Thousand Dollars, bearing interest at three per Centum per Annum from the first day of April 1792, payable quarterly and subject to redemption by payment of the principal whenever provision shall be made therefor by Law. The debt is recorded in the Register's Office and is transferable only by appearance in person or by attorney at the proper office. The document is signed by Joseph Nourse as Register of the Treasury, with an attestation below from Clement Biddle, Notary Public for the Commonwealth of Pennsylvania in Philadelphia, certifying the authenticity of the instrument on the fourteenth day of June, 1792. This certificate represents one of the early U.S. federal debt instruments issued under Alexander Hamilton's funding system.
creator: United States. Treasury Department
notes: U.S. Treasury 3% Debt Certificate No. 4693. $14,000 issued to Donato A. Burton of London. Signed by Joseph Nourse, Register of the Treasury. Notarized by Clement Biddle, Notary Public, Philadelphia, June 14, 1792.
transcription:
# Transcription

**Treasury of the United States**

No. 4693, Register's Office 14 June 1792

**BE IT KNOWN** that there is due from the United States of America unto **Donato A. Burton** of London, or their Assigns the Sum of **Fourteen Thousand and Dollars** bearing interest at three per Centum per Annum from the **First of April 1792** inclusively; payable quarterly, at such place as Redemption, by the Payment of said Sum, when Provision shall be made therefor by Law; which Debt is recorded in this Office, and is transferable only by Appearance in Person, or by Attorney, at the proper Office, according to the Rules and Forms instituted for that Purpose.

**$14,000 Dollars**

**Joseph Nourse Reg.**

title: Compagnie de Colonisation Américaine: Action de 100 Acres de Terres en Virginie et Kentucki, Série B, No. A/234 (1300 Francs)
description: This printed and engraved share certificate is an action (share) of the Compagnie de Colonisation Américaine (American Colonization Company), representing the ownership of 100 acres of land in the states of Virginia and Kentucky, United States. The certificate is from Série B, No. A/234, at a face value of 1300 Francs, issued to Monsieur Charles Guillaume Juste Jerome as proprietor of the 100 acres. The text explains that the land forms part of 1,500,000 acres belonging to the Company, under the guarantee of De Beers and Compagnie, a Paris banking house. The certificate carries extensive dividend coupon strips on both the left and right margins, each labeled 'Coupon d'Action, Série B, de la Compagnie de Colonisation Américaine' with sequential coupon numbers and dates. The central text sets out the terms of the share, including the Company's cultivation and colonization obligations, the 80-franc annual dividend target, and the shareholder's rights under French law. This document is an example of French speculative investment in American land companies during the 1790s.
creator: Compagnie de Colonisation Américaine
notes: Compagnie de Colonisation Américaine, Action Série B, No. A/234, 100 acres in Virginia and Kentucky, 1300 Francs. Issued to Charles Guillaume Juste Jerome. Guaranteed by De Beers et Compagnie, Paris. With dividend coupon strips.
transcription:
# Transcription of Compagnie de Colonisation Américaine Share Certificate

## Original (French):

**[Left margin - repeating coupon strips]**

Coupons d'Action, Série B, de la Compagnie de Colonisation Américaine.
N° 30. Coupon de dividende de l'Action n° 2434 à recevoir le 15 juillet 1850, au Comptoir de la Compagnie, à Paris.

[Similar entries for coupons numbered: 27, 25, 23, 21, 19, 17, 15, 13, 11 with dates ranging from 1845-1849]

**[Central document text]**

**Compagnie de Colonisation Américaine.**

**Action**

**de 100 Acres de Terres dans les États de Virginie et de Kentucky.**

**Série B, N° A/234          France 1300**

M. Charles Guillaume Juste Jérôme est propriétaire de cent acres de Terres situées dans les États de Virginie et de Kentucky, libres de toutes hypothèques et privilèges, faisant partie de 1,849,000 acres de Terres appartenant à la Compagnie de Colonisation Américaine, constituée sous la raison de De Bedern et Compagnie, suivant acte passé devant Maître Froger Deschesnes, et son Sucesseur, Notaires à Paris; lequel et affidé conformément à la loi.

La Compagnie prend les mesures propres à livrer la plus grand partie possible de ses Propriétés par la Colonisation et opérations industrielles et commerciales qui se rattachent naturellement à son Entreprise. Elle isocupe des moyens de faciliter des Établissements dans ses terres à ceux qui désirent s'y fixer dans les États-Unis d'Amérique, et d'affermer leur Culture et leur vente de l'Exploitation européenne.

À cet effet, les Terres assuises sont réparties de la manière suivante: 349,000 acres sont destinées à être vendues ½ à gré à une Colons, qui préfèrent devenir Propriétaires. Le produit de la vente est laissé par les members fondateurs de la Compagnie dans l'Entreprise de la Colonisation, sans interet pendant les dix premières années; après l'expiration desquelles, toute Somme rentrée est intéressée à 5 p.c. à laquelle l'Entreprise pourra conserver annuelles.

1,500,000 acres restant ont été converties en Quinze mille Actions, chacune de Cent acres de Terres.

La Compagnie mettra ces terres en valeur par des Usines à forme, des Moulins à blé et à Scie, des Exploitations de Bois de Construction, et par tous les Établissements de commerce et de manufactures que la culture et la richesse du sol amèneront naturellement.

Les revenus périodiques qui en découleront, selon les bénéfices sont transmissibles par simple endossement. L'action ne pourra jamais être sujette à aucun appel de fonds; le bénéfice net, résultant des opérations de la Compagnie, formera le dividende que l'Actionnaire recevra annuellement sur la présentation des Coupons annuels.

Trois mille Actions, depuis le N° 1 jusqu'à 3,000, formant la série A, sont affectées à l'augmentation du Capital de la Compagnie, destinée à fixer opérations de la Colonisation.

Les autres actions seules (Série B depuis N° 1 jusqu'à 15,000) formeront la série ouverte, à compléter du 15 Juillet 1831.

Cet intérêt bien les dividends jusqu'à ce que celui-ci arrêté Six pour cent. Attendu que les derniers, provenant de la vente de ces actions, sont employés dans la Colonisation au profit de tous les actionnaires, la Compagnie aura le droit d'arrêter une science sur le dividende de 15,000 Actions, leur fin à d'accorder. Dès jour cela, nous détenons ne surplus garanti, pour la fin de mettre les actions premières vile pourvau avoir si dans ces cas de force leur paiement des Coupons de la serie A.

**[Right margin - repeating coupon strips]**

Coupons d'Action, Série B, de la Compagnie de Colonisation Américaine.
N° 30. Coupon de dividende de l'Action n° 2434 à recevoir le 15 juillet 1850, au Comptoir de la Compagnie, à Paris.

[Similar entries for coupons numbered: 28, 26, 24, 22, 20, 18, 16, 14, 12 with dates ranging from 1846-1849]

---

## English Translation:

**[Left margin - repeating coupon strips]**

Share Coupons, Series B, of the American Colonization Company.
No. 30. Dividend coupon of Share No. 2434 to be received on July 15, 1850, at the Company's Office in Paris.

[Similar entries for coupons numbered 27, 25, 23, 21, 19, 17, 15, 13, 11 with dates ranging from 1845-1849]

**[Central document text]**

**American Colonization Company.**

**Share**

**of 100 Acres of Land in the States of Virginia and Kentucky.**

**Series B, No. A/234          1300 Francs**

Mr. Charles Guillaume Juste Jérôme is the proprietor of one hundred acres of Land situated in the States of Virginia and Kentucky, free of all mortgages and liens, forming part of 1,849,000 acres of Land belonging to the American Colonization Company, constituted under the name of De Bedern and Company, according to deed executed before Master Froger Deschesnes, and his Successor, Notaries in Paris; which has been officially recorded in accordance with the law.

The Company takes appropriate measures to deliver the greatest possible part of its Properties through Colonization and the industrial and commercial operations that naturally relate to its Enterprise. It will employ the means to facilitate Establishments in its lands for those who wish to settle in the United States of America, and to promote the cultivation and sale of agricultural products.

To this effect, the Lands are distributed in the following manner: 349,000 acres are intended to be sold at will to Colonists who prefer to become Proprietors. The proceeds of the sale are left by the founding members of the Company in the Colonization Enterprise, without interest for the first ten years; after the expiration of which, all sums received shall bear interest at 5 percent per annum, to which the Enterprise may retain annually.

The remaining 1,500,000 acres have been converted into Fifteen thousand Shares, each of One hundred acres of Land.

The Company will develop these lands through Factories, Mills for grain and sawing, Timber Processing Operations, and through all Establishments of commerce and manufactures that the cultivation and richness of the soil will naturally bring forth.

The periodic revenues resulting from them, according to the profits, are transferable by simple endorsement. The share may never be subject to any call for funds; the net profit resulting from the Company's operations shall form the dividend that the Shareholder will receive annually upon presentation of the annual Coupons.

Three thousand Shares, from No. 1 to 3,000, forming Series A, are assigned to the increase of the Company's Capital, intended to advance the operations of Colonization.

The other shares alone (Series B from No. 1 to 15,000) will form the open series, to be completed on July 15, 1831.

This interest well the dividends until this one is fixed at Six percent. Considering that the latter, proceeding from the sale of these shares, are employed in Colonization for the profit of all shareholders, the Company will have the right to retain a portion of the dividend of 15,000 Shares, so that it may accumulate. From this day on, we retain no surplus guaranteed, so as to be able to pay the actions first provided, we shall be able to do so in these cases of necessity to pay the Coupons of Series A.

title: France, Emprunt Forcé de l'An 4: Récépissé No. 3869, Commune No. 9 (1795-96)
description: This printed document is a receipt (récépissé) for the French Republican Forced Loan of Year 4 (Emprunt Forcé de l'An 4), corresponding to 1795-96 on the Gregorian calendar. The upper portion is a formal receipt from the designated Receiver of the Commune, acknowledging payment of a sum 'en numéraire ou valeur représentative aux termes de la Loi' (in coin or representative value as specified by the Law of 19 Frimaire, Year 4). The document carries the heading 'DÉPARTEMENT... COMMUNE No. 9' and is numbered 3869. Below the receipt is a detachable sheet of nine coupon bonds labeled 'Emprunt Forcé de l'An 4,' each bearing signatures, indicating the various installment payments of the forced loan that were scheduled across the year. The Emprunt Forcé de l'An 4 was a compulsory capital levy imposed by the French Directory on wealthy citizens to fund the Revolutionary government, and represented one of the most significant fiscal measures of the mid-1790s.
creator: France. République française
notes: Emprunt Forcé de l'An 4 (Forced Loan of Year 4 = 1795-96). Récépissé No. 3869, Commune No. 9. Includes detachable coupon strip sheet with 9+ coupon bonds. Issued pursuant to the Law of 19 Frimaire, Year 4.
transcription:
# EMPRUNT FORCÉ DE L'AN 4. RÉCÉPISSÉ

**Original:**

DÉPARTEMENT de l'Yonne | EMPRUNT FORCÉ DE L'AN 4. | COMMUNE de Gand
N°3869

Je soussigné Pierre Mallet, Percepteur de la Commune de Gand, Canton de Gand, Département de l'Yonne, déclare avoir reçu du Cit.n Infant [Ravignan?] taxé au rôle de l'Emprunt forcé établi par la Loi du 19 Frimaire, 4e année de la République, à la somme de Vingt cinq Cinquante [25,50] ou valeur représentative aux termes de la Loi, dont je lui ai délivré la présente quittance; divisée en dix coupons, suivant la forme prescrite par l'article IX de la Loi du 19 Frimaire dernier.

FAIT à Gand le 28 Juillet an 4e de la République française, une et indivisible.

P. Mallet
Percepteur de la Commune de Gand

---

**EMPRUNT | FORCÉ**

[Four columns of detachable coupon bonds, each labeled:]

DÉPARTEMENT de l'Yonne
Municipalité de Gand
COMMUNE de Gand

**Emprunt forcé de l'an 4.**

Bon pour la somme de Vingt cinq Francs, faisant la dixième [partie/fraction] de la taxe d'Emprunt forcé de l'an 4, au Cit.n [Infant Ravignan] à valoir en paiement des Contributions directes de l'an unité. On admissible, dix-à-présent, en paiement du Droit d'enregistrement du pour cause de successions.

Conformément aux articles XI et XII de la Loi du 19 Frimaire an 4.

(N° numbered 10, 9, 5, and 4 in respective columns)

**Notable markings:**

- Receipt number: 3869
- Multiple handwritten signatures by authorized municipal representatives
- Hatched cancellation marks (cross-hatching) on several coupons
- Printed serial designations (N° 10, 9, 5, 4) on coupon bonds
- Binding line running vertically between left and right sections marked "Emprunt Forcé"

title: Insurance Company of the State of Pennsylvania: Stock Transfer Endorsement, John Oldden to Henry Schlaesman (October 30, 1795)
description: This handwritten document is a stock transfer endorsement on the back of a share certificate of the Insurance Company of the State of Pennsylvania. The text reads: 'I John Oldden — Do hereby assign and transfer to Henry Schlaesman Two and a half — Shares in the Capital or Joint Stock of the Insurance Company of the State of Pennsylvania. In witness whereof I have hereunto set my Hand the Thirtieth day of October 1795.' The document is witnessed by Jo. Palmer, and signed by Alex. Elmslie as Attorney. The Insurance Company of the State of Pennsylvania, founded in 1794, was among the earliest joint-stock insurance companies in the United States and represents an important early chapter in American corporate finance. This transfer endorsement was typically written on the blank verso of the original share certificate and executed in order to formally record a change of ownership.

title: Insurance Company of the State of Pennsylvania: Stock Transfer Certificate, Henry Philips to Alexander Wiltocks (March 20, 1797)
description: This handwritten document is a stock transfer certificate for the Insurance Company of the State of Pennsylvania, combining a ledger record on the left with the formal assignment text on the right. The left portion contains a tabular record with columns for Date, Folio, Ledger, Transferer and Transferee, Number of Shares, and Number of Certificate. The entry records the date March 20, 1797, a transfer from Henry Philips (Folio 228) to Alexander Wiltocks (Folio 135), for 3 shares. The right portion of the document reads: 'I Henry Philips Do hereby assign and transfer to Alexander Wiltocks Three — Shares in the Capital or Joint Stock of the Insurance Company of the State of Pennsylvania. In witness whereof I have hereunto set my Hand the Twentieth day of March 1797.' The document is witnessed by John Lewis and signed by Henry Philips. This certificate illustrates the early mechanisms of equity transfer used in American corporate practice at the close of the eighteenth century.

title: Massachusetts Bay: Consolidated Note, No. 287, £285 (Richard Fry, January 1, 1780)
description: This printed and handwritten document is a Massachusetts Bay Consolidated Note, No. 287, dated the First Day of January, A.D. 1780, issued during the American Revolutionary War. The text reads: 'IN Behalf of the State of Massachusetts Bay, I the Subscriber do hereby promise and oblige Myself and Successors in the Office of TREASURER of said State, to pay unto Richard Fry or to his Order, the Sum of Two hundred and eighty five Pounds on or before the First Day of March... 1783, with Interest at Six per Cent. per Annum.' The note specifies that both principal and interest were to be paid in the then current money of the State, adjusted to compensate for wartime inflation—calibrated against the prices of specified commodities (corn, beef, sheeps wool, sole leather). The sum of £285 represents thirty-two and a half times the value of these commodities at 1777 prices. The note references an Act of the General Assembly of February 6, 1779, providing for Massachusetts' contribution to the Continental Army. The document is signed by Wm. Dawes and R. Cranch as Committee members, and by Hy. Jones as Treasurer.
creator: Massachusetts Bay. Treasury
notes: Massachusetts Bay Consolidated Note No. 287, £285, January 1, 1780. Issued to Richard Fry. 6% interest, due March 1, 1783. Commodity-indexed principal. Signed by Wm. Dawes, R. Cranch (Committee) and Hy. Jones, Treasurer.
transcription:
# Transcription

**No.** [struck through]

287

**The FIRST Day of JANUARY, A. D. 1780.**

IN Behalf of the State of Massachusetts-Bay, I the Subscriber do hereby promise and oblige Myself and Successors in the Office of TREASURER of said STATE; to pay unto Richard Fry or to his Order, the SUM of Two hundred and eighty five Pounds on or before the FIRST Day of MARCH, in the Year of our Lord One Thousand Seven Hundred and Eighty-three, with Interest at Six per Cent. per Annum: Both Principal and Interest to be paid in the then current Money of said STATE, in a manner as Five Bushels of CORN, Sixty-eight Pounds and four-seventh Parts of a Pound of BEEF, Ten Pounds of SHEEPS WOOL, and Sixteen Pounds of SOLE LEATHER, shall then current Money, at the then current Prices of said ARTICLES.—This SUM being THIRTY TWO and a HALF what the same Quantities of the same Articles would sell at the Prices fixed to them in a Law of this STATE made in the Year of our Lord One Thousand Seven Hundred and Seventy-seven, intituled, "An Act to prevent Monopoly and Oppression" The current Prices of said Articles, and the consequent Value of every Pound of the Sum herein promised, to be determined agreeably to a LAW of this STATE, intituled, "AN ACT to provide for the Security and Payment of the Balances that may appear to be due by Virtue of a Resolution of the General Assembly of the Sixth of February One Thousand Seven Hundred and Seventy-nine, to THIS STATE'S Quota of the CONTINENTAL ARMY, agreeably to the Recommendation of CONGRESS, and for Supplying the TREASURY with a Sum of Money for that Purpose."

**Signatures:**
- Wm. Dawes } Committee
- H. Cranch }
- Hy. Jones Treasurer [signature]

- Serial number "287" handwritten at top

- Aged, cream-colored paper with visible wear and foxing

title: United States Loan Office: 3% Debt Certificate, No. 4513, $533.33 (Jean Francois Paul Grand of Switzerland, 1790)
description: This handwritten and engrossed document is a United States Loan Office Certificate, No. 4513, issued at the State of New York Loan Office on the 17th of October, 1790. The document reads: 'BE it known that there is due from the United States of America unto Jean Francois Paul Grand of Switzerland or his Assigns, the Sum of Five hundred & thirty three Dollars & thirty three Cents As a Debt bearing Interest at Three per Centum per Annum, from the first day of Oct. 1790 inclusively, payable quarter-yearly, and subject to Redemption by Payment of said Sum whenever Provision shall be made therefor by Law.' The certificate is signed by M. Clarkson as Commissioner of Loans. An attestation follows from John Wilkes, Notary Public of the State of New York, certifying the document's authenticity. The document bears the United States arms stamp and was issued to Jean Francois Paul Grand, a member of the prominent Swiss-French banking family Grand & Co. who were major holders of early U.S. federal debt. It represents an early instance of European investment in the new American republic's funded debt.

title: France, Garde du Trésor Royal: Quittance for 8,000 Livres (400 Livres de Rente sur l'Hôtel de Ville de Paris, April 10, 1698)
description: This handwritten document is a receipt (quittance) issued on April 10, 1698, by Jean de Turmenyes, Conseiller du Roi and Garde du Trésor Royal (Keeper of the Royal Treasury), acknowledging receipt from Jean Eloy Udtin, argentier (financial agent) of the Archbishop of Rouen, of eight thousand livres in gold louis, silver, and other coin. The payment constitutes the principal for four hundred livres of annual perpetual rente, to be constituted by the Prévôts des Marchands et Échevins (Provost of Merchants and Aldermen) of the City of Paris on the one million livres of annual and perpetual rente newly alienated by His Majesty pursuant to the Royal Edit of March 1698, registered as required. The rente is to be drawn from the revenues of the Aides et Gabelles (excise and salt taxes) of France, for the benefit of Udtin at the rate of the Denier Vingt (5% per annum). The document is signed by 'de Turmenyes' and captioned 'Quittance du Garde du Trésor Royal, Année mil six cent quatre-vingt-dix-huit.' It represents one of the early modern instruments of French royal debt finance, linking crown fiscal necessity to municipal rente issuance.
creator: France. Trésor Royal
notes: Quittance du Garde du Trésor Royal, No. 391, April 10, 1698. 8,000 livres principal for 400 livres of annual rente on the Hôtel de Ville de Paris (Aides et Gabelles). Issued to Jean Eloy Udtin. Signed by Jean de Turmenyes, Garde du Trésor Royal. Pursuant to Edit of March 1698.
transcription:
# Document Transcription

**Original (French):**

N° 391                                    DENIER 20. Mars 1698

JE JEAN DE TURMENYES, Conseiller du Roy en fes Confeils, Garde de fon Trefor Royal, Confeile avoir receu comptant en cette Ville de Paris, de Jean Eloy Udtin [argentier de Monsieur l'Archevêque de Rouen]

[several lines with handwritten corrections and annotations]

la Somme de huil mille livres en Louis d'or, d'argent, & autre Monnoye pour le principal de quatre cens livres de Rente qu'il ly feront vendus & constituez par Messieurs; les Prevost des Marchands & Efchevins de ladite Ville de Paris fur le Million de livres auquels & effectifs de rente annuelle & perpetuelle à eux nouvellemnt alienez par Sa Majefté en confequence de fon Edit du mois de Mars 1698. regiftré où befoin a été, à prendre fur les droits d'Aydes & Gabelles de France, pour jouïr par ledit Udtin à raifon de Denier Vingt, ainfi qu'il fera plus au long expliqué par le Contrat de confti tution qui luy fera expedié de ladite Rente par lesdits Sieurs Prevost des Marchands & Efchevins, en execution dudit Edit. De laquelle Somme de huit mille livres

à moy ordonnée pour employer au fait de ma Charge, je quitte lesdits Sieurs & tous autres. A Paris le [date]

jour d'[illegible] mil fix cent quatre-vingt-dix-huit.

Quittance du Garde du Trésor Royal, Année mil fix cent quatre-vingt-dix-huit.

[Signature: de Turmenyes]

M. Sougis                N.e

---

**English Translation:**

I, JEAN DE TURMENYES, Counselor to the King in his Councils, Keeper of his Royal Treasury, certify that I have received in cash in this City of Paris, from Jean Eloy Udtin [financial agent of the Archbishop of Rouen]

the Sum of eight thousand livres in gold louis, silver, and other coin for the principal of four hundred livres of annual perpetual rente which will be sold and constituted to him by the Messieurs Provost of Merchants and Aldermen of the said City of Paris on the Million livres of annual and perpetual rente newly alienated to them by His Majesty in consequence of his Edit of the month of March 1698, registered where necessary has been, to be drawn from the rights of Aides and Gabelles of France, for the enjoyment by the said Udtin at the rate of Denier Vingt, as will be more fully explained by the Contract of constitution which will be issued to him for said Rente by the said Messieurs Provost of Merchants and Aldermen, in execution of said Edit. From which Sum of eight thousand livres

ordered to me for employment in the discharge of my office, I acquit the said Messieurs and all others. At Paris on [date]

day of [illegible] thousand six hundred ninety-eight.

Receipt of the Keeper of the Royal Treasury, Year thousand six hundred ninety-eight.

---

**Notable markings:**

- Document number: N° 391 (upper left)
- Date notation: "DENIER 20. Mars 1698" (upper right)
- Signature: "de Turmenyes" (cursive, center-right)
- Multiple handwritten corrections and annotations in the margins and between lines
- Names at bottom: "M. Sougis" and "N.e"
- Several passages marked with strikethrough or revision marks

title: Piscataqua Bridge Corporation: Share Certificate No. 262, Town of Portsmouth, New Hampshire (December 7, 1793)
description: This printed and handwritten document is a share certificate of the Piscataqua Bridge Corporation, certifying that the Town of Portsmouth in the County of Rockingham, in the State of New Hampshire, is the proprietor of Share Number Two hundred sixty two in the Piscataqua Bridge. The certificate states that the share is transferable by making an assignment on the back and causing the same to be recorded on the Proprietors' Records. The document is dated the seventh day of December, One thousand seven hundred and ninety three, and is signed by James Sheafe as President, by a second officer as Treasurer, and attested by Nathaniel Adams as Proprietors' Clerk. The Piscataqua Bridge was a privately chartered toll bridge spanning the Piscataqua River between Portsmouth, New Hampshire and Kittery, Maine, one of the earliest bridge corporations in the United States. The certificate illustrates the early American practice of financing public infrastructure through joint-stock corporations with transferable shares.
creator: Piscataqua Bridge Corporation
notes: Piscataqua Bridge Corporation, Share No. 262, Town of Portsmouth, Rockingham County, New Hampshire, December 7, 1793. President: James Sheafe. Proprietors' Clerk: Nathaniel Adams. Early American toll bridge infrastructure share.
transcription:
# Transcription

**No.262**

**Town of Portsmouth**

---

BE IT KNOWN, That the Town of Portsmouth in the County of Rockingham in the State of New Hampshire is the Proprietor of Share Number Two hundred sixty two in the Piscataqua Bridge, which Share is transferable by making an assignment on the back of this Certificate, and causing the fame to be recorded on the Proprietors' Records.

IN testimony whereof, the Seal of the Corporation is hereto affixed, the seventh day of December One thousand seven hundred and ninety three

James Sheafe President.

[illegible] Treasurer.

Attest,

Nathl. Adams Proprietors' Clerk.

---

- Large blank rectangular area (approximately 3" x 4") appears to be space for a seal or official stamp (seal not visible/impressed in this image)
- Document shows age-related discoloration and creasing consistent with an 18th-century paper document

title: Caisse d'Épargnes et de Bienfaisance Lafarge, Seconde Société: Action No. 124824 (90 Livres, Paris, September 30, 1798)
description: This printed and handwritten document is an action (share) of the Caisse d'Épargnes et de Bienfaisance Lafarge, Seconde Société (Lafarge Savings and Charitable Society, Second Society), No. 124824, Paris. The document records that Paul Beauttier and Catherine Ferraudin, his wife, have paid to the Caisse the sum of ninety livres as the price of the present action, conferring on the life of Catherine Ferraudin the right to participate in the rentes and accroissements (growth) of the fund up to a maximum of three thousand livres of rente per annum, to be established by the certificat de vie (certificate of life). The document is dated at Paris, the thirtieth of September 1798 (or the nearby period), and bears the signatures of the Directeur Général and the Inspecteur Général, along with registration stamps confirming it was registered under No. 10317. A rubric of rules for the institution appears at the foot of the document. The Caisse d'Épargnes Lafarge was a late-eighteenth-century Parisian mutual savings institution offering tontine-style life annuities, an early precursor to modern insurance and retirement savings schemes.
creator: Caisse d'Épargnes et de Bienfaisance Lafarge
notes: Caisse d'Épargnes et de Bienfaisance Lafarge, Seconde Société. Action No. 124824, 90 livres, Paris, September 30, 1798. Issued to Paul Beauttier and Catherine Ferraudin (on the life of Catherine Ferraudin). Tontine-style life annuity savings instrument. Registration No. 10317.

Département d'Yonne.

SECONDE SOCIÉTÉ.

ACTION DE LA CAISSE

la somme de quatre-vingt-dix livres, prix de la présente Action, délivrée sous le N° Cent Vingt

tête de Catherine Ferraudin [illegible text crossed out]

de la Rente qui écherra à ladite Action, et participer aux Accroissemens qui auront lieu à mesure des
extinctions qui surviendront, jusqu'au maximum de trois mille livres de Rente, en rapportant par
les Certificat d'attestation de Certe Stationner. L. [signature mark]

À Paris, ce Onze Septembre 1798.                    [Signature]
                                                     Directeur général.

Vu par nous Administrateur.
[Signature]

Enregistrée sous ledit N° 124824.          au folio 139, du 3ᵉ Registre des classes.

[SEAL/MEDALLION IMAGE]
                                           Auzbert
                                           Vérificateur général.

CLAUDE DE RICHIER. L'Actionnaire est tenu, aux termes de l'art 26 du chap. Ier. des Statuts, de faire viser son Action chaque année, cette foi au bureau général de la Caisse d'Épargnes; sans cette formalité, qui est rigueur, il ne participera pas au tirage de l'année.
Ce visa n'est pas celu exigé par les décrets qui a lieu pour les effets au porteur: la Caisse n'a délivré aucune Action de cette nature dans sa seconde Société.—Les Actions émises sur les têtes de Créanciers ne sont pas soumises à cette formalité de visa.

                                           Auzbert

CLAUDE DE RICHIER. The Shareholder is obligated, by the terms of art. 26 of chap. I of the Statutes, to have his Action visaed each year, this to the general office of the Savings Fund; without this formality, which is strictly required, he shall not participate in the drawing of the year.
