title: Principality of Bulgaria, 6% State Mortgage Loan 1892, Horizontal Interest Coupon Strip
description: Horizontal coupon strip for the Principality of Bulgaria 6% State Mortgage Loan of 1892. Contains a row of rectangular semi-annual interest coupons arranged in a long horizontal format, each showing the bond number and coupon number. This strip format was typical for Bulgarian government bonds of the period. The coupons enabled holders to collect the 6% annual interest in two semi-annual instalments. Part of the same bond document as the companion two-obligation certificate (see related document).
creator: Principality of Bulgaria, Ministry of Finance
notes: Coupon strip for Bulgaria 6% State Mortgage Loan 1892; horizontal format with rectangular coupons in row; semi-annual interest coupons

title: Russian Provisional Government, Liberty Loan (Zaym Svobody), 5% Bond, 50 Rubles, IV Series, No. 059857, Petrograd, 1917
description: Bearer bond (5% Obligatsiya v Pyatdesyat Rubley naritsatelnykh) of the Russian Provisional Government for the Liberty Loan (Zaym Svobody / Freedom Loan), IV Series, No. 059857. Denomination: 50 rubles (face value). Dated Petrograd, 27 March 1917. Same issue as No. 0643022 (goetzmann0677) but lower denomination. Features the State Duma building illustration. Patriotic text appeals to citizens to support free Russia. Signed by Minister-President (Prince Lvov) and multiple ministers. Handwritten annotation at top.
creator: Russian Provisional Government
notes: Russian Freedom Loan (Заем Свободы), 5% bond, 50 rubles nominal, IV Series, No. 059857. Petrograd, March 27, 1917. Provisional Government. Same issue as goetzmann0677; lower denomination. Handwritten "½ of 50R" annotation at top.
transcription:
# Russian Provisional Government Liberty Loan Bond - 1917

## Original Text (Russian):

**Header:**
№059857                                                                                    №059857
IV серія.                                                                                    IV серія.

**Central Text:**
ЗАЕМЪ СВОБОДЫ

*ГОСУДАРСТВЕННАЯ ДУМА*

5% ОБЛИГАЦИЯ
въ ПЯТЬДЕСЯТЬ РУБЛЕЙ нарицательныхъ.

Къ намъ, граждане великой свободной России, къ вамъ къ Васъ, кому дорого будущее великой Родины, обращаемъ мы нашъ горячій призывъ.

Сильный враг глубоко вторгся въ нашъ предѣлы, грозить сломить нась и вернуть страну къ старому, ныне мертвому строю.

Только напряженно всѣхъ нашихъ силъ поможетъ дать намъ желанную побѣду. Нужна затрата миллиардовъ, чтобы спасти отчизну и завершить строенiе свободной России на началахъ равенства и правды.

Не мертвы требуетъ отъ насъ "родина, а исполненiя долга.

Одолжимъ деньги Государству, поміщтвьте ихъ въ новый заемъ, и спасемъ этимъ отъ гибели свободу и достоянiе.

Министръ-Предсѣдатель [signature] Кн.Львовъ

[Multiple ministerial signatures below - largely illegible due to handwriting]

**Footer:**
Петроградъ, 27 марта 1917 года.

---

## English Translation:

**LIBERTY LOAN**

*STATE DUMA*

5% BOND
in the amount of FIFTY RUBLES nominal value.

To us, citizens of great free Russia, to you, to whom the future of our great Homeland is dear, we make our fervent appeal.

A mighty enemy has penetrated deeply into our territories, threatening to crush us and return the country to the old, now dead regime.

Only the intense exertion of all our forces will help us achieve the desired victory. Billions in expenditures are needed to save our fatherland and complete the construction of free Russia on the principles of equality and justice.

The Homeland demands not our deaths, but the fulfillment of our duty.

Let us lend money to the State, place it in the new loan, and thereby save freedom and property from destruction.

Minister-President [signature] Prince Lvov

[Multiple ministerial signatures]

Petrograd, 27 March 1917.

---

- **Multiple ministerial signatures** in cursive script below main text (largely illegible)
- **Date:** 27 марта 1917 года (27 March 1917)
- **Location:** Петроградъ (Petrograd)
- **Central vignette:** Illustration of the State Duma building with classical architecture
- **Decorative borders:** Ornate geometric patterns in green
- **Laurel wreaths** flanking the central building illustration

title: Aktiebolaget Kreuger & Toll, Participating Debenture, Kr. 20 (Twenty Kronor), No. 1163457, Stockholm, 1928
description: Participating debenture (Kr. 20 / Twenty Kronor) of Aktiebolaget Kreuger & Toll (the Match King Ivar Kreuger's holding company), No. 1163457. Issued under the Swedish Companies Act. Share capital of Aktiebolaget Kreuger & Toll: Kr. 63,660,009, divided into 636,600 shares of Kr. 100 each. The debenture carries interest at 5% per annum from 1 January 1928 until redemption. Holders have proportional participation rights in profits. Additional debentures can be issued ranking equally with these. Printed by Aktiebolaget Bonnier, Stockholm.
creator: Aktiebolaget Kreuger & Toll
notes: Aktiebolaget Kreuger & Toll (Swedish match empire), Participating Debenture, Kr. 20, No. 1163457. 5% interest per annum from January 1, 1928. With coupons and talon. Ivar Kreuger's match monopoly empire collapsed in 1932.
transcription:
# Transcription

**Original (Swedish/English Mixed):**

KR. 20 KR.                                                                                                No. 1163457

PARTICIPATING DEBENTURE

Aktiebolaget
KREUGER & TOLL

Issue of Honour Kr.20,000 participating debentures in denominations of Honour 20, 40, 100 and 500 pursuant to resolution of the shareholders of the Company passed on 15th May 1928.

The undersigned, Aktiebolaget Kreuger & Toll, a limited liability Company of Stockholm, (hereinafter called "the Company") registered under the Swedish Companies' Act and having a share capital of Kronor 63,000,000 divided into 636,000 shares of Kronor 100 each (which shares together with all other shares which may be issued ranking equally therewith as regards participation in profits are hereinafter called "Ordinary shares"), hereby promises to pay to the HOLDER of this Debenture on notice given at the time and in the manner hereinafter mentioned, the sum of

TWENTY KRONOR

with interest thereon at the rate of five (5) per cent per annum from 1st January, 1928, until redemption:

1. On 1st July, 2003, and on any interest date thereafter the Holder hereof has the right, on giving six months' previous notice in writing to the Company and upon surrender of this Debenture, to call for payment of this Debenture at par and accrued interest at the rate of five (5) per cent per annum.

2. Should the dividend paid or declared on the Ordinary shares in any year to the Holders of this Debenture shall be entitled in respect of such fiscal year to additional interest of one (1) per cent so paid or declared on the Ordinary shares in excess of five (5) per cent the Holder of this Debenture shall be given on the Ordinary shares in excess of five (5) per cent per annum, such sum shall be paid or declared for the year ending on the preceding 31st December, will be paid annually on 1st July, 1929, and will represent interest at five (5) per cent, plus additional interest, if any, in respect of the year ending on 31st December 1928.

3. Attached to this Debenture are 30 coupons and a talon for further coupons. Interest on this Debenture, including additional interest, if any, calculated on the dividend paid or declared for the year ending on the preceding 31st December, will be paid annually on 1st July against surrender of the appropriate coupon. The first interest payment will be made on 1st July, 1929, and will represent interest at five (5) per cent, plus additional interest, if any, in respect of the year ending on 31st December 1928.

[Right column text continues with detailed terms regarding capital increases, new debentures, ranking rights, and conditions - text becomes increasingly difficult to read but discusses:]

- If the increase in capital should be in the form of shares not ranking equally as regards participation in profits with the Ordinary shares, the new Debentures to be issued shall carry similar rights as regards participation in profits to the shares then being issued but shall in all other respects rank equally with and be subject to the same conditions as the present Debentures.

- All issues of new Debentures under this clause shall be on such terms that the position of the holders of the existing Debentures [continues but becomes less legible]

---

**Notable markings:**

- Serial number: No. 1163457
- Denomination: KR. 20 KR. (Twenty Kronor)
- Ornate decorative border in beige/cream with geometric and floral patterns
- Corner medallions featuring "20" in each corner
- Printer attribution at bottom: "Printed in Sweden by Göteborgs-bolagets Nyckelda" [or similar - partially unclear]
- Document date: 1928
- Issued by Aktiebolaget Bonnier, Stockholm (as noted in context)
- No visible signatures on this specimen

title: Monte del Sale della Citta di Firenze, Transfer of 44.5 Luoghi (Shares), Florence, 12 September 1693
description: Transfer certificate of the Monte non vacabile del Sale della Citta di Firenze (Non-Redeemable Salt Fund of the City of Florence), a Florentine public debt instrument, issued on 12 September 1693. The Protettori del Monte (trustees) certify the transfer of 44.5 luoghi (places/shares) at 4 scudi per luogo annual income from Pier Carlo Giraldini to Caterina Giamfigliazzi ne' Giraldini, with an annual annuity of 4 scudi per luogo payable quarterly. Signed by Attilio Vettor, one of the Protettori, and the Cancelliere. Wax seal affixed. Printed form with manuscript insertions. Issued in the name of the Serenissimo Gran Duca di Toscana (Grand Duke of Tuscany).
creator: Protettori del Monte del Sale, Firenze
notes: Monte non vacabile del Sale della Città di Firenze (Non-vacuous Salt Fund of Florence). Transfer of 44.5 luoghi to Caterina del fu Giov. Battista Gianfigliazzi (née Giraldini). Signed by Protettori del Monte. Wax seal at lower left. Florence, September 10, 1693. Grand Duchy of Tuscany.
transcription:
# Transcription

**Original (Italian):**

Noi Protettori del Monte non vacabile del Sale della Città di Firenze, ereetto d'ordine del Serenissimo Gran Duca di Toscana nostro Signore, per la Prouisione pubblicata nell' Amplissimo Senato de' Quarant' otto il dì di 12. Settembre 1645. A tutti quelli, che vedranno la presente nostra facciamo piena, & indubitata fede, come [handwritten: Cav.r Pierino delgi Albzi Balt. Gioan Batl Bina Gianfigliazzi Vedovante Giraldini]

di Luoghi [handwritten: Giuarantaquadro Omezzo] Apparisic — credit. al Libro di detto Monte segn. [handwritten: Giuarantaquadro —] di Luoghi [handwritten: Giuarantaquadro Omezzo] —

in furuuro rilegatialda conto di [handwritten: Giuarantaquadro —] come per Partito nostro di questo giorno al libro delle Permute à. [handwritten: Giraldini] al quale si abbia relatione. Però annullando ogni altra Patente fatta per giorno al libro delle Permute e, et per l'avvenire facciamo, e dichiaiaamo detta [handwritten: Marierina Gianfigliazzi —] di detto Monte di detti luoghi [handwritten: Giuarantaquadro] assegniamo con credit di detto Monte di detti luoghi Giuarantaquadro à Mesi in iste Mesi per rata, e con li Priuilegi, condi zioni, & dichiarazioni di che in detta Prouisione; et in virtù della presente ordinamio al nostro Camarlengo presente, & che per li tempi farà, che li paghi detti frutti di tre Mesi in tre Mesi: Et in fede ib— aiamo fatta la presente, quale farà sottocritta da vno di Noi Protettori, e dal nostro Cancelliere, e figliata con il nostro solito figillo

Dat. in Firenze nell' Vfizio del nostro Monte del Sale il dì [handwritten: 12 Settembrẽ 1693.]

[Handwritten signature: Attilii Vettor Uno de'Protettori]

[Handwritten: Ottaui Langliane]

---

**English Translation:**

We, Protectors of the Non-Redeemable Salt Fund of the City of Florence, established by order of the Most Serene Grand Duke of Tuscany, our Lord, pursuant to the Provision published in the Most Ample Senate of the Forty-Eight on the 12th of September 1645. To all those who shall see the present [document], we make full and undoubted faith, as [names inserted in manuscript] 

of Places [44.5 inserted in manuscript]—

appearing—credit to the Book of said Monte [signature, properties listed in manuscript]—of Places [44.5 inserted in manuscript]—

in future [assigned] to the account of [names in manuscript]—as per Our Decree of this day in the Book of Exchanges [to Giraldini] to which reference shall be made. However, annulling every other Patent made for the day in the Book of Exchanges, and henceforth we make and declare said [Caterina Giamfigliazzi inserted in manuscript] of said Monte of said places [44.5 inserted in manuscript] assigned with credit of said Monte of said places 44.5 at 4 scudi in these months per rate, and with the Privileges, conditions, & declarations which are in said Provision; and in virtue of the present we ordain to our Chamberlain present, & that for all times shall make, that he pay said fruits of three months in three months: And in faith we have made the present, which shall be subscribed by one of Us Protectors, and by our Chancellor, and sealed with our customary seal.

Given in Florence in the Office of our Monte del Sale on the day [12 September 1693 inserted in manuscript.]

[Signature: Attilio Vettor, One of the Protectors]

[Signature: Ottavio Langliane]

---

**Notable markings:**

- **Wax seal:** Large affixed wax seal in lower left corner (tan/beige colored, partially visible)
- **Coat of arms:** Three heraldic emblems at top of document (left, center, right) depicting crowned shields and ornamental flourishes
- **Signatures:** Two manuscript signatures at bottom (Attilio Vettor and Ottavio Langliane)
- **Date:** 12 September 1693 (handwritten insertion)
- **Printed form:** Document is a printed template with extensive manuscript insertions throughout
- **Document class:** Printed official form with wax seal affixation and notarial signatures

- Multiple identical coupon designs with decorative borders
- Red circular stamps/seals visible on multiple coupons
- Text indicating these are interest-bearing coupons

Each coupon strip contains similar text repeated, with reference number **175,406** appearing consistently throughout.

---

**Notable Markings:**

1. **Red Circular Seal** - Visible on both pages, appears to be official Romanian government or financial authority stamp
2. **Decorative Border** - Ornate turquoise/teal Art Deco pattern framing entire document
3. **Serial Number**: **175,406** - appears on all four bond certificates and throughout coupon sheet
4. **Official Stamps** - Red circular stamps on multiple coupons on right page
5. **Date References** - Series references including "1928" visible on bond titles
6. **Multiple Language Headers** - Document presented in French, English, German, and Turkish to facilitate international trading

**Document Type**: This is a **coupon bond** (obligation au porteur) - a bearer security allowing for interest collection through detachable coupons.

transcription:
# Ottoman Public Debt Administration Bond - Contract Terms and Coupon Sheet

## Document Structure

This is a multi-language document with four parallel columns containing the same contract terms in:
1. **French** (EXTRAITS DU CONTRAT)
2. **English** (EXTRACTS FROM THE CONTRACT)
3. **German** (AUSZÜGE AUS DEM VERTRÄGE)
4. **Turkish** (MUKAVELENİN MÜSTAHSıL ANKAİM)

---

## FRENCH TEXT (Left Column)

**EXTRAITS DU CONTRAT**

Renvoyant à autorisation gouvernementale des Articles:

Art. I
La présente Obligation représente une part de la Dette Consolidée de l'Empire Ottoman créée en vertu du Décret en date du 28 février 1903 à 5 p.c. par an, en Livres Turques, de capital nominal, laquelle Dette a été augmentée par Décrets ultérieurs, notamment par celui du 10 novembre 1908 et celui du 19 octobre 1913. [Text continues with detailed contract terms...]

[The French text continues with multiple articles detailing the bond terms, interest payments, redemption procedures, and administrative provisions]

---

## ENGLISH TEXT (Second Column)

**EXTRACTS FROM THE CONTRACT**

Renvoyant to governmental authorization of the Articles:

Art. I
The present Bond represents a share of the Consolidated Debt of the Ottoman Empire created by virtue of the Decree dated the 28th February 1903 at 5 p.c. per annum, in Turkish Pounds, of nominal capital, which Debt was increased by subsequent Decrees, in particular by that of the 10th November 1908 and that of the 19th October 1913. [Text continues with equivalent English terms...]

[The English text contains parallel provisions to the French version]

---

## GERMAN TEXT (Third Column)

**AUSZÜGE AUS DEM VERTRÄGE**

Bezugnahme auf die Genehmigung der Vertragsbestimmungen:

Art. I
Die vorliegende Schuldverschreibung stellt einen Anteil der Konsolidierten Schuld des Osmanischen Reiches dar, welche kraft des Dekretes vom 28. Februar 1903 zu 5 v.H. jährlich in Türkischen Pfunden des Nominalwertes geschaffen wurde, welche Schuld durch spätere Dekrete, insbesondere durch das Dekret vom 10. November 1908 und das Dekret vom 19. Oktober 1913, erhöht wurde. [Text continues...]

---

## TURKISH TEXT (Fourth Column)

**MUKAVELENİN MÜSTAHSıL ANKAIMI**

[Turkish text containing equivalent contract provisions in Ottoman Turkish]

---

## KEY CONTRACT ARTICLES (Common to All Versions)

The document outlines:

- **Article I**: Definition of the bond as representing a share of Ottoman Consolidated Debt
- **Article II**: Interest payment terms (5% per annum)
- **Article III**: Coupon redemption procedures
- **Article IV-VI**: Administrative provisions regarding payment locations and procedures
- **Subsequent Articles**: Details on bond redemption, exchange procedures, and general provisions

---

## COUPON SHEET SECTION

The document includes a detailed table/schedule showing:
- Coupon numbers
- Payment dates
- Interest amounts
- Redemption information

---

## Notable Markings:

1. **Official Seals**: Multiple red circular stamps and official seals from the Ottoman Public Debt Administration visible throughout the document
2. **Document Classification**: Marked as official contract documentation
3. **Language Headers**: Four distinct columns clearly separated with language designations
4. **Decorative Border**: Ornamental border frame around the entire document
5. **Serial/Reference Numbers**: Various administrative reference numbers present

---

**Note**: This is page 1 of 2 of a comprehensive bond documentation. The parallel text structure in four languages was typical of Ottoman-era international financial instruments to facilitate commerce across the empire's diverse multilingual regions and international financial markets.

---

# Transcription of Ottoman Public Debt Bond Document

## Original Text (Mixed Turkish/French/English):

**Left Side Header:**
КНЕСОБОЯЖАЯ
[Russian: KHNESOBIAZHNAYA - appears to be transliterated text]

**Main Title:**
DAMPF-SCHIFF GESELLSCHAFT FÜR DONAU UND SCHWARZES MEER

**Crédit Foncier [?]**
4% Pflicht-Brief
GOAD-ARE 1897

PRICE: 45,000 Francs
[Additional numerical details and terms]

Redeemable in [year - unclear]

Crédit Foncier
4% Pflicht-Brief
GOAD-ARE 1897

29,000 OBLIGATIONS

Österreichischer Boden

5% Interest Bonds
Price in Gulden [currency]

**Notable Markings:**

- Official seal/medallion (circular) at bottom left
- Ornamental border frame (reddish-brown decorative pattern) surrounding entire document
- Handwritten signatures in bottom right corner
- Document number/reference markings
- Text appears rotated 180 degrees on this page (reverse side)
- Multiple coupon sections visible for interest collection

---

**Note:** This document is heavily degraded and partially illegible. The text appears to be a multi-language financial document mixing German, French, and possibly Russian text, typical of Austro-Hungarian period financial instruments.

pages.description: Page 1 of 2 - Kingdom of Romania external obligation or 7% depreciable and guaranteed by the state debt stabilization and development in 1929 The Kingdom of Romania (Romanian: Regatul României), was the Romanian state based on a form of a constitutional monarchy between 13 March 1881 and 30 December 1947, specified by the first three Constitutions of Romania (1866, 1923, 1938). Thus, the Kingdom of Romania began with the reign of King Carol I of Romania who gained Romanian's independence in the Romanian War of Independence, and ended with the abdication of King Michael I of Romania in 30 December 1947, imposed by the Soviet Union with the tacit and secret, implicit consent of its allies (as a result of the Yalta Conference and secret agreements). As such, it is quite distinct from the Romanian Old Kingdom, which refers strictly to the reign of King Carol I of Romania, between 14 March (O.S.) (26 May (N.S.)) 1881 and 27 September (O.S.) (10 October (N.S.)) 1914._x000B_From 1859 to 1877, Romania evolved from a personal union of two vassal principalities (Moldavia and Wallachia) under a single prince to a full-fledged independent kingdom with a Hohenzollern monarchy. In 1918, at the end of World War I, Transylvania, Eastern Moldavia (Bessarabia), and Bukovina united with the Kingdom of Romania, resulting in a "Greater Romania". In 1940, Bessarabia, Northern Bukowina, Northern Transylvania and Southern Dobruja were ceded to the Soviet Union, Hungary and Bulgaria respectively, only Northern Transylvania being recovered after World War II ended. In 1947 the last king was compelled to abdicate and a republic ruled by the Romanian Communist Party replaced the monarchy_x000B_

Page 2 of 2 - Kingdom of Romania external obligation or 7% depreciable and guaranteed by the state debt stabilization and development in 1929 The Kingdom of Romania (Romanian: Regatul României), was the Romanian state based on a form of a constitutional monarchy between 13 March 1881 and 30 December 1947, specified by the first three Constitutions of Romania (1866, 1923, 1938). Thus, the Kingdom of Romania began with the reign of King Carol I of Romania who gained Romanian's independence in the Romanian War of Independence, and ended with the abdication of King Michael I of Romania in 30 December 1947, imposed by the Soviet Union with the tacit and secret, implicit consent of its allies (as a result of the Yalta Conference and secret agreements). As such, it is quite distinct from the Romanian Old Kingdom, which refers strictly to the reign of King Carol I of Romania, between 14 March (O.S.) (26 May (N.S.)) 1881 and 27 September (O.S.) # Transcription

## Original:

**5 Prozent                                                                    5 Prozent**

**Benzolwert-Anleihe**

**des**

**Städtischen Gas- und Elektrizitätswerks**
**Hagenow i.M.**

**Lit. E                                                                      No. 173**

**Schuldverschreibung**

**über den Geldwert von 50 kg Benzol.**

Das Städtische Gas- und Elektrizitätswerk Hagenow i.M. schuldet dem Inhaber dieser Schuldverschreibung den Geldwert von

**fill (fünfzig) Kilogramm Benzol**

Die Schuld wird vom 1. Juli 1925 ab mit 5 (fünf) vom Hundert auf das Jahr in der Weise verzinst, daß der Geldwert von 2½ kg Benzol in halbjährlichen, am 2. Januar und 1. Juli jeden Jahres fälligen Treidträgen nachträglich ausgeäahlt wird. Die Schuld-verschreibung ist tilgbar des Inhabers Untuntigung.

Für die Anteile haffen die Bestimmungen der Stadt Hagenow und das Städtische Gas- und Elektrizitätswerk.

Im übrigen gelten die umseitig abgedruckten Bestimmungen.

Hagenow, den 20. Juli 1925.

**Der Rat**

**D. Stump**                                                    [Signature]

**Der Stadtverodrfnetenvorsteher**

**[Signature - illegible]**

---

## English translation:

**5 Percent                                                                    5 Percent**

**Benzol Value Bond**

**of the**

**Municipal Gas and Electricity Works**
**Hagenow i.M.**

**Lit. E                                                                      No. 173**

**Bond Certificate**

**for the monetary value of 50 kg benzol.**

The Municipal Gas and Electricity Works Hagenow i.M. owes to the holder of this bond certificate the monetary value of

**fifty (50) kilograms of benzol**

The debt accrues interest from July 1, 1925, at 5 (five) percent per annum, in such a way that the monetary value of 2½ kg benzol in semi-annual installments due on January 2 and July 1 of each year is paid retroactively. The bond certificate is redeemable at the holder's request.

For the shares, the provisions of the City of Hagenow and the Municipal Gas and Electricity Works apply.

For all other matters, the provisions printed on the reverse side apply.

Hagenow, July 20, 1925.

**The Council**

**D. Stump**                                                    [Signature]

**The City Council Chairman**

**[Signature - illegible]**

---

## Notable markings:

- **Red circular stamp** (top center): "PREISSTEUERSTELLE" (Price Control Authority) with partial date
- **Serial number**: No. 173
- **Classification**: Lit. E
- **Red wax seal** (bottom left)
- **Handwritten signatures** by D. Stump and the City Council Chairman (Der Stadtverodrfnetenvorsteher)
- **Decorative border**: Ornate Art Nouveau-style frame with geometric and floral patterns
- **Document reference**: Appears to be page 1 of 4

# Transcription

**Original:**

Buchstabe E. Reichsmark 200.—

Ablösungsanleihe der Stadt Rostock.

Buchstabe E.                                Nr. 08939

**Schuldverschreibung**

über

ZWEIHUNDERT REICHSMARK

Ausfertigig: auf Grund der durch das Meckl.-Schweinische Ministerium des Innern in Schwerin erteilten Genehmigung vom 6. November 1927 Nr. B 49514.

Die Stadt Rostock bekennt sich durch diese, für jeden Inhaber gültige Schuldverschreibung zu einer sieben des Gläubigers unkündbar Darlehnsschuld im Betrage von

ZWEIHUNDERT REICHSMARK.

Eine Verzinsung der Ablösungsanleihe kann bis zum Erlaßchen der Reparationsverpflichtungen nicht gefordert werden. Der Beginn und die Höhe der Verzinsung werden durch die Landesregierung bestimmt werden. Für den Fall der Verzinsung bleibt vorbehalten, dem Inhaber dieser Schuldverschreibung gegen deren Rückgabe an die gleich geltenden Nennwerts mit Zinszinsen und Ersparnisschleinen auszuzählungen. Die Tilgung der Ablösungsanleihe ist auf besonders gewährte, selbstständig veräußerliche Auslosungsanrechte beschränkt und wird durch Ziehung der Gültigung dieser Rechte vollzogen. — Über diese Rechte werden besondere Auslosungsscheine ausgegebem werden. — Im übrigen kann eine Tilgung der Ablösungsanleihe nur nach Bestimmung der Landesregierung verlangen werden (§ 44 des Reichsgesetzes über die Ablösung öffentlicher Anleihen). Für die Sicherheit der Kapitalien und der Zinsen haftet die Stadt Rostock mit ihrem gesamten gegenwärtigen und zukünftigen Vermögen und mit ihrer Steuerkraft.

Rostock, den 12. November 1927.

**Der Rat der Stadt Rostock.**

[Signature] Dr. Fleßheim Hedtka.

Der Stadtverobnetevorsteher

[Signature] Dr. jun. Friedrich Mowske

Der Kontrollbeamte: [signature - illegible]

---

# English Translation

**Redemption Loan of the City of Rostock**

Letter E.                                 No. 08939

**Debt Certificate**

for

TWO HUNDRED REICHSMARK

Issued: on the basis of the authorization granted by the Mecklenburg-Schwerin Ministry of the Interior in Schwerin on November 6, 1927 No. B 49514.

The City of Rostock hereby confirms through this document, valid for every bearer, a debt obligation of the creditor that cannot be terminated in the amount of

TWO HUNDRED REICHSMARK.

The redemption loan cannot accrue interest until the reparations obligations are extinguished. The beginning and rate of interest shall be determined by the State Government. In the event interest accrues, it remains reserved that the bearer of this debt certificate may exchange it for its equivalent nominal value plus interest and savings payments upon return. The redemption of the redemption loan is limited to specially granted, independently transferable redemption rights and is carried out through the drawing of the validity of these rights. — Separate redemption certificates shall be issued for these rights. — Moreover, redemption of the redemption loan can only take place in accordance with the provisions of the State Government (§ 44 of the Reich Law on the Redemption of Public Loans). The City of Rostock is liable for the security of capital and interest with all its present and future assets and with its tax power.

Rostock, November 12, 1927.

**The Council of the City of Rostock**

---

**Notable markings:**

- Serial number: 08939
- Two handwritten signatures (Der Rat der Stadt Rostock and Der Stadtverobnetevorsteher)
- Ornate decorative border with circular and geometric patterns in green
- Official seal impression visible in center background
- Printer mark at bottom: "Hannover, J. C. König & Ebhardt, Typ. Inst"

---

# Document Transcription

über

Die Stadt Rostock bekennt sich durch diese, für jeden Inhaber gültige Schuldverschreibung zu einer weiter unten angegebenen Darlehnsschuld von
**FÜNFZIG REICHSMARK.**

Eine Verzinsung der Ablösungsanleihe kann bis zum Erlöschen der Reparationsverpflichtungen nicht stattfinden und kann nur die Verzinsung erfolgen, wenn durch die Landesregierung bestimmt werden. Für den Fall der Verzinsung kann die Ablösungsanleihe durch Zeichnung einer Einigung dieser Schuldverschreibung ganz oder teilweise gelöst werden. Im übrigen können Auslosungsscheine ausgegeben werden.

[Additional detailed text about terms and conditions]

Rostock, den 13. November 1927.

**Der Rat der Stadt Rostock.**

for

The City of Rostock hereby acknowledges through this certificate, valid for any bearer, a debt obligation of
**FIFTY REICHSMARK.**

Interest on the redemption loan cannot be paid until the termination of reparation obligations and interest can only be paid if determined by the State Administration. In the event of interest payment, the redemption loan can be redeemed in whole or in part through the drawing of lots of this debt certificate. Furthermore, redemption certificates can be issued.

Rostock, November 13, 1927.

**The City Council of Rostock.**

**Redemption Loan of the City of Rostock**
for a redemption right in the amount of

Rostock, November 13, 1927.

**The City Council of Rostock.**

# Document Transcription

**Original:**

Ausschreibung.

5%ige Benzolwert-Anleihe
des
Städtischen Gas- und Elektrizitätswerkes
Hagenow i. M.

Das Städtische Gas- und Elektrizitätswerk Hagenow i. M. nimmt mit Genehmigung des Rates der Stadt Hagenow und des Landesverrwaltungsrates eine Anleihe in Höhe des Wertes von 14 000 Kilogramm Benzol auf. Die einzelnen Anleihestücke lauten auf den jeweiligen Wert der befindlichen Benzolmenge. Es werden Stücke in 5, 10 und 50 Kilogramm Benzol ausgereicht. Die Stücke sind auf den Inhaber ausgestellt. Zu jedem Anleihestück gehören 14 halbj[ährliche] Zinsscheine. Die Zinsscheine lauten auf den jeweiligen Wert der Hälfte von 5%, der auf dem zugehörigen Anleihestück gehörigen Benzolmenge. Es wird also bei einer auf 10 Kilogramm Benzol lautenden Anleiheistück ein halbj[ährlicher] Zins von 1/4 Kilogramm Benzol gegeben. Der Zins wird halbj[ährlich] nachträglich am 2. Januar brw. 1. Juli gegen Einsendung der Zinsscheine in Gold entrichtet. Der zur Ausschreibung kommende Gebrauch wird noch dem letzten amtlichen Benzolpreis vom November brw. Mai berechnet und wird in Hagenower Kreisblatt und in der Amtlichen Beilage des Mecklenburg-Schwerin[schen] Regierungsblattes bekannt gegeben.

Die Pflügung der Anleihe erfolgt vom 1. Juli 1924 ab mit jährlich mindestens 2000 Kilogramm Benzol durch freihändigen Rückkauf oder durch Auslosung. Die Ausszahlung der ausgelosten Stücke erfolgt am 2. Januar und 1. Juli jedesJahres und zwar zuerst am 1. Juli 1924. Im Jahre 1930 erfolgt die Ausszahlung der gelösten bis dahin noch nicht ausgelosten Anleiheistücke. Ausgelählt wird der, in derselben Weise wie bei der Feststellung des jeweiligen Zinsbetrags, ermittelte Preis für Benzol. Die Bekanntmachung der ausgelosten Stücke erfolgt im Hagenower Kreisblatt und in der Amtlichen Beilage des Mecklenburg-Schwerin[schen] Regierungsblattes.

Für die Anleihe haften die Bestiftungen der Stadt Hagenow und das Städtische Gas- und Elektrizitätswerk.

Der Ertrag der Anleihe ist bestimmt zur Errichtung einer Benzolgewinnungs- und Abhitzeverwertungsanlage im Städt. Gaswerk Hagenow.

# English Translation:

**Announcement**

5% Benzene-Value Loan
of the
Municipal Gas and Electricity Works
Hagenow i. M.

The Municipal Gas and Electricity Works Hagenow i. M. is raising, with the approval of the City Council of Hagenow and the State Administration Council, a loan in the amount of 14,000 kilograms of benzene. The individual loan documents are denominated in the respective value of the benzene quantity contained therein. Pieces of 5, 10, and 50 kilograms of benzene will be issued. The pieces are made out to bearer. Each loan document is accompanied by 14 semi-annual interest coupons. The interest coupons are denominated in the respective value of half of 5% of the benzene quantity belonging to the corresponding loan document. Thus, for a loan document denominated at 10 kilograms of benzene, a semi-annual interest of 1/4 kilogram of benzene is given. The interest is paid semi-annually in arrears on January 2 and July 1 respectively upon submission of the interest coupons in gold. The loan to be announced will be calculated according to the last official benzene price from November and May respectively and will be published in the Hagenow District Gazette and in the Official Bulletin of the Mecklenburg-Schwerin Government Gazette.

The redemption of the loan begins from July 1, 1924 onwards with at least 2,000 kilograms of benzene annually through free purchase or by drawing. Payment of the drawn pieces occurs on January 2 and July 1 of each year, first on July 1, 1924. In 1930, payment is made for the drawn pieces not yet drawn by then. The price for benzene is determined in the same manner as for determining the respective interest amount. Notification of the drawn pieces is made in the Hagenow District Gazette and in the Official Bulletin of the Mecklenburg-Schwerin Government Gazette.

The city of Hagenow and the Municipal Gas and Electricity Works are liable for the loan.

The proceeds of the loan are designated for the establishment of a benzene recovery and waste heat utilization system at the City Gas Works Hagenow.

title: Silesian Loan of Holy Roman Emperor Karl VI
description: AMSTERDAM. 5% Obligatie Fl.1000. black. No 1254. The Habsburg emperors were always short of money. A large refinancing operation was required by 1734, and Karl VI raised a loan with the Amsterdam banking-house of Willem Gideon Deutz. In guarantee, he offered the Silesia tribute fund, into which payments were made by the princes and nobles of Silesia (thus, the Silesian loan). As interest rates declined, a conversion operation was carried out in 1736. The new bonds, paying 5% interest, were for a total of 700.000 gulden. The new loan was very successful, and within two months had attracted all the capital needed. At first, interest was paid regularly from the contributions by the Silesian nobility. However, after 1742, things changed, when, after the war between Maria Theresia, daughter and successor of Karl VI, and Frederick the Great of Prussia led to Prussian rule over the Silesian territories. There were no further interest payments ; the investors lost everything they had lent. The founder of the important house of Deutz, Willem Gideon Deutz, has signed this bond in person. He was burgomaster of Amsterdam at this time.

# Transcription

**Original (Dutch):**

(I)

WY KAREL de Zesde, door Godsgénaden verkooren Roams Keyzer, ten allen tyde Vermeerderaar des Kyks, Koning in Germanien en Spanjen, Hongarien, Bohemen, Arrds-Hertzog van Oostenryk, Hertzog van Burgiindien, Stirien, Carinthien, Crain, en Wurtenberg, in Opper-en Neder-Silselen, Markgraaf tot Mähren, in Opper en Neder-Lausitz, Graaf tot Habsburg, Tyrol en Goritzy &c. bekennen, voor Ons, onze Erven en Nakomellingen, in kragt dezes Schulthbrief openlyk, en doen daar nevens kond aan een igelyk, dat op onze genadiafte bewilfiginge en goedkeurige, door middel en directie van Willem Gideon Deutz, tot Amsterdam, van versckeydene Persoonen en Particulieren, ten decle ook op andere plaatsen voorgesteld, een declen ook op andere plaatsen voorgesteld, dienst van onseu van het gemeneete weezen genegotieerd, en by forme van beleeninge opgebragt, gelyk meede in handen van onze Keizerlyke Schatkamer in contant geld, tegens de da vour volle krijtige beteonibg valg gelyk vol zekere somme contant geld, bedragende zevenmal hondert duytzend guldens Hollands Courant, en wy derhalven de gemelde somme van zevenmal hondert duytzend Guldens Hollands Courant, aantmen hier by derhalven de gemelde somme van zevenmal hondert duytzend Guldens Hollands Courant, aantmen hier by Willem Gideon Deutz, en verdiere mede Geinteresseerdenis, dewelkee boventialade leeningen te zamen hebben opgestoroten, verthioldigt zyn geworden, en aan entregens voor Ons, ondat Wy en Nakomellingen, voorwerens en verplicht agte tot voorgaande onderhandeling daar over met zyne daar toe benoernde Gevolmagtigidens gehouden, toegezegd, betaald, en en wy voor het gemeelde ter lenen opgenomene Kapitaal s jaarlrks aan Intereellen, na rato des tyds betalen zullen vyf per cent, dat wy ook de voornoelmde opgenomene, en vertrekte somsme van zevenmal hondert duytzend guldens Hollands Courant, wyftien Jaaren binnen Amsterdam, in gelyck Hollandtze Courant Munt weder zullen lettelderen Obligation dertelonen, ondat namntlyk in de tien eerft naalkoomende Jaaren, synde Anno een duytzend zeventhondert zeven en derttig, tot Anno een duytzend zeventhondert zes en veertig ingeslotene, die invoeging der voornoemde kapitaals, zal in de tyd pertiode elk van vijf pertiode elk van vijf jaaren, als meede ten naastvolf der jaaren, dertelonen, tot bilyk een volkomeh gemongen van hem Willem Gideon Deutz, en der overige mede Geinteresseerdenrs; zonder enig manjagement afgeloid, en onder volkdan ze boramen worden, wyl wy nu het zelve Obligatoire Instrument door onze Voorten en Standen in Silselen deswegns opgemaakt, dat namntyk de afflositinge zo wel van het ter lenen veniltaande wyze, en in de vailgeldeelde ternynen, zonder ceedige exception oft uyttel gelchieiden zal ; alleins voor aangenaem gehouven, en daer over onze genadigte toe teminyge en goedkeurige verleend hebben.

WY

## English Translation:

WE CHARLES the Sixth, by the Grace of God elected Roman Emperor, at all times increaser of the Empire, King in Germany and Spain, Hungary, Bohemia, Archduke of Austria, Duke of Burgundy, Styria, Carinthia, Crain, and Württemberg, in Upper and Lower Silesia, Marquis of Moravia, in Upper and Lower Lusatia, Count of Habsburg, Tyrol and Gorizia etc. acknowledge, for Us, our Heirs and Successors, by virtue of this deed of bond publicly and hereby declare to all that at our gracious will and good pleasure, through the mediation and direction of Willem Gideon Deutz, of Amsterdam, from various Persons and Individuals, partly also at other places, a sum required for the service of our State has been negotiated and through means of borrowing raised, in like manner deposited in our Imperial Treasury in ready money, against the said for full discharge thereof. And we therefore acknowledge the mentioned sum of seven hundred thousand guilders, Hollands Courant, and we do hereby, through Willem Gideon Deutz, and further interested parties, who have together contributed these loans, confirm and obligate ourselves to the same.

And we declare that we owe and are bound for the named loan, that we shall to the mentioned gathered borrowed Capital annually pay interest, at the rate of five percent, and that we will also the aforesaid borrowed and received sum of seven hundred thousand guilders, Hollands Courant, fifteen years within Amsterdam, in like Hollandse Courant money repay to the Obligation herein mentioned, namely in the ten first coming years, being in the year one thousand seven hundred and thirty-seven, until the year one thousand seven hundred and forty-six, with the consequence that in each five-year period, as also at the following of the years, shall be paid, without full and complete discharge of him Willem Gideon Deutz, and the other interested parties; without any management excluded, and under full faith and credit, we have caused this Obligatoire instrument through our authorities and estates in Silesia to be made, namely that the payment, as well of the principal and interest to be loaned, in the agreed and appointed terms, without any exception or failure shall happen; solely for acceptance esteemed, and thereover our gracious approval and good pleasure granted.

WE

# English Translation:

We the Princes and Estates of Upper and Lower Silesia, hereby acknowledge, declare, and make known to all those to whom it may concern, in what manner His Most Serene and Royal Majesty, our Most Gracious Emperor, King, and Lord, has most graciously permitted us, that His Imperial Majesty was pleased, on account of His Most Pressing Expenditures, to raise a Capital of seven hundred thousand guilders of the Holland Currency in Amsterdam, to be negotiated and taken up at the rate of Five percent per annum, which shall be repaid within the period of Fifteen Years, namely, in the Years 1739, 1740, 1742, 1743, 1744, 1745, and 1746, from the aforesaid Capital of seven hundred thousand guilders Holland Currency, according to all required stipulations, whereby in the earlier Years the Interest for a quarter of a Year shall be paid, but in the latter five Years, namely, in the Years 1747, 1748, 1749, 1750, and 1751, one fifth of the Capital itself, for a period of twenty percent per annum, from six months to six months, and the Interests calculated pro rata for the time and periods, shall be paid every three months from the aforesaid Silesian levies for the Revenues, collected and paid promptly; His Imperial and Royal Majesty has further desired that we shall bind ourselves for the aforesaid Sum by the Name of the Public to pay the same with and together with His Most Serene Imperial Majesty.

Given that we, from the most subordinate consideration, with which we are bound to His Most Exalted and Most Gracious Imperial Majesty and the Most Honored House of Austria, consider ourselves obliged and willing to comply with such most gracious request, we bind ourselves herewith, through this present Instrument, for Us and our Successors, in the most solemn manner, with full knowledge and deliberate intent, to pay the aforesaid sum of seven hundred thousand guilders Holland Currency, and the Interest promised thereupon at the rate of Five percent per annum to those to whom His Most Serene Imperial and Catholic Majesty, our Most Gracious Lord, shall lend the same, or to those who may come into possession thereof by inheritance, without the least deduction, from the annual Silesian levies for the Revenues, namely, in the first ten Years only the Interest, payable quarterly, and in the following five Years the Capital itself, with the aforesaid payment of twenty percent, or one hundred and twenty thousand guilders Holland Currency, (in which case we shall repay and return this Bond without deduction), and in such manner that all of this

Thus done and executed without any reserve, and we have to the greater agreement drawn up the method of collection, and signed and sealed it with our lawful and Free Lordly born and customary secret Seal, here printed alongside. As in Breslau on the birth-day [of] the Diet of the Lands in the thirty-third day of the month of August in the year one thousand seven hundred thirty-six, being there next Red which, having expired, being paper printed twenty-two different Seals.

SO have we for confirmation of the Content of the forenamed, there on this obligation of payment, and the insurances thereof; further any marque shall be pursued, ourselves and our Successors, upon Compliance all ready vowing, and good-thoughtfully will bind ourselves and similarly also of all exceptions and Benefits, which to facilitate and management of the Content of the aforementioned Obligation, some proprietors could be brought, however the same also oopt would be able to be excluded, one for all in Strength of these our further and proper Debt obligations, to the full renounce and forego. Failing we and release also to him Willem Gideon Deutz, as well as to all the others, who by this Negotiation and for loan affected interest are, besides their Heirs and future Occasions the power, to seek to the obtaining of all the right, and promised obligations to their people on account of the furnished Capital and Interests taken, from all means and ways of Taking, Titles, Actions, and whatsoever moreover may come, without any objection to serve, and that so long, until that the same punctually, and each in particular, wholly shall be paid, as well as the same allodially and payment to the aforementioned Willem Gideon Deutz, and further also Interested-ones, or their respective Heirs, on the appointed terms promptly and completely shall be discharged.

Where and further we still moreover to the mentioned Willem Gideon Deutz, to greater certainty of him and His other fellow Interested-ones, beyond the aforementioned willing means, and the Land's Income of our trusty and obedient Princes and States of Silesia, which as here above is said, to payment of the therein opened Principal Sum, as well as of the paid Interest specifically are bound, to an individual fund fastened and properly all the Income, rents, and Distributions, of what nature, condition, or legal status, which also are present, which themselves in the aforementioned Dukedom of Upper and Lower Silesia find, and us appertain, so that by some unforeseen marque of payment of the Obligation of the mentioned states, the said and Capital of Interest, in such manner the proper sufficing from the aforementioned Income here before mentioned Subsidiary Funds, for the whole

title: Commerce D'Asie & D'Afrique, Soc. De (Trieste Company)
description: ANVERS. Action de 1000 Florins. black. No 194. - Date: October 1, 1781 -  In 1775 Guilleaume Bolts obtained permission from Empress Maria Theresa to establish a company in the Austrian free port of Trieste, for trading with the East Indies. In 1781 the first shares were issued, with great success. The director was the famous Antwerp businessman Charles de Proli, son of the founder of the Keyserlijke Indische Compagnie, of Ostend; he was also linked with the formation of the Assurance d'Anvers. The company could not compete with the French and English competition, and after an unfortunate expedition to China, the company was unable to meet its liabilities. The Trieste Company is the last of the great trading companies of the Habsburgs. The share is printed on hand-made paper, and is signed by Count Charles de Proli and by Guillaume Bolts.

title: The Caracas Company, the Real Compania Guipuzcoana de Caracas
description: MADRID. 1 June 1752. Accion de 500 escudos. black. #2000. No 2530. The 'Caracas Company', the Real Compania Guipuzcoana de Caracas, was formed by a group of merchants in the Guipuzcoa province, around San Sebastian, in the Basque region of Spain, to trade with the Caracas province of Venezuela, with the further aims of (a) developing its agriculture, especially cocoa, and (b) keeping watch on the coast, from the mouth of the river Orinoco to the river Hacha (later extended to cover Maracaibo). This latter aim was to prevent illegal (in Spanish eyes) trading, particularly by the Dutch from Curaçao. The leader of this group was the Count of Penaflorida. This was the first of the great Spanish overseas trading companies, and the first Spanish trading company known to have issued shares to the general public. The company had the right to send two ships per year from San Sebastian and nearby Pasajes to Venezuela, carrying all types of Spanish goods. For the return voyage the ships brought precious metals, cocoa, sugar, tobacco and leathers, back to Sevilla and, later, Cadiz. In 1730 the company set about establishing a chain of trading-posts along the coast of what is now Venezuela and the offshore islands of Margarita and Trinidad, and in 1732 obtained a monopoly of trade between Caracas and Spain; in 1742 this was extended for an indefinite period. In that same year the company's Head Office was moved from San Sebastian to Madrid. From the outset the company was very profitable, paying large dividends to its shareholders. King Felipe V and the Queen took 100 shares each, for a combined investment of 100,000 escudos. The initial capital was 1,000,000 escudos (2,000 shares of 500 escudos of 15 reales each), but shares were a new idea to the Spanish public, and they did not sell well at first; only 500,000 escudos were sold (including those to the royal house, as above). The capital was later increased, in 1752, and again in 1766. The company's activities expanded rapidly, especially after 1734, when restrictions on the number of sailings were removed. The Caracas production, especially of cocoa, rose sharply. By mid-century the company owned 12 large ocean-going ships and 19 coastal ships, with some 2,500 crew, and carrying 2,500 or more tons of freight annually in each direction. However, the company mis-used its monopoly powers, by charging excessive prices for imported goods, and cutting steadily the already low prices paid to Caracas growers for their produce; this, together with interference in the government of Caracas, led in the end to a rebellion by the the colonists. Not until 1752 was this finally subdued, and these events led the Spanish government to reduce the company's privileges and introduce fixed prices for buying cocoa and other produce. Profits fell sharply, and the company's fortunes went into steady decline. Its investments in Spain were unsuccessful; wars against England in 1761-3 and 1779-83 cost it dearly; the free-trading policies of King Carlos III were the final blow. In 1781 the company lost the monopoly with Venezuela, and in 1785 it was absorbed by the Real Compania de Filipinas. The Caracas shares were exchanged for those of the Filipinas company. The share is very attractive, printed on vellum, with a fine copper-engraving. At the top are the royal arms of Guipuzcoa; on each side, and down the sides is a fine design of leaves, fruit,weapons, and an Indian man, with bow-and-arrow, and woman. Boxes of company freight are all around. There is a fine paper seal, also with the royal arms of Guipuzcoa. This is a share from the fist capital increase.

title: Swedish West-India Company
description: STOCKHOLM. 15 December 1787. Share of 100 Reichstaler. black. No 259. Folds. As early as 1745 the trading house Abr. Jocab Arfwedson & Co. was given the privilege of creating a West India company, but due to a sharp protest from the Spanish envoy in Stockholm against this undertaking, the firm renounced its privilege. After the Swedish acquisition in 1784 of Saint Barthélemy in the West Indies, town secretary Johan Liljencrants put a proposition to the Chamber of Commerce for the formation of a share-based company to conduct trade in the West Indies. In 1786, the company received a 15-year privilege for commerce and shipping with the Swedish island of Saint Barthélemy and other islands of the West Indies as well as with North America. Liljencrants and the Stockholm wholesale dealers Arfwedson, Schinkel and Pauli were to supervise the subscription. The business did not develop as hoped for, and the first expedition was lost as a consequence of the war with Russia in 1788. Later on, the business was paralysed due to prohibitions of importation of certain goods, especially coffee. High costs caused by captures of ships brought bad commercial results and the company was dissolved in 1805. The island became French, as part of Guadeloupe, in 1878. A 4-page document, ink-cancelled with a large 'X'. Printed, with manuscript additions of payment

title: Strand Bridge Company, Share No. 4696, John Colegate, London 1809
description: Share certificate No. 4696 of the Strand Bridge Company (later Waterloo Bridge), London. Certifies that John Colegate of Buckingham Row is a proprietor of one share (1/496 of the undertaking), incorporated by Act of Parliament. Signed under the Common Seal of the Company. London, 30 December 1809.

pages.description: Page 1 of 2 - London, 30 December 1809, 1 Share, #4696,  black, beige, printed on vellum, folds, golden seal with imprinted bridge - issued to John Colegate of Buckingham Street._x000B_The Strand Bridge was a project of the great English bridge constructor John Rennie. It was built in the years from 1811 to 1813 and was considered to be the largest and most beautiful bridge over the river Thames. The Strand Bridge was renamed Waterloo Bridge by the English in memory of the victorious battle against Napoleon. Later on it was restored a couple of times and today it is still situated on its original place in London.

title: Derby Canal Company, Share No. 320, Matthew How, Derby 1793
description: Share certificate No. 320 of the Derby Canal Company, certifying Matthew How of Derby (Hatter) as entitled to one share in the undertaking. Established by Act of Parliament for a navigable canal from the River Trent near Swarkiton Bridge. Second General Assembly held 7 October 1793, Derby.
creator: Derby Canal Company
notes: Derby Canal Company share certificate, Share No. 320; certified to Matthew [illegible] of Derby; established under Act of Parliament for canal from River Trent through Borough of Derby to Little Eaton; issued at second General Assembly, October 7, 1793
transcription:
# DERBY CANAL

THIS Ticket, or Instrument, is to certify, that Matthew How of Derby in the County of Derby (Hatter)

is entitled to the share No. 320 in this undertaking; and that her name and proper addition, with the number of the said share, are fairly and distinctly entered in the book kept by "The Derby Canal Company" for that purpose, agreeable to the directions of the act passed in the last session of Parliament, intituled, "An Act for making and maintaining a Navigable Canal from the River Trent at or near Swarklton Bridge, to and through the Borough of Derby, to Little Eaton, with a Cut out of the said Canal in or near the said Borough, to join the Erewash Canal near Sandiacre; and for making Railways from such Canal to several Collieries in the parishes or liberties of Denby, Horfley, and Smalley, all in the county of Derby." Given under the Common Seal of the said Derby Canal Company, at their second General Assembly, held the seventh day of October, in the year One Thousand Seven Hundred and Ninety Three.

N.B. There must be Duplicates of all Transfers of Shares, each of them executed both by the Seller and the Purchaser, one part whereof must be delivered to the Clerk to the *Derby Canal Company*, and entered by him before the purchaser can have any share of the profits of this undertaking, or be entitled to vote as a proprietor.

**Notable markings:**
- Circular wax seal (red/ochre colored) affixed to lower right of document
- Handwritten annotation at top left corner (partially visible)
- Serial number: 320
- Date: 7 October 1793

title: Societe Toulousaine du Bazacle, Part de Fondateur au Porteur No. 010532, Toulouse
description: Founder's bearer share (Part de Fondateur au Porteur) No. 010532 of the Societe Toulousaine du Bazacle, Societe Anonyme, Toulouse. Capital social: 13,150,000 Francs (131,500 shares of 1,000 Francs each). Statutes deposited with notary Moyne in Paris, modified by extraordinary assemblies of 19 March 1927 and 26 April 1929. Registered office: 10 Quai Saint-Pierre, Toulouse. This company traces its origins to a medieval water mill cooperative, one of the oldest joint-stock companies in history.

# SOCIETE TOULOUSAINE DU BAZACLE

## Original (French):

SOCIETE TOULOUSAINE DU BAZACLE
SOCIETE ANONYME

Statuts déposés chez M° MOYNE, Notaire à Paris
modifiés par les assemblées extraordinaires des 18 Mars 1927 et 26 Avril 1928.

CAPITAL SOCIAL : 13.150.000 FRANCS
Divisé en 131.500 Actions de 100 francs chacune

Siège Social à TOULOUSE, 10, Quai Saint-Pierre

PART DE FONDATEUR AU PORTEUR

Délibération de l'Assemblée Générale extraordinaire du 26 Avril 1928.
Donnant droit à 1/30000° des avantages attribués aux dites parts, par les statuts

Le Vice-Président,                                   Un Administrateur.

[Signature]                                          [Signature]

N° 010532

## English Translation:

TOULOUSAIN SOCIETY OF BAZACLE
ANONYMOUS SOCIETY

Statutes deposited with Mr. MOYNE, Notary in Paris
modified by the extraordinary assemblies of 18 March 1927 and 26 April 1928.

SOCIAL CAPITAL: 13,150,000 FRANCS
Divided into 131,500 Shares of 100 francs each

Registered Office at TOULOUSE, 10, Quai Saint-Pierre

FOUNDER'S BEARER SHARE

Resolution of the Extraordinary General Assembly of 26 April 1928.
Giving the right to 1/30000th of the advantages attributed to said shares, by the statutes

The Vice-President,                                  An Administrator.

[Signature]                                          [Signature]

No. 010532

## Notable markings:

- **Serial Number:** 010532
- **Red circular stamp (left):** "SOCIETE TOULOUSAINE DU BAZACLE / CAPITAL / 13.150.000" with company seal
- **Black circular stamp (right):** Authorization stamp dated 11 February 1929, registered with the Journal Officiel
- **Two handwritten signatures:** Vice-President and Administrator
- **Ornate decorative border:** Brown and cream Art Deco style frame throughout
- **Watermark:** Faint "BAZACLE" text visible in background

pages.description: Page 1 of 2 - Certificate for 300,000 shares funded by the First National Bank of Boston. The new Waldorf-Astoria was commissioned by Boomer-DuPont Properties as a replacement for the original Waldorf-Astoria which was torn down in 1929 to make room for the Empire State Building. The new Waldorf was relocated on an entire block leased from the New York Central Railroad, and was designed by architectural firm of Schultze & Weaver (Sherry Netherland, Pierre, Lexington Park Lane). Construction funds totaling $40,000,000 were provided by a consortium of banks and railroads. On October 1, 1931, the new Waldorf-Astoria opened to the public.

Construction funds totaling $40,000,000 were provided by a consortium of banks and railroads. On October 1, 1931, the new Waldorf-Astoria opened to the public.

transcription:
# Hotel Waldorf-Astoria Corporation Stock Certificate - Transcription

## Main Text:

**INCORPORATED UNDER THE LAWS OF THE STATE OF NEW YORK**

**Hotel Waldorf-Astoria Corporation**

THIS CERTIFICATE IS TRANSFERABLE IN NEW YORK, N.Y. OR IN BOSTON, MASS.
AUTHORIZED CAPITAL STOCK–300,000 SHARES WITHOUT PAR VALUE

**This is to Certify that** [blank] **is the owner of** [blank] **fully paid and non assessable shares of Limited Stock without par value of Hotel Waldorf-Astoria Corporation transferable only on the books of the Corporation by the holder hereof in person or by duly authorized attorney upon surrender of this Certificate properly endorsed. The shares hereof shall be held subject to all the terms and conditions of the Certificate of Incorporation on file in the office of the Secretary of State and to the By-laws of the Corporation heretofore adopted and now in effect, a copy of which is on file herewith, and the holder is entitled to all the rights and privileges incident to the ownership of such shares.

**Witness the facsimile seals of the Corporation and the facsimile signatures of duly authorized officers.**

**Dated** [blank]

**Treasurer** [signature - marked SPECIMEN]

**President** [signature - marked SPECIMEN]

## Notable Markings:

- Serial number at top: **110537**
- Red text stamps: **SPECIMEN** (appears twice - below Treasurer and President signature lines)
- Large corporate seal in center bottom with text around perimeter (partially legible): **[SEAL OF THE ASTORIA CORPORATION]**
- "THE FIRST NATIONAL BANK OF BOSTON" printed vertically on left margin
- "THE COMMONWEALTH BANK OF BOSTON" printed vertically on right margin
- Ornate green border with decorative floral and geometric patterns throughout
- Empty green cartouches/boxes on either side of central engraving (for denomination or identifying numbers)
- Central allegorical engraving showing two figures seated on barrel or vessel

---

# Transcription

The Certificate of Incorporation, as amended, provides, among other things, as follows:

The Corporation may issue and sell its authorized shares without par value for such consideration as from time to time may be fixed by the Board of Directors.

"No contract or other transaction between the corporation and any other corporation shall be affected or invalidated by the fact that any one or more of the directors of this corporation is or are interested in, or is a director or officer or are directors or officers of, such other corporation, and any director or directors, individually or jointly, may be a party or parties to, or may be interested in, any contract or transaction of this corporation or in which this corporation is interested; and no contract, act or transaction of this corporation with any person or persons, firm or corporation, shall be affected or invalidated by the fact that any director or directors of this corporation is a party, or are parties to or interested in such contract, act or transaction, or in

any way connected with such person or persons, firm or association, and each and every person who may become a director of this corporation is hereby relieved from any liability that might otherwise exist from contracting with the corporation for the benefit of itself or of any firm, association or corporation in which it may be in anywise interested."

"No holder of the capital stock of this corporation shall be entitled as such as a matter of right, to subscribe for or purchase any part of any new or additional issue of stock of any class whatsoever whether now or hereafter authorized, or of securities convertible into stock of any class whatsoever, whether now or hereafter authorized, or whether issued for cash, property or services or by way of dividend, and all such rights are waived by each holder of the capital stock of this corporation."

---

Forwardly received, hereby sell, assign, and transfer unto

_____________________________________________________________________________

Shares of the Stock represented by the within Certificate, and do hereby irrevocably constitute and appoint _____________ Attorney, to transfer the said Stock, on the books of the within-named Corporation, with full power of Substitution in the premises.

Dated _________________ 19 _____

In Presence of:

_____________________________________________________________________________

_____________________________________________________________________________

_____________________________________________________________________________

THIS SPACE MUST NOT BE COVERED IN ANY WAY

---

**Notable markings:**
- Three black circular punch holes on the right side of the document
- Stamps/text visible on the right margin (partially illegible due to document condition)
- Signature lines in cursive script
- Date field marked "19____" indicating vintage document

title: Connecticut Land Company, Certificate No. 151, Calvin Austin, Hartford 1795
description: Certificate No. 151 of the Connecticut Land Company, Hartford, Connecticut, September 5, 1795. Certifies that Calvin Austin of Sheffield, Hartford County, is entitled to the trust and benefit of Three Thousand Twelve Hundred Thousandths (3,000/1,200,000) of the Connecticut Western Reserve, as held by Trustees John Caldwell, Jonathan Brace, and John Morgan under a Deed of Trust dated 5 September 1795. Signed by all three trustees. The Connecticut Land Company purchased the Western Reserve (in present-day Ohio) from the State of Connecticut.

pages.description: Page 1 of 4 -  Dated September 5, 1795 HARTFORD. Certificate of 3000 Founders' Shares. A very important company in the histories of Connecticut and Ohio, founder of the city of Cleveland and once owner of much of the State of Ohio. A grant from the English King Charles II in 1662 had given to Connecticut the title to all the territory between 41 and 42.2 degrees North in a band across the whole of America to the Pacific, some of this conflicting with rights also given to Pennsylvania. In 1763 the British government designated all lands east of the Appalachians and west of the Mississippi to be an Indian Reserve, so blocking the western expansion of all the colonies, and this was one of the grievances leading to the War of Independence. By the Treaty of Paris that ended the hostilities in 1783, Britain ceded the Indian Reserve to the United States. In the subsequent negotiations amongst the states, Connecticut ceded to Pennsylvania in the east and to the federal government in the west most of its 41st Parallel claim, retaining an area 120 miles wide to the west of Pennsylvania, later called the Connecticut Western Reserve (separated by several hundred miles from Connecticut). In 1795 a group of investors paid the State $1,200,000 for the Western Reserve and formed themselves into the Connecticut Land Company with 1,200,000 shares. The largest shareholder was Oliver Phelps, a major speculator in western New York lands also, who subscribed for 168,000 shares in his sole name and another 80,000 jointly with a partner. In 1796 the company began the development of what was to become the State of Ohio. They sent Moses Cleaveland to survey the Reserve and lay out townships, amongst others the foundations of the city of Cleveland, and set about selling land to settlers from the East. In 1800 Connecticut ceded sovereignty over the Western Reserve and it was absorbed into the Northwest Territory, then in 1803 became the north-east part of the State of Ohio. Issued to Calvin Austin, and an attached manuscript transfer of the shares to James K Garnsey for a price of $8,000 dated 20th December 1796.

Page 2 of 4 -  Dated September 5, 1795 HARTFORD. Certificate of 3000 Founders' Shares. A very important company in the histories of Connecticut and Ohio, founder of the city of Cleveland and once owner of much of the State of Ohio. In 1795 a group of investors paid the State $1,200,000 for the Western Reserve and formed themselves into the Connecticut Land Company with 1,200,000 shares. The largest shareholder was Oliver Phelps, a major speculator in western New York lands also, who subscribed for 168,000 shares in his sole name and another 80,000 jointly with a partner. In 1796 the company began the development of what was to become the State of Ohio. Issued to Calvin Austin, and an attached manuscript transfer of the shares to James K Garnsey for a price of $8,000 dated 20th December 1796.

In the subsequent negotiations amongst the states, Connecticut ceded to Pennsylvania in the east and to the federal government in the west most of its 41st Parallel claim, retaining an area 120 miles wide to the west of Pennsylvania, later called the Connecticut Western Reserve (separated by several hundred miles from Connecticut). In 1795 a group of investors paid the State $1,200,000 for the Western Reserve and formed themselves into the Connecticut Land Company with 1,200,000 shares. The largest shareholder was Oliver Phelps, a major speculator in western New York lands also, who subscribed for 168,000 shares in his sole name and another 80,000 jointly with a partner. In 1796 the company began the development of what was to become the State of Ohio. They sent Moses Cleaveland to survey the Reserve and lay out townships, amongst others the foundations of the city of Cleveland, and set about selling land to settlers from the East. In 1800 Connecticut ceded sovereignty over the Western Reserve and it was absorbed into the Northwest Territory, then in 1803 became the north-east part of the State of Ohio. Issued to Calvin Austin, and an attached manuscript transfer of the shares to James K Garnsey for a price of $8,000 dated 20th December 1796.

Page 4 of 4 -  Dated September 5, 1795 HARTFORD. Certificate of 3000 Founders' Shares. A very important company in the histories of Connecticut and Ohio, founder of the city of Cleveland and once owner of much of the State of Ohio. A grant from the English King Charles II in 1662 had given to Connecticut the title to all the territory between 41 and 42.2 degrees North in a band across the whole of America to the Pacific, some of this conflicting with rights also given to Pennsylvania. In 1763 the British government designated all lands east of the Appalachians and west of the Mississippi to be an Indian Reserve, so blocking the western expansion of all the colonies, and this was one of the grievances leading to the War of Independence. By the Treaty of Paris that ended the hostilities in 1783, Britain ceded the Indian Reserve to the United States. In the subsequent negotiations amongst the states, Connecticut ceded to Pennsylvania in the east and to the federal government in the west most of its 41st Parallel claim, retaining an area 120 miles wide to the west of Pennsylvania, later called the Connecticut Western Reserve (separated by several hundred miles from Connecticut). In 1795 a group of investors paid the State $1,200,000 for the Western Reserve and formed themselves into the Connecticut Land Company with 1,200,000 shares. The largest shareholder was Oliver Phelps, a major speculator in western New York lands also, who subscribed for 168,000 shares in his sole name and another 80,000 jointly with a partner. In 1796 the company began the development of what was to become the State of Ohio. They sent Moses Cleaveland to survey the Reserve and lay out townships, amongst others the foundations of the city of Cleveland, and set about selling land to settlers from the East. In 1800 Connecticut ceded sovereignty over the Western Reserve and it was absorbed into the Northwest Territory, then in 1803 became the north-east part of the State of Ohio. Issued to Calvin Austin, and an attached manuscript transfer of the shares to James K Garnsey for a price of $8,000 dated 20th December 1796.

# Transcription

**Connecticut Land Company, fs.**

HARTFORD, Sept. 5th, 1795.

THIS CERTIFIES, that Calvin Austin of gla[s]field, in the County of Hartford, Estate of Connecticut—
is entitled to the Trust and Benefit of Three Thousand—

Twelve Hundred Thousandths of the Connecticut Western
Reserve, fs called, as held by John Caldwell, Jonathan
Brace, and John Morgan, Trustees in a Deed of Trust, dated
the Sixtieth Day of September One Thousand Seven Hundred and
Ninety Five, to hold said Proportion or Share to exist the said
Calvin Austin—
to his Heirs and Assigns, according to the Terms, Conditions, Co-
venants and Exceptions contained in the said Deed of Trust, and
in certain Articles of Agreement entered into by the Persons com-
posing the Connecticut Land Company; which said Share is
transferrable by Assignment, under Hand and Seal, witnessed by
two Witnesses, and acknowledged before any Justice of the Peace
in the State of Connecticut, or before a Notary Public, or a
Judge of the Common Pleas in any of the United States, and to
be recorded by the Clerk of the Board of Directors.

Garnsey of the City and County of Hartford and State aforesaid, do herby [sic] assign and transfer unto the Said Garnsey the annuled [sic] Certificate for Three Thousand Twelve hundred Thousandths of the Connecticut Western Reserve (So called) to which I am entitled under the Deed of Trust and Articles of Agreement of the Connecticut Land Company to have and to hold the same Certificate for Three Thousand Twelve hun dred Thousandths to the Said of Garnsey his heirs and assigns, Subject to the Terms, Conditions, Covenants and Exceptions contained in said Deed of Trust and Articles of Agreement.... And I hereby by myself my heirs Executors, and Administrators that at and until the enjoying and delivering of this Present I am will entitled to the assigned hum bers, and have good right to see assign and transfer the same in manner aforesaid... In Witness Whereof I have hereunto Signed my Name and affixed my Seal this Twentieth Day of December in the year Seventeen hundred and Ninety Six—

Signed sealed and delivered
in presence of —
**Calvin Austin**

Ebenezer Gaulking
Alfred Boyd

# Transcription

**THIS INDENTURE, Made the [illegible] Day of [illegible] in the Twelfth Year of His Majesty's Reign, Annoque Domini, 1739. Between [illegible] of the one Part, and John Weston, George Golding, Jabez Brunson, [illegible] of the other Part.**

IN the County of [illegible], &c. [illegible] of the one Part, a d John Weston, George Golding, Jabez Brunson, Dwell Untrick, John Cutler and [illegible] Sheffield, all of Newport, in the County of Newport, in His Colony's Trustees for said Colony (by a special Act of Assembly of said Colony, made the [illegible] Day of [illegible]) in the Year [illegible] is to Loan on the other Part Wit-nesseth, That the [illegible] [illegible] in good Bills of public Credit of said Colony to him in Hand, paid by the d Trustees, the Receipt whereof he doth hereby acknowledge, hath given, granted, conveyed and confirmed, and hereby doth give, grant, convey and confirm unto the above named Trustees, and their others as [illegible] at any Time by the General Assembly of said Colony be appointed in their Stead to manage said Trust.

[Extensive middle section with water damage and illegibility]

Together with all the Profits, Improvements and Appurtenances thereunto belonging or appertaining, **TO HAVE AND TO HOLD** the above bargained Premises, with the Appurtenances, Unto the said Trustees, and their Successors in said Office, for the Life and Behoof of said Colony for ever. And the said [illegible] for himself, his Heirs, Executors and Administrators, doth hereby covenant and agree with the said Trustees and their Successors, that at the Time of said Grant and Sale, he the said [illegible] is the lawful Owner of the Premises, and it[s] rightfully seized thereof in Fee Simple, free from all Incumbrances whatsoever; and that he will [illegible] Warrant and defend the same to the [illegible] Trustees and their Successors against the Claims of all Persons whatsoever. Provided always, and upon Condition nevertheless, That if the said Executors or Administrators, shall well and truly pay to the said Trustees, or their Successors (by Act of Assembly appointed) the aforesaid Sum of [illegible] in Bills Day of Lord 1748, together with the Interest thereon accruing at Five pr. Centum pr. Annum, from the Day of the Date of this Indenture, to the aforesaid Time of Payment, and that without Fraud or Delay, then this present Indenture, and every Clause thereof shall be null and void, and of no Effect: But in Default, shall be and remain in full Force and Virtue.

**IN WITNESS whereof, the said [illegible] hath hereunto set his Hand and Seal, the Day [illegible] Month and Year above written.**

**Signed, Sealed and Delivered, in the Presence of**

[Handwritten signatures and marks below]

title: Share Receipt (Recepis) for 1/1063 of Garphytte Iron and Alum Works and Beata Christina Alum Works, Sweden, Amsterdam 1776
description: Dutch-language share receipt (Recepis) No. 27, certifying ownership of 1/1063 share in the Iron Manufacturer and Alum Works Garphytte (Yser-Manufactuur en Aluyn-Werken Garphytte) and in the Alum Work Beata Christina (Aluyn-Werk Beata Christina), both in Sweden. Received in exchange for a share from the outstanding negotiation on these works, including unpaid interest coupons. Issued Amsterdam, 1 April 1776. Signed by directors G.L. Caaypranger and Bran Heeschuyssensfe. Registered by Notary Paulus Runtum.

transcription:
# Transcription

**Original (Dutch):**

N°. 27

RECEPIS,
WEGENS 1/1063 AANDEEL

In de YSER-MANUFACTUUR en ALUYN-WERKEN

GARPHYTTE,
En in het ALUYN-WERK

BEATA CHRISTINA,
IN ZWEEDEN.

Ontfangen van
een der aandelen die nog loopende waren, ten Laste van eene der Negotiatiën op de Yfer- Manufactuur- en Aluyn- Werken Garphytte, en op het Aluyn-Werk Beata Christina, en zulks met deszelfs onbetaald gebleeve Intrest-Coupons; en werd den Houder dezes uit dien hoofde door de ondergeteckenden, bij deze Recepis erkend voor Eigenaar van 1/1063 Aandeel, in de Yfer- Manufactuur- en Aluyn- Werken Garphytte, en in het Aluyn- Werk Beata Christina beyde, met alles wat nu daar aan- en toebehoard, als welke Werken door het Koninglyke Majestcits en Rylks Zweedde Hof Gericht, aan de Geintereseerdens in de gezegde Negotiation toegewefen zyn, voor en tot voldoening van de daar op nog verfchuldigd gebleeven zynde f 10,650000- en onbetaald gebleeve Intereffen van dien, als welke f 10,650000- met en benevens nog f 120000- die wegens eene der gezegde Negotiatiën immidels bij loting afgefold zyn, daar op alhier genegocieerd waren.

Aelum Amsterdam deser [unclear] April 1776

Deze Recepis N°. 27 is
behoorsk gereproduceerd el
gereglstreerd by ms

[Signature] Paulus Huntum
1776.

[Center signature: appears to be official authorization]

Als Directeuren.

---

## English translation:

**No. 27**

RECEIPT,
CONCERNING 1/1063 SHARE

In the IRON-MANUFACTORY and ALUM-WORKS

GARPHYTTE,
And in the ALUM-WORK

BEATA CHRISTINA,
IN SWEDEN.

Received from
one of the shares which was still outstanding, charged to one of the Negotiations on the Iron-Manufactory and Alum-Works Garphytte, and on the Alum-Work Beata Christina, and thus with its unpaid Interest-Coupons; and hereby the Holder of this is recognized by the undersigned by means of this Receipt as Owner of 1/1063 Share, in the Iron-Manufactory and Alum-Works Garphytte, and in the Alum-Work Beata Christina both, with everything that now belongs to and is attached thereto, as which Works by the Royal Majesty and the Realm's Swedish Court, were awarded to the Interested Parties in the aforementioned Negotiation, for and towards satisfaction of the amount still owed thereon of f 10,650,000- and the unpaid Interest of that, as which f 10,650,000- together with and besides still f 120,000- which concerning one of the aforementioned Negotiations was meanwhile settled by lottery, were here negotiated.

Aelum Amsterdam this [unclear] April 1776

This Receipt No. 27 is
duly reproduced and
registered by me

[Signature] Paulus Huntum
1776.

[Center signature]

As Directors.

---

- Serial/reference notation: "Deze Recepis N°. 27"
- Financial amounts specified in florins (f): 10,650,000- and 120,000-
- Year noted: 1776
