# Historical Document Transcription

**Original (French):**

AU NOM DE DIEU AMEN.

JE souffigné affuire à Vous Monsieur James Forman pour vôtre Compte, ou de tout autre à qui il pourroit appartenir en total ou en partie, I--- i si ou ennemi levoit la forme de Dieu par Cas d'une argueil le Change pour imitable à la Courlet d'Amstedam le Regiethe ——— ——

———— dont est maître (Après Dieu) Capitaine Guillaume Altalie d'Appelt fans pouvoir faire aucune conteflation fur le nom du Capitaine ou Vaiffeau (quel qu'il foit) le rifque à courir fur l'a'offet Commençant du jour & heure que ledit Vaiffeau étoit encore à la relâche de Sontlett —— jufqu'à ce qu'il foit arrivé à d'Amfterdam, ou lieu de fa deftination ou les rifques finiront fur ledit Vaiffeau & jufqu'à heure après qu'il y aura Dieu & ancres, à leur liberté, & indemnité; bien entendu qu'en cas d'avarie à bon équipement ——

il fera permis au dit Vaiffeau de Naviguer à droite, à gauche, en avant ou en arrière pendant fon Voyage, fans que cela puiffe cauter aucun préjudice à la préfente affurance je me toumette de courir les rifques & perils auxquels ledit Vaiffeau et [illegible] [unclear]d pourroit être affujetti, & en cas de tous indemnagements qui pourraient y furvenir, foit en totalité ou en partie, dans toutes les rencontres imaginables, de quelle manière ou façon qu'elles puiffent arriver, car je m'oblige de garantir tous perils de Mer, Vent, Tempêtes, Naufrage, Échouemens, Abordage, jcts en Mer, Feu, Arcets, Pirates, Corfaires, Voleurs, Ennemis, Vaiffeau de Guerre, lettres de marque ou conrabandc, prife inimicale, furprife, détention, confifcation, reprefailles, vexations, des Rois, Princes, Puiffances, & Peuple quelconque ; & generalement de tous autres perils, pertes, fortunes ou cas fortuits, qui font, ou pourraient avenir, en quelque manière que ce foit que tout puiffe imaginer au derniemr ou dommage [illegible] [unclear] ——ou d'une partie d'ice car je me mets entièrement dans le lieu & la place d'edit l'heur naturel pour le garantie & indemniter de toutes pertes qui pourront arriver ; & en cas de perte ou dommage au [illegible] [unclear] (Ce que Dieu ne veuille) promets & m'oblige de payer ou rembourfer à vous Monsieur James Forman ou à vôtre ordre toute la perto & dommage que vous aurez reçu (qui excedera les trois par cent) à proportion de la forme affurée, & ce dans le delai immediment aprés que je ferai duëment informé de la perte ou dommage arrivé au D'Aifterdam ou maxtrakirdem en diminuant feulement deux par cent en cas de perte ; & en tel cas, je donne pouvoir special à vous Monsieur James Forman ou à vos commis, pour tant à mon dammage que profit, travaillé, à la falvation d'edit l'Aifterdam & D'édits [unclear] promettant en tout évenemeins de payer les fraix & depens faits pour la falvation d'edit Vaiffeau & D'Édits [unclear]——

[The document continues with extensive insurance and maritime contract terms regarding risks, damages, payments, and dispute resolution procedures]

S. D'Aceches

title: Unified Debt of Egypt (Dette d'Egypte Unifiee), Bearer Bond, 2,500 Francs / 400 Pounds Sterling, No. 1,036,101
description: Bearer bond (Obligation au Porteur) of the Unified Debt of Egypt (Dette d'Egypte Unifiee), bilingual French/English. Denomination: 2,500 Francs / 400 Pounds Sterling. Bond No. 1,036,101. Total capital stock: 1,470,500,000 Francs / 29,500,000 Pounds Sterling. Amortizable. Issued under decrees by His Highness the Khedive. Coupon sheet attached. The Unified Debt of Egypt was created in 1876 to consolidate Egyptian government debt under European (Caisse de la Dette Publique) financial control.

# Transcription: Unified Debt of Egypt Bond

## Original (French and English text):

**LEFT SIDE - FRENCH VERSION:**

Dette d'Egypte unifiée
DECRETS
A LA SANCTION DU KHEDIVE EN DATE DU 7 MAI ET 17 NOVEMBRE 1876

Remboursable au pair en 40 ans, par voie de tirages semestriels

SECURITE.—Ces obligations sont garanties par les revenus de l'État Égyptien.

SECURITE. Les obligations de 7 % du capital émises au capital.

Les intérêts sont payables à raison de 7 % l'an à titre d'intérêt composé.

Des obligations émises antérieurement par le Gouvernement

title: Chambre Imperiale et Royale d'Assurance d'Anvers, Marine Insurance Policy, 1811
description: Marine insurance policy issued by the Chambre Imperiale et Royale d'Assurance d'Anvers (Antwerp Imperial and Royal Insurance Chamber). Headed 'AU NOM DE DIEU.' Insures cargo belonging to M. Daniele Borges, merchant of Holland, for a voyage destined for Paris/Constantinople/Brussels. Handwritten details of insured party, goods, amount, and route. Dated 18 April 1811. Signed by officers of the Chamber with wax seals.

CHAMBRE IMPERIALE ET ROYALE D'ASSURANCE D'ANVERS

# Transcription

CONTRACT VAN OVERLEEVING
Letter G

ONDER DE ZINSPREUKE

DETYD BAARD ROOZEN.

CONDITIEN en VOORWAARDEN, waar op de ondergeteckende Persoonen met den anderen geaccordeert en overeен gekomen zyn, wegens het beleggen van by hun te fourneerene penningen, in Obligatien op alzulke Negotatien, als door de Heeren dezer Directie en Administratie zal werden goedeurd, best aan de Intentie deze te zullen voldoen, en die te conflictueeren op Lyven by de Inteckening te noemen.

Letter G

THE TIME BEARDED ROOZEN

tetir, de op dezelve Lyffen waarbij de Portionen of Persoonen, oveteckeuend op dezelve Lyffen in Art. 16, en de dezelve bepaalde meegenaamde heeft beide, zal hy dezelve teekenen, en door Heeren Directeuren laten Onderteekenen, zulks aan de Heeren bepalingen gememoert wordt in Art. 20.

JAN HARTGERS AERSCHENBERGH, en

title: French Consular Certificate of Egyptian Financial Document, Alexandria, 1866
description: Handwritten French administrative certificate from Alexandria, Egypt. Bears official seals of the French consulate. References a financial document number (numero 932) and a monetary sum. Certified 'conforme' and legalized for signature by the French Consul General in Alexandria, 17 June 1866. Likely a transfer or certification document for Egyptian government debt bonds.

transcription:
# Transcription of French Consular Certificate of Egyptian Financial Document, Alexandria, 1866

**Original (French):**

Bon sur le ministère des finances.

En vertu de l'arrêté du ministre de l'intérieur en date de [unclear] 1865 correspondant au [unclear] 1281, le ministre de l'intérieur a ordonné à l'Imprimerie de l'État, à Alexandrie, la confection de 300 imprimés contenant les formules à remplir de leur ordre, en ministère des finances.

Ait seulement de formule ayant reçu l'ordre impérial au d'imprimer ledit Drogman d'apparat et Dames.

Du 23 juillet 1799.

Le ministère de finances

P.D. mahmoud Raïs Pacha.

Pour Ratification, certifié aux, Alexandrie le 12 Mai 1866.

Le Drogman [illegible]

B[illegible]

Vu pour légalisation de la signature ci Bassou N.B D. [Affermier Bonne?] Drogman, le Consul Général de France à Alexandrie, d'appui.

Alexandrie, le 12 Mai 1866.

L'Agent du Conseil Général
Le Consul

---

**English translation:**

Bond on the Ministry of Finances.

By virtue of the decree of the Minister of the Interior dated [unclear] 1865 corresponding to [unclear] 1281, the Minister of the Interior ordered the State Printing Office in Alexandria to produce 300 printed forms containing the formulas to be completed at their order, by the Ministry of Finances.

Only had received the imperial order to print said Dragoman of apparatus and Ladies.

From 23 July 1799.

The Ministry of Finances

P.D. Mahmoud Raïs Pasha.

For Ratification, certified in Alexandria on 12 May 1866.

The Dragoman [illegible]

B[illegible]

Seen for legalization of signature herein Bass N.B D. [Farmer Good?] Dragoman, the Consul General of France in Alexandria, for approval.

Alexandria, 12 May 1866.

The Agent of the General Council
The Consul

---

**Notable markings:**

- Two official seals of the French Consulate General at Alexandria (circular stamps with French imperial eagle and text)
- Multiple handwritten signatures
- Date: 12 Mai 1866 (12 May 1866)
- Reference to 195,000 Piastres in the document context
- Notarial attestations by French Consular officials

title: Dutch Negotiation Contract, Abraham van Berk & Vollemioven - Subscriber List, 1787 (p.1 of 3)
description: First page of a 3-page Dutch negotiation/annuity share document, commencing 'Heden den Eerste May des Jaars Seventeen Honderd Seven-en-Tachtig' (1 May 1787). Directed by Abraham van Berk and Hendrik Vollemioven with 10 directors total. Lists numbered female shareholders with names including Johanna Freriks, Carolina Elfens, Carolina Belfleur, and others. Printed conditions follow. Amsterdam, 1787.

pages.description: Page 1 of 3 -  Bond for 1,000 Gulden, only 750 copies issued, signature of Pieter Stadnitzski dated 5 May 1787_x000B__x000B_The securitization of receivables, which is exactly what triggers the current credit crisis, are not an invention of those days. 1787 already securitized Pieter Hendrik Vollenhoven 60 Stadnitski and annuities in this bond with a volume of 750,000 guilders. Stadnitski was a clever financier: In 1789 he founded, together with three other banking firms, the Holland Land Company. They initially acquired 3.3 million acres in the western part of the State of New York. The region was known as The Holland Purchase. The name Batavia, the settlement was based on the poetic name of the Netherlands. In 1802 was named the Genesee County area. In 1846 the Holland Land Company was liquidated, the land was sold.

transcription:
# Transcription

**Original (Dutch):**

Copia

HEDEN DEN EERSTE MAY DES JAARS SEVENTEEN HONDERD SEVEN-EN TACHTIG.

Compareerden voor mij ABRAHAM VAN BEEMS, openbaar Notaris, bij den Hove van Holland geplaatst, en Getuigen onderleede,
De Heeren PIETER STADNITSKI en Mr. HENDRIK VOLLENHOVEN, als Directeuren,
En de Weduwe, Heeren ABRAHAM FOCK en ADRIAAN SCHARPF, als Commissarissen
In het Levens-Verzekeringe-Maatschappy.

Woonende de Heeren Comparanten binnen deze Stad, my Notaris bekend.

De Komeni overwegende dat door de twee Heeren eerfte Comparanten, ingevolge hun uitgegevene Bericjts, van diverfe Participanten, volgens gereeckende Receptlift is geneociteert, een Comptoir van Negocie maal Honderd Vyftig Duizend Guldens Hollandsch Courant, tengevolge zoodanige Jaarlyks Interest, als hier na zal worden gezegd, ten einde d-zelve ongenomen Hoeninzgen & empolementen, te don aanköop van Een Millison en Vyfmaal Honderd Duizend Livres tournoos, gevestigd op 50 Contracten, ieder van Livres 50,000: of 60 Contracten, ieder van Livres 50,000:, en de Capitaal van de Koninglyke Francke Lyrente Negotiatie van Miert 1781, rentende in pCt. op een Hould, dog tastet met de korting van de tiendte Penning, en dezelve in bescheiden hier geno'd der Interest zedert Primo July 1786, tot dan Stuyvers de Livres, daar en tegen schuldig en Zegels, vooral allerlei Interest, Copijen en Zegels, voorte alle onkosten van Provelie, Premie, en Courtage Cc, hier ook gonsanten, negens't ven gereckene hier Negotiatie, en voorts dat dezolve Contracten zouden onder warborg van 50 of 20 Jonge Jufvrouwen, alliestuschen de 3 en 8 Jaren, en gefield op de manen en in houvaing van de Heeren laaste Comparanten en van my Notaris, alles on soldage voelen—

en met alzalke precaution en voorzorgen, als by zeekere Acte op heeden ten overlllan van de Notaris GERRIT RODAN en Getuigen, door gemelde twee Heeren laaste Comparanten gepssfocrt, thaat vermeld, en welcke Acte, under de Dispensie van dezer Negotiatie, ter Ville van de Eigenaars en Geinteresseerdm, een Comptoir van my Notaris, als Notaris van dit Fonds, berustiende blylft.

Insgevolgs, en ter volleindiging van 't invoege voormeld uitgegevens Bericjts, zijn door de twee Heeren laaste Comparanten, binne deze Hand gefield; Zo is'k Notaris, ook bekenne ten Fine megenfeld, overige

Negen-en-Vyftig Contract, van Vyf-en-Twintig Duizend Livres Capitaal, ieder op een Hould, rentende op pCt., onder korting van de tolle Penning, van de Koninglyke Francke Lyrente Negotiatie, insgelijker bij Edict van Maart 1781. Stände alles op de Naamen van ABRAHAM SCHARPF en ABRAHAM VAN BEEM, genoemde en beleijd op de Lyven hier in aangeleijd. Zijnde reeds na de Contritie, een Contract van 2-50,000 - belegd een Lyve ELISABERTI VRIES, N°. 74, door haar overileven (voorgevoijdt bij het 14 February 1782, Waar van aan my Notaris de dood Cedul is geëxhibeert) Vervallen, zijnde de overige Contracten gemummert & geconstitueert als volgt:

N°. 50. Jurry Gertrude Elisabetn Vooet.
51. —— Constantia Gerardina Nobiet.
52. —— Cornelia Elizabeth Spilker.
53. —— Johanna Jofina van de Velde.
54. —— Cardina Jaffina van Wiche.
55. —— Anna Dünckertis.
56. —— Johanna Hermina Dapoter.
57. —— Floresia Maria Perffer.
58. —— Maria Anna de Lange.
59. —— Sibilla Helena Bregn.
60. —— Cornelia Janfen.
61. —— Anna Carafina de Wit.
62. —— Gertruyda Helena Hildrop.
63. —— Margaretha de Vriet.
64. —— Adriana Wilhelmina van Pembrock.
65. —— Maria ten Cate.
66. —— Henriette Gillegonda Wend.
67. —— Eliseria Gerarda Nalarey.
68. —— Elisabeth Syger.
69. —— Henriette Charlotta Hagues.
70. —— Jacoba Maria van Laar.
71. —— Sophie Reliandt.
72. —— Maria Cornelia Alsyn.
73. —— Clara Paulina Brunsbauer.

79. —— Maria de Vries.

**English Translation:**

TODAY THE FIRST OF MAY OF THE YEAR SEVENTEEN HUNDRED SEVEN AND EIGHTY.

Appeared before me ABRAHAM VAN BEEMS, public Notary, at the Court of Holland appointed, and Witnesses hereto,
The Gentlemen PIETER STADNITSKI and Mr. HENDRIK VOLLENHOVEN, as Directors,
And the Widow, Gentlemen ABRAHAM FOCK and ADRIAAN SCHARPF, as Commissioners
In the Life Insurance Company.

Residing the Gentlemen Appearing within this City, my Notary known.

The Company considering that by the two Gentlemen first Appearing, pursuant to their issued Reports, from diverse Participants, according to received Receipt Lists has been noted, an Office of Negotiation of one Hundred Fifty Thousand Guilders Hollandsch Courant, in consequence of such Yearly Interest, as hereafter shall be stated, to the end that the same unexecuted Transactions & employees [to do] purchase of One Million and Five times Hundred Thousand Livres tournoos, invested in 50 Contracts, each of Livres 50,000: or 60 Contracts, each of Livres 50,000:, and the Capital of the Royal French Rente Negotiation of March 1781, yielding in pCt. on a Hold, but subject to the deduction of the tenth Penny, and the same in documents here named of Interest since 1st July 1786, until then Stuyvers the Livre, and thereagainst liable for Seals, above all other Interest, Copies and Seals, as well as all costs of Provision, Premium, and Courtage Cc, here also constituted, pursuant to the reckoned here Negotiation, and furthermore that such Contracts would be under guarantee of 50 or 20 Young Maidens, aged between 3 and 8 Years, and placed in the names and in keeping of the Gentlemen last Appearing and of my Notary, all on solid ground—

and with all such precaution and care, as by certain Deed on today executed before Notary GERRIT RODAN and Witnesses, by the said two Gentlemen last Appearing

**English translation:**

[The subscriber list contains 30 numbered female names (60-89), each preceded by "Jufvr." (Miss/Madam)]

All those who have agreed as my Notary in the presence of the Gentlemen Comparants are recorded in the following list. The aforementioned three different weekend locations, where a Notary will receive an Aandeel and a Quittantie [receipt], are as Notaries appointed: [paragraph continues with legal details about the negotiation process]

[Articles I and II detail the issuance of 750 copies of bonds worth 1,000 Guilden each, with terms for interest payments on the 1st of November and 1st of May to the bearers of the bonds, and conditions regarding the transfer of coupons and receipts]

**Notable markings:**

- Contains numbered subscriber list (entries 60-89)
- Legal document in Dutch from 1787
- References to bond issuance by Pieter Hendrik Vollenhoven and Pieter Stadnitski

---

# Dutch Negotiation Contract Transcription

**Original (Dutch):**

Art. III.
Dat vermits volgens de gevoonde kans Reekening in ieder Actien Maanden, een hoofd, van de renten, kan en moet Sterven, als dan het verlies door ieder liefdeburen zou worden gedeeld, en naar dezen hoofd, in evenredigheijd met ½ pCo., ½ jaars vermrindering van Interest zou kunnen vergoed worden, zo als ook ieders, becom bij zijne keuring hoofd ook het vermeêrderen der Interest; met ½ pCo., ½ jaars goude toeslachen, waar der beide gevallen, het opgeteggen Capitaal, geneogzaam zoude blijven Accresfeefering op by overgalving van dit lastfte hoofd weder galyck aan de Inlang te zyn. De Heeren Eerste Compagnen hebben vermeinerd dit het eevenaudige middel tot herttel van 't goene tegen de Intrestenfoorders mogte, zoude zyn, dog vermits de veranderering van Interest egter juist niet om middelyk, op het Eerthe omregulering in den loop der Vereffeningen nodig is, zo sullen de Heeren Directeuren zhooren tot het eerste over te gaan de Gemeentesfoerdens Convocestenyen en als dan overgelegd worden of men de ½ pCo. zal blijven onderseeren, en dus de Negotiatie eene hoewel twijsom, oger en einde boornen, dan of men 't Interest jets verminnderen zal, om daardoor betatig te doen zyn, zullende Usthun in dit geval genoomen worden bij meerdeheid van Stemmen.
Gehandhaafd en de hoe grootheid van ieders Interest.

Art. IV.
Dat indien deze Negotiatie niet den uiteinde haar reguliere loop houd en de Interesfenste tot ½ pCo., differentia even bij Harta de doorgetting benoemd, bestaald zyn: de gezanmuddyke Gemeintesfoerdens de aangekocke Effecten verifleeten zullen of zodanige andere Schikingen beraa men zij op die Tijd door de Meerderheld zal grodgevonden worden.

Art. V.
De Directeuren zullen niet dan de onvermijdbare Kosten en 1 pCo., provisie van 't totanst der uitvangene Renten (en geyne van de uitsloopene Effectsten) in Reekening brengen.

Art. VI.
De Directeuren zullen bij de betaling der Coupons een exacte Reekening der Negotiatie ter Visfie en Examen van de Deelnemers Suppletieteren op dat een ieder initaat zal kannen zyn, hun beffier te bonoordelen.

Art. VII.
En eindelijk dat bij overlijden of affland van een der Heeren Directeuren, Commissarissen of Notaris van dit Fonds, zal worden gehanddeld conform de bepalingen bij 't uitgeegeven Bericht behoeeten, zooal van een Exemplaar aan de Groste dezer Geamneceerte is, om zig daar na in allen gevallen te regulieeren.

En verzoeken de Heeren Commissaristen hier van op te maaken Acte, de Groste dezes te bewaa hebben en van de Negotiatie volgends der rato van hun Aandeel in de Negotiatie Copien Authenclie dezer Acte wit te leeveren.

Gesloteken nimmen Aantekendain in Presentie van ABRAHAM JACOB BROES en JAN BORG-HORST als Getuigen. (Was Geteekend)

PIETER STADNITZSKI, HEINZ. VOLLEMIOVEN, JOB ADAM POOCH, A. SCHAHTZ, A. J. BROES, P. Borgh horst, en A. van BEEM Notaris.

In Amsteldam 5 Mei A°. 1787.

Accordeerd met de Minutte
[signature]

---

Deze Sub. N°. 597 door
mij Ondergetekende No-
taris en Protocolis aange-
tekend.
[signature]

**Ontvangen van**
een Somma van EEN DUYZEND GULDENS, voor een
Aandeel in deez' Negotiatie, waar van de Interesfien, conform de bijgevoegde Coupons, op primo November en primo Mei zullen betaald worden.

Amsteldam, 8 Mei A°. 1787.

[signature] als Directeuren.

---

**English Translation:**

Art. III.
That because according to the customary chance calculation in every stock month, one principal share of the annuity may and must die, as then the loss shall be shared by each beneficiary, and after this principal, in proportion with ½ pCo., ½ yearly reduction of Interest shall be able to be compensated, just as also each [person] by his choice principal also the increase of Interest; with ½ pCo., ½ yearly good addition, where in both cases, the stated Capital would remain sufficiently Accretion upon passing of this last principal again equal to the Input. The Gentlemen First Companies have determined that the equal means to remedy what against the Interest creditors might be, would be, because the change of Interest just not immediately, on the Earth of regulation in the course of the Settlements is necessary, so the Gentlemen Directors shall hear to take the first step to the Community creditors Convocation and then when delivered whether one the ½ pCo. shall remain agreed, and thus the Negotiation some doubt, more and end thereof, or whether one the Interest somewhat reduce shall, to thereby make payment be possible, providing you in this case understood be by majority of Votes.
Maintained and the proportionality of each one's Interest.

Art. IV.
That if this Negotiation not the utmost its regular course holds and the Interest to ½ pCo. difference even by Heart the accomplishment named, be paid: the arranged Community creditors the purchased Effects shall verify or such other arrangements about men by which on that Time by the Majority shall be found good.

Art. V.
The Directors shall except the unavoidable Costs and 1 pCo. provision of the total of the collected Rents (and none of the terminated Effects) in the accounting.

Art. VI.
The Directors shall by the payment of the Coupons an exact accounting of the Negotiation for Verification and Examination of the Participants deliver so that each individual shall be able to be, their better to understand.

Art. VII.
And finally that upon death or departure of one of the Gentlemen Directors, Commissioners or Notary of this Fund, shall be handled according to the determinations in the issued Notice stated, just as from an Example of the Community this, to regulate oneself after in all cases.

And request the Gentlemen Commissioners here of to make Act, the Community this to preserve and of the Negotiation according to their ratio of their share in the Negotiation Copies Authenticate this Act to deliver.

Signed with witness Signed in Presence of ABRAHAM JACOB BROES and JAN BORG-HORST as Witnesses. (Was Signed)

PIETER STADNITZSKI, HEINZ. VOLLEMIOVEN, JOB ADAM POOCH, A. SCHAHTZ, A. J. BROES, P. Borg horst, and A. van BEEM Notary.

In Amsterdam 5 May A°. 1787.

Agreed with the Minute
[signature]

---

This Sub. N°. 597 by
me, the Undersigned Notary and Protocol officer noted.
[signature]

**Received**
of a Sum of ONE THOUSAND GULDENS, for a
Share in this Negotiation, whereof the Interest, according to the attached Coupons, on first November and first May shall be paid.

Amsterdam, 8 May A°. 1787.

[signature] as Directors.

---

**Notable Markings:**

- Multiple handwritten signatures throughout the document
- Serial number: 597
- Date stamps: "5 Mei A°. 1787" and "8 Mei

title: Imperial Chinese Government 5% Tientsin-Pukow Railway Loan, 100 Pound Bond No. 24305, 1908

title: Compagnie du Pont de la Mulatiere, Lyon - 500 Livres Action, No. 106
description: Share certificate (action) No. 106 for 500 livres invested in the Compagnie des Associes aux travaux du Pont de la Mulatiere, Lyon, France. The action produces 30 livres annual interest and a 100-livres premium upon redemption. Secured by special mortgage on toll revenues of the bridge and general mortgage on company assets. Signed at Lyon by company officers including Chard de [?], George [?], Sartore, and others. Dated ca. 1780s.
creator: Compagnie des Associes aux travaux du Pont de la Mulatiere
notes: Compagnie des Associés aux travaux du Pont de la Mulatière (Lyon bridge company), Action de 500 livres tournois; produces 30 livres annual interest with 100 livres premium on redemption; authorized by Merchants of Lyon; No. 106
transcription:
# Document Transcription

**Original (French):**

*[Left margin, vertical text:]*
SUR LE PONT DE LA MULATIERE.

*[Header ornament]*

Action de cinq cents livres sur le pont de la Mulatiere, produisant trente livres d'intérêt, avec une prime de cent livres lors du remboursement.

Nous, souffignés, Directeur, Tréforier, & Commiffaires de la Compagnie des Affociés aux travaux du midi de Lyon, duement autorisés à cet effet, reconnoiffons avoir reçu de M. la fomme de cinq cents livres, pour être employée aux travaux du pont de la Mulatière, fous les clauses & conditions.

1°. Que la préfente action eft fpécialement affectée fur les produits du péage dudit Pont.

2°. Que, fans deroger à cette hypotheque particuliere, le porteur jouira d'une hypotheque générale fur les biens de la Société, avec fubrogation expresse fur lefdits biens aux droits, privileges & hypotheques résultant, soit de l'emprunt de Gênes, montant à 1,500,000 livres, soit de l'avance de 350,000 livres, faite par Sa Majesté à la Compagnie, qui, ne pouvant être affranchie de ces deux créances qu'à la charge du Pont, déclare que sa libération fera l'ouvrage de ceux qui fourniront des fonds pour le construire, & qui, par conséquent, auront les mêmes droits sur l'entreprise que les anciens créanciers avoient acquis.

3°. Que ladite action produira trente livres d'intérêt, dont le premier paiement fera fait par M. le préfident de Fleurieu, tréforier de la Compagnie, dans un an, à dater de ce jour; le second, à la même époque de l'année fuivante, & ainsi de fuite.

4°. Qu'au cas que la Compagnie rachete ladire action, comme elle s'en réserve la faculté, le porteur en fera remboursé fur le pied de 600 l., c'est-à-dire, avec une prime de 100 l. en capital.

5°. Que, dans dix années au plus tard, à compter du 1.er Janvier 1790, il fera procédé chaque année, & par la voie du fort, au remboursement de vingt actions, jusqu'à l'extinction totale des actions qui auront été vendues, & dont le nombre n'excèdera pas 880.

A LYON;

*[Signatures below]*

---

## English Translation:

**Action for five hundred livres on the bridge of Mulatiere, producing thirty livres of interest, with a premium of one hundred livres upon redemption.**

We, the undersigned, Director, Treasurer, & Commissioners of the Company of Associates for the works of the south of Lyon, duly authorized for this purpose, acknowledge having received from M. the sum of five hundred livres, to be employed in the works of the Mulatière bridge, under the following clauses and conditions.

1°. That this present action is specially charged upon the revenue from tolls of the said Bridge.

2°. That, without derogating from this particular mortgage, the holder shall enjoy a general mortgage on the assets of the Company, with express subrogation on said assets to the rights, privileges & mortgages resulting, either from the Genoese loan, amounting to 1,500,000 livres, or from the advance of 350,000 livres, made by His Majesty to the Company, which, being unable to be freed from these two debts except at the charge of the Bridge, declares that its liberation shall be the work of those who shall provide the funds to construct it, & who, consequently, shall have the same rights on the enterprise as the former creditors had acquired.

3°. That the said action shall produce thirty livres of interest, the first payment of which shall be made by M. the president of Fleurieu, treasurer of the Company, within one year from this date; the second, at the same time in the following year, & so on.

4°. That in the case that the Company redeems the said action, as it reserves the faculty to do so, the holder shall be reimbursed at the rate of 600 l., that is to say, with a premium of 100 l. in capital.

5°. That, within ten years at the latest, commencing from the 1st of January 1790, there shall be proceeded each year, & by means of the lottery, to the redemption of twenty actions, until the total extinction of the actions which shall have been sold, & of which the number shall not exceed 880.

AT LYON;

---

## Notable Markings:

- **Serial Number:** No. 106 (handwritten in upper left margin)
- **Reference Numbers:** £506 and £506 (marked in left margin)
- **Signatures:** Four handwritten signatures at bottom:
  - Chard de [Fleurieu] (first signature)
  - George Sartore (second signature - partially legible)
  - Sartore (third signature)
  - Laurencin (fourth signature)
- **Date:** ca. 1780s (document context indicates dating)
- **Decorative ornaments:** Horizontal rule at top and bottom of title section

The President and Directors of the Mississippi Union Bank designate the Bank of the United States in London as payment agent and assign the bond for value received. Signed by Steven Cass (Cashier) and H. S. Runnels (President). Overprinted in red: 'Mississippi State Bond. No. 372. 450 pounds. Redeemable Feb. 5th, 1850.' Lower coupon sheet shows individual coupons for 'Eleven Pounds five shillings sterling, payable at agency Bank United States, London.'
creator: State of Mississippi / Mississippi Union Bank
notes: Reverse/back page of goetzmann0954 (Mississippi $2,000 bond); transfer by Mississippi Union Bank designating Agency Bank of the United States in London for payment; signed by Stevens (Cashier) and H.G. Runnels (President); labeled Mississippi State Bond No. 372, £450, redeemable Feb. 5, 1850; coupon stubs for £11 5s. sterling payable in London

**TO**

**OF THE**

*Stevens* [signature] *H.S Runnels* [signature]

title: Chanin Building (Lexington A. 42nd St. Corporation) First Mortgage Leasehold 5% Bond $100, No. MC530
description: First Mortgage Leasehold 5% Bond for $100, issued by Lexington A. 42nd St. Corporation (Chanin Building), 46 W. Lexington Avenue between 41st and 42nd Streets, New York City. Bond no. MC530, due September 1, 1955. Printed in blue with image of the skyscraper and decorative border. Signed and sealed. Page 1 of 2.

pages.description: Page 1 of 2 - Chanin Building signed by Irwin Chanin - New York 1945 Chanin Building - 122 E 42nd Street, Sloan & Robertson [1929]_x000B__x000B_Built as a monument to Irwin Chanin, a graduate of Cooper Union who became a major New York developer of the 1920s, this 56-story tower was erected in just 205 days. The base of the building is decorated with the flattened, abstract ornament characteristic of the Art Deco period. All of the ornament was custom-designed for the building including a floral bas relief in terra-cotta by Edward Trumbull._x000B__x000B_In contrast to its neighbor, Grand Central Terminal, the Chanin Building's Art Deco facade represents a turn away from the Beaux Arts style towards a more humanistic and modern imagery appropriate to the industrial age. In particular, the facade illustrates the introduction of colored glass, stone and metal on the exterior of tall buildings. Materials such as bronze, Belgian marble and terra-cotta are used here in an inventive and exuberant way. Inside the lobby, the walls are decorated with reliefs by set-designer Jacques Delamarre and Renee Chambellan that represent "The City of Opportunity" and "The Active Life of the Individual," the latter perhaps a reference to Chanin's own rise to power and wealth._x000B__x000B_Above the base, the building's steel frame is clad in buff brick and terra cotta and it is set back in conformance with the 1916 Zoning Law. At the top, it is capped by a dramatic crown that was once illuminated at night. To attract tenants, Chanin provided centralized services at the base of the building including an underground connection to Grand Central Terminal and ground-floor commercial spaces. A movie theater and a subterranean bus terminal were included in the original project but no longer remain. Chanin himself had his own offices on the 50th and 51st floors of the tower that were famous for a theater and an award-winning bathroom.

Page 2 of 2 - Chanin Building signed by Irwin Chanin - New York 1945 Chanin Building - 122 E 42nd Street, Sloan & Robertson [1929]_x000B__x000B_Built as a monument to Irwin Chanin, a graduate of Cooper Union who became a major New York developer of the 1920s, this 56-story tower was erected in just 205 days. The base of the building is decorated with the flattened, abstract ornament characteristic of the Art Deco period. All of the ornament was custom-designed for the building including a floral bas relief in terra-cotta by Edward Trumbull._x000B__x000B_In contrast to its neighbor, Grand Central Terminal, the Chanin Building's Art Deco facade represents a turn away from the Beaux Arts style towards a more humanistic and modern imagery appropriate to the industrial age. In particular, the facade illustrates the introduction of colored glass, stone and metal on the exterior of tall buildings. Materials such as bronze, Belgian marble and terra-cotta are used here in an inventive and exuberant way. Inside the lobby, the walls are decorated with reliefs by set-designer Jacques Delamarre and Renee Chambellan that represent "The City of Opportunity" and "The Active Life of the Individual," the latter perhaps a reference to Chanin's own rise to power and wealth._x000B__x000B_Above the base, the building's steel frame is clad in buff brick and terra cotta and it is set back in conformance with the 1916 Zoning Law. At the top, it is capped by a dramatic crown that was once illuminated at night. To attract tenants, Chanin provided centralized services at the base of the building including an underground connection to Grand Central Terminal and ground-floor commercial spaces. A movie theater and a subterranean bus terminal were included in the original project but no longer remain. Chanin himself had his own offices on the 50th and 51st floors of the tower that were famous for a theater and an award-winning bathroom.

transcription:
# Times Square 46th Street Building Bond Transcription

**SPECIMEN**
**No. N840**

---

## TIMES SQUARE
## 46TH STREET BUILDING

## 1556 BROADWAY CORPORATION

[Decorative header text - partially legible]
"...known as Nos. 1554-1556 Broadway and 169-171 West 46th Street in the Borough of Manhattan, City and State of New York."

---

## BOND TEXT (Main Body)

1556 BROADWAY CORPORATION, a corporation organized and existing under the laws of the State of New York (hereinafter called "the Corporation"), for value received, acknowledges itself indebted and hereby promises to pay to the bearer hereof, or if this bond be registered, to the registered holder hereof, on the first day of April, 1963 (unless this bond shall have been called for previous redemption as provided herein, and in the indenture hereinafter mentioned),

and to pay interest thereon at the rate of six per cent. (6%) per annum, payable semi-annually on the first day of April and the first day of October in each year, and such interest hereon until maturity hereof (unless this bond shall have been called for previous redemption) being payable only upon surrender of the interest coupons hereto attached as they severally mature.

This bond is one of a series of bonds issued under and pursuant to an indenture dated the principal office of Interstate Trust Company, in the Borough of Manhattan, City and State of New York, or at the option of the bearer or registered holder hereof, in the manner and upon the conditions and subject to the limitations provided in the indenture herein, any taxes which, under any present or future law of any State, Territory or possession of the United States of America, or of the District of Columbia, the bearer or holder hereof, as a resident of any said state, territory or possession, is, or shall be, obligated to pay by reason of the ownership hereof, upon or on account of the principal or interest of this bond, shall be paid by the Corporation, as an additional sum to the dollar of the principal amount hereof in any year, provided that application for such reimbursement shall be made to the Corporation as provided in the provisions above referred to by [illegible].

For the payment of any such tax, or in case default be made in the payment of any interest hereon as it First Mortgage Leasehold Sinking Fund Gold Bonds, all of like tenor (except as to designated number and denomination), issued and to be issued in an aggregate principal amount equal to Seven Million Four Hundred Thousand Dollars ($7,400,000) at any time outstanding, under the indenture dated the 1st day of April, 1928 (hereinafter referred to as the "Indenture"), and duly executed and delivered, mortgaging, transferring and conveying unto Irtus [Trustees?] of Columbia University in the City of New York, as lessor, to Bethlehem Engineering Corporation, as lessee, dated February [date], [illegible], mortgaging, transferring and conveying unto [Trustees?] of Columbia University in the City of New York as lessor to Bethlehem Engineering Corporation, as lessee, by assignment dated May 20, 1924, recorded in the Office of the Register of New York County on July 11, 1924 as Liber 3456 [illegible]; and also by leases from the Trustees of Columbia University, in the City of New York, as lessor, to Bethlehem [Engineering Corporation?], as lessee, all the real property situate, lying and being in the Borough of Manhattan, City and State of New York, and more particularly described in the mortgages aforesaid, together with all the improvements thereon, and all the appurtenances thereto, all of which real property is particularly described in the mortgage, and the rights and interests thereon is payable at the office of agency of the Corporation, in the Borough of Manhattan, City and State of New York, or at the option of the bearer or registered holder hereof, at the principal office of Interstate Trust Company, in the Borough of Manhattan, City and State of New York.

[The text continues with extensive legal terms regarding the sinking fund, redemption conditions, and bond holder rights. Much of this middle section is overprinted with red text that is largely illegible in the scan]

[Continuing from legible portions:]

...This bond shall pass by delivery and registration by the Trustee hereto until such registration by being in like manner transferred to bearer and thereupon transferability by delivery shall be restored, but this bond may again and from time to time be registered and transferred to bearer as before. The registered holder hereof shall be entitled to the benefit of the lien created by the mortgage hereinabove mentioned, which shall continue to be transferable by delivery, and payment to the bearer thereof shall discharge the Corporation with respect to the principal and interest thereon.

[Additional legal text regarding bond conditions, the Corporation's representations, and transferability...]

This bond shall not be entitled to any benefit hereof, and shall not be valid as against any purpose until it shall have been authenticated by the execution of the certificate hereon endorsed by Interstate Trust Company, under said indenture.

IN WITNESS WHEREOF, 1556 Broadway Corporation has caused this bond to be signed by its President or a Vice-President, and its corporate seal to be hereunto affixed and attested by a Secretary or an Assistant Secretary and coupons for such interest, bearing the facsimile signature of its Treasurer, to be hereto attached, all as of the first day of April, 1928.

1556 BROADWAY CORPORATION.

transcription:
# Transcription

UNITED STATES OF AMERICA
STATE OF NEW YORK

170 BROADWAY BUILDING
CORNER BROADWAY-MAIDEN LANE, INC.
FIRST MORTGAGE LEASEHOLD 4½% SINKING FUND GOLD BOND
No. B25

Secured by Leasehold Estate in the Improved Property at the Southeast Corner of Broadway and Maiden Lane in the Borough of Manhattan, City and State of New York.

CORNER BROADWAY-MAIDEN LANE, INC., a corporation organized and existing under the laws of the State of New York (hereinafter called the "Company") for value received acknowledges itself indebted and hereby promises to pay to the bearer hereof, or if this bond be registered, to the registered holder hereof, on the first day of May, 1948 [unless this bond shall have been called for previous redemption], the sum of

FIVE HUNDRED DOLLARS ($500)

and to pay interest thereon at the rate of four and one-half per cent (4½%) per annum payable semiannually on the first day of May and the first day of November in each year, and interest hereon until maturity hereof (unless this bond shall have been called for previous redemption) upon presentation and surrender hereof at the office of the Trustee, or at the principal office of the Company in the Borough of Manhattan, City and State of New York, in gold coin of the United States of America or of equal to the present standard of weight and fineness; without deduction for any normal federal income tax (except the excess, if any of any normal federal income tax imposed upon the holders of this bond by the United States government), and without deduction for any other tax, duty or governmental charge whatsoever.

The Trustee hereunder named, or any paying agent of the Company may be required or permitted to pay hereon or to deliver or receive hereunder under any present or future law of the United States of America.

If default shall be made in the payment of any installment of interest due on this bond on the date thereof or in the payment of the principal hereof, then the registered holder hereof may, in the sole discretion thereof, offer mentioned, reimburse such bearer or registered holder hereof who shall be a resident of the District of Columbia or of certain States of the United States of America referred to in said indenture, any and all taxes assessed or to be assessed upon such bond or the interest hereon, provided that no such obligation shall exist nor shall any such reimbursement shall be made to the Company, as provided in said indenture, within thirty (30) days after payment of such and all other charges shall not have become due and payable.

The Company also agrees, in accordance with the provisions of the Sinking Fund Gold Bonds, all of the bonds (save of the outstanding member 2nd debentures) issued and to be issued in an aggregate principal amount of not exceeding one hundred thousand dollars ($100,000) during the continuation of the indenture, under the lien and provisions thereof, dated May 1, 1925 (hereinafter referred to as the "Indenture"), and duly recorded and delivered, and conveying, with Trustees First Trust Company of New York, as Trustee, are upon the Indenture estate securities to hereby made to said indenture, as is full and complete in description of the property mortgaged, the nature and extent of the security hereby owned and the rights of the holders of said bonds and of the Trustee to enforce said security.

This bond shall pass by delivery unless registered as provided in the indenture or unless such registration be made at the office of the Trustee and unless such registration is made and cancelled, in which case registration shall be valid.

transcription:
# 170 BROADWAY BUILDING BOND - TRANSCRIPTION

**UNITED STATES OF AMERICA**
**STATE OF NEW YORK**

[EAGLE VIGNETTE]

**500** [left and right corners]

**170 BROADWAY BUILDING**

**CORNER BROADWAY-MAIDEN LANE, INC.**

**FIRST MORTGAGE LEASEHOLD 4½% SINKING FUND GOLD BOND**

No. **B25** Secured by Leasehold Estate in the Improved Property at the Southeast Corner of Broadway and Maiden Lane in the Borough of Manhattan, City and State of New York. No. **B25**

---

**CORNER BROADWAY-MAIDEN LANE, INC.**, a corporation organized and existing under the laws of the State of New York (hereinafter called the Company), for value received, hereby acknowledges itself indebted and hereby promises to pay to the bearer hereof, or if registered, to the registered holder hereof, at the office of **MANUFACTURERS TRUST COMPANY**, Trustee, in the Borough of Manhattan, City and State of New York, or at such other place as the Trustee may designate, on the **first day of May, 1975**, the principal sum of

**FIVE HUNDRED DOLLARS ($500)**

with interest thereon until maturity hereof (unless this bond shall have been called for previous redemption), payable semi-annually on the first day of May and the first day of November in each year, and interest hereon until maturity hereof (unless this bond shall have been called for previous redemption), at the rate of four and one-half per centum (4½%) per annum, on the presentation and surrender of the interest coupons hereto attached at the principal office of the Trustee in the Borough of Manhattan, City and State of New York.

The bonds are issued under and pursuant to the terms and conditions of a certain Indenture of Mortgage, Security Agreement and Indenture of Trust, dated as of the first day of May, 1952, executed and delivered by the Company to the **MANUFACTURERS TRUST COMPANY**, as Trustee (hereinafter called the Indenture), to which Indenture and the modification and amendment thereof (if any), reference is hereby made for a description of the property mortgaged, the nature and extent of the security for these bonds and the rights of the holders hereof, and for the definition of the terms herein used.

This bond is entitled to the benefit of a certain sinking fund established in the Indenture, and is subject to the conditions specified in said Indenture relative to the application of such sinking fund.

The principal of and interest on this bond are payable in gold coin of the United States of America of or equal to the present standard of weight and fineness without deduction for or on account of any tax or taxes which the Company, as the issuer on the principal thereof, may be required or permitted to pay or deduct therefrom.

The Company covenants, in the manner, upon the conditions and subject to the limitations provided in the Indenture hereto-fore mentioned, to redeem on such dates as therein set forth, any of these bonds issued by registered holder thereof who shall be a resident of the State of Columbia or of the United States of America, at the request of such registered holder by reason of such residence and the ownership of, or receipt of income from, this bond, provided that application for such redemption shall be made to the Company as provided in said Indenture, within forty (40) days after payment of the then last coupon.

This bond is one of a duly authorized issue of bonds of the Company known as its First Mortgage Leasehold 4½% Sinking Fund Gold Bond of the issue (unless and deduction) issued and to be issued in an aggregate principal amount not exceeding **FIVE MILLION DOLLARS ($5,000,000)**, all of which bonds are issued under and are secured, respectively, of the same date of issue, by the indenture of mortgage dated as of the first day of May, 1952, executed and delivered by the Company, and are entitled equally and ratably with all other bonds issued or to be issued under said Indenture to the benefits and security of the Indenture and the property mortgaged thereby.

Lessor to the Company, as Lessor, [unclear], or to be recorded in the office of the Register of the County of New York. Reference is hereby made to the Indenture, for full and complete description of the security mortgaged, the nature and extent of the security.

This bond is entitled to the benefit of a certain sinking fund established in the Indenture and, upon the operation of said sinking fund, the principal of this bond, or any installment of interest due on this bond or, the Worthlessness or observance thereof at the times hereinafter specified in said Indenture shall be applied toward such principal or interest, and in case of default in the payment of the principal hereof, be declared, void and ceased to become due [illegible] in the manner, at the time of times and with the effect provided in said Indenture.

The bond shall be subject to the Company's obligation to effect and set to the security, in addition to the issue and effect thereof. But this bond is a corporate obligation only and to secure it whatsoever, either directly or through any trustee, receiver, personal or other agent shall be liable on the payment of the principal of or interest on this bond, for the enforcement thereof, except as may be prescribed by law, except as may be prescribed, or the enforcement of the Company or, that American corporation as follows of any original or contribution or by the amendment of any assessment, penalty, subscription or limitation affecting obligations and provisions thereof, all as provided for in the Indenture.

This bond shall pass by delivery unless endorsed as to principal in the holder's name at the office of the Trustee and unless such endorsement shall not relieve the original holder from any liability hereunder, unless bonds by the registered holder in person or by duly authorized attorney and strictly noted herein, but this bond may be discharged from registration in lawver and thereafter transferred by delivery of the registered holder hereof shall be entitled to receive the principal hereof, but such registration shall not affect the negotiability of the interest coupons hereto attached, which coupons in themselves be transferable by delivery of registered holder hereof should the Company pay or discharge this bond held by the Trustee or held hereunder or by any person whomsoever for any purpose until it shall have been authenticated by the dropsignature or endorsement of the Trustee and underestimate, shall be in possession in the past trust.

**170 BROADWAY MAIDEN LANE, INC.** has caused this bond to be signed by the president or a vice-president and the corporate seal to be hereunto affixed, attested by its secretary or an assistant secretary, and coupon for such interest bearing the facsimile signature of its treasurer to be hereto affixed, all as of the first day of May, 1952.

---

**Attest:**

[SIGNATURE] **H. L. Darbo**
**Secretary**

[CORPORATE SEAL - circular with decorative border]

[SIGNATURE] **Charles R. Handa**
**President**

# Tyler Building Bond Certificate - Transcription

**No. M 1259**

**UNITED STATES OF AMERICA**

**Share or Part $1,000**

---

State of New York, City of New York

## TYLER BUILDING

**Nineteen John Street Corporation**

Certificate Representing a Share or Part in Nineteen John Street Corporation's

**FIRST MORTGAGE SIX PER CENT SINKING FUND GOLD LOAN**

Secured by the premises situated at 17 to 23 John Street in the Borough of Manhattan, City and State of New York.

---

THIS IS TO CERTIFY, that the bearer, hereof, or if this certificate be registered, the registered holder hereof, is the owner of a share or part of the principal sum of

**ONE THOUSAND DOLLARS ($1,000)**

In certain bonds and mortgages of or assumed by Nineteen John Street Corporation, a corporation existing under and by virtue of the laws of the State of New York (hereinafter called the "Mortgagor"), for an aggregate principal amount of Four Million Four Hundred Thousand Dollars ($4,400,000), executed and delivered to The New York Trust Company, as Trustee (hereinafter called the "Trustee"), and secured by a mortgage dated as of October 1, 1925, of the real property known as the Tyler Building, given to secure the Nineteen John Street Corporation's First Mortgage Six Per Cent Sinking Fund Gold Loan, bearing date as of October 1, 1925, and bearing interest from October 1, 1925.

The said bonds and mortgages are payable in gold coin of the United States of America of the present standard of weight and fineness, or in gold certificates, legal tender for gold coin, with accrued interest thereon, at the principal office of the Trustee in the City of New York.

This certificate shall be payable (unless the same shall have been called for redemption) in gold coin of the United States of America or in gold certificates as aforesaid, at the principal office of the Trustee in the City of New York, on the surrender thereof duly endorsed on the back hereof, and upon the surrender of the interest warrants hereto attached as they severally mature.

Both the principal amount herein expressed and the interest in respect thereof shall be payable at the principal office of the Trustee in the City of New York, provided that, at the option of the Trustee, payment of interest may be made by transfer to any banking house in the City of New York. [Text continues with extensive legal terms regarding bonds, mortgages, and conditions - significant portions are obscured by red stamp markings]

The principal of and the interest on this certificate, and all series thereof, shall be paid in full without deduction or diminution on account of any taxes, assessments, governmental charges or claims of any kind whatsoever, which now are or hereafter may be imposed by the United States or any State or Municipality therein, by any foreign Government, or by any other authority, upon the Trustee, the Mortgagee or the holder hereof...

[Extensive legal text continues regarding mortgage terms, conditions, prepayment provisions, and sinking fund requirements - much obscured by red administrative stamps and markings]

---

IN WITNESS WHEREOF, the undersigned has executed this certificate as of October 1, 1925.

**THE NEW YORK TRUST COMPANY.**

By

**Assistant Secretary**

Printed in green with residential estate vignette and decorative border. Signed by President and Treasurer. Due August 1, 1900. Trustee: The American Loan Trust Company. Page 1 of 3.

transcription:
# Transcription: Maplewood Suburban Home Company Bond

**UNITED STATES OF AMERICA**

**Maplewood Suburban Home Company**
*Incorporated 1890*

**6%**
**MORTGAGE SINKING FUND BOND**
**FIVE HUNDRED DOLLARS**

---

*This Maplewood Suburban Home Company, a corporation duly created and existing under the laws of the State of Maine, hereby acknowledges itself indebted and promises to pay to the bearer or registered owner, at the Chemical National Bank in the City of New York, on the first day of [illegible] in the year Nineteen Hundred [illegible], the sum of Five Hundred Dollars, with interest thereon at the rate of six per centum per annum, payable semi-annually on the first day of [illegible] and the first day of [illegible] in each year, upon presentation and surrender hereof.*

*The Maplewood Suburban Home Company will apply a Sinking Fund for the redemption of these bonds. [Several lines of legal text regarding the sinking fund and bond terms - partially illegible due to handwriting overlay]*

*In testimony whereof, the Maplewood Suburban Home Company has caused this bond to be signed by its President and Treasurer, and its corporate seal to be hereunto affixed, this [date] day of [month], 189[2].*

**Signed with the corporate seal of Maplewood Suburban Home Company and signed by its President and Treasurer, this 31st day of August, 189[2].**

[Signatures]
*President*
*Treasurer*

---

**Notable markings:**
- Brown wax seal with company insignia (lower left)
- Serial number visible: **$500**
- Ornamental green border throughout
- Capital Stock: $2,000,000 notation (upper right)
- Decorative building illustration at top center
- Handwritten annotations/signatures crossing the document

title: Dutch East India Company (VOC) – Middelburg Chamber Obligation Receipts, 1622–1623
description: Two early VOC (Vereenigde Oost-Indische Compagnie) financial documents from the Zeeland/Middelburg Chamber, photographed together. Top: a printed payment order form from the Ontfangers der Oost-Indische Compagnie Middelburg, with handwritten receipt acknowledging satisfaction of an obligation, signed by Dominicus van Hoontshoerle, dated Amsterdam/Middelburg, 9 November/December 1623. Bottom: a handwritten bond/obligation (obligatie) issued by the Reecken-meesters of the VOC Middelburg Chamber, for a sum of £1,333:6:8 Flemish at 6% per year, repayable after 44 months, signed by Jocvry[?] and Cornelis Jauckly, Middelburg ca. October 26, 1622.
creator: VOC (Vereenigde Oost-Indische Compagnie), Kamer Middelburg
notes: Dutch East India Company (VOC) – Middelburg Chamber Obligation Receipts, 1622–1623
transcription:
# Transcription of VOC Financial Documents (1622-1623)

## DOCUMENT 1 (Top Page)

**Original (Dutch):**

Ontfangersder Oost-Indische Compagnie gelieven te betalen
aen
de somme van
tot voldoeninghe vanden inhoudt deser Obligatie met den Intereste
van dien, synde vervallen den
Als te weten voor t'Capitael der selver Obligatie,          gl.
Ende voor         maenden Intereft van ditto Capitael
tegens              ten hondert int laer          gl.
Comt samen als vooren                                    gl.
Actum in Middelburgh.

[Handwritten section in brown ink:]
Ick ondergheftchreven bekenne vande boven-ftaende
[several lines of illegible/unclear handwriting]
voldaen te zijn, Actum in Middelburgh

Dominicus van Hoontshoerle

**English Translation:**

The Receivers of the East India Company are requested to pay
to
the sum of
for satisfying the contents of this Obligation with the Interest
thereof, which is due
Namely for the Capital of said Obligation,          gl. [guilders]
And for         months Interest of said Capital
at            per hundred per year          gl.
Total as above                                    gl.
Drawn up in Middelburgh.

[Handwritten receipt:]
I, the undersigned, acknowledge that the above-mentioned [sum]
[further text unclear]
has been paid to me, Drawn up in Middelburgh

Dominicus van Hoontshoerle

---

## DOCUMENT 2 (Bottom Page)

**Original (Dutch):**

Wy onderftchreven Reecken-meesters vande Camere der Ooft-Indifche Compagnie binnen Middelburgh, Bekennen midts defen
gheaccordeert te hebben, ende by den Ontfangers der voorftchreven Compagnie ontfanghen te welen, vanden Erfamen [?] ... de Somme van [?]
Welcke voorftchreven somme gan metren Interefse van dien jegens . . . ten hond. Int jaer gherekent, wy aen voorn. . . [several lines with heavy handwritten annotations and cross-outs]
oifte ligt, Actum in Middelburgh de[n] . . . Oct[ober] . . . 1622

£1333 : 6 : 8

[Signature: Jocvry? and Cornelis Jauckly]

**English Translation:**

We, the undersigned, Reckoning-masters of the Chamber of the East India Company within Middelburgh, do hereby acknowledge
that we have agreed and received by the Receivers of the aforesaid Company, from the Worthy [?] ... the sum of [?]
Which aforementioned sum shall be paid with Interest thereof at ... per hundred per year, being calculated, we as aforesaid [several lines with heavy handwritten amendments and deletions]
or otherwise, Drawn up in Middelburgh the ... October ... 1622

£1333 : 6 : 8

---

## Notable Markings:

- **Signatures:** Dominicus van Hoontshoerle (top document); Jocvry[?] and Cornelis Jauckly (bottom document)
- **Dates:** Top document dated 9 November/December 1623 (Amsterdam/Middelburgh); Bottom document dated October 26, 1622
- **Sum:** £1,333:6:8 Flemish (shown as "£1333 : 6 : 8" on bottom document)
- **Interest Rate:** 6% per annum
- **Annotations:** Both documents contain extensive handwritten annotations, amendments, and cross-outs in period handwriting
- **Serial marking:** "£1333 : 6 : 8" circled in lower left of bottom page
- **Archival markings:** Visible in upper right corner of top page

title: Monte di Pietà di Firenze – Assegnazione di Luoghi del Monte (Bond Certificate)
description: Official printed bond certificate (assegnazione di luoghi) issued by the Monte di Pietà of Florence, assigning units (luoghi) of the Monte's capital to a named holder. Each luogo is valued at 100 scudi fiorentini (at 7 lire per scudo) and pays 5 scudi annual interest. Dated January 11, 1647. The Monte di Pietà was a charitable lending institution established by order of the Grand Duke of Tuscany in 1616. Decorated with two allegorical figures flanking the Medici/Tuscan coat of arms. Carries wax seal and manuscript signatures of the Secretary and officials.
creator: Monte di Pietà della Città di Firenze
notes: Bond certificate assigning luoghi of the Florentine Monte di Pietà. Interest 5 scudi per luogo per year. Established under Grand Ducal senate ordinance of 1616.
transcription:
# Transcription

**Original (Italian):**

NOI Officiali del Monte di Pietà della Città di Firenze, Protettori, & Amministratori del Monte non vacabile eretto d'ordine del S. G. D. di Toscana nostro Sig. per la Prouisione publicata nell'Amplissimo Senato de' 48. il dì 23. di Aprile 1616. cediamo, & assegniamo à [manuscript: Mario di Iacopo della Pia] Luoghi [manuscript notation] di detto Monte di valuta di [scudi] cento per ogni luogo di lire sette per scudo moneta Fiorentina con i frutti à ragione di scudi cinque l'anno simili per ogni luogo da hoggi in auuenire. Il prezzo de' quali luoghi di detto Monte ci è stato pagato di contanui in mano al nostro Camarlingo generale in scudi [manuscript: scudi cento] come à Entrata Da [manuscript notation], di feniere credittore, e padrone di sudetti luoghi al libro di detto Monte eretto per detta Prouisione segnato D de' Liberti à pagarii i frutti di fei mesi in fei mesi la rata portione, e con i priuileggi, condizioni, e dichiarazioni di che in detta Prouisione, e non altrimenti, nè in altro modo. Et in virtù della presente ordinamo al Camarlingo generale del nostro Monte, che per i tempi farà, che paghi detti frutti di fei mesi in fei mesi, & in fede habbiamo fatta fare la presente, quale sarà lortocritta da vno de' nostri Officiali, e dal nostro Prouedittore, e Cancelliere, e sfigliata con il liolito figillo di detto Monte.

Data in Firenze nell'Offizio di detto Monte di Pietà il [manuscript: 11 Novembre 1647]

---

**English Translation:**

We, Officials of the Monte di Pietà of the City of Florence, Protectors and Administrators of the perpetual Monte established by order of His Serene Highness, the Grand Duke of Tuscany, our Lord, for the Provision published in the Most Serene Senate of 48, on the 23rd day of April 1616, do hereby cede and assign to [Mario di Iacopo della Pia] Places of the said Monte with a value of one hundred scudi for each place at seven lire per scudo in Florentine currency, with interest at the rate of five scudi per year for each place from this day forward. The price of which places of the said Monte has been paid to us in cash into the hands of our general Treasurer in scudi [one hundred scudi], as Entry, [notation], as creditor owner and proprietor of the said places in the ledger of the said Monte established for that Provision marked D of the Liberti, to be paid the interest every six months at the aforesaid rate and portion, and with the privileges, conditions, and declarations contained in that Provision and in no other manner. And by virtue of this present document we hereby order the general Treasurer of our Monte that for the time being he shall pay the said interest every six months, and in witness thereof we have caused this document to be made, which shall be signed by one of our Officials and by our Provision Officer and Chancellor, and sealed with the holy seal of the said Monte.

Given in Florence in the Office of the said Monte di Pietà on [11 November 1647]

---

**Notable markings:**

- **Coat of arms**: Medici/Tuscan coat of arms with crown at top center
- **Allegorical figures**: Two classical female figures (likely Charity and Justice) flanking the coat of arms on either side
- **Wax seal**: Large wax seal impression visible in lower left portion
- **Manuscript annotations**: Multiple handwritten insertions and notations in the printed text, indicating names and specific details filled in by hand
- **Signatures**: Manuscript signatures visible at bottom right [illegible due to image quality]
- **Serial/reference notations**: Various manuscript numbers and abbreviations throughout

title: De Woning-Maatschappij, Batavia – ƒ100 Bearer Share No. 1499
description: Bearer share certificate (aan toonder) No. 1499 for ƒ100 of the Naamloze Vennootschap De Woning-Maatschappij (Housing Company Ltd.), incorporated in Batavia, Dutch East Indies. Total capital ƒ500,000 divided into 5,000 shares of ƒ100 each. Incorporated by notarial deed of February 27, 1908 before Notary H. Schotel in Batavia (No. 62); articles amended January 20, 1909 before Notary E.H. Carpenter & Alting (No. 81) and approved by Government Resolution February 18, 1909 No. 31.
creator: De Woning-Maatschappij
notes: Colonial Dutch East Indies housing company bearer share, Batavia (Jakarta). ƒ100 share No. 1499, out of 5,000 total shares. Company established 1908.
transcription:
# Transcription

**Original (Dutch):**

NAAMLOZE
VENNOOTSCHAP
DE WONING-MAATSCHAPPIJ
GEVESTIGD TE BATAVIA

Opgericht bij acte, den 27sten Februari 1908 onder No. 82 voor den t. v. Notaris H. SCHOTEL te Batavia verleden, op welke acte bewilliging is verleend bij Gouvernements-Besluit van den 9de Maart 1908, No. 39; zijnde de statuten in haar geheel gewijzigd bij acte, den 25ste Januari 1909 onder No. 81 voor den Notaris E. H. CARPENTIER ALTING te Batavia verleden, goedgekeurd bij Gouvernements-Besluit dd. 18 Februari 1909 No. 31.

Maatschappelijk Kapitaal ƒ 500.000.—

verdeeld in 5000 aandelen, elk groot ƒ 100.—

Ingevolge artikel 5 der Statuten luidende

AAN TOONDER

No 1499

Groot ƒ 100.—

[Signatures]
Commissaris.                    Directeur.

---

**English Translation:**

LIMITED LIABILITY COMPANY
HOUSING COMPANY
ESTABLISHED IN BATAVIA

Incorporated by deed, February 27, 1908 under No. 82 before Notary H. SCHOTEL in Batavia, to which deed approval was granted by Government Resolution of March 9, 1908, No. 39; the articles having been amended in their entirety by deed, January 25, 1909 under No. 81 before Notary E. H. CARPENTER ALTING in Batavia, approved by Government Resolution of February 18, 1909 No. 31.

Company Capital ƒ 500,000.—

divided into 5000 shares, each valued at ƒ 100.—

Pursuant to Article 5 of the Articles

BEARER

No 1499

Value ƒ 100.—

---

**Notable markings:**

- Two signatures at bottom: one labeled "Commissaris" (Commissioner), one labeled "Directeur" (Director)
- Ornamental Art Nouveau border design in burgundy/red
- Decorative vignette showing colonial building with palm trees (top left)
- Serial number: **1499**
- Share denomination: **ƒ 100**
- Total company capital: **ƒ 500,000** (5,000 shares × ƒ 100)

title: Generale Keyserlyche Indische Compagnie (Ostend Company) – Share Subscription Receipt, 250 Guilden, Antwerp 1723
description: Share subscription receipt from the Generale Keyserlyche Indische Compagnie (General Imperial Indian Company, the Ostend Company), directing cashier Jan Baptist Cegels junior to receive 250 guilden from a subscriber as first payment on company capital, per the terms of the Imperial Charter. Dated August 13, 1723 in Antwerp. Bears multiple subsequent payment endorsements (October 1723, December 1723, November 1726) documenting installment payments. The Ostend Company (1722–1731) was the Habsburg East India trading company, chartered by Emperor Charles VI.
creator: Generale Keyserlyche Indische Compagnie
notes: Ostend Company share subscription receipt, 250 guilden, Antwerp 1723. Multiple payment endorsements through 1726. Ostend Company chartered 1722, suppressed 1731.
transcription:
# Transcription

**Original (Dutch):**

O. 1198

De Directeurs van de generale Keyserlyche Indische compagnie, ordonneren aen hunnen cassier Joan Baptist Cogels junior, te ontfangen van D.M. Joseph Bergkin in Wernen de Somme van twee hondert en vijftigh guldens wissel gelt, voor het eerste payement Syndercactie vaneenduijsent guldens in het Capitael vande Selve compagnie, op de conditien in het Octroy breeder Vermelt, Stellende quitantie hier onder, actum in Antwerpen derthien Augusti Seventhien hondert drij en twintiagh

Ontfangenvan D.Heer Jodeph Bergkin de Somme van twee hondert vijftigh guldens Wissel gelt voor het eerste paijement adje 8. October 1723

Solvit als boven het 2de kayement 8. December 1723

Solvit als boven d'Helft van het 3de Bayement 13 decemb. 1724

Solvit als boven onder de helft van het Derde Rayement 2. Novemb. 1725

**English translation:**

The Directors of the General Imperial Indian Company order their cashier Joan Baptist Cogels junior to receive from D.M. Joseph Bergkin in Wernen the sum of two hundred and fifty guilden in exchange money, for the first payment of one thousand guilden in the Capital of the same company, under the conditions more broadly set forth in the Charter, granting receipt hereof, executed in Antwerp the thirteenth of August seventeen hundred and twenty-three.

Received from D. Mr. Joseph Bergkin the sum of two hundred fifty guilden in exchange money for the first payment on 8 October 1723.

He paid as above the 2nd payment 8 December 1723.

He paid as above the half of the 3rd payment 13 December 1724.

He paid as above the remaining half of the third payment 2 November 1725.

**Notable markings:**

- Coat of arms (double-headed eagle with Imperial insignia) at top center
- Reference number "O. 1198" (upper left)
- Multiple signatures in cursive script (right margin):
  - Corveyaard
  - Jacomo de Poli
  - Peroletti
  - B. Bogells Lame (appears multiple times on payment endorsements)
- Handwritten payment endorsements with dates: October 8, 1723; December 8, 1723; December 13, 1724; November 2, 1725
- Aged, cream-colored parchment or paper with visible wear and foxing

title: Generale Keyserlyche Indische Compagnie (Ostend Company) – Payment Receipt, 300 Florins, Antwerp 1726
description: French-language payment receipt from the Directors of the Generale Keyserlyche Indische Compagnie (Ostend Company) for 300 florins, issued in Antwerp on January 24, 1726. Bears the company's coat of arms at top, multiple director signatures, and administrative endorsements. The Ostend Company (1722–1731) was the Habsburg-chartered East India trading company based in the Austrian Netherlands.
creator: Generale Keyserlyche Indische Compagnie
notes: French-language Ostend Company payment receipt, 300 florins, Antwerp January 24, 1726. See also goetzmann1029 (Dutch-language receipt, 1723).
transcription:
# Transcription

**Original (French):**

Les Directeurs de la Compagnie générale des Indes, déclarent avoir engagé au fixe à l'édifier Compagnie à X[?] Jean d'Jonckhe, en qualité d'Anguinoir mise hardi, quer faire le Commerce à l'Ordonpolis derno justification, lui, Worden pour tow appointemens le ferme de Mil deus Ecu [?] form augna deChange spir le Voyage, al privilege Despouvoir raporter are lui fros Caisse de la Démetez – à bien entendu que les ordinarie des Caipes est Aadoirs [?] pour les hierdes en riter Caipus fablem Bayoqs; telle at la countertion faite are lui, es a Rogaihe de la Grandeur S—d pourvoit continer l l sengaps per for demnafuit—O

Drest au Bureau générel de la Compagnie
à Anvers le 24 Janvier 1726.

Detail de generall Vicer
Dorien Schinell
Louis Demousae
Ordonen don Figuerdt Doledat
Et Jean Jonghe funim

Par Ordonnance de Messire
Le Directeur
[Illegible signature]

---

**English Translation:**

The Directors of the General Company of the Indies declare to have engaged at a fixed rate for the Company to [?] Jean d'Jonckhe, in the capacity of [?] hard, to conduct commerce at [?] for its justification, him, [?] for all appointments in the form of One Thousand Two Hundred Ecu[?] form of Exchange for the Voyage, with privilege to report to him from the Company's Cashier – it being understood that the ordinary [?] of the Caipes for the orders to [?] the Caipes [?] Bayoqs; such being the agreement made with him, is by request of the [?] – [shall] be able to continue engagement by [?] –O

Drawn at the General Bureau of the Company
at Antwerp on 24 January 1726.

Detail [?] General Vicar
[Signature]
[Signature]
[Signature]
[Signature]
And Jean Jonghe [?]

By Order of Sir
The Director
[Signature]

---

**Notable markings:**

- Coat of arms (Imperial Austrian Eagles and crossed symbols) at top left
- Document number "N° 7" in top left corner
- Multiple signatures of company directors and officials
- Date: 24 Janvier 1726 (January 24, 1726)
- Location: Anvers (Antwerp)
- Amount: 300 Florins (referenced in title)
- Handwritten administrative notations throughout
- Various illegible passages due to cursive handwriting and document age

title: Monte di Pietà di Firenze – Assegnazione di Luoghi del Monte (Bond Certificate)
description: Bond certificate (assegnazione di luoghi) of the same printed type as goetzmann1027, issued by the Monte di Pietà of Florence. Assigns units (luoghi) of the Monte's capital to a named holder; each luogo valued at 100 scudi fiorentini (at 7 lire per scudo), paying 5 scudi annual interest. A separate issuance to a different assignee using the same pre-printed form authorized under the Grand Ducal senate ordinance of 1616. Carries wax seal and manuscript signatures.
creator: Monte di Pietà della Città di Firenze
notes: Second Monte di Pietà bond of same printed type as goetzmann1027; different assignee. Interest 5 scudi per luogo per year.
transcription:
# Transcription

**Original:**

NOI Offiziali del Monte di Pietà della Città di Firenze, Protettori, & Amministratori del Monte non vacabile retto d'ordine del S.G.D. di Toscana nostro Sig. per la Prouisione publicata nell'Amplissimo Senato de' 48. il dì 24.di Aprile 1616. cediamo, & assegniamo a [manuscript: Girolamo da Montauto?] Luoghi quattro è[?] mille di detto Monte di valuta di scudi cento per ogni luogo di lire sette per scudo moneta Fiorentina con i frutti a ragione di scudi cinque l'anno simili per ogni luogo da hoggi in auuenire. Il prezzo de' quali luoghi di detto Monte ci è stato pagato di contanti in mano al nostro Camarlingo generale in scudi [manuscript: Duecento cinquanta come è Entrata D a 300]

E noi l'habbiamo fatto descrivere creditor, e padrone di sudetti luoghi al libro di detto Monte ereto per detta Prouisione segnato D de' Liberi à per pagarli i frutti di fei mesi in fei mesi la rata porzione, e con i priulegij, condizioni, e dichiarazioni di che in detta Prouisione, e non altrimenti, nè in altro modo. Et in virtù della presente ordinamo al Camarlingo generale del nostro Monte, che per i tempi sarà, che paghi detti frutti di fei mesi in fei mesi, & in fede habbiamo fatta fare la presente, quale farà sottoscritta da vno de' nostri Offiziali, e dal nostro Prouedutore, e Cancelliere, e figillata con il solito sigillo di detto Monte.

Data in Firenze nell'Offizio di detto Monte di Pietà il [manuscript: undecimo giorno]. [manuscript: 1622?]

[Manuscript signatures and notations appear at bottom]

**English Translation:**

We, Officials of the Monte di Pietà of the City of Florence, Protectors and Administrators of the perpetual Monte, established by order of His Grand Ducal Highness of Tuscany, our Lord, for the Provision published in the Most Ample Senate of 48, on the 24th day of April 1616, do grant and assign to [Girolamo da Montauto?] four thousand places (luoghi) of the said Monte, valued at one hundred scudi each, at the rate of seven lire per scudo in Florentine currency, with interest at the rate of five scudi per year, in like manner for each place, from this day forward. The price of these places of the said Monte has been paid to us in cash into the hands of our general Camerlengo (treasurer) in scudi [two hundred fifty, entered as 300].

And we have caused this to be written and registered as creditor and owner of the said places in the book of the said Monte established for the said Provision, marked D of the Books, to be paid the interest every six months at the proportional rate, with the privileges, conditions, and declarations contained in the said Provision, and not otherwise, nor in any other manner. And by virtue of this present [ordinance] we do command the general Camerlengo of our Monte, whosoever shall hold that office for the time being, to pay said interest every six months, and in witness thereof we have caused this present [document] to be made, which shall be subscribed by one of our Officials and by our Provost (Provveditore) and Chancellor, and sealed with the usual seal of the said Monte.

Given in Florence in the Office of the said Monte di Pietà on the [eleventh day], [1622?]

**Notable markings:**

- Decorative coat of arms with crown at top center
- Two woodcut illustrations of allegorical figures (left and right sides)
- Wax seal impression (lower left)
- Multiple manuscript annotations and signatures at bottom
- Date notation in manuscript: appears to reference 1622
- Name of assignee in manuscript: [unclear, possibly "Girolamo da Montauto" or similar]
- Amounts and details filled in manuscript

title: North American Land Company – 10 Shares Certificate No. 15, Philadelphia 1795
description: Share certificate No. 15 of the North American Land Company, certifying that James Greene is entitled to ten shares in the entire property of the Company, with a guaranteed minimum annual dividend of Six Dollars per share, per the Articles of Agreement dated February 7, 1795 in Philadelphia. Signed at Philadelphia on March 10, 1795 by James Marshall (Secretary) and Robert Morris (President). Transferable only at the Company's Philadelphia office by the owner in person or legal representative. Robert Morris (1734–1806) was the principal financier of the American Revolution.
creator: North American Land Company
notes: No. 15, 10 shares, min. $6/share annual dividend. Signed by Robert Morris (President) and James Marshall (Secretary). Morris was a principal financier of the American Revolution.
transcription:
# Transcription

No. 15 representing Witnesses
from 1st to 130.

This is to Certify, that James Greenleaf
is entitled to ten __________ Shares in the entire Property
of the NORTH AMERICAN LAND COMPANY, the Dividend whereof shall not
be less than Six Dollars, on each Share Annually, conformable to
Articles of Agreement Duly Executed, dated at PHILADELPHIA
the twentieth __ day of February 1795, Transferable only at the
Company's Office in that CITY, by the Owner in Person, or by his Executor,
Administrator, Attorney, or legal Representative, Signed in the presence,
and by Order of the Board of Managers, at Philadelphia, this tenth
day of March— One thousand seven hundred and ninety five

James Marshall Secretary B. Morris President—

---

## Notable markings:

- **Signatures**: James Marshall (Secretary), B. Morris/Robert Morris (President)
- **Serial number**: No. 15
